# Resonance Dream IDEAS — living queue

Each idea has a status (`queued` / `in-progress` / `demoable` / `polished` / `dead`),
a slug (becomes the route `/dream/<n>-<slug>`), and a spec terse enough for
the Dream Agent to build from. Add new ideas to the bottom; promote
items by editing status in place.

The agent appends new ideas here from research cycles. Karel adds ideas
via Claude Code conversation; assistant transcribes into this file.

---

## §486 (Cycle 486 — kids WIDE fire, 2 banked explorers)

- **`763-kids-flower-duet`** ⭐ **RESURRECT FIRST (kids).** *Two kids (or parent + kid) plant a sunny flower meadow TOGETHER with two fingers at once — every flower sings, and any two flowers ALWAYS harmonize, so playing together always sounds beautiful.* INPUT = **multi-touch** (each finger tracked by `pointerId`, plants its own flower, live finger-rings so two kids see each other) · OUTPUT = **Canvas2D** (bright meadow: smiling sun, clouds, growing flowers, butterflies, rainbow bridges) · TECH = each touch plants a flower that grows + rings a soft marimba/bell note; **every pitch snapped to a 3-octave C-major-pentatonic so any combination from either player is always consonant** ("no wrong chord together"); flower color = pitch; two touches close together → a rainbow bridge + sparkle-chord reward · VIBE = bright sunny meadow. **Why it's the resurrect-first:** it fills the explicit **KIDS.md social-bonding / two-player gap** (the highest-value kids interaction in the design research — joint attention, turn-taking) AND it is **the most robust piece of the fire** — pure Canvas2D, *no sensor / no permission / no WebGL dependency at all*, works on literally any device including Karel's 06:30 review phone; degrades to perfect solo single-touch + a ~3s ghost-hand two-flower auto-chord. Self-reported tsc + eslint clean (separate `audio.ts` engine module). Refs: social-bonding/group-synchrony early-childhood-music research · Toca Boca / Sago Mini cooperative play · Toca Band "no wrong note." Ambition honest 2/5 (#2 ≥3 subsystems + #3 named refs; the social/multi-touch layer is the differentiator). **Only lost** because (a) its core *tap→flower→sings-pentatonic* is the lab's single most-worn kids pattern (211/236/205/188…), and (b) Canvas2D is warming to 3–4× while the winner's SVG/DOM + tilt was the fresher diversity pick. The moment Canvas2D ages out of the window, this is the pick — robust, social, bright. Built clean at `src/app/dream/763-kids-flower-duet/` then `rm -rf`'d per the orchestration rule (seed is text, never a half-built folder); rebuild from this spec.

- **`762-kids-balloon-band`** (kids, banked). *Press-and-hold to INFLATE a balloon whose pitch glides UP as it grows — let go, and the balloons float up and stack into a happy consonant chord you can POP.* INPUT = **touch / press-and-hold** (pointer events; hold to inflate, release to let go, tap a floating balloon to pop) · OUTPUT = **three.js** (real 3D balloon meshes — squashed `SphereGeometry` + `MeshStandardMaterial` under hemisphere+directional sunlight, sun disc, drifting cloud groups; **geometry, NOT a fullscreen shader**) · TECH = **size→pitch** (a held balloon's growing radius glides its pitch upward across E3→E5, the inflating-balloon glissando), release snaps to C-major-pentatonic + sustains, released voices accrete into a consonant stacked sky-chord, tap = soft "pop" (band-passed noise blip + tiny up-sine) + confetti, voice fades; 7-voice cap · VIBE = bright blue-sky daylight, fat glossy candy balloons, exuberant. Kid-safe `master 0.28 → lowpass 7000 → comp(−10/20:1)`; always-on C3+G3 pad; ~3s ghost auto-demo inflates/releases/pops hands-free; full three.js teardown (`renderer.dispose()` + `forceContextLoss()`). Refs: inflating-balloon-glissando pitch metaphor · Toca Band / Sago Mini · consonant-chord accretion. Ambition honest 2/5 (#2 ≥3 subsystems + #3 named refs). **Lost on robustness:** its WebGL-failure path currently only surfaces a `text-rose-300` notice — **NO working fallback** (dead piece if WebGL fails on the review device), and three.js is warming (2× recent). **Resurrect requirement: add a real Canvas2D billboard fallback** (inflate + pop + sound with no WebGL) BEFORE shipping; then it's a lovely bright/joyful piece. Built at `src/app/dream/762-kids-balloon-band/` then `rm -rf`'d per the orchestration rule; rebuild from this spec + the fallback fix.

## §485 — 2026-06-19 · adult WIDE fire (3 divergent explorers, each off every JURY 2026-06-19 ban — grain-cloud TECH 7× · GPU-shader-field OUTPUT 9× · mic/camera INPUT 4× each · near-dark-glow VIBE 9×; orthogonal in INPUT × TECHNIQUE × his-music-usage × VIBE) — winner `760-wiki-carillon`; **2 banked seeds**

Cycle 485 ran an adult **WIDE** fan-out (RESEARCH §485: the live real-time data-sonification register — Helioradar AV [NOAA space-weather → infinite non-looping ambient] + KLING KLANG KLONG *Sounds of the Unseen* [~2M migration points → never-repeating 3-day journey] — "bind a live real-world signal to a never-repeating generative ensemble" is the active 2025–2026 lane). The dive's negative-result job killed three candidate briefs: **Nancarrow/polytempo is TAKEN** (`375-tempo-canon`/`514-polytempo-loom`/`632-polymeter-loom`), **weather sonification TAKEN** (`502-atmosphere-veil`), **score-following TAKEN** (`380`/`391`/`748`). All three surviving briefs cleared every jury ban. **Winner: `760-wiki-carillon`** — the boldest anti-monoculture move (the only candidate free of the his-piano cluster the jury called "used exactly one way" 7×), the literal JURY #2 answer (Canvas2D, the scarce no-shader surface), a lab-first input (live SSE), and the highest-surprise. Both losers were demoable + self-reported clean tsc/eslint; `rm -rf`'d before the authoritative winner-only `npm run build` (compile/type/lint clean, zero issues in 760; static-gen blocked by the container's ~4096-fd EMFILE — proven environmental THIS cycle: pristine `origin/main` with 760 moved aside fails at the identical font-manifest open). **Max committed folder after this fire is `760`.** Resurrect by re-spawning the brief:

### `758-sky-almanac` — `queued` ⭐ RESURRECT FIRST (the strongest banked seed of the fire — highest ambition floor 3–4/5, the calmest most-beautiful zero-interaction unattended glance, and his-music-used-a-new-way [whole phrases, NOT grains]; pick it the moment the his-piano cluster ages out of the 10-window). **What if the SKY composed with Karel's piano? — the local clock + the computed positions of the sun and moon conduct a slow, ever-changing, NON-LOOPING arrangement of whole PHRASES from his real *Welcome Home* recording; minute 5 is not minute 1.** Built files (self-reported clean tsc/ESLint; removed): `page.tsx` (Start gate = iOS audio unlock + geolocation request w/ San-Francisco 37.77/-122.42 default; animated **SVG sun-arc dome** with altitude-driven sky gradient + sun's full daily track + altitude rings + phase-aware moon + a 12-tick phrase ring that lights as phrases play; **Live-clock / scrub-the-time-of-day** slider to A/B noon vs midnight; fallback + default-location notices; design-notes panel; full teardown) + `solar.ts` (pure-JS astronomy: solar declination [Cooper], equation of time, hour angle, `altitude = asin(sin·lat·sin·decl + cos·lat·cos·decl·cos·H)`, azimuth, daylight fraction, synodic moon phase + illuminated fraction; altitude→warm-gold→indigo palette) + `audio.ts` (verbatim `fetchPianoBuffer` loader + `renderFallbackBuffer` ~16s warm detuned-piano lyrical phrase + `buildPhraseRegions` evenly-spaced continuous regions; a self-rescheduling **phrase scheduler** playing `bufferSource.start(when, offset, duration)` — NOT granular; sun altitude → lowpass brightness/register, daylight fraction → density/tempo, moon phase → reverb wash + slow detune drift). **Tags:** clock + optional-geolocation INPUT (auto-evolving, zero required interaction) · SVG sun-arc-orrery OUTPUT (no shader) · local-astronomical-computation → whole-phrase scheduling of his real recording, long-form/stateful/non-looping TECH · diurnal/almanac/DAYLIGHT VIBE (the directest anti-dark-glow). **Named refs:** John Cage *Atlas Eclipticalis* · Jem Finer *Longplayer* · the almanac tradition. **Why it lost (a hair):** the highest ambition floor and the best *unattended* glance, but it is a *third* his-piano-phrase ship (after `748-duet-follower` + the related banked `750`) right when the jury's loudest complaint is the his-piano monoculture — so `760` (free of his piano entirely) was the bolder diversity move. **Resurrect FIRST** for the next adult slot once the his-piano cluster ages out, or merge its astronomy engine into any long-form piece. (NOTE: supersedes the older `750-sky-almanac` §481 seed — this is the richer realization at a fresh number.) Honest 3–4/5 (#1 first celestial/solar-position sonification of his recording + long-form stateful · #2 ≥4 subsystems [solar compute + phrase scheduler + region playback + reverb + SVG orrery] · #3 named refs · #5 RESEARCH §485).

### `759-phrase-loom` — `queued` (the literal JURY 2026-06-19 #1 answer — "use his recording without shattering it into a grain cloud: remix its PHRASING, not its grains"; resurrect for an interactive/editorial adult slot). **What if you could re-weave Karel's actual performance into an ever-new arrangement by remixing its PHRASES — never its grains? — his recording is split into whole phrases (~3–8s continuous regions); a Markov transition model reweaves them into a generative, never-the-same arrangement you STEER by dragging a luminous SVG phrase-timeline.** Built files (self-reported clean tsc/ESLint; removed): `page.tsx` (Start gate + SVG phrase-timeline ribbon [block height=energy, hue=tone] + drag-to-pin steering + wander/density sliders + live playhead sweep + violet woven thread tracing the last few chosen phrases + design-notes panel + full teardown) + `audio.ts` (verbatim `fetchPianoBuffer` + `renderFallbackBuffer` ~18s with distinct sub-phrases + `segmentPhrases` energy/rest-based split into 6–10 whole regions with even-slice fallback; each voice a continuous `bufferSource.start(when, offset, duration)` joined by equal-power cosine crossfades) + `weave.ts` (pure Markov: transition matrix [neighbour bias + tonal attraction + energy pull], temperature/"wander" shaping, pinned-phrase pull, density→overlap/crossfade math, Mulberry32 RNG). **Tags:** touch/drag + sliders INPUT (no mic/camera) · SVG/DOM phrase-timeline OUTPUT (no shader) · phrase-level Markov reweaving of whole recording regions + equal-power crossfade TECH (explicitly NOT granular) · bright editorial/architectural VIBE. **Named refs:** Brian Eno generative/systems music · Burroughs/Bowie cut-up technique · Markov-chain composition. **Why it lost:** the directest hit on the jury's literal "remix his phrasing" wording, but it is interaction-first (less striking on an unattended glance than `758`'s autoplay) AND a 3rd his-piano ship into the monoculture window. **Resurrect when** an interactive/editorial adult slot is wanted, or merge its phrase-Markov weave into `758` (let the sky bias a phrase-Markov rather than a simple scheduler). Honest 2–3/5 (#2 ≥3 subsystems [segmentation + Markov weave + crossfade region playback + SVG timeline] + #3 named refs; soft #1 — user-steered phrase-Markov of his real recording).

## §481 — 2026-06-19 · adult WIDE fire (3 divergent explorers, each a DIFFERENT non-grain way to use Karel's real recording × a non-shader SVG/DOM output × a non-mic/non-camera input × a non-dark-glow vibe — the textbook attack on JURY 2026-06-19's four hardened monocultures: grain-cloud TECH 7×, GPU-shader-field OUTPUT 9×, mic-hum INPUT 4×, near-dark-glow VIBE 9×) — winner `748-duet-follower`; **2 banked seeds**

Cycle 481 ran an adult **WIDE** fan-out (RESEARCH §481: real-time score-following / automatic accompaniment is a live 2024–2026 lane — Matchmaker arXiv 2510.10087, Structure-Aware Piano Accompaniment arXiv 2602.15074, edge-device accompaniment Springer 2026 — and the lab has used his recording only as visuals or grains, never as a *score you play along with*). All three briefs cleared every one of the jury's four bans: his recording used as **whole continuous phrases/performance, NOT a CataRT grain cloud**; **SVG/DOM output, zero GPU shader**; **no mic, no camera** input; and a **non-dark-glow vibe**. The orthogonality was in INPUT × TECHNIQUE × VIBE: tap-pulse/score-follow/warm-duet · speech-ASR/phrase-arrange/literary-paper · time-data/sky-conduct/daylight. **Winner: `748-duet-follower`** — highest ambition floor (3–4/5), the visible today's-research→today's-build chain, the cleanest lab-first (his recording as a *followed performance*), and genuinely interactive/live-performance-fit. Both losers were demoable + self-reported clean tsc/eslint; `rm -rf`'d before the authoritative winner-only `npm run build` (compile/type/lint clean, zero issues in 748; static-gen blocked by the container's 4096-fd EMFILE — proven environmental THIS cycle: pristine `main` with 748 moved aside fails at the identical font-manifest open). **Max committed folder after this fire is `748`.** NOTE: the §480 folderless banked seed-names `748-kids-aurora-silk` / `749-kids-shadow-garden` now collide with this fire's committed/used numbers — they take the next free folder numbers when resurrected (the standing "renumber if a later fire consumes them first" rule). Resurrect the seeds below by re-spawning the brief:

### `750-sky-almanac` — `queued` ⭐ RESURRECT FIRST (lands TWO thin categories at once — long-form/STATEFUL + a daylight vibe — and the only sibling with zero required interaction, so it is the strongest unattended-glance candidate). **What if the SKY composed with Karel's piano? — the current time of day + the sun's position (computed locally, no network) conduct a slow, ever-changing arrangement of PHRASES from his real *Welcome Home* recording; minute 5 is not minute 1.** Built files (self-reported clean tsc/ESLint; removed): `page.tsx` (Start gate = iOS audio unlock + geolocation request w/ San-Francisco 37.77/-122.42 default; animated **SVG sun-arc dial** with sky-driven gradient palette + sun/moon glyph following computed altitude/azimuth + phrase-flash rings; **Live-clock / Explore-time-of-day** toggle with a time slider to A/B noon vs midnight; fallback + default-location notices; design-notes panel; full teardown) + `solar.ts` (pure-JS NOAA-style math: solar declination from day-of-year, equation of time, hour angle, `altitude = asin(sin·lat·sin·decl + cos·lat·cos·decl·cos·H)`, azimuth, daylight fraction, moon phase = days-since-known-new-moon mod 29.53 + illuminated fraction; `dateAtHour` for the scrub) + `audio.ts` (`fetchPianoBuffer` + offline ~16s fallback phrase + `buildAlmanacEngine`: a self-rescheduling **phrase scheduler** playing continuous buffer regions `start(at,offset,duration)` — NOT granular; sun altitude → lowpass brightness + register, daylight fraction → density/tempo, moon phase → reverb wash + detune drift; master per-phrase gain → panner → master 0.3 → analyser(+reverb send) → lowpass → comp → dest). **Tags:** time/clock + optional-geolocation INPUT (auto-evolving, no required interaction) · SVG sun-arc-dial OUTPUT · local-astronomical-computation → phrase-scheduling sonification of his real recording, long-form/stateful/non-looping TECH · diurnal/almanac/daylight VIBE (warm gold midday → cool indigo night — the directest anti-dark-glow). **Named refs:** John Cage *Atlas Eclipticalis* · Jem Finer *Longplayer* · the almanac tradition. **Why it lost (a hair):** 748 is the visible research-chain + interactive/live-performance-fit + the cleaner #1; but 750 is the better *unattended* glance and hits long-form-stateful + daylight (two categories the jury keeps begging for). **Resurrect FIRST.** Honest 2–3/5 (#1 first real-world-data/celestial sonification of his recording + long-form stateful · #2 ≥4 subsystems [solar compute + phrase scheduler + region playback + reverb + SVG orrery] · #3 named refs).

### `749-spoken-libretto` — `queued` (the speech-ASR phrase-arranger — resurrect for a literary/typographic adult slot, or once browser Web-Speech reliability is acceptable; distinct from `570-spoken-spell` on TWO axes). **What if you could SPEAK and your words arranged his real piano into a score — recognized words choosing & sequencing whole PHRASE-regions of his *Welcome Home* recording, each spoken word landing as living typography on an SVG staff?** Built files (self-reported clean tsc/ESLint; removed): `page.tsx` (Start gesture audio unlock + Web Speech API lifecycle [continuous + auto-restart] + word→phrase emission + five-line SVG staff with note-head dots + recurring-word italic motifs + interim text + stanza list + **typed-word fallback** + **ghost demo** auto-speaking a line on idle + density meter + full teardown + design-notes toggle) + `audio.ts` (`fetchPianoBuffer` + offline detuned fallback + `buildPhraseRegions` continuous time-slices + `buildLibrettoEngine` master gain→lowpass→comp + a quiet recording-derived pedal drone keyed to density) + `score.ts` (pure mapping: `hashWord`/`regionForWord` word-identity→region, length→phrase-length, sentence-rhythm→density, staff placement) + `speech.ts` (self-contained Web Speech API shim, no shared dep). **Distinct from 570:** 570 turned words into SYNTHESIZED pentatonic pitches/ostinati over a violet glow field; 749 triggers his REAL recorded piano phrases AND is editorial/typographic SVG (luminous-paper staff), not a glow field. **Tags:** speech / recognized-WORDS INPUT (Web Speech API — NOT mic-pitch/hum analysis) · SVG/DOM typography OUTPUT (no shader) · ASR word → real-recording PHRASE selection & sequencing + typographic score TECH (whole regions, not granular) · literary/editorial/luminous-paper VIBE. **Named refs:** Steve Reich *Different Trains* · Alvin Lucier *I Am Sitting in a Room* · Scott Johnson *John Somebody*. **Why it lost:** the most distinctive *visual* register but conceptually adjacent to the existing `570-spoken-spell`, and the Web Speech API is the most browser-fragile input of the three (it has a typed fallback + ghost). **Resurrect when** a literary/typographic slot is wanted, or merge its real-phrase-selection into 570 to give that piece his actual recording. Honest 2–3/5 (#2 ≥3 subsystems [ASR + phrase-selection mapping + region playback + SVG typography] + #3 named refs; soft #1 — real-phrase-selection-by-speech vs 570's synth).

## §480 — 2026-06-19 · kids WIDE fire (3 divergent explorers, each a different scarce-renderer × off-glass input × technique × non-silly palette — the textbook attack on JURY 2026-06-18 #2 "spread the kids register, stop running one template") — winner `747-kids-inkblot-bloom` (WebGPU Gray-Scott inkblot from a child's hum); **2 banked seeds**

Cycle 480 ran a kids **WIDE** fan-out (research path a — RESEARCH §480: Entagma/Bileam-Tschepe "Inkblots — Steal from TouchDesigner", May 2026 → symmetric reaction-diffusion grown from a child's voice). Renderers were limited to **WebGPU** and **three.js** (raw-WebGL2 hit 4× in the last-10 → my-audit-banned; Canvas2D jury-banned). Inputs spread mic / tilt / camera (touch-primary jury-banned); all contemplative/wonder (silly jury-banned); none a loop-groove. **Winner: `747-kids-inkblot-bloom`** (the visible today's-research→today's-build chain + JURY #1 "give a kid the scarce renderer"). Both losers were demoable + self-reported clean tsc/eslint; `rm -rf`'d before the authoritative winner-only `npm run build` (compile/type/lint clean; static-gen blocked by the container's 4096-fd EMFILE — proven environmental, pristine `origin/main` fails identically). **Max committed folder after this fire is `747`** (745/746 remain folderless banked seed names from §479; 748/749 below are folderless — renumber if a later fire consumes them first). Resurrect by re-spawning the brief:

### `748-kids-aurora-silk` — `queued` ⭐ RESURRECT FIRST (the iOS-bulletproof first-cloth-sim sibling — resurrect for the next kids slot; needs NO camera/mic permission and three.js/WebGL is the safest iOS renderer). **A 4-year-old TILTS the tablet and a glowing 3D silk veil of aurora light billows, folds, and SINGS like wind through cloth.** The lab's **first true mass-spring CLOTH** (a structured 28×40 grid with structural+shear+bend distance constraints; soft-body `451` exists but never a cloth sheet). Tilt → a wind force; the cloth's mean speed swells a soft just-intoned pad and travelling folds pluck a sparse aeolian harp; auto-breeze after ~1.8s of no tilt so it stays alive hands-free; always-on drone. Built files (self-reported clean tsc/ESLint; removed): `page.tsx` (three.js scene — additive veil mesh + wireframe + glowing nodes + starfield + ExpFog + drifting camera; tilt/pointer input; iOS `DeviceOrientationEvent.requestPermission()` in the Begin tap; full teardown with `forceContextLoss`) + `cloth.ts` (Verlet integrate + gravity + tilt-wind + per-point swirl, 6 relaxation passes, Provot 10% stretch clamp, pinned top row; tracks per-point speed + curvature) + `audio.ts` (kids-safe `master ≤0.3 → lowpass 7.5k → comp −10/20:1`, swelling pad + Karplus/triangle plucks, pentatonic/JI). **Tags:** device-tilt INPUT · three.js OUTPUT · mass-spring-cloth (Verlet) TECH · serene aurora-silk VIBE. **Named refs:** Xavier Provot "Deformation Constraints in a Mass-Spring Model" (Graphics Interface 1995) · Thomas Jakobsen "Advanced Character Physics" (GDC 2001) · aeolian harp / Pauline Oliveros *Deep Listening*. **Love signal:** directly `262-aurora-particle`❤️ + `130-tsl-particle-compute`❤️. **Why it lost (a hair):** the most iOS-bulletproof and most novel *technique* of the three, but `747` is the visible today's-research chain + the literal JURY #1 WebGPU-for-a-kid answer; `748`'s hook (Provot 1995) is foundational, not today's dive. **Resurrect FIRST.** Honest 2–3/5 (#2 ≥4 subsystems [tilt + Verlet cloth solver + cloth-stat sonification + three.js render] + #3 named refs; arguably #1 first cloth sheet).

### `749-kids-shadow-garden` — `queued` ⭐ (the body-silhouette "gentle giant" sibling — resurrect for a camera/embodied kids slot once the segmentation model load can be verified, ideally keeping the WebGPU renderer). **A 4-year-old's own SHADOW waters a garden of glowing flowers that SING where they stand and sway — the child is a gentle giant moving over a luminous night-meadow.** Front camera → body silhouette; where it falls, instanced flowers grow and ring soft chimes (pitch by row, pan by column); standing still blooms a sustained chord, a sweeping arm sends a glittering arpeggio. Built files (self-reported clean tsc/ESLint; removed): `page.tsx` (start gate, per-frame pump, teardown) + `constants.ts` (64×48 ≈3072-flower grid + growth model) + `silhouette.ts` (**three sources**: MediaPipe ImageSegmenter via `webpackIgnore`'d CDN dynamic import → frame-difference luminance motion mask → scripted ghost-blob demo) + `garden-field.ts` (CPU growth sim + bloom-event detection — authoritative for audio) + `garden-audio.ts` (kids-safe chain, always-on drone, pentatonic bells + held-bloom chord, refractory + voice cap) + `renderer-gpu.ts` (**primary** WebGPU: WGSL compute advances GPU growth from the uploaded silhouette; WGSL render draws instanced additive glow sprites; usage bits hardcoded, no `@webgpu/types`) + `renderer.ts` (renderer contract + **required** Canvas2D additive-glow fallback). **Tags:** camera body-silhouette INPUT · WebGPU-instanced OUTPUT · person-segmentation → spatial growth-field TECH · luminous-meadow/tender VIBE. **Named refs:** MediaPipe Selfie-Segmentation / Image Segmenter · Daniel Rozin (body-as-brush mirror installations) · *Journey* (thatgamecompany). **Love signal:** `101-camera-song`❤️ + `104-kids-mirror-draw`❤️ + `234-kids-hand-creature`❤️ (embodied camera lane). **Why it lost:** the most moving concept but the most fragile for an unattended ship — camera permission + an unverifiable CDN segmentation model + WebGPU (it has frame-diff + ghost + Canvas2D fallbacks, but three failure layers is the most surface). The builder also flagged a deliberate dual-integration (GPU-resident growth for visuals, CPU growth for audio — same model, two copies). **Resurrect when** the model load can be verified on a real device, or simplify to frame-diff-only segmentation. Honest 2–3/5 (#2 ≥5 subsystems + #3 named refs; arguably #1 first selfie-segmentation).

## §478 — 2026-06-19 · kids DEEP fire (ONE concept — *cycle-2 of `738-kids-song-sprout`: a child's hums hatch a growing FAMILY of luminous companions who remember the child's songs and sing them back to EACH OTHER in developing harmony, all on raw WebGL2* — attacked via 3 musical models [round/canon · chord-harmony · Reich phasing]; the directest hit on JURY 2026-06-18 #3 "reward DEPTH, return and extend the ceiling, stop banking and walking away" — 738 was monophonic, this adds the harmonic/multi-voice dimension it explicitly banked for cycle-2) — winner `743-kids-song-family`; **2 banked seeds**

Cycle 478 ran a kids **DEEP** fan-out (research path b: continue the multi-cycle ambitious build — RESEARCH §478: arXiv `2506.18143` *AI Harmonizer* turns ONE voice into evolving four-part harmony [the live 2026 frontier, all adult/ML]; the canon/round/phasing process-music lineage [Reich/Riley/Eno] is the original kid-legible "one melody → many developing voices" engine; the lab's 700+ pieces have NO multi-voice/harmonic memory — 738 is monophonic). All three: mic-voice INPUT (off banned touch-primary) · **raw WebGL2 OUTPUT** (the scarcest non-banned renderer — WebGPU 4× in the last-10 → my-audit-banned, three.js 3× → would-hit-4×, Canvas2D jury-banned; raw WebGL2 is also the iOS-bulletproof glance) · accreting **harmonic** memory of the child's remembered phrases TECH (emphatically NOT a loop-groove) · tender/wonder VIBE (off banned silly-comedy). **Winner: `743-kids-song-family`** — the only one that ALSO lands JURY #5 (Karel's real Welcome Home piano on the kids side, his music being in only 1 of the last 15), the deepest (harmony + concatenative grains of his real recording), the most "Resonance." Both losers were demoable + self-reported clean tsc/eslint; the authoritative winner-only `npm run build` is the gate (compile/type/lint clean; static-gen blocked by the container's 4096-fd EMFILE — proven environmental, pristine `origin/main` fails identically). **Max committed folder after this fire is `743`** (735/736/737/739/740 remain folderless banked seed names — renumber if a later fire consumes them first). Resurrect by re-spawning the brief:

### `742-kids-song-round` — `queued` ⭐ RESURRECT FIRST (the bulletproof zero-network ROUND sibling — resurrect when network-free reliability is the priority, or for a kids slot wanting the most kid-legible musical model). **A 4-year-old hums; a FAMILY of glowing companions (cap 4) hatch & grow, then sing the child's remembered melodies back as a developing ROUND/CANON** — the same subject, each companion entering staggered and transposed to a consonant scale degree (`CANON_DEGREE_OFFSETS = [0, +3rd, +5th, +oct]`), the round thickening 1→4 voices and entry-spacing tightening (call-and-response → overlapping woven harmony) as family growth rises (`1−e^(−heard/22)`), so minute-5 is audibly fuller than minute-1 — the "Row, Row, Row Your Boat" a kid intuitively knows. Built files (self-reported clean tsc/ESLint; removed): `page.tsx` (start gate mic/ghost + rAF round-scheduler brain + WebGL2 primary / Canvas2D fallback + full teardown) + `audio.ts` (kids-safe `master ≤0.3 → lowpass 7.5k → comp −10/20:1`, analysis-only mic + autocorrelation pitch, D-Dorian, per-companion stereo-panned FM/additive bell-vox `singNote`, warm drone bed) + `memory.ts` (phrase memory + family cap 4 + `composeRound` canon scheduler) + `field.ts` (raw WebGL2 GLSL ES 3.00 fullscreen-triangle additive glow blobs from uniform arrays + nebula + sing-motes; Canvas2D fallback). **Tags:** mic-voice INPUT · raw-WebGL2 OUTPUT · accreting canon/round harmonic-memory TECH · tender/wonder VIBE. **Named refs:** Steve Reich · Terry Riley *In C* · Brian Eno *Reflection* · Pauline Oliveros. **Why it lost:** a clean, bulletproof realization, but 743 voices the family with grains of Karel's REAL piano (the open JURY #5) and chord-stack harmony is a touch richer than a unison round; 742 is the no-network reliability champion. **Resurrect FIRST** for a network-free kids slot, or merge its `composeRound` canon entries INTO 743 so the family can alternate round vs chord-choir. Honest 2–3/5 (#2 ≥4 subsystems [mic pitch + phrase memory + canon scheduler + FM voices + WebGL2 field] + #4 cycle-2 of 738 + #5 RESEARCH §478).

### `744-kids-song-phase` — `queued` (the Steve Reich PHASING sibling — resurrect for a kids slot wanting an ever-evolving shimmer rather than a settling harmony, or to make the music-as-process idea visible). **A 4-year-old hums; a family of glowing companions (cap 5) each loop the child's same remembered phrase at a SLIGHTLY different tempo (rates `1.0…1.036`), so they start in unison and gradually slip out of PHASE — Reich's *Piano Phase* — producing a constantly shifting weave from the child's own melody, with each companion's sweeping glow-ring/tick tracking its loop position so the drift is SEEN as well as heard (live drift-% HUD).** Built files (self-reported clean tsc/ESLint; removed): `page.tsx` (start gate + rAF wiring audio brain to renderer + drift HUD + full teardown) + `audio.ts` (kids-safe master chain, analysis-only mic + autocorrelation, D-Dorian, per-companion FM bell with stereo spread) + `memory.ts` (phrase memory + family cap 5 + per-companion detuned loop rates) + `phase.ts` (Web Audio look-ahead phasing scheduler + `phaseSpread` drift readout) + `field.ts` (raw WebGL2 GLSL ES 3.00 additive blobs + bloom + sweeping phase rings/ticks) + `fallback.ts` (Canvas2D glow-dot fallback with the same phase-tick idea). **Tags:** mic-voice INPUT · raw-WebGL2 OUTPUT · Reich-phasing accreting-memory TECH · dreamy/wonder VIBE. **Named refs:** Steve Reich *Piano Phase*/*Clapping Music* · Terry Riley *In C* · Brian Eno *Reflection*. **Why it lost:** the most "art-music" concept and the prettiest evolving texture, but phasing is the least *legible-as-a-relationship* of the three (it reads as a shimmering field rather than a family answering the child), and 743 lands JURY #5. **Resurrect when** the kids side wants an ever-unfolding shimmer or to teach process-music by sight. Honest 2–3/5 (#2 ≥4 subsystems [mic pitch + phrase memory + phasing scheduler + FM voices + WebGL2 phase-ring field] + #3 named refs [Reich/Riley] + #4 cycle-2 of 738).

## §477 — 2026-06-19 · adult DEEP fire (ONE concept — *a frictionless cross-device two-piano room: type the same short room code on two phones and play Karel's piano together, no SDP paste; only note-events travel, both render locally; a solo ghost partner so the one-phone glance always sounds* — attacked via 3 network substrates; the directest hit on JURY 2026-06-18 #4 "make one multi-user piece ACTUALLY cross-device, retire the asterisk" + the cycle-2 of `729-piano-portal-jam` whose manual SDP copy-paste was the real friction) — winner `741-piano-room-pulse`; **2 banked seeds**

Cycle 477 ran an adult **DEEP** fan-out (research path a: RESEARCH §477 — Supabase Realtime *Broadcast* is already in the repo [`@supabase/supabase-js` dep + `NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_*` env], an ephemeral cross-device WebSocket pub/sub never used in 700+ prototypes — a frictionless/dep-free/keyless/side-effect-free transport that retires 729's manual handshake; + the NTP-over-WebSocket shared-clock finding [WebSockets sub-10ms in 2026; the hard part of a two-phone jam is the *shared clock*, not the notes — Ableton Link's answer]). All three: room-code join (no SDP paste) · MIDI/keyboard-first INPUT (off banned touch-primary) · three.js or WebGL2 OUTPUT (off banned Canvas2D-primary) · cross-device realtime-sync TECH (NOT a loop-groove) · intimate-duet VIBE (off banned silly) · note-events-only (never audio, both render locally) · solo-ghost (*Voyager*) so the one-phone glance always sounds. **Winner: `741-piano-room-pulse`** — the deepest (the only one that solves the actual hard problem of networked music: a shared clock + ensemble lock, not just a note relay). Both losers were demoable + self-reported clean tsc/eslint; the authoritative winner-only `npm run build` is the gate; loser folders `rm -rf`'d before it. **Max committed folder after this fire is `741`** (735/736/737 remain folderless banked seed names from §475/§476 — renumber if consumed). Resurrect by re-spawning the brief:

### `739-piano-room-relay` — `queued` ⭐ RESURRECT FIRST (the reliability/frictionless champion — resurrect when robustness is the priority over the clock-lock, or as the simplest base to layer Karel's real sampled piano onto). **Type the same 4-letter ROOM CODE on two phones and instantly play Karel's piano together — zero handshake.** Transport = **Supabase Realtime Broadcast** direct: client built lazily/env-guarded, channel `dream-piano-room-<CODE>`, `broadcast.self:false`, **Presence**-driven duet detection, only `{note,vel,who,t}` events travel (both render locally, you warm/panned-left + partner cool/panned-right). Built files (self-reported clean tsc/eslint; removed): `page.tsx` (MIDI `0x90`/`0x80` + emerald badge + `onstatechange` hot-plug · computer-keyboard `a s d f g h j k l`/`w e t y u o`/`z`·`x` octave, no key-repeat retrigger · on-screen strip · *Voyager* ghost on ~2.5s idle that steps aside when a partner joins · full teardown) + `audio.ts` (warm FM/additive Rhodes-y e-piano + always-on D-Dorian pad bed + `snapToDorian` + `master 0.28 → lowpass 7.5k → comp −10/20:1 → dest`) + `room.ts` (Supabase Realtime Broadcast + Presence; all failures → solo + amber notice; `unsubscribe`/`removeChannel`) + `scene.ts` (three.js `Points` two-constellation + Canvas2D fallback). **Tags:** MIDI/keyboard INPUT · three.js OUTPUT · Supabase-Realtime-Broadcast cross-device relay TECH · intimate-duet VIBE. **Named refs:** Chrome Music Lab *Shared Piano* · JackTrip/Chris Chafe *SoundWIRE* · George Lewis *Voyager*. **Why it lost:** it moves notes flawlessly but doesn't address latency — without a shared clock two players smear; 741 adds the ensemble lock that makes a latency-spanning duet feel like one room. **Resurrect FIRST** when bulletproof simplicity > the lock, or merge its clean Presence/relay into a piece that swaps the synth for Karel's real Welcome Home grains (the missing #5). Honest 3–4/5 (#1 first Supabase-Realtime transport + #2 ≥4 subsystems [realtime relay + multi-input + e-piano + three.js] + arguably #4 multi-cycle + #5 RESEARCH §477).

### `740-piano-room-portal` — `queued` ⭐ (the low-latency P2P champion + the literal cycle-2 of `729-piano-portal-jam` — resurrect when P2P latency matters more than reliability, or to retire 729's manual handshake on the record). **Two phones open a true peer-to-peer WebRTC piano duet — lowest latency — but JOIN by typing the same room code, with offer/answer/ICE exchanged AUTOMATICALLY over a Supabase Realtime signaling channel** (no SDP copy-paste — the exact friction 729 had). Built files (self-reported clean tsc/eslint; removed): `page.tsx` (same MIDI/keyboard/on-screen input stack + ghost + teardown of rAF/listeners/MIDI/GL/`RTCPeerConnection`/data-channel/`AudioContext`/channel) + `portal.ts` (`RTCPeerConnection` + STUN google + one ordered `createDataChannel("piano")`; **Supabase Realtime as the signaling bus** `dream-piano-portal-<CODE>`: `hello`+random-id → **deterministic role rule** [lexicographically smaller id = offerer so two phones never both offer] → trickle-ICE relayed over a `signal` broadcast; emerald "duet live — peer connected" on `connectionstatechange==="connected"`) + `audio.ts` (FM e-piano + D-minor-9 pad bed + D-Dorian snap + same master chain) + `field.ts` (**raw WebGL2 GLSL ES 3.00** fullscreen-triangle fragment-shader bloom field — note-bloom uniform array, you warm/partner cool — + Canvas2D fallback). **Tags:** MIDI/keyboard INPUT · raw-WebGL2 GLSL OUTPUT · WebRTC-P2P-data-channel-auto-signaled-over-Supabase TECH · intimate-duet VIBE. **Named refs:** Chrome Music Lab *Shared Piano* · JackTrip/Chris Chafe · George Lewis *Voyager*; cycle-2 of `729`. **Why it lost:** WebRTC P2P is the lowest-latency but the most fragile cross-device (role negotiation + trickle ICE + **STUN-only → can fail on symmetric-NAT without a TURN server**), and 729 already proved the lab can do cross-device WebRTC — 741's shared-clock is the genuinely NEW capability + the deeper answer to JURY #3. **Resurrect when** a real two-phone test shows the WS relay's latency is too loose for tight playing and P2P is worth the fragility, OR to formally retire 729's manual handshake. Honest 3–4/5 (#1 first auto-signaled-WebRTC + #2 ≥4 subsystems [WebRTC + Supabase-signaling + multi-input + e-piano + WebGL2 field] + #4 cycle-2 of 729 + #5 RESEARCH §477).

## §476 — 2026-06-19 · kids DEEP fire (ONE concept — *a luminous companion-world that REMEMBERS the child's voice and EVOLVES over minutes, singing their own earlier sounds back transformed* — attacked via 3 scarce non-banned renderers; the lab's FIRST long-form/stateful KIDS piece, the directest structural answer to JURY 2026-06-18 #3 "reward depth, build the accreting-memory ceiling, stop banking and walking away" brought DOWN to a 4-year-old) — winner `738-kids-song-sprout`; **2 banked seeds**

Cycle 476 ran a kids **DEEP** fan-out (research path a: RESEARCH §476 — long-form-evolving generative is the live 2026 frontier [WebGPU audio-reactive slime-mold updated 2026-06-18] but adult-only; every one of the lab's 700+ kids pieces is instant cause→effect that *resets* — none has memory or a minutes-long arc). All three share the memory + long-form-evolution core, all on a scarce non-banned renderer (three.js / WebGL2 — emphatically NOT banned Canvas2D-primary, NOT WebGPU which is 3× in the window), all mic-voice INPUT (off banned touch-primary, camera-free vs the camera-heavy recent kids), all tender/wonder VIBE (off banned silly-comedy), all accreting-memory TECH (NOT a loop-groove). **Winner: `738-kids-song-sprout`** — the deepest memory model (recombines remembered fragments into NEW replies, not just re-voicing; an explicit maturation arc with life-stage labels). Both losers were demoable + self-reported clean tsc/eslint; the authoritative winner-only `npm run build` is the gate (compile/type/lint clean; static-gen blocked by the container's 4096-fd EMFILE — proven environmental, pristine `origin/main` fails identically). **Max committed folder after this fire is `738`** (735 remains the folderless banked `735-paths-revenant` name; 736/737 below are folderless seeds — renumber if a later fire consumes them first). ### `736-kids-echo-grove` — `queued` ⭐ RESURRECT FIRST (the three.js accreting-grove sibling — the warmest "a choir builds from your own past voice"; resurrect for a kids slot wanting the accumulating-field reading of the memory concept). **Hum to a near-dark 3D grove and each sustained note PLANTS a glowing tree (pitch→height+hue, loudness→size); the grove REMEMBERS — older trees gently re-sing a transformed echo (re-pitched/softened/time-stretched) of the hum that planted them, so over minutes a shimmering choir builds from the child's own earlier voice.** Built files (self-reported clean tsc/eslint; removed): `page.tsx` (start gate + rAF loop: mic hum-detection via RMS-gated autocorrelation, ghost auto-demo that plants notes on mic-deny OR 9s silence, Canvas2D-fallback badge, "Read the design notes" link) + `audio.ts` (kids-safe `master ≤0.3 → lowpass 7.5k → comp −10/20:1 → dest`; mic analysis-only; persisting `Tree[]` store = visual source of truth; slow echo scheduler whose cadence speeds up and voice-count rises 1→4 as the grove fills = the accretion that makes minute-5≠minute-1; D-Dorian snap; always-on drone) + `grove.ts` (three.js additive-glow `Points` + `FogExp2` + golden-spiral placement + slow drifting camera + full GPU disposal; Canvas2D glow-dot fallback). **Tags:** mic voice/hum INPUT · three.js 3D-points OUTPUT · accreting-memory + transformed-echo re-voicing TECH · tender/wonder VIBE. **Named refs:** Brian Eno *Reflection* · Tamagotchi/a-life · Refik Anadol luminous fields. **Why it lost:** the accumulating-glowing-field reading is the closest of the three to `721-kids-piano-garden`'s breath→bloom field (it adds memory/echo, which is real, but the surface reads most familiar); 738's single-companion-that-grows-up-and-recomposes is the more surprising, more "Resonance" (a relationship, not a field). **Resurrect first** for the next kids long-form slot, or merge its 1→4 echo-thickening scheduler INTO 738. Honest 2–3/5 (#2 ≥3 subsystems [mic pitch + memory/echo engine + three.js grove + drone] + #4 multi-cycle + arguably #5 RESEARCH §476 cite).

### `737-kids-memory-reef` — `queued` (the bulletproof iOS-safe **raw WebGL2 GLSL** sibling — iOS HAS WebGL2; zero CDN; resurrect when robustness/iOS-safety is the priority OR when three.js is hot). **Sing to a near-dark underwater field (raw WebGL2 domain-warped-fbm currents + caustics, IQ "warp" lineage) and each sustained note grows a glowing CORAL bloom (pitch→position+hue, loudness→size); the reef REMEMBERS — up to 96 accumulated blooms persist in a uniform array the shader reads, older corals seed nearby child-corals + re-sing transformed echoes on a ~5s cadence, and the shader warm-patinas corals by age, so the dark water fills into a shimmering singing reef genuinely denser at minute 5.** Built files (self-reported clean tsc/eslint; removed): `page.tsx` (WebGL2/Canvas2D init, the `Bloom[]` memory store + slow re-singing scheduler + child-coral seeding, mic loop, ghost auto-demo that auto-starts on mount + rescues mic after ~9s silence, "Sing to the reef" / "Just watch it dream" buttons) + `audio.ts` (kids-safe `master 0.26 → lowpass 7.5k → comp −10/20:1`; always-on underwater-hush drone; mic analysis-only; gated autocorrelation pitch + RMS onset; D-Dorian snap; gentle FM coral-voice + softened/time-stretched echo synth) + `reef-field.ts` (raw WebGL2 GLSL ES 3.00 fullscreen-triangle + fbm current/caustic fragment shader reading up to 96 blooms from two `vec4` uniform arrays + the `Bloom` model + GL build/draw helpers). **Tags:** mic voice/hum INPUT · raw-WebGL2 GLSL OUTPUT · accreting-memory + transformed-echo re-singing TECH · dreamy/wonder VIBE. **Named refs:** Brian Eno *Reflection* · Inigo Quilez fbm/warp · coral-reef growth / a-life. **Why it lost:** an accumulating reef-field, like 736, reads as a richer texture rather than a *companion that develops*; and WebGL2 is 2× in the window where three.js is 1× (scarcer). **Resurrect when** iOS-bulletproofness is the priority (raw WebGL2 is the most reliable cross-device renderer; no CDN/GPU-compute/ML risk) or when three.js has warmed. Honest 2/5 (#2 ≥3 subsystems [mic pitch + reef memory engine + WebGL2 fbm shader + drone] + arguably #5 RESEARCH §476 cite).

## §472 — 2026-06-18 · kids WIDE fire (3 divergent explorers, each on a DIFFERENT scarce non-Canvas2D renderer × different off-glass input × different non-silly vibe, none a loop/groove — the textbook attack on JURY 2026-06-18's kids bans [Canvas2D 9× · loop-groove 5× · touch 5× · silly 6×] + #1 "give a kid the scarce renderer" + #2 "texture/cause-effect, not a groove; vary the register") — winner `727-kids-color-hunt`; **2 banked seeds**

Cycle 472 ran a kids **WIDE** fan-out. Research path (a+c): RESEARCH §472 found the live-2026 GPU-particle creative-coding frontier is **almost entirely adult/installation** — the scarce renderers are essentially never aimed at a 4-year-old, so JURY #1 ("give a kid the scarce renderer") is a genuinely unoccupied corner; the two off-glass seeds were consumed from IDEAS §470 (born of RESEARCH §470's iOS-WebGPU-26 finding). **Winner: `727-kids-color-hunt`** — point at the room's colors, paint music with what you see (camera-color → WebGPU bloom → cross-modal chord → wonder); the most surprising + scarcest renderer + furthest from the banned silly pole. Both losers were demoable + self-reported clean tsc+eslint; `rm -rf`'d before the authoritative build. ⚠️ The authoritative `npm run build` compiled/typed/linted clean but couldn't run static generation — the container's anomalous **4096 fd limit** killed the worker spawn (`EMFILE`); proven environmental (pristine main fails identically — see STATE.md cycle 472). **Max committed folder after this fire is `727`.** Resurrect by re-spawning the brief:

### `726-kids-star-scoop` — `queued` ⭐ RESURRECT FIRST (the embodied/off-glass kids swing). **Reach UP into a deep 3D night sky with your bare hands and scoop handfuls of glowing stars that chime — no screen to touch, just hands in the air.** MediaPipe **Hand tracking** (front camera, analysis-only, `webpackIgnore` CDN dynamic import, zero npm deps) maps each hand into a **three.js** `THREE.Points` starfield (~2,400 additive glow sprites + `FogExp2`). Close a hand → nearest stars gather to the palm, each ringing one soft pentatonic bell voice (nothing ever "wrong"); open wide → they spill in a shimmering glissando arc; hands high = brighter/higher register, low = warm/low. No beat, no loop, no groove — an **awe/wonder** piece. Built demoable this fire (self-reported clean tsc+eslint): `page.tsx` (Start-gated camera + HandLandmarker, gesture edge-detection, ghost auto-demo with two virtual hands on camera-deny/idle, full teardown) + `scene.ts` (2,400-star sim with gather/push/spring physics behind a common `StarRenderer` interface — three.js primary + inline Canvas2D glow-dot fallback) + `audio.ts` (always-on open-fifth drone + pooled FM-ish pentatonic bells, `scoop()` chord cluster + `spill()` rising glissando, kids-safe `gain 0.3 → lowpass 7.5k → comp −10/20:1`) + README. **Tags:** MediaPipe-hands camera INPUT (embodied, off-glass) · three.js 3D-points OUTPUT · gesture-driven particle-gathering + spatial chord-voicing TECH · awe/wonder VIBE. **Named refs:** MediaPipe Hands · *Journey* (thatgamecompany, wordless awe) · Refik Anadol (luminous point-fields). **Why it lost:** (a) MediaPipe Hands already exists in the lab (`524-kids-hand-firebird`) so it's weaker on technique-#1; (b) three.js is warming (3×) where the winner's WebGPU is scarcest; (c) it carries **two** CDN deps (three.js + MediaPipe) vs the winner's zero. **Resurrect first** for an embodied/off-glass kids slot — it's the directest "get the kids side off the glass." Honest 2–3/5 (#2 ≥3 subsystems [MediaPipe hand-tracking + three.js 3D point-field + spatial bell-cluster audio engine] + #3 named refs).

### `725-kids-aurora-sail` — `queued` (the bulletproof iOS-safe WebGL2 sibling — iOS HAS WebGL2; zero CDN). **Tilt the tablet like a steering tray to sail a tiny glowing boat across a living aurora sea — and the shimmering light bands SING as the boat drifts through them.** A full-screen **raw WebGL2 GLSL fragment-shader aurora** (domain-warped fbm ribbons, slow drift, IQ "warp" lineage, water reflection + boat halo); **device tilt** (`DeviceOrientationEvent`, iOS permission in the Start tap; pointer-drag fallback) steers the boat. The boat "reads" the brightness under it each frame via a **faithful JS replica of the shader's exact fbm field** → maps brightness to register in consonant **D-Dorian** (nothing wrong), speed → note density, calm dark water → a low warm drone. No beat, no loop, no groove — a calm **dreamy sailing** texture. Built demoable this fire (self-reported clean tsc+eslint): `page.tsx` (WebGL2 aurora shader + Canvas2D aurora-band fallback, tilt + pointer steering, idle ghost Lissajous auto-demo, full teardown) + `aurora-field.ts` (JS mirror of the GLSL field) + `audio.ts` (master 0.3 → lowpass 7.5k → comp −10/20:1, always-on "sea hush" bed, D-root drone + D-Dorian brightness/speed triggering) + README. **Tags:** device-tilt INPUT (off banned touch-primary) · raw-WebGL2 GLSL OUTPUT (off banned Canvas2D-primary + three.js) · shader-brightness-field-reading → modal sonification TECH (NOT a groove) · dreamy/calm/awe VIBE. **Named refs:** Inigo Quilez "warp"/fbm · real-time aurora GLSL sims · *Journey* (calm wordless sailing). **Why it lost:** lowest ambition of the three (honest 2/5 — tilt + GLSL worlds have lab precedent, e.g. `575`) and the comfortable median the mandate warns against; but it's the **most bulletproof iOS-safe glance** (iOS has WebGL2 reliably; no camera/CDN/GPU-compute risk). **Resurrect when** the kids side wants a calm contemplative tilt piece or when WebGPU/MediaPipe are too fragile for the target device. Honest 2/5 (#2 ≥3 subsystems [WebGL2 fbm aurora shader + tilt/pointer steering + brightness-driven modal audio] + #3 named refs).

## §471 — 2026-06-18 · adult DEEP fire ("Presence Bloom II — the room remembers his piano" — ONE concept, 3 body→grain mapping/temporal models; the directest hit on JURY 2026-06-18 #3 "stop banking ceilings and walking away — push 710 to a cycle-2, reward depth over breadth" + #5 "use his real music, it's in only 1 of the last 15" + #1 "ban Canvas2D, use the scarce WebGPU stack") — winner `724-presence-drift`; **2 banked seeds**

Cycle 471 ran an adult **DEEP** fan-out: ONE concept — *extend the lab's only new ceiling build (`710-presence-bloom`: WebGPU-compute particle storm + MediaPipe full-body pose + HRTF spatialization-as-instrument) by making its persistent accreting voices be **grains of Karel's REAL Welcome Home recording** instead of synth oscillators* — attacked via three different body→grain mapping/temporal models. Research-led (RESEARCH §471: the 2026 *Synaptic Sanctum Neuroscape* — CataRT-MuBu concatenative grains + per-source binaural HOA locked to head-tracking — is the exact named architecture). All three reuse 710's proven stack + 720's verbatim real-recording grain loader (`PIANO_RECORDING_ID = "549fc519-…"`, `fetchPianoBuffer` 4s-abort, `renderFallbackBuffer`); all three dodge every JURY 2026-06-18 ban (WebGPU output not Canvas2D-primary, body-pose input not touch, concatenative-grains not loop-groove, meditative-adult not silly). **Winner: `724-presence-drift`** — the deepest extension: keeps 710's exact reach→dwell→flick placement gesture (so it's unmistakably a cycle-2) AND adds a genuinely new axis neither 710 nor 720 had — per-voice **moving read-heads** so the spatial chord re-voices itself over minutes (long-form/state/evolution), the richest use of his actual performance. Both losers built to demoable + self-reported clean tsc+eslint; the authoritative winner-only `npm run build` is the gate; loser folders `rm -rf`'d before it. **Max committed folder after this fire is `724`** (722/723 below are free to reuse as real numbers; renumber if a later fire consumes them first). Resurrect by re-spawning the brief:

### `722-presence-paths` — `queued` ⭐ RESURRECT FIRST (the most faithful cycle-2 + the cleanest "reach into his recording with your body"; the diversity sibling for an adult night that wants *navigation* over *evolution*). **Reach a hand to a point in the air, dwell ~0.65s, flick — and leave a grain of Karel's REAL piano singing there in 3D; hand HEIGHT picks brightness (high=treble, low=warm), reach OPENNESS picks how deep into his performance the grain is drawn from — build a spatial chord by navigating the descriptor map of his own playing with your body.** Built files (self-reported clean tsc+eslint; in `/tmp/dream-losers/`): `page.tsx` (470 lines), `audio.ts` (664 lines — 720's verbatim loader + corpus + new `PathsAudio` engine: each persistent HRTF voice softly re-triggers Hann grains selected by descriptor-space reach via `selectNearest`), `render.ts` (WebGPU storm + Canvas2D fallback, palette warmed amber), `pose.ts` (710's, + `reach`=wrist-distance-from-torso-centre), `README.md`. **Tags:** webcam full-body pose INPUT (reach/dwell/flick + reach-openness; ghost-body fallback) · WebGPU compute storm + HRTF-binaural grain chorus OUTPUT (Canvas2D fallback) · CataRT descriptor-space (time×brightness) grain navigation TECH · cosmic/meditative VIBE. **Named refs:** Diemo Schwarz CataRT · HRTF/binaural PannerNode · Refik Anadol (GPU particle data-architecture). **Why it lost:** the descriptor-space navigation-of-his-recording is essentially `720-paths-grainfield`'s core idea (720 already flies a cursor through a time×brightness grain map of the same recording) — so it's the *least new musical idea* of the three; 724 adds an axis 720 never had. **Resurrect FIRST** for an adult slot wanting the embodied version of 720, or merge its descriptor-space reach mapping INTO 724 (place a voice by reaching to a brightness/time, *then* let it drift). Honest ambition **4/5** (#2 ≥3 subsystems [pose + WebGPU + HRTF + concatenative real-recording grains] + #3 named refs + #4 cycle-2 multi-cycle + #5 RESEARCH §471 cite).

### `723-presence-tide` — `queued` ⭐ (the boldest INTERACTION departure — the truest "real-room install" answer to JURY #3; a whole-body *conductor*, no gesture, no placement). **Conduct a room made of Karel's real piano with your whole body: be STILL and his softest, longest, most distant grains drift in wide and reverberant; MOVE vigorously and his bright attack-grains scatter near and dry; lean and reach to steer which side of you the tide arrives from — the entire 3D space breathes his performance with you.** Built files (self-reported clean tsc+eslint; in `/tmp/dream-losers/`): `page.tsx` (459 lines — continuous conductor smoothing, ghost auto-demo that breathes stillness→vigour→calm over ~40s, centre-of-gravity HUD), `audio.ts` (580 lines — 720's verbatim loader + `buildGrainCorpus` [~80ms grains, RMS + ZCR-brightness] + `TideAudio` continuous HRTF grain-tide scheduler: motion-energy → target brightness/RMS/density, stillness=wide/far/wet/long vs vigour=near/dry/short, direction from per-side limb activity + lean, slow centre-of-gravity drift through the recording), `render.ts` (60k-particle WGSL storm + Canvas2D fallback; attractors carry `bright` so dark grains read violet, bright attacks flare rose-gold), `pose.ts` (710's, continuous conductor-features instead of gesture detection), `README.md`. **Tags:** webcam full-body pose INPUT reduced to *continuous* conductor signals (energy/lean/per-side activity/reach; NO discrete gesture) · WebGPU storm + continuous HRTF grain-tide OUTPUT (Canvas2D fallback) · motion-energy → corpus grain selection (CataRT by [time×brightness×energy]) TECH · cosmic/immersive-meditative VIBE. **Named refs:** Diemo Schwarz CataRT · HRTF/binaural PannerNode. **Why it lost:** dropping 710's signature "leave a persistent voice" placement gesture makes it read as a *sibling concept* rather than a cycle-2 *deepening* of 710 (and the jury's binding ask was "push 710 to a cycle-2"); the energy→grain-density conductor mapping is also the most well-trodden of the three. **Why resurrect ⭐:** it's the most installation/room-scale of the three and the boldest interaction departure — **lead a future adult fire framed as "stand in a room of his piano and conduct it"** (Tauri/installation candidate, deepen with a second body and projection per JURY #3's literal "real-room install, projection, a second body"). Honest ambition **4/5** (#2 ≥3 subsystems + #3 named refs + #4 cycle-2 + #5 RESEARCH §471 cite).

## §470 — 2026-06-18 · kids WIDE fire (3 divergent explorers, each on a SCARCE non-banned renderer — the directest attack on JURY 2026-06-18's "Canvas2D 9× monoculture" + "kids loop-groove is the new pentatonic" diagnosis) — winner `721-kids-piano-garden`; **2 banked seeds**

Cycle 470 ran a kids **WIDE** fan-out (research path a: RESEARCH §470's iOS-Safari-26-WebGPU-now-shippable finding + the proven CataRT real-piano loader). **Three scarce renderers + three inputs by design** so the siblings don't collide: BREATH→**WebGPU** (won) · HANDS→**three.js** · TILT→**WebGL2**. All three dodge every JURY 2026-06-18 ban (no Canvas2D-primary, no loop-groove, no touch-primary, no silly). **Winner: `721-kids-piano-garden`** — hits *two* jury provocations at once (#1 scarce-renderer-for-a-kid + #5 his real music), on the scarcest renderer, with zero CDN dependency. Both losers were demoable + self-reported clean tsc+eslint; the authoritative winner-only `npm run build` (exit 0, 508/508 static) is the gate; loser folders `rm -rf`'d before it. **Max committed folder after this fire is `721`** (722/723 below are free to reuse as real numbers; renumber if consumed first). Resurrect by re-spawning the brief:

### `722-kids-star-scoop` — `queued` ⭐ RESURRECT FIRST (the embodied/off-glass kids swing). **Reach UP into a deep 3D night sky with your bare hands and scoop handfuls of glowing stars that chime — no screen to touch, just hands in the air.** MediaPipe **Hand tracking** (front camera, analysis-only) maps each hand into a **three.js** `THREE.Points` starfield (~2,400 additive glow sprites + `FogExp2`, loaded via `webpackIgnore`'d CDN dynamic import — zero npm deps). Close a hand → nearest stars gather to the palm, each ringing one soft pentatonic bell voice (nothing ever "wrong"); open wide → they spill in a shimmering glissando arc; hands high = brighter/higher register, low = warm/low. No beat, no loop, no groove — an **awe/wonder** piece. Always-on open-fifth drone; kids-safe `gain→lowpass(7.5k)→comp(−10/20:1)`; ghost auto-demo (two virtual hands scoop/spill on idle/camera-deny); no-WebGL → inline Canvas2D glow-dot fallback that still gathers + sings; full teardown (stream/MediaPipe/three.js dispose/AudioContext/rAF). **Tags:** MediaPipe-hands camera INPUT (embodied, off-glass) · three.js 3D-points OUTPUT · gesture-driven particle-gathering + spatial chord-voicing TECH · awe/wonder VIBE. **Named refs:** MediaPipe Hands · *Journey* (thatgamecompany, wordless awe) · Refik Anadol (luminous point-fields). **Why it lost:** (a) MediaPipe Hands already exists in the lab (`524-kids-hand-firebird`) so it's weaker on technique-#1; (b) three.js is warming (3×) where the winner's WebGPU is scarcest (1×); (c) it carries **two** CDN dependencies (three.js + MediaPipe) vs 721's zero. **Resurrect first** for an embodied/off-glass kids slot — it's the directest "get the kids side off the glass." Honest 2–3/5 (#2 ≥3 subsystems [MediaPipe hand-tracking + three.js 3D point-field + spatial bell-cluster audio engine] + #3 named refs).

### `723-kids-aurora-sail` — `queued` (the iOS-safe WebGL2 sibling — iOS HAS WebGL2). **Tilt the tablet like a steering tray to sail a tiny glowing boat across a living aurora sea — and the shimmering light bands SING as the boat drifts through them.** A full-screen **raw WebGL2 GLSL fragment-shader aurora** (domain-warped fbm ribbons, slow drift, IQ "warp" lineage); **device tilt** (`DeviceOrientationEvent`, iOS permission in the Start tap; pointer-drag fallback) steers a small boat. The boat "reads" the brightness under it each frame via a **faithful JS replica of the shader's fbm field** → maps brightness to register in a consonant D-Dorian (nothing wrong), speed → note density, calm dark water → a low warm drone. No beat, no loop, no groove — a calm **dreamy sailing** texture. Always-on drone; kids-safe `gain→lowpass(7.5k)→comp(−10/20:1)`; ghost auto-demo sails a scripted course on idle/tilt-deny; no-WebGL2 → inline Canvas2D aurora-band fallback that still drifts + sails + sings; full teardown. **Tags:** device-tilt INPUT (off banned touch-primary) · raw-WebGL2 GLSL OUTPUT (off banned Canvas2D-primary + three.js) · shader-brightness-field-reading → modal sonification TECH (NOT a groove) · dreamy/calm/awe VIBE. **Named refs:** Inigo Quilez "warp"/fbm · real-time aurora GLSL sims · *Journey* (calm wordless sailing). **Why it lost:** lowest ambition of the three (honest 2/5 — tilt + GLSL worlds have lab precedent, e.g. `575-kids-sky-song`); but it's the most bulletproof iOS-safe glance (iOS has WebGL2 reliably; no camera/CDN/GPU-compute risk). **Resurrect when** the kids side wants a calm contemplative tilt piece or when WebGPU/MediaPipe are too fragile for the target device. Honest 2/5 (#2 ≥3 subsystems [WebGL2 fbm aurora shader + tilt/pointer steering + brightness-driven modal audio] + #3 named refs).

## §468 — 2026-06-18 · kids WIDE fire (3 divergent laugh-first EMBODIED explorers on 3 different non-glass INPUTS — camera-motion / device-shake / mic-breath; answers JURY 2026-06-17 #2 "the next off-glass swing should be embodied, not another glow-screen" + #5 "make a kid laugh — the kids side has gone uniformly solemn" + #1 "groove/play, NOT a cadence/voice-leading") — winner `719-kids-stomp-zoo`; **2 banked seeds**

Research path (c): consumed the §466-banked stomp-zoo seed (born of RESEARCH §466's VTech-2026 "your-movement-is-the-controller" thesis); today's dive (RESEARCH §468) added the BodyBeats/Laban named ref + the Ghost-Arcade June-2026 finding. **Three embodied inputs by design** so the siblings don't collide: SEE (camera), SHAKE (devicemotion), BLOW (mic-breath). **Winner: `719-kids-stomp-zoo`** — whole-body stomp parade; the directest answer to JURY #2 (most embodied/off-glass) + #5 (parade ripple, tuba BONK, freeze-dance). ### `720-kids-shake-pop` — `queued` ⭐ RESURRECT FIRST (a genuinely fresh INPUT for the lab — **device-SHAKE** via `accelerationIncludingGravity`, distinct from the lab's existing tilt/orientation pieces; a clean haptic/anticipation register). **Shake the iPad like a fizzy bottle: pressure builds (the googly bottle trembles, fills with bubbles, a bubbly-POP groove quickens + a rising whistle), then it ERUPTS — a comic descending BLURP + confetti fountain + screen-shake — and resets, ready again.** Built files (self-reported clean tsc+eslint; in `/tmp/dream-losers/`): `page.tsx` (devicemotion jerk-magnitude → low-passed fizz-pressure accumulator 0→1 that leaks when idle; squash-stretch trembling bottle, blue→orange/pink color build; accelerating pitched-POP percussion + rising whistle; eruption on ~0.9 OR shake-stop = BLURP + Canvas2D confetti; always-on bubbly bed; **iOS `DeviceMotionEvent.requestPermission()` in the Start gesture**; big tap-PUMP fallback + `text-rose-300` notice for no-sensor/denied; ghost auto-shaker on idle/load; safe chain `master(≤0.3)→lowpass(6500)→comp(−10/20:1)`; full teardown incl. listener removal). **Tags:** DeviceMotion SHAKE INPUT (embodied, off-glass) · Canvas2D OUTPUT · shake-magnitude accumulator → comic build-and-release + accelerating popping-groove TECH · silly anticipation-JOKE + popping-groove VIBE. **Why it lost:** 719 is *more* embodied (whole room/body vs device-held) and the truer literal answer to JURY #2's named "embodied-spatial/installation" lane, and it's the standing RESURRECT-FIRST seed; 720 is very close behind and arguably carries a lab-first input. **Named refs:** Disney's *12 Principles of Animation* (Anticipation + Squash-and-Stretch) · Toca Boca playful-physics. **Resurrect** as its own fire (verify the shake-input is a true lab-#1 via grep; if so it clears the floor at 3/5), or as the haptic/anticipation sibling of a "your body is the controller" kids suite. Honest 2/5 (#2 ≥4 subsystems [shake detector + pressure accumulator + popping scheduler/synth + Canvas2D eruption particles] + #3 named refs).

### `721-kids-blow-balloon` — `queued` ("Blow the Balloon Buddy" — warm breath-controlled toy). **Blow into the mic to inflate a wobbling googly balloon-buddy that HUMS a rising tone; stop and it either POPS with confetti (if huge) or lets out a long comic descending RASPBERRY; tap to add more bobbing colored balloons = a silly balloon band.** Built files (self-reported clean — builder ran `npx tsc --noEmit` + `eslint` = 0 errors/0 warnings; in `/tmp/dream-losers/`): `page.tsx` (`getUserMedia({audio:true})` → `AnalyserNode` RMS = blow-strength, **mic analysis-only, never routed to destination**; breath accumulates radius [capped] + spring/velocity wobble; hum = triangle through a rising lowpass [a swelling drone, NOT a melody]; raspberry = detuned saw+square through a 28→12 Hz LFO-wobbled lowpass, pitch sliding down; pop = sine boing + noise puff + confetti; always-on drone+breeze bed; press-&-HOLD fallback + `text-rose-300` notice for no-mic/denied; ghost auto-blower on idle/load; safe chain `master(≤0.3)→lowpass(7000)→comp(−10/20:1)`; full teardown incl. mic-track stop). **Tags:** mic BREATH/blow INPUT (embodied) · Canvas2D OUTPUT · mic-amplitude-envelope + breath-onset → comic inflation synth + soft-body wobble physics TECH · silly raspberry-balloon VIBE. **Why it lost:** lowest diversity of the three — mic/breath input has the most lab precedent (`158-kids-hum-paint`❤️, `393`/`413` vowel-from-voice, `705-kids-silly-voice`, `609` blow-balloons), and Ocarina-style blow-to-play is the most *expected* of the three mechanics. **Named refs:** Smule *Ocarina* (2008) · EWI breath-controller lineage. **Resurrect when** the kids side has been off mic-input for several cycles and wants a warm, gentle (vs. loud-stomp) toy — the raspberry is its genuine laugh; deepen with balloon-animal shapes that change the timbre and a two-balloon "duet" that beats against itself. Honest 2/5 (#2 ≥4 subsystems [mic breath envelope + blow/release detector + comic inflation/raspberry synth + soft-body balloon physics] + #3 named refs).

### `714-kids-feed-monster` — `queued` (genuinely funny + a fresh *framing* of the loop-stacker as FEEDING). **Drag silly foods into a hungry googly monster's mouth — it CHOMPS them in rhythm and BURPS a bubbly note, and the more you feed it the groovier the beat gets.** Built files (self-reported clean; removed): `page.tsx` (5 draggable foods [🍩 round kick · 🍌 boing pluck · 🌶️ spicy hat + comic red-shiver · 🧁 bell ding · 🍦 brr tremolo], each on its own 16-step pattern; drop-in-mouth toggles that food's looping layer into a **Chris-Wilson look-ahead scheduler** [25ms tick / 120ms ahead / 100 BPM] over an always-on soft shaker pulse; squash-stretch jaw synced to chomps + belly that grows + comic descending **BURP** w/ bubble particles, more frequent the more it's eaten; belly-tap spits a layer out [no penalty]; instant <50ms chomp blip on feed; safe chain `master(0.28)→lowpass(7500)→comp(−10/20:1)`; 2.5s-idle ghost-hand auto-demo) + `README.md`. **Tags:** touch-drag (object-to-target as the sequencer-trigger) INPUT · Canvas2D googly-monster OUTPUT · "feeding as a step-sequencer" loop-stacking groove TECH · silly hungry-monster VIBE (laugh-first). **Why it lost:** its underlying mechanic (Incredibox look-ahead loop-stacking) is the EXACT thing `702`/`711`/`696` already shipped — lowest diversity of the three despite the charming feeding framing. **Refs:** Toca Kitchen · Incredibox/Sprunki · Cookie Monster/Pou. **Resurrect when** the kids side has been off loop-stackers for several cycles and wants a warm character toy; deepen with more foods, a "too-full" comic hiccup mode, and foods that change the monster's color/shape.

## §465 — 2026-06-18 · adult WIDE fire (3 divergent ambitious explorers on 3 different INPUTS × techniques × vibes — deliberately breaking the adult side's 4-cycle DEEP streak per JURY 2026-06-17 "the median has gone comfortable"; lead = the literal realization of JURY #4 "sonify a non-market live stream for free") — 2 banked seeds

Cycle 465 ran an adult **WIDE** fan-out: 3 briefs on 3 different inputs (external-live-data / WebMIDI / autonomous-sim), each clearing the ambition floor via different tags, none a banned tag. Research-led by RESEARCH §465 (the EMSC / SeismicPortal **free no-auth real-time WebSocket** — `wss://www.seismicportal.eu/standing_order/websocket` — is one URL away from the JURY-#4 ask). **Winner: `712-seismic-room`** — the lab's first real-world external-live-data instrument (each earthquake on Earth → a struck tone + a ripple on a Canvas2D world map; Gutenberg–Richter Poisson fallback always sounds). Chosen as the literal JURY-#4 build + the today's-research→today's-build chain + the most bulletproof glance. Both losers were demoable + self-reported clean tsc+eslint for their folders; the authoritative winner-only `npm run build` (exit 0, 503/503 static) is the gate; losers `rm -rf`'d before it. Resurrect by re-spawning the brief — **max committed folder after this fire is `712`** (so 713/714 below are free to reuse as real numbers; renumber only if a later fire consumes them first):

### `713-shadow-duet` — `SHIPPED §467` (realized as `718-duet-paths`, the concatenative "wired to your Paths piano" version Karel named; heuristic `716` + style-learner `717` variants banked §467) ⭐ RESURRECT FIRST [now points at the §467 banks] (the lab's FIRST Web MIDI piece + the jazz-responsive alt-arc Karel asked for + serves Karel-the-pianist directly). **Play a phrase (MIDI keyboard / computer-keyboard / on-screen keys) and a machine improviser ANSWERS IN THE GAPS — it listens to your last phrase and trades fours with you, like a duet partner with taste.** Built files (clean tsc+eslint, no `any` — minimal Web MIDI typings; removed): `page.tsx` (single-file: `navigator.requestMIDIAccess()` w/ note-on/off `0x90`/`0x80` parse + device-name emerald badge + `onstatechange` hot-plug; computer-keyboard fallback `a s d f g h j k l` + `w e t y u o` pre-snapped to **D Dorian** w/ `z`/`x` octave shift + on-screen mini-keyboard; **phrase detection** [ends after ~750ms silence past ≥2 notes → analyze pitch-set / register / density / contour]; **response generation** [score 12 tonics against Dorian/Mixolassign → infer key+mode; answer with COMPLEMENTARY register + INVERTED contour + density-matched + consonant chord-tone landing, via a weighted Markov-ish step-walk — an answer, never an echo]; **ducking turn-taking** [strike mid-shadow → it yields]; idle ~6s auto-demo; warm FM e-piano [pan L] vs cool synth-flute [pan R] + binding pad + limiter; Canvas2D piano-roll **river** [your notes warm amber/rose, shadow's cool cyan, flowing past a now-line]; full teardown) + `README.md`. **Technique:** real-time machine-improviser call-and-response (phrase analysis → key inference → intentional answer). **Why it lost:** 712 is the *literal* JURY-named build (#4 non-market live stream) + the today's-research chain + more bulletproof (no interaction needed for a glance); 713's WebMIDI wasn't from this cycle's dive. **Why resurrect ⭐:** it's the lab's **FIRST WebMIDI** (a genuine never-used technique = ambition #1), it directly serves **Karel-the-pianist** + realizes the **jazz-responsive alternate journey-arc** Karel explicitly asked for + the live-performance-fitness priority, and it softens the standing solo monoculture (you're never alone — a second voice answers). Honest 3/5 (#1 first WebMIDI + #2 ≥4 subsystems [MIDI/keyboard input + phrase analyzer + generative responder + dual-voice synth + Canvas piano-roll] + #3 named refs [George Lewis *Voyager* · jazz trading-fours]). Tags: **MIDI/keyboard INPUT** (off banned touch-primary) · Canvas2D OUTPUT · machine-improviser call-and-response TECH (NOT a harmonic event as subject — the subject is *conversation/turn-taking*) · jazz-responsive VIBE (NOT hushed). **Deepen on revival** (from its README): velocity-sensitive dynamics (soft phrase → tender answer); style-learning over a session (the shadow grows to sound like *your* partner); harmonic memory (the pad follows a slow form seeded by your recurring pitch sets). Strongest next step: feed it Karel's actual Paths piano (analyze a real recording's phrases → the shadow answers *his* playing).

### `714-physarum-choir` — `queued` (the autonomous slime-mold agent-network choir — resurrect for an autonomous-generative / WebGPU-compute slot). **Thousands of agents grow a living Physarum transport network across the screen, and you HEAR the network forming, branching, and collapsing as an evolving choir; tap to drop "food" and the colony converges over the next seconds (with a bloom tone).** Built files (clean tsc+eslint, no `any`, helpers `make*`/`step*`/`diffuse*`/`draw*`/`sample*`/`read*`; removed): `page.tsx` (single-file: Jones-2010 agent model — ~6k agents sense [3 sensors] / steer [TURN_ANGLE + jitter] / move / deposit on a 360×220 `Float32Array` trail field, then 3×3 diffuse + decay; luminous black→violet→amber→white LUT rendered via offscreen `ImageData` scaled full-bleed; **sonification** from a downsampled 16×16 feature grid every ~100ms [density→drone richness, busiest columns→3–6 Dorian voices, Δdensity→swell plucks]; pointer food drop = bright trail blob + x-position-pitched stereo bloom; limiter; WebGPU feature-detected for a badge only; full teardown) + `README.md`. **Technique:** Physarum agent-based transport network (a genuinely never-used lab technique). **Why it lost:** lowest floor of the three (honest **2/5** — #1 first-physarum + #3 named refs); shipped **Canvas2D-primary** with the WebGPU path softened to a capability badge, so the renderer is the hot Canvas2D and the GPU *reach* wasn't realized; autonomous generative-viz is a register the lab is less *starved* on than live-data or WebMIDI. **Why resurrect:** the physarum network is beautiful + long-form-evolving (minute 3 ≠ second 10), and the **real** version is the deepening — a genuine **WebGPU-compute port** (storage-buffer agents + storage-texture trail, 50k–200k agents, async feature read-back) would make it the lab's first true compute-shader agent sim and a real Anadol-scale reach. Honest 2/5 (#1 first physarum agent-sim + #3 named refs [Jeff Jones 2010 *Artificial Life* 16(2) · Sage Jenson/mxsage "mold"]). Tags: autonomous-sim + pointer-food INPUT · Canvas2D (→ WebGPU on revival) OUTPUT · physarum agent-network sonification TECH (NOT a harmonic event) · organic/emergent/luminous VIBE. **Deepen on revival:** the WebGPU-compute port (the headline); multi-species physarum (two cross-attracting trail channels → two interleaved choirs, call-and-response between colonies); long-memory audio (drifting modal centre + reverb send swelling with cumulative network area for a true 10-minute arc).

## §464 — 2026-06-18 · kids WIDE fire (3 divergent GROOVE + LAUGH explorers on 3 different INPUTS — answering JURY 2026-06-17 #1 "groove/rhythm, not a cadence" [harmonic-event banned on kids], #3 "ship a multi-user / two-player piece" [STILL 0× across the window — the most-starved register], #5 "MAKE A KID LAUGH"; none a banned tag [no voice-leading, no hushed, no audio-first+SVG, no WebGL2, no lone-finger touch]) — 2 banked seeds

Resurrect by re-spawning the brief, renumber on revival — **max committed folder now 702**:

### `703-kids-boing-zoo` — `queued` ⭐ (the most BULLETPROOF + outright funny — RESURRECT FIRST when a fresh-input kids slot opens; zero deps, zero GPU surface, no permission gate that can deny). **Tilt the iPad → a zoo of 8 fat googly jelly animals tumbles, piles, and BONKS, and every bonk honks a silly note** — a tumbling pile becomes emergent slapstick rhythm, never a chord. Each animal owns one fixed pitch (G3 C4 D4 E4 G4 A4 C5 D5); a hard collision or fast wall-smack honks *that* animal's note + squash-stretches it. Tilt harder → faster tumbling → busier groove. Built files (clean by builder inspection; removed): `page.tsx` + `README.md`. **Technique:** hand-rolled plain-TS **2D physics** (circular bodies, gravity-from-tilt via DeviceOrientation beta/gamma, wall bounce w/ restitution, O(n²) pairwise impulse resolution, n=8 = cheap); **collision-velocity → sound** with impact speed **clamped** (`MAX_BONK_SPEED`, never harsh) + **debounced ~80ms per body & per pair** (a resting pile never machine-guns); Web Audio bonk = triangle pitch-swoop + FM wobble + band-passed noise thock; master `0.3 → lowpass(2.6kHz) → compressor`; Canvas2D squash-&-stretch blobs w/ googly eyes that flick toward velocity. Fallback: iOS `requestPermission()` tilt, Android auto-listen, **mouse/touch "tip-the-box + flick"** on desktop/denied, + 2.5s idle auto-demo gravity wobble; full teardown. Refs: **Toca Boca** (no-fail no-words play); cartoon **"Boing!" Foley**; the **2D physics-sandbox lineage** (Crayon Physics / Powder Game). **Why it lost:** physics + tilt are *trodden* lab tags (286-kids-jelly-choir, 303-kids-wind-harp, 603-kids-yell-blob; tilt is a familiar input) and it misses the jury's loudest ask (multi-user), so it's a lower-novelty *machine* than the multi-user winner. **Why resurrect ⭐:** it is the **most bulletproof glance of the three** (no network, no GPU, no camera/permission that can fail — works for *any* reviewer on *any* device including desktop via the drag fallback) and the most reliably *funny*; ship it the moment a low-risk silly-kids slot is wanted, or when the winner needs a no-permission sibling. Honest 2/5 (#2 ≥4 subsystems [2D physics engine + collision-sonification + tilt input + Canvas2D viz] + #3 named refs). Deepen on revival: Verlet soft-body jelly (bonk timbre from constraint stress); per-species voices (lion roar-honk / duck quack-honk); tilt-to-tempo quantized layer + collectible-treats bassline.

### `704-kids-funny-face-party` — `queued` (the directest EMBODIED / off-glass camera piece — resurrect for a body-input kids slot; revives the freeze-dance register). **Your own silly faces and wiggles drive a goofy cartoon creature in a FREEZE-DANCE party** — front camera (on-device only) → the googly creature mirrors & exaggerates every bob/wiggle, the silly groove gets busier the more you move, a big move / open mouth fires WHOOP/raspberry/boing, and every 8–12s the music shouts **FREEZE!** and cuts: hold still → confetti + happy chime; keep wiggling → goofy wobble + giggle. A real game *and* a joke, never a cadence. Built files (clean by builder inspection; removed): `page.tsx` + `README.md`. **Technique:** **no MediaPipe / no deps** — self-rolled: hidden `<video>` → tiny **32×24 offscreen canvas** → per-pixel luma **frame-difference** = motion energy (0–1) + a weighted motion **centroid** (drives googly-eye gaze) + a **center-region** sum approximating "big move / open-mouth" (the gag trigger, armed/re-armed so one big move = one gag); cheap, inside rAF, <100ms. Web Audio look-ahead scheduler runs a ~100 BPM silly loop whose shaker/blip density scales with motion + **drives a groove→freeze→result state machine**; master `lowpass → compressor → 0.3`. Fallback: camera denied/unsupported → an auto-dancing **ghost** synthesizes its own motion + performs the freeze-dance itself (also nudges after 2.5s idle); mic never used; privacy stated with a 📷 icon, denied message in `text-rose-300`. Refs: Apple **Photo Booth / Animoji**; classic **freeze-dance** party games; **Daniel Rozin's interactive mirrors**. **Why it lost:** camera-motion → character is a *recently-trodden* input (677-presence-field, 690-kids-weather-hands, 652-kids-dance-band) — less of a novel *machine* than a multi-user first, and the "open mouth" cue is approximated by center-region activity rather than true mouth detection (honest, but soft). **Why resurrect:** it is the directest **off-glass / embodied-body** answer (JURY's standing "get off the glass") and the **freeze-dance game loop is a genuinely fresh mechanic** the lab hasn't built — the comedy payload is the freeze, not the mirror; resurrect when a body-input kids slot is open and 677/690 have cooled. Honest 2/5 (#2 ≥4 subsystems [camera-motion analysis + creature renderer + groove scheduler + freeze game state machine] + #3 named refs).
