Every GitHub event accretes onto a single luminous **diffusion-limited-aggregation reef**: pushes attach branch nodes, stars light them, PRs thicken branches, forks sprout new seeds — so the structure is **visibly fuller at minute 5 than minute 1** (an hour of the planet making things, held as long-form memory). Honest 2/5 (#2 ≥4 subsystems + #3 named refs). Tags: live-external-data INPUT · WebGPU(+Canvas2D fallback) OUTPUT · accreting-growth / long-form-memory TECH · organic-Anadol VIBE.

### `700-commit-rain` — `queued` (the **Ikeda austere** take — the world's creation falling as data-rain). GitHub events stream as **falling grains of light + monospace `glyph + repo` labels** down a black field, white/cyan, with a registration grid + star scan-flashes + a live throughput readout (ev/min · total · LIVE vs SYNTHETIC); pitch by repo-hash in a drifting D-Dorian set, timbre by event type, an **inharmonic 5-partial drone** that thickens/brightens with global throughput. **Canvas2D** (retina), zero GPU surface. Built files (clean — self-reported `tsc -p` + `eslint` zero errors; removed): `page.tsx` (field+feeds start instantly = idle auto-demo, Begin unlocks audio, readout strip, amber source notice, design-notes toggle, full teardown), `field.ts` (rolling 12s throughput aggregate + Canvas2D renderer), `audio.ts` (inharmonic drone bed + per-event grains, throughput→gain/brightness, repo-hash→pitch, screen-x→pan; master ≤0.32→lowpass→comp→dest), `feed.ts` (SyntheticFeed instant + LiveFeed keyless poll ~78s, metered, graceful fallback), `README.md`. Refs: **Ryoji Ikeda** *data.matrix* / *test pattern*; **Hatnote "Listen to Wikipedia"**; **github.audio**; **Brian Foo**. **Why it lost:** the falling-grain data-spectacle is the most *familiar* AV form of the three (lower surprise, against the jury's "the median has gone comfortable"), and shipping it would push **Canvas2D to 4× in the rolling-10 (triggering a next-cycle ban)**. **Why resurrect:** the most **bulletproof** of the three (no GPU path at all) and the cleanest austere/clinical register; resurrect when Canvas2D has cooled and an Ikeda data-spectacle is wanted. Honest 2/5 (#2 ≥3 subsystems + #3 named refs). Tags: live-external-data INPUT · Canvas2D OUTPUT · data-sonification / streaming-grain TECH · Ikeda-clinical VIBE.

## §458 — 2026-06-17 · kids WIDE fire (3 divergent SILLY / GROOVE / off-glass explorers — directly answering JURY 2026-06-17 #5 "MAKE A KID LAUGH" [silly pole 0×] + #1 "groove, not a cadence" [harmonic-event banned on kids] + #2 "get off the glass"; each a different embodied input × renderer × comedy mechanic) — 2 banked seeds

Cycle 458 ran a kids **WIDE** fan-out: 3 briefs whose *subject* is groove, play, and laughter (not a harmonic event), each off the glass and on a different embodied input × renderer × silly mechanic. Research-led by RESEARCH §458 (Incredibox; the vocal-percussion / beatbox onset-classification literature — Hazan & Stowell 2010, the AVP dataset 2019). **Winner: `696-kids-mouth-band`** (make a mouth noise → onset+spectral classification → a silly drum in a looping beat a goofy creature bops to; the directest research-chain build, a qualified lab-first vocal-percussion classifier, serves KIDS.md's vocalization goal, bulletproof Canvas2D glance). All three were demoable + self-reported tsc/eslint-clean for their folders; the two losers were `rm -rf`'d only after the winner's authoritative `npm run build` passed. Resurrect by re-spawning the brief, renumber on revival — **max committed folder now 696**:

### `698-kids-dance-mirror` — `queued` ⭐ (the directest **OFF-GLASS body/dance** piece — RESURRECT FIRST for an off-glass kids slot; revives the jury-named abandoned dance register). Dance in front of the webcam → a rubbery **DOM/CSS** cartoon character **mirrors & EXAGGERATES** your moves (lean→overshoot-lean, jump→boing-up+squash, wild→jelly-wobble) while a bouncy groove **builds** with motion energy (more wiggling = more honks/boings/sparkle over a ~100 BPM look-ahead beat); **FREEZE comedy** — stop moving and the character freezes mid-pose with a slide-whistle "screech-halt" + held breath, springing back to silly life when you move (the freeze-dance beat is the emotional payload). Camera is **analysis-only**: each frame → a tiny 64×48 offscreen canvas → frame-diff motion-energy + centroid, then discarded — **never shown, recorded, or sent** (no network/api/guard). Built files (clean — self-reported `tsc --noEmit` + `eslint` clean; removed): `page.tsx` (getUserMedia in the Start tap, 64×48 motion analysis, DOM/CSS springy character via CSS transforms, build-with-energy groove scheduler, freeze/wake detection, Ghost-Dancer auto-demo on deny + 2.5s idle visual preview, full teardown) + `README.md`. Refs: **Daniel Rozin's mechanical mirrors** (*Wooden Mirror* — the piece-mirrors-you lineage); **Just Dance** move-and-copy; **Toca Boca / Sago Mini** consequence-free play. **Why it lost:** camera-motion→character is a *trodden* lab input (652-kids-dance-band, 524-kids-hand-firebird, 234-kids-hand-creature, 101-camera-song), so it's less of a novel *machine* than the mouth→drum-kit winner, and it has no same-day research chain (refs foundational). **Why resurrect ⭐:** it is the **directest "GET OFF THE GLASS"** answer (JURY #2, the never-moved ask) and literally *revives the danceable/groovy/body register the jury named as built-once-at-652-then-abandoned* — "the kids side fled rhythm for harmony"; **DOM/CSS is the freshest renderer** (0× in the recent window, zero GPU surface). Honest 2/5 (#2 ≥3 subsystems [webcam motion analysis + DOM/CSS character physics + build-with-energy groove scheduler + comedy synth] + #3 named refs). Tags: webcam-motion (off-glass, analysis-only) INPUT · DOM/CSS OUTPUT · mirror-&-exaggerate + freeze-comedy + building-groove TECH · dance-party/silly VIBE. Risk on revival: motion-energy normalization needs per-device/lighting tuning (ghost-demo + smoothing cover the glance).

### `697-kids-shake-circus` — `queued` (the charming **three.js squash-&-stretch** shake toy — resurrect when a fresh-GPU kids slot opens). Hold the tablet like a giant rattle and **shake it** → a rubbery 3D jelly clown on a striped circus podium **squashes, springs, and BOINGS** (volume-preserving Pixar squash-&-stretch via a damped 1-D spring + per-vertex jelly wobble), every shake honks a cartoon horn / springs a boing, drops a bead into a looping ~100 BPM bounce groove, and makes the creature sillier (brighter, googlier, bigger grin); a big shake occasionally **POPS confetti** + a slide-whistle reveal. Built files (clean — self-reported `tsc --noEmit` + `eslint` clean; removed): `page.tsx` (three.js via dynamic `import("three")`, DeviceMotion shake [gravity high-passed] + DeviceOrientation tilt with iOS `requestPermission()` in the Start tap, drag/spacebar fallback, ghost auto-shake demo, **Canvas2D wobble-blob fallback** on WebGL fail, full `renderer.dispose()` teardown) + `README.md`. Refs: **the 12 Principles of Animation — "Squash & Stretch"** (Thomas & Johnston, *The Illusion of Life*, Disney); circus/slapstick comedy SFX (boing / clown-honk / slide-whistle). **Why it lost:** **three.js was just used** (677/689 adult) — renderer rotation — and shake→creature is a lower-surprise *machine* than the mouth→drum-kit winner; no research chain. **Why resurrect:** the **purest charm/character** of the three (the squash-stretch jelly clown is the most outright *funny* object), and shake-as-a-rattle is a genuinely embodied kids input the lab barely uses; resurrect when WebGL/three.js is cold again, or ship its Canvas2D path as primary to dodge the GPU count. Honest 2/5 (#2 ≥3 subsystems [motion+tilt sensor + spring/jiggle physics + bounce-groove scheduler + comedy synth + three.js scene] + #3 named refs). Tags: DeviceMotion-shake+tilt INPUT · three.js OUTPUT (Canvas2D fallback) · shake-energy→squash-stretch + building-bounce-groove TECH · circus/silly VIBE. Risk on revival: real-device shake threshold calibration.

## §457 — 2026-06-17 · adult DEEP fire (one concept — *a shared multi-participant groove room: networked control-signals, local synthesis, one shared clock* — 3 interaction models; the lab's return to MULTI-USER, the JURY 2026-06-17 most-starved register [0× in the last 15], with GROOVE not a cadence) — 2 banked seeds

Cycle 457 ran an adult **DEEP** fan-out: ONE concept — a serverless same-origin `BroadcastChannel` groove room where clients broadcast only lightweight *control signals* (presence, a shared beat-zero epoch, conflict-free last-write-wins edits) and **synthesize all audio locally**, phase-locked to one shared clock (the 2026 collaborative-web-music principle, RESEARCH §457) — attacked via 3 interaction models. Directly answers JURY 2026-06-17 #3 (multi-user, the genuinely unbuilt register) + #1/#5 (groove/rhythm/play, NOT another tension-resolution cadence). All three are Canvas2D, multi-user, groove-first, and clear an honest **2/5** floor (#2 ≥3 subsystems + #3 named refs; #1 not claimed — BroadcastChannel exists in 319/334/341). **Winner: `694-pulse-ring`** (shared rotating clock → emergent group polyrhythm via per-player subdivisions; the freshest *machine*, the purest multi-user payoff, the directest "groove not cadence," bulletproof Canvas2D). Both losers were demoable + clean-by-inspection for their folders; `rm -rf`'d only after the winner's authoritative `npm run build` passed (exit 0, 494/494). Resurrect by re-spawning the brief, renumber on revival — **max committed folder now 694**:

### `693-ensemble-floor` — `queued` ⭐ (the most LEGIBLE / crowd-pleasing multi-user piece — RESURRECT FIRST when an accessible "band room" co-play is wanted). A collaborative **16-step drum machine** as a lit dance floor: 5 instrument lanes (Kick, Snare, Hat, Bass, Chord-Stab) on one shared step-grid at 110 BPM in A-minor/dorian. Each participant **claims a lane** (tap a lane header → it takes their color; can't steal a human-owned lane) and everyone toggles a **shared groove**; all clients hear the same combined beat, locally synthesized, phase-locked to one broadcast `performance.now` epoch. CRDT-lite grid (per-(lane,step) last-write-wins timestamps); late joiners request + merge full state. Solo → bots claim empty lanes after 3s with tasteful patterns (amber "🤖 session players"), retire when a real peer is heard (emerald "👥 N players"); 2.5s-idle auto-demo builds the groove. Canvas2D lit pulse-grid with sweeping playhead + trigger flashes, one rAF loop, full teardown. Refs: **The Hub / League of Automatic Music Composers** ("send data, not audio"); **Toshio Iwai's Tenori-on** (the lit grid-instrument). **Why it lost:** a step-sequencer is the most *familiar* form of the three (lower surprise, against the jury's "the median has gone comfortable"), and its independent lanes are less of an *emergent* collective than 694's polyrhythm. **Why resurrect ⭐:** it's the most immediately readable "everyone makes the groove together," the friendliest first multi-user piece to put in front of someone, AND the cleanest base to layer a real **WebRTC/WebSocket cross-device** transport onto (the control-signal design ports directly). Honest 2/5 (#2 ≥3 subsystems [4: BroadcastChannel sync + shared-epoch scheduler + 5-voice synth + Canvas2D grid] + #3 named refs). Tags: multi-participant/lane-claim INPUT · Canvas2D OUTPUT · shared-clock step-sequencer groove TECH · band-room/dance-floor VIBE. Pure Web Audio + Canvas2D + same-origin BroadcastChannel (no network/api/guard — runs forever via auto-demo).

### `695-trade-fours` — `queued` (the accumulating LOOP-STATION jam — love-aligned to `172-loop-station`❤️). A shared always-on group **groove** (kick/snare/hat + walking-ish bass) runs at 100 BPM for everyone; the **solo spotlight** rotates round-robin through the roster **every 4 bars** (jazz "trading fours"). Whoever holds it plays an A-dorian riff (pads or keys A–K); their 4-bar riff is **captured and folded into a shared arrangement** that every client keeps hearing on later passes — so the piece **accumulates** into a full collective jam (loop-station, made multiplayer). Networked-control (spotlight handoffs + captured note-lists + shared epoch over `BroadcastChannel`); solo → 1–2 bot session players join the rotation + auto-play your turns on idle. Canvas2D jam-room: player orbs around a ring, the travelling spotlight beam + bar-countdown, drifting note-trails for the accumulating arrangement, a solo-pad strip. Refs: jazz **"trading fours"**; the **loop-station / live-looping tradition** (Reggie Watts / KT Tunstall). **Why it lost:** the most complex/fragile of the three (riff capture + replay across bars, ~970 lines), and its *turn-taking* mechanic already exists in the lab at `334-kids-pass-the-song` (sequential, not the simultaneous-emergent collective the multi-user register most wants to showcase). **Why resurrect:** the **accumulation/long-form** angle is genuinely under-served (the arrangement is *different* at minute 5 than minute 1 — a multi-user long-form piece) and it's the directest extension of Karel's loved `172-loop-station`; resurrect by leaning hard on the accumulation/arrangement-grows angle (and a fresh framing) to dodge the `334` turn-taking overlap. Honest 2/5 (#2 ≥3 subsystems [4: BroadcastChannel relay + shared-transport scheduler + groove+lead synth + Canvas2D stage] + #3 named refs). Tags: multi-participant/spotlight-relay INPUT · Canvas2D OUTPUT · rotating-spotlight + captured-riff-accumulation TECH · jam-room/loop-station VIBE.

## §456 — 2026-06-17 · kids WIDE fire (3 divergent explorers, each a *real non-pentatonic harmonic event* via different input × output × technique × vibe; one fully off-glass) — 2 banked seeds

Cycle 456 ran a kids **WIDE** fan-out: 3 distinct harmonic-event briefs, deliberately spread across tags so none repeated the rolling-10. Research-led by RESEARCH §456 (embodied music-cognition; three.js-2026 velocity-feedback; RoboJam/ToCoPlay collaboration). **Winner: `690-kids-weather-hands`** (off-glass, eyes-off, tilt+motion+mic energy → continuous major↔minor **mode-morph**; the only off-glass candidate → lands JURY 2026-06-16 #2 AND #1 at once). Both losers were demoable + ESLint/tsc-clean for their folders; `rm -rf`'d only after the winner's authoritative `npm run build` passed (exit 0). Resurrect by re-spawning the brief, renumber on revival — **max committed folder now 690**:

### `691-kids-sky-steps` — `queued` ⭐ (the cleanest **on-glass modulation** piece — RESURRECT FIRST for an on-glass kids harmonic-event slot). A glowing cloud-staircase rising into a night sky; every tap climbs one step AND the sustained drone chord (root+fifth+third+octave) **modulates up a perfect fifth around the circle of fifths** via a common-tone glide — the harmonic *floor* shifts under the child (JURY #1's exact "a drone that shifts under them"). The climb is **Shepard-tone-tempered** (each voice a 7-octave bell-weighted stack with a wrapped fractional-octave offset `+7/12 mod 1`) so the key rises *endlessly* while register stays child-safe. Raw **WebGL2** fragment shader (night sky + stars + aurora + perspective staircase, hue driven by circle-of-fifths position) + Canvas2D fallback. Refs: Shepard/Risset endless-glissando; circle of fifths as a map of nearby keys. **Why it lost:** on-glass (the winner had to be the off-glass one to move the 13/15 screen metric) + Shepard tones already exist in the lab (132/40/187/313). **Why resurrect ⭐:** it's the directest, most child-perceptible "the whole world travels to a new key" — a clean modulation lesson with a genuinely clever endless-ascent. WebGL2 is the coldest GPU renderer (last 675 @ 450). Pure Web Audio + WebGL2 + Canvas2D fallback (no network/key — runs forever via auto-climb).

### `692-kids-two-friends` — `queued` (the **collaborative / social** harmonic event — two-finger multi-touch, two players). Two glowing creatures (amber left, cyan right); each player drags one up/down to pick a note on a 2-octave C-major scale; the **real interval** between them is scored consonant (unison/3rd/4th/5th/6th/octave → warm glow + a catenary "hold-hands" light bridge) vs. dissonant (2nd/tritone/7th → shimmer/wobble), and on a dissonance the piece **holds the tension ~1.1s then performs an actual voice-leading resolution** (`resolveStep` nudges the nearer voice one scale-step, preferring upper-voice-down — the classic suspension gesture) into a consonant lock. three.js (orbs + additive halos + light bridge) + Canvas2D fallback; solo degrade = a free auto-partner. Refs: RoboJam (Martin et al.); ToCoPlay; group-synchrony / joint-attention in early-childhood music. **Why it lost:** on-glass + the most crowded space of the three — two-player kids pieces already exist (154-clap-back, 156-star-connect, 341-star-pair, 489-pond-pair, 508-accord-call) and tension→resolution overlaps 669-hum-answer + 272-tune-purr. **Why resurrect:** "harmony as a thing two people MAKE together" is a genuinely under-served *social* register; resurrect by leaning harder on the **group-synchrony timing** angle (shared pulse, turn-taking) rather than just interval-scoring, to clear the crowded "two-voice resolution" space. three.js (used 677/689 adult — re-audit on revival).

## §455 — 2026-06-17 · adult DEEP fire (one concept — *a generative meditation that DREAMS its own visuals through a real AI image pipeline* — 3 technical approaches; directly answering JURY 2026-06-16 #4 "AI-pipeline chain, STILL ZERO in last 15; Karel wants AI image INSIDE an AV piece") — 2 banked seeds

Cycle 455 ran an adult **DEEP** fan-out: ONE big concept (the lab's first AI-image-pipeline-inside-AV in this whole window), 3 parallel approaches. Research-led by RESEARCH §455 (the TouchDesigner+StreamDiffusion "audio-reactive AI" trend — built the slow, long-form *inverse*). **Winner: `689-dream-chapters`** (a named journey-engine arc Threshold→Drift→Deepening→Aurora→Return → one flux/schnell "chapter card" per movement → a three.js accumulating depth-field of remembered dreams = *long-form memory made visible*; dodges banned Canvas2D, hits Karel care #4 journey-engine-alternative). The two losing approaches were demoable (each self-reported ESLint-clean for its folder); folders `rm -rf`'d only after the winner's authoritative `npm run build` passed (exit 0). Both reuse the proven guarded `fal-ai/flux/schnell` + procedural-fallback pattern. Resurrect by re-spawning the brief, renumber on revival — **max committed folder now 689**:

### `687-latent-oracle` — `queued` ⭐ (the cleanest **closed loop** of the three: a drifting D-Dorian drone auto-evolves through weather phases [calm→luminous→veiled→resolving]; every ~40s it composes a prompt from its current weather → flux image → **WebGL2 ping-pong feedback dissolve** [Anadol latent-flow, old dreams smear into new] → dominant color sampled offscreen → fed back into the synth's **filter cutoff / dissonance / tint** so the SOUND moves toward what it just dreamed. A genuine audio→image→**audio** loop, long-form & stateful.) Refs: Refik Anadol latent data-paintings + Memo Akten *Learning to See*. **Why it lost:** the warping-shader-backdrop register is the most familiar of the three in the lab; 689's navigable accumulating-memory gallery is the bigger, more Karel-surprising concept. **Why resurrect:** it's the only one that closes the loop all the way back into the synthesis — the most conceptually complete "the music dreams and is changed by its dream." **Resurrect when** the rolling-10 is cold on WebGL2 (it was 3× at cycle 455). Pure Web Audio + WebGL2 + guarded flux route + procedural value-noise fog fallback (runs forever, zero network).

### `688-spectral-seance` — `SHIPPED` ✅ as **`704-spectral-seance`** (cycle 461, 2026-06-17) — the audio→image→**audio** *return trip* was resurrected off Canvas2D onto a raw **WebGL2** raster/scan-line output (dodging the jury's Canvas2D ban exactly as this seed prescribed), re-sonifying each dreamed image column-by-column through a 48-osc bank. Mic-PCM caveat was sidestepped: the descriptor reads the *generative* bed's own spectrum (no mic), so the loop is fully closed without raw-PCM capture. See INDEX `704-spectral-seance`. *Original seed below for provenance:*
### `688-spectral-seance` — `queued` (the most *surprising* single mechanic — the **return trip**: a generative inharmonic spectral bed [ratios 1, 2.07, 3.31… so it never collapses to a chord] → a windowed-DFT spectral descriptor [low/mid/high split, centroid brightness, flatness roughness, density] → controlled-vocabulary prompt → flux image → the image **re-sonified** column-by-column through a 64-osc bank [low rows = low pitch, bright = loud], a cyan Ikeda scan-line sweeping in time = "play the picture, the image is now an instrument"). Refs: Ryoji Ikeda data-spectacle + the image-as-spectrogram lineage (Xenakis **UPIC**, Metasynth, **ANS synthesizer**). **Why it lost:** **Canvas2D output** (jury-banned 4× + just-used in 454) and a mic caveat (the shared mic hook doesn't expose raw PCM, so mic biases the descriptor rather than being the literal captured waveform). **Why resurrect:** the audio→image→audio *return trip* is the most original idea in the batch and a true cross-modal-translation menu hit; **resurrect off Canvas2D** (re-skin onto a WebGL grain/raster output to dodge the ban) and wire literal mic PCM via a dedicated AudioWorklet/ScriptProcessor capture. Guarded flux route + deterministic procedural-image fallback re-sonified identically (closed loop runs with zero network).

## §454 — 2026-06-17 · kids WIDE fire (3 orthogonal "real harmonic event" explorers, all NON-pentatonic, each a different harmonic *event* × renderer × input — directly continuing JURY 2026-06-16 #1 "kill the pentatonic crutch; let a kid HEAR a real harmonic event") — 2 banked seeds

Cycle 454 ran a kids **WIDE** fan-out across three orthogonal ways to give a 4yo a *real harmonic event* without a pentatonic scale-snap, each a different INPUT × OUTPUT × TECH × VIBE, all dodging the over-represented audio-first/SVG output (4× in the last 10) and the hot WebGL2 (3×). Lead bent by RESEARCH §454 (arXiv:2605.07489, He/Li/Sun/Huang, May 8 2026 — a *retrieve→edit→rerank* chord-generation decomposition, which is almost exactly the architecture of a child-driven reharmonizer). **Winner: `684-kids-every-note`** (chromatic-touch → real-time reharmonization/chord-chasing → Canvas2D aurora; the directest hit on JURY #1's spirit "let a kid meet a wrong note and discover it isn't scary," the only candidate clearing an honest **3/5** floor, the *coldest* renderer [Canvas2D 0× in the last 10, zero GPU-failure surface], and the visible same-day research chain). The two losing explorers were demoable + each self-reported **clean `tsc --noEmit` + `next lint`** for their files; folders removed only after the winner's authoritative `npm run build` passed. Resurrect by re-spawning the brief, renumber on revival — **max committed folder now 684**:

### `685-kids-two-moons` — `queued` (the 6+/FAMILY-tier flagship — tilt a moon between a GOLD sun [MAJOR] and a BLUE sun [MINOR] → continuous major↔minor MODE-GLIDE via a cents-glided third → **three.js** cosmic scene · kids 6+ / cosmic-awe). The 4th bank of the two-suns/moons concept (663→668→674→682→685); still seeking a ship slot.
Root + fifth stay pinned while the THIRD glides continuously by cents (+400¢ major → the un-fretboardable neutral ~+350¢ "blue third" → +300¢ minor) via `osc.frequency.setTargetAtTime` (~0.12s), with coupled secondary cues (lowpass opens toward major 1.8k→6k, a small arpeggio-tempo nudge) + a morphing parallel sixth — so the child hears the single note that turns happy↔sad continuously, not as a switch. Built files (clean, removed): `page.tsx` (three.js via dynamic `await import("three")` w/ WebGL-fail notice; two breathing emissive suns w/ halos, drifting moon that tints toward the nearer sun, 1400-pt starfield, sky/fog color-lerp; `DeviceOrientationEvent` w/ iOS `requestPermission` in the Begin tap + mouse-drag + ←/→ fallbacks; ~16s hands-free auto-demo gold→blue→gold; full rAF/listener/`renderer.dispose()`/geometry+material/audio cleanup) + `audio.ts` (`TwoMoonsAudio`: pinned root 220Hz + fifth, cents-glided third, morphing parallel sixth, kids-safe `gain ≤0.34 → lowpass ≤7.5k → comp → dest`) + `README.md`. **Why banked, not shipped:** (1) **6+/family tier, not the 4yo core** — the major/minor→affect mapping lands reliably ~age 7 (per §448/§450/§452 anchor), so its payload is shakier for the kids-default audience 684 serves; (2) renderer **three.js was just used** (677-presence-field) — shipping it back-to-back works against renderer rotation; (3) it's the 3rd-4th iteration of this concept without yet finding its slot. **Why resurrect:** the cleanest **opener of a deliberate Kids-6+/family tier** (where mode reads), OR — its strongest next-cycle hook — let the mode-glide drive a real *modulation* (the whole key, not just the third, shifts at the extremes) so it reads as travel between two worlds; or fold the cents-glided third into an adult piece. Honest ambition 2/5 (#2 ≥3 subsystems [tilt · cents-glide mode engine · tempo-coupled detuned synth · three.js scene = 4] + #3 named refs [Deryck Cooke *The Language of Music*; the third-as-affective-pivot; the neutral/"blue" ~350¢ third]). Tags: tilt INPUT · three.js OUTPUT · continuous-mode-glide TECH · cosmic-awe / 6+-family VIBE.

### `686-kids-wish-well` — `queued` ⭐ the most FUNDAMENTAL harmonic event (a note stretched into TENSION that then RESOLVES home) — drag-up-then-release → real **suspension / appoggiatura** over a drone → **WebGPU** particle bloom (real Canvas2D fallback) · kids 4+ / tender-magical.
Drag a glowing wishing-star UP and a "wish voice" glides up a whole step from the consonant third to the **suspended 4th** — a genuine non-chord-tone that grates against the always-on root+fifth drone; the harder the pull, the more pronounced the lean (slightly louder + a *detuned partial fading in* so the ear hears real **beating/roughness**, psychoacoustic dissonance, not just a different note). On RELEASE the voice **glides down by step, sus4→3** (`setTargetAtTime`) + a soft bell scaled by pull marks the warm arrival — the satisfying *click* of resolution. Built files (clean — `tsc --noEmit` clean, `eslint` 0, `next build` compiled the route exit 0; removed): `page.tsx` (drag/release input, ghost-hand auto-demo loop [pull→hold→release, stops on first touch], full unmount: rAF cancel, GPU destroy, audio close, listener removal) + `audio.ts` (drone + suspension voice + arrival bell, kids-safe `gain ≤0.30 → lowpass ≤6.2k → comp → dest`) + `gpu.ts` (hand-written WGSL compute advecting ~40k particles, gather-on-tension / expanding-ring-on-bloom, instanced additive render; silent fallthrough if `navigator.gpu` absent/init throws) + `render2d.ts` (real Canvas2D ~2.6k-particle fallback — the EXPECTED path on most devices, first-class strain→bloom) + `star.ts` (shared star+tether overlay) + `field.ts` (shared scene state) + `README.md`. **Why banked, not shipped:** (1) renderer **WebGPU was just used last cycle** (683-world-pulse) — back-to-back works against rotation; (2) 684 uniquely cleared 3/5 + had the same-day research chain, where 686 is an honest 2/5; (3) 684's Canvas2D is even *more* glance-bulletproof (no GPU path at all). **Why resurrect FIRST when a fresh-renderer kids harmonic-event slot opens** — tension→resolution is arguably the single most fundamental harmonic event and 686 teaches it viscerally; **re-audit WebGPU on revival** (now warm — or ship the Canvas2D path as primary to dodge the count and add zero GPU-failure surface). Honest ambition 2/5 (#2 ≥3 subsystems [drag input · suspension/appoggiatura audio engine · WebGPU/WGSL field · Canvas2D fallback = 4] + #3 named refs [suspension/appoggiatura 4–3; J.S. Bach chorale 4–3 suspensions BWV 253–438; Helmholtz roughness / Meyer *Emotion and Meaning in Music* / Huron *Sweet Anticipation*]). ## §452 — 2026-06-17 · kids WIDE fire (3 orthogonal "real harmonic event" explorers, all NON-pentatonic, all off banned Canvas2D, one fully off-glass — directly attacking JURY 2026-06-16 #1 "kill the pentatonic crutch" + #2 "get off the glass") — 2 banked seeds

Cycle 452 ran a kids **WIDE** fan-out across three orthogonal ways to give a child a *real harmonic event* without a pentatonic scale-snap, each a different input × output × technique × vibe (none Canvas2D), one fully **off-glass**. Lead bent by RESEARCH §452 (the inverse of the pentatonic crutch = *consonance is contextual* → reharmonization; firm 4yo ground = cadence/closure). **Winner: `680-kids-night-boat`** (off-glass / audio-first — rock the tablet → deceptive→authentic CADENCE; the only off-glass candidate [JURY's never-moved standing ask], the scarcest+most-bulletproof renderer [inline-SVG, zero GPU surface], and a harmonic event on the firmest 4yo developmental ground [closure]; realizes the §450 banked `676-kids-night-boat`). The two losing explorers were demoable + each self-reported **clean full-repo `tsc --noEmit` + `next lint`**; folders removed only after the winner's authoritative `npm run build` passed (488/488). Resurrect by re-spawning the brief, renumber on revival — **max committed folder now 680**:

### `681-kids-every-note` — `queued` ⭐ the FRESHEST-CONCEPT resurrect (the directest answer to JURY #1's spirit "let a kid meet a wrong note and discover it isn't scary") — touch 12 chromatic petals → real-time REHARMONIZATION / chord-chasing → raw **WebGL2** aurora garden · kids / luminous-wonder.
The deliberate **INVERSE of the pentatonic "no-wrong-notes" crutch**: instead of *restricting* the child to five safe pads, give the **full chromatic palette** (12 petals, ≥64px) and let the **harmony chase the note** so any note becomes consonant — play a "wrong" note and the world blooms a new color to embrace it. Built files (all clean, removed): `page.tsx` (12-petal ring + auto-demo that taps a phrase including deliberate out-of-key notes + WebGL2 canvas + Canvas2D fallback + live chord readout + full unmount cleanup) + `harmony.ts` (the engine: 10-chord lush candidate palette — diatonic I/ii/iii/IV/V/vi `Cmaj9…Am9` **plus borrowed colors** ♭VI A♭maj7, ♭VII B♭maj9, V/V D7, chromatic-mediant E♭maj7; `scoreFit` [chord-tone > 9/13 extension > ♭9-clash penalty], `voiceLeadCost` [greedy nearest-tone], `pickReharmonization` [fit-dominant, voice-leading tie-break]) + `audio.ts` (4 gliding pad voices + bell on top, master ≤0.36 → lowpass 7k → comp) + `gl.ts` (GLSL ES 3.00 aurora that re-tints to the chord hue + blooms a ring per tap, extra shimmer on borrowed chords, `WEBGL_lose_context` dispose) + `README.md`. **Why banked, not shipped:** it is the freshest *concept* and the directest jury-#1-spirit hit, BUT (1) it is **on-glass** while 680 lands the jury's never-moved off-glass ask; (2) its renderer **WebGL2 is HOT** (658/659/675 all WebGL2 in the recent window) where 680's inline-SVG is the scarcest + has zero GPU failure surface; (3) the reharmonization *payload* is subtler for a 4yo than 680's felt closure (per §452 developmental anchor). **Why resurrect FIRST** when an on-glass kids harmonic-event fire is wanted — but **re-audit the renderer on revival** (re-skin to inline-SVG or three.js to dodge the hot WebGL2 count, which would also lift its diversity score). Honest ambition 2/5 (#2 ≥3 subsystems [chromatic touch · reharmonization engine · WebGL2 garden · gliding synth = 4] + #3 named refs [FlowComposer reharmonization; *consonance is contextual*]; **#1 NOT claimed** — the lab already uses "reharmonization" in `637-slow-burn`; the fresh axis is *child-driven chord-chasing that embraces chromatic notes*). Tags: chromatic-touch INPUT · WebGL2 OUTPUT · real-time-reharmonization/chord-chasing TECH · luminous-wonder VIBE.

### `682-kids-two-moons` — `queued` (the 6+/FAMILY-tier flagship — tilt a moon between two suns → continuous major↔minor MODE-GLIDE via a cents-glided third → **three.js** cosmic scene · kids 6+ / cosmic-awe). Realizes the §450 banked `674-kids-two-moons` (upgrades §448 `668-two-suns` / §446 `663-two-suns`).
Tilt to drift a moon between a warm GOLD sun (MAJOR) and a cool BLUE sun (MINOR); root+fifth stay pinned while the **THIRD glides continuously by cents** (+400¢ → the un-fretboardable neutral ~+350¢ "blue third" → +300¢) via `frequency.setTargetAtTime` (0.12s), with coupled secondary cues (filter opens/dims, tempo nudges) + a morphing parallel sixth — so the child hears the single note that turns happy into sad, continuously not as a switch. Built files (clean — full-repo `tsc` + `next lint` zero errors, removed): `page.tsx` (three.js scene: two breathing emissive suns w/ halos, drifting moon that tints toward the nearer sun, 1,400-pt starfield, sky-color lerp; tilt + drag + ←/→ fallbacks; hands-free auto-demo sine-drift; full rAF/listener/`renderer.dispose()`/geometry+material/audio cleanup) + `README.md`. **Why banked, not shipped:** (1) **6+/family tier, not the 4yo core** — the major/minor→emotion mapping lands reliably ~age 7 (per §448/§450/§452 anchor), so its payload is shakier for the kids-default audience 680 serves; (2) renderer **three.js was just used last cycle** (677-presence-field) — shipping it back-to-back works against renderer rotation; (3) it is the third "two-suns/moons mode-morph" bank (663→668→674→682) without yet finding its ship slot. **Why resurrect:** the cleanest **opener of a deliberate Kids-6+/family tier** (where mode reads), or fold the cents-glided third into an adult piece; its strong next-cycle hook: let the mode-glide drive a real *modulation* (the whole key, not just the third, shifts at the extremes) so it reads as travel between two worlds. Honest ambition 2/5 (#2 ≥3 subsystems [tilt · cents-glide mode engine · tempo-coupled detuned synth · three.js scene = 4] + #3 named refs [third-as-affective-pivot; the neutral/"blue" ~350¢ third; Deryck Cooke *The Language of Music*]). Tags: tilt INPUT · three.js OUTPUT · continuous-mode-glide TECH · cosmic-awe / 6+-family VIBE.

## §451 — 2026-06-17 · adult DEEP fire (one concept — body-PRESENCE → SPATIAL-AUDIO room — three body-sensing approaches; realizes the long-banked `442-body-orchestra`, lands the jury's #1 standing untouched ask "embodied-spatial / room-presence") — 2 banked seeds

Cycle 451 ran an adult **DEEP** fan-out: ONE concept (spatialization-as-instrument — *where your body is becomes where the sound is*, via per-voice HRTF `PannerNode`s around one `AudioListener`) attacked by three different body-sensing techniques. Lead = the jury's single biggest untouched first, named across THREE consecutive juries (depth-camera / room-presence / projection installation — "zero embodied-spatial builds still"), bent by RESEARCH §451 (spatialization itself raises felt presence — arXiv:2601.22082; pose→spatial-sound — "Sounding Bodies" 2311.06285). **Winner: `677-presence-field`** (MediaPipe Pose skeleton → three.js luminous-body room; each joint drives a voice placed at its 3D position — the most literal installation-room realization, scarcest renderer [three.js 0× in last 10], glance path is MediaPipe-independent [idle preview + auto-demo run a synthetic body]). Built files: `page.tsx` + `audio.ts` (6 D-Dorian HRTF voices, Dm9→Gmaj9→Em11→Dadd9, master ≤0.34 → comp + convolution reverb) + `motion.ts` (64×48 frame-diff: global presence + centroid + per-voice column energy, no ML) + `render.ts` (WebGL2 violet→gold aura + Canvas2D fallback) + `README.md`. *Why banked, not shipped:* it shares 677's exact concept (spatial ensemble) but its INPUT (camera frame-diff) has a recent precedent (`652-kids-dance-band`) and its renderer (WebGL2) is hotter than 677's three.js; 677 reads more spectacularly as an *installation*. *Why resurrect FIRST:* it is the **zero-CDN, zero-dependency** version — no MediaPipe load risk at all — so it is the safest spatial-presence ship when robustness is the priority, and the cleanest cycle-2 base for the spatial-room thread. Refs: arXiv:2601.22082 · Krueger *Videoplace* (1974) · Anadol.

### `679-presence-veil` — `queued` (the ENVELOPMENT take — **MediaPipe selfie-segmentation** silhouette MASS [not joints]: closeness/coverage widens the per-voice azimuth fan from a frontal arc toward a full surround RING, so *approaching the camera wraps the ensemble around you* · raw **WebGL2** luminous-veil shader sculpted by the mask · adult ethereal/enveloping). Built files: `page.tsx` + `presence.ts` (centroid/coverage/spread/motion from a 64×48 mask + breathing-ghost auto-demo) + `segment.ts` (ImageSegmenter via CDN `/* webpackIgnore */` dynamic import — build never resolves the URL) + `audio.ts` (6 D-Dorian/Lydian HRTF voices, coverage→angular wrap+distance+reverb, master ≤0.34) + `gl.ts` (R8-mask veil shader + Canvas2D fallback) + `README.md`. *Why banked:* the most conceptually distinct (envelopment-by-mass, not joint-placement) but the subtlest to feel — coverage→surround-radius is a quieter payload than 677's spread-your-arms-and-the-voice-flies-there, and like 677 it carries the MediaPipe-CDN load path. *Why resurrect:* the lab's purest "your presence becomes an enveloping field" — strongest paired with headphones; the natural sibling/cycle-2 if the spatial-room thread wants *envelopment* rather than *placement*. ## §449 — 2026-06-16 · adult WIDE fire (3 orthogonal explorers across under-built adult registers, all dodging the JURY 2026-06-16 bans) — 2 banked seeds

Cycle 449 ran an adult **WIDE** fan-out across three orthogonal under-built adult registers, each a different input × output × technique × vibe, none touching a banned tag (no pentatonic, no Canvas2D, no scheduler-bed-as-only-idea, no warm-cozy, none the frozen groove/EDM). Lead = JURY #5's explicitly-named open register, **tender/intimate**. **Winner: `671-glasswork`** (the realization of the long-banked `660-glasswork` tender seed — a generative glass music-box driven by real exhaustive nearest-tone voice-leading, inline-SVG; the directest hit on the jury's named adult ask AND the most bulletproof glance candidate [pure SVG + Web Audio, zero GPU/sensor/network surface]). The two losing explorers, both demoable + each self-reporting clean isolated `tsc`/ESLint, moved to `/tmp` and removed only after the winner's authoritative `npm run build` passed; resurrect by re-spawning the brief, renumber on revival — **max committed folder now 671**:

### `672-cinema-swell` — `queued` (a CINEMATIC orchestral-swell instrument — hold chords → film-score string divisi blooms · MIDI + computer-keyboard + on-screen keys · **three.js** · cinematic-narrative / Zimmer-Richter)
The **directest fill of the cinematic / narrative journey-engine register** Karel names in his care #4 ("EDM build-and-drop, ritual, jazz responsive, **cinematic narrative** … as alternate arcs") and a clean companion to the tender winner. You HOLD chords (MIDI keyboard via Web MIDI, or `a w s e d f t g y h u j k o l p ;` on the computer keyboard, or an on-screen mini-keyboard) and a full film-score string section blooms around them in real time — it RESPONDS, it is not a fixed look-ahead bed. **Rule-based responsive orchestration** (`orchestra.ts → setChord`): the held chord is spread into **divisi** across three register groups — celli (low + octave-down doubling), violas (mid inner triad + octave doubling), violins (high top voice + octave-up sheen) — re-arranged on every chord change; each group is detuned-saw bowed-string voices through a lowpass with ensemble vibrato. **Swell:** the longer you hold, the louder/brighter the section grows (crescendo env), softly releasing on lift. A slow drifting **tension curve** (over ~30–60 s, nudged up by dense/dissonant chords, down by consonant rest) adds a low ostinato/heartbeat pulse and brightens the top when high, so the piece has a felt narrative arc ("different at minute 2"). **Output: three.js** pooled concert-hall stage — three banks of emissive columns (warm→cool by register) whose height/brightness/glow track each section's live swell, tension tinting the whole stage, a heartbeat floor-disc flash, slow camera drift, full `dispose()`. **Auto-demo:** if no input within ~2 s, a **ghost conductor** plays a slow Richter-ish progression so the stage is fully alive hands-off; real input takes over seamlessly. Master ends in a `DynamicsCompressor` brick-wall limiter (master 0.36). Graceful: no Web MIDI (Safari) → amber notice + working keys; no WebGL → rose notice (audio + keys still play). No API route, no guard, no new deps (`three` already in package.json). Named refs: **Hans Zimmer** (cumulative orchestral swells) · **Max Richter** (*On the Nature of Daylight* — string voicing) · **Jóhann Jóhannsson** (slow cinematic builds); technique anchor: orchestral **divisi** + crescendo-as-expression. **Ambition honest ~2/5** (#2 ≥3 subsystems [MIDI+keyboard input handler · rule-based divisi orchestration engine · swell + tension-curve state machine · three.js stage = 4] + #3 named refs; **#1 NOT claimed** — Web MIDI already exists in the lab [`291-harmonograph`], the fresh move is the responsive cinematic-orchestration instrument). **Why it lost (lane-leverage, not quality — it was a strong, build-clean candidate):** the binding jury directive THIS cycle is #5's explicitly-named **tender/intimate** register (671 hits it dead-on), and the jury also froze groove/EDM-adjacent energy for a week — cinematic is the lower-leverage swing here and three.js is the hotter renderer vs. 671's scarce inline-SVG. **Resurrect** as the lead of a cinematic/journey-engine-alternative adult fire (it IS the cinematic-narrative arc Karel keeps asking for), or as the orchestral companion in a "registers" suite; its strong next-cycle hook: let the tension curve drive a real harmonic *modulation* (key changes at arc peaks) so the narrative has acts, not just dynamics. Builder reported `tsc --noEmit` + ESLint clean in isolation; full working code is a fast re-spawn.

### `673-kepler-chimes` — `queued` (the literal MUSIC OF THE SPHERES — orbital mean-motion resonance → audible consonant polyrhythm that LOCKS · tilt + tap/arrow fallback · raw **WebGL2** · cosmic-Kepler awe)
The **awe/cosmic reach** of this fire and the most *surprising* concept — a star with orbiting planets where each planet chimes once per orbit and the orbital **periods snap toward small-integer mean-motion resonance** (2:1, 3:2, 4:3, 5:3 — the Laplace chain that locks the real TRAPPIST-1 system), so **stable orbital resonance becomes an audible, consonant polyrhythm you HEAR slide from phasing into a locked groove.** Deliberately NOT the chaotic three-body problem (that's `432-three-body`) — this is about *stable consonance*. **Orbital integrator** (`orbits.ts`): circular orbits tied by Kepler's third law (`T² ∝ a³`, inner = faster/higher), a chime when phase crosses the +x perihelion gate. **Resonance-snap engine:** each non-anchor planet's period is exponentially pulled toward `anchorPeriod × ratio` toward the nearest small-integer ratio, with a per-planet badge flipping `○` (drifting) → `●` (in resonance) — you watch AND hear the lock. With 3–5 planets the combined chime pattern is a long, slowly-phasing polyrhythm realigning only after the LCM of the periods (genuinely different at minute 2). **Synth** (`synth.ts`): always-on detuned star drone + breathing LFO; a 4-partial bell chime per planet, pitch from its just-intonation resonance ratio, register from orbital period, panned by screen-x; master ends in a `DynamicsCompressor` brick-wall limiter (0.34). **Output: raw WebGL2 / GLSL ES 3.00** (`gl.ts`): fullscreen `gl_VertexID` starfield, additive orbit trails/bodies/flares/rings, a drone-pulsing central star, full GL teardown. **Input:** device tilt (beta/gamma nudge the newest planet up/down the chain; iOS `requestPermission()` in-gesture) with **tap-to-add-planet-at-radius + arrow/drag fallback** so it's fully playable with no tilt. **Auto-demo:** seeds 3 planets in a TRAPPIST-like chain, slightly detuned so they audibly slide into lock over the first seconds — alive at a glance with zero hardware. No API route, no guard, no new deps. Named refs: **Johannes Kepler**, *Harmonices Mundi* (1619 — the harmony of the spheres) · **Matt Russo / System Sounds** TRAPPIST-1 sonification (the 8:5:3 mean-motion chain as rhythm/pitch) · mean-motion orbital resonance. **Ambition honest ~2/5** (#2 ≥3 subsystems [orbital integrator · resonance-snap polyrhythm engine · consonance-mapped bell synth + drone · raw-WebGL2 renderer = 4] + #3 named refs; **#1 NOT claimed** — orbital/n-body visuals exist [`432-three-body`, `308-orbit-choir`]; the fresh move is STABLE resonance-chain → consonant polyrhythm + tilt input). **Why it lost (lane-leverage + glance-verifiability, not quality):** the binding directive is #5's tender register (671), and 673 is the riskier glance candidate (raw WebGL2 + tilt + the resonance-lock is a by-ear effect hard to verify in a silent sandbox), where 671 is the lowest-risk pure-SVG piece; cosmic/orbital is also partly-trodden territory. **Resurrect** when an awe/cosmic register is wanted or as the headline of a "sonify a physical system" fire — it's genuinely fresh and beautiful; its strong next-cycle hook: let tilt/drag actually *break* a resonance (push past `●` back to `○`) so the player can hear the polyrhythm dissolve into chaos and re-lock — making the lock/unlock the playable instrument. Builder reported `tsc --noEmit` + ESLint clean in isolation; full working code is a fast re-spawn.

## §448 — 2026-06-16 · kids WIDE fire (3 orthogonal harmonic-event explorers, all dodging the JURY 2026-06-16 bans) — 2 banked seeds

Cycle 448 ran a kids **WIDE** fan-out across three orthogonal ways to give a 4-year-old a *real harmonic event* — the three the jury named that 662-gamelan-bend didn't already cover: **mode flip**, **dissonance-that-resolves**, **shifting drone**. None pentatonic-snap, none Canvas2D, none warm-cozy, one fully off-glass. **Winner: `669-kids-hum-answer`** (off-the-glass / audio-first — the directest hit on the jury's loudest standing directive "get a builder off the glass; the kids side has never shipped one" + the ⭐ PRIORITY banked-seed flag from §446; and on the firmest developmental ground per RESEARCH §448 — children perceive tonal *closure/its denial* emotionally before mode). The two losing explorers, both demoable + each self-reporting clean isolated `tsc`/ESLint, moved to `/tmp` and removed only after the winner's authoritative `npm run build` passed; resurrect by re-spawning the brief, renumber on revival — **max committed folder now 669**:

### `668-kids-two-suns` — `queued` (continuous major↔minor MODE MORPH via a cents-glided third · tilt + drag · **three.js** · cosmic-dramatic)
*Tilt a pale moon between a warm gold sun (MAJOR) and a cool blue sun (MINOR); root+fifth stay pinned while the **third glides continuously by cents** — +400¢ → the neutral "blue third" ~+350¢ (a note no piano can play) → +300¢ — so the child hears the ONE note that bends happy into sad. Tempo-coupled (gold faster/brighter, blue slower/dimmer) so it works for a pre-mode-literate kid.* Built clean + demoable: raw three.js scene (two breathing/glowing suns, drifting moon, 1400-pt starfield, sky-color lerp), `frequency.setTargetAtTime(...,0.04)` continuous third-glide over a detuned pad, tilt (iOS-permission-in-gesture) + auto drag fallback + 1.5s no-event probe + `text-rose-300` note, ~5s idle auto-demo, safe master chain. **Why banked, not shipped:** the strongest *renderer-diversity* candidate (three.js, off banned Canvas2D) and a genuinely fresh continuous-third-glide technique — but lost to 669 on two counts: (1) the off-glass directive is the jury's single loudest *unaddressed* kids ask and 669 is the only off-glass sibling; (2) RESEARCH §448 — the "happy↔sad mode flip" premise is developmentally shaky for the 4yo target (mode→emotion mapping doesn't land reliably until ~7; tempo and *change* do). **Resurrect:** the fastest "continuous mode-morph" resurrect, ideally re-pitched as an **older-kid / family** piece (the age where mode reads), or fold the cents-glided third into an adult piece. This upgrades §446's banked 663-two-suns (SVG) to three.js. Tags: tilt INPUT · three.js OUTPUT · continuous-mode-morph TECH · cosmic-dramatic VIBE.

### `670-kids-drone-cave` — `queued` (SHIFTING DRONE / moving pedal-point · gamepad + keyboard · **WebGL2** · mysterious-discovery)
*Guide a glowing firefly through a luminous cave; ring a small FIXED set of crystal bells while a sustained tanpura-style drone (root+fifth) slowly **glides its root** room to room — A → F → C → E → home (i–♭VI–♭III–V). The same bells sound bright over home, warm over F, open over C, genuinely tense over E, then resolve — the child discovers "the floor note changes the feeling," even though their own notes never changed. Harmony is relational to the bass.* Built clean + demoable: hand-rolled WebGL2 fbm cave shader (mood uniform lerps cyan/gold↔violet/indigo with the harmony), two-osc drone with `exponentialRampToValueAtTime` ~3.2s root glides, Gamepad API + arrow/WASD fallback + `gamepadconnected` live-switch, ~5s idle auto-demo, safe master chain. **Why banked, not shipped:** a genuinely fresh harmonic mechanism for the lab (moving pedal-point) and a strong mysterious register — but lost on (1) renderer: WebGL2 is over-represented (4× in the jury's last-15), while 669's audio-first is the rarest/best-rated output; (2) the off-glass directive again favored 669; (3) gamepad-fly-through-musical-space overlaps shipped 636-star-pilot. **Resurrect:** ship next kids cycle when WebGL2 is cold and a screen-render is wanted; the drone-walk is worth keeping — consider re-skinning to three.js or pairing the moving-pedal engine with a different input. Tags: gamepad INPUT · WebGL2 OUTPUT · shifting-drone/pedal-point TECH · mysterious-discovery VIBE.

---

## §446 — 2026-06-16 · kids WIDE fire (3 orthogonal explorers, all attacking JURY 2026-06-16 #1 "ban the pentatonic crutch — give a kid a real harmonic event") — 2 banked seeds

Cycle 446 ran a kids **WIDE** fan-out across three orthogonal ways to give a 4-year-old a *real harmonic event* (no pentatonic, no scale-snap), with one builder pushed fully **off the glass** (JURY #2). **Winner: `662-kids-gamelan-bend`** (the only never-used technique — inharmonic modal bronze synthesis — and the most literal answer to JURY #1: bend a bronze bar into and out of tune across real slendro/pélog *laras*). The two losing explorers, both demoable + each self-reporting clean `tsc --noEmit` in isolation, `rm -rf`'d after all three builders' completion notifications confirmed; resurrect by re-spawning the brief, renumber on revival — **max committed folder now 662**:

### `663-kids-two-suns` — `queued` (continuous major↔minor MODE MORPH · tilt + drag · inline-SVG · cosmic-night)
*Tilt the tablet to drift a pale moon between a warm gold sun (happy/MAJOR) and a cool blue sun (sad/MINOR) — the chord's THIRD glides continuously by cents (≈+400→+300, passing through the neutral ~+350 "blue" third no piano can play) while root+fifth stay pinned, so the child hears the one note that turns happy into sad.* Built clean and demoable: SVG night sky (two breathing suns, drifting moon, twinkling stars, sky tint that warms↔cools with the harmony), `frequency.setTargetAtTime(...)` ~40ms glide so it's a smooth morph not a snap, a gentler parallel sixth morph (+900→+800), always-on detuned pad, tilt (iOS-permission-in-gesture) with auto drag fallback + `text-rose-300` note + 1.5s no-event probe, auto-demo sine-drift between the suns. **Why banked, not shipped:** lovely + clean, but the *concept* (a happy↔sad slider) is the simplest of the three and the technique morph is a 2/5 ambition vs. 662's never-used bronze synthesis. **Resurrect:** the strongest fast resurrect for a "continuous mode-morph" lane — pairs well with a future adult version. Tags: tilt INPUT · SVG OUTPUT · continuous-mode-morph TECH · cosmic-night VIBE.

### `664-kids-hum-answer` — `queued` ⭐ PRIORITY off-glass resurrect (mic pitch-detect → 4–3 SUSPENSION that resolves · audio-FIRST/eyes-closed)
*A child hums one note, closes their eyes, and an unseen choir answers — leaning on a tense held note (the 4th), then stepping DOWN to resolve it (the major 3rd, the sigh of release).* The fire's **off-the-glass builder** (JURY 2026-06-16 #2 — "get at least one builder off the glass; 13 of 15 are screens"): near-black screen, ONE breathing radial glow that pulses with the post-limiter amplitude (hue shifts violet→blue tense→resolved), works fully eyes-closed. Built clean + demoable: autocorrelation pitch detection (Chris-Wilson approach, RMS gate + parabolic refine + clarity gate, 75–1000Hz, octave-collapse), formant-bandpass "ah" voices (NO AI voice, NO samples), `answerWithSuspension` (textbook 4–3 suspension), settle-detector with 6.3s cooldown, self-playing demo loop on mic-denied + `text-rose-300` note, full track/rAF/timer/node cleanup. **Why banked, not shipped:** hits BOTH top jury provocations, but its *core technique* (mic autocorrelation → harmonized answer) closely repeats `280-kids-echo-canyon` — fresh framing, most-trodden technique. By the lab's tie-breaker (novel technique > redundant technique w/ fresh framing) 662 won. **The screen-monoculture metric still needs an off-glass SHIP — resurrect this first next kids cycle** (or push it further: harmonize the child's real pitch with a *suspension chain* that walks several resolutions, or add a second eyes-closed "breath" layer). Tags: mic INPUT · audio-only/off-glass OUTPUT · pitch-detect + suspension-resolution TECH · hushed/sacred VIBE.

## §445 — 2026-06-16 · adult WIDE fire (3 orthogonal explorers across genuinely-open adult registers) — 2 banked seeds

Cycle 445 ran an adult **WIDE** fan-out across three under-built adult registers, each a different no-banned-tag input × output × technique × vibe, bent toward the RESEARCH §445 "continuous, live-steered, never-landing causal stream" frame (Lyria RealTime / LiveBand arXiv 2606.03803, June 2026). **Winner: `659-pulse-cathedral`** (the jury's most-named missing adult register — *ecstatic/euphoric* — finally **shipped**: an EDM build-and-drop journey-engine arc with an audible+visible sidechain pump and a Shepard-folded forever-climbing bass; the directest hit on JURY 2026-06-15 provocation #2 "build the missing middle — ecstatic/danceable" AND Karel's explicit care #4 "EDM build-and-drop as an alternate journey arc"). The two losing explorers, both demoable + each self-reporting clean `tsc --noEmit` + `eslint` in isolation, `rm -rf`'d AFTER all three builders' completion notifications confirmed; resurrect by re-spawning the brief, renumber on revival — **max committed folder now 659**:

### `660-glasswork` — `queued` (the TENDER / wistful 3am music-box · inline-SVG · adult / intimate-melancholy)
The lab's tender-register fill, and the strongest single resurrect for an adult fire that wants intimacy over energy (the jury named *tender* as part of the unbuilt missing middle alongside ecstatic). A slow generative celesta/glass piece in **D Dorian** (rare Lydian #11 tint): the next chord is a tension/resolution-weighted random walk (never a fixed loop, ~6–10s/chord); on each change every voice moves to the **nearest** chord tone (real voice-leading, doubling-penalized) so the pad audibly glides; a never-repeating melody is weighted over chord tones + Dorian passing tones with a per-phrase sine register-arch and woven-in rests. Voices (all synth, no samples): FM music-box bell (fast attack/long decay) · glassy triangle pad · soft sub · synthetic-impulse ConvolverNode reverb + filtered feedback-delay shimmer. **Inline-SVG** renderer (the scarce renderer): a constellation of soft glowing nodes (`<radialGradient>` + `<feGaussianBlur>`) that bloom on each note, faint threads tracing voice-leading between sounding chord tones; React redraw throttled ~26fps, most state in refs. Pointer bends it (up → brighter/higher register, right → busier), click "drops a stone" pulling harmony toward rest; idle ~1.4s → autonomous. `page.tsx`(443) + `theory.ts`(chords/voiceLead/melody palette) + `compose.ts`(look-ahead grid + melody) + `synth.ts` + `README.md`. Named refs: **Brian Eno** (*Music for Airports*, *Reflection*) · **Harold Budd** · **Erik Satie** (*Gymnopédies*). Ambition honest **2/5** (#2 ≥3 subsystems → 5 [harmony walk · voice-leading · melody+scheduler · 4-voice synth+reverb · SVG] + #3 named refs; **no #1** [generative-ambient voice-leading is worked territory], no #5). **WHY IT LOST (lane-leverage, not quality — it was a bulletproof, beautiful candidate):** the ecstatic winner fills a register that is **0× shipped** on the adult side and answers a *second* standing Karel directive (EDM journey arc), where tender — though genuinely missing — is the lower-leverage swing and the lower ambition (2/5, no #1); 660 also flirts with cozy-adjacency (mitigated by framing as melancholy, not warm). **Resurrect** as the tender-register fire, or as the intimate companion in a "registers of the missing middle" suite. The full SVG build is a fast re-spawn.

### `661-murmur-engine` — `queued` (boids murmuration AS the music — emergence-as-composition · Canvas2D · adult / awe-organic dusk-ambient)
The lab's emergence-sonification reach: a self-organizing **boids** flock (~320 agents, Reynolds separation/alignment/cohesion, O(n) via a uniform spatial-hash grid, fixed-60Hz sim decoupled from render) whose **aggregate statistics ARE the score** — centroid height → pitch register, mean scatter/speed → swell, the **alignment order parameter** `|⟨ĥ⟩|`∈[0,1] → harmonic consonance/chorus stability, local density/cohesion → chord voicing richness — low-pass smoothed so the music never jitters; 5 detuned-saw choir-ish pad voices in a consonant D-dorian/pentatonic scale + soft sine flutter-grains on scatter/regroup events; genuinely different at minute 3 than minute 0 (the flock is). Pointer = a diving hawk (repulsion → dramatic scatter-and-regroup). **Canvas2D** dusk-gradient sky (tinted by flock height) + additive `lighter` light-trail flock over a low-alpha persistence wash + predator glow. `page.tsx`(377) + `boids.ts`(sim + `computeStats`) + `synth.ts` + `README.md`. Named refs: **Craig Reynolds, "Flocks, Herds and Schools" (SIGGRAPH 1987)** · **Vicsek et al. 1995** (order-parameter / collective-motion phase transition). Ambition honest **2/5** (#2 ≥3 subsystems → 4 [boids sim · flock-analysis · generative synth · Canvas2D] + #3 named refs; **no #1** [`537-kids-sky-murmuration` + `8-particle-life` already in the corpus — flocking is not lab-first], no #5). **WHY IT LOST:** (1) **renderer** — Canvas2D is HOT (3× in the rolling-10: 636/640/652); shipping it pushes the screen-renderer monoculture the jury warns about to 4×, where the winner's WebGL2 is the *scarcest* screen renderer; (2) **lowest novelty** — emergence-as-music + boids are both already in the corpus, so no #1; (3) lower-leverage register (awe-ambient) vs. the winner's 0×-shipped ecstatic. **Resurrect** with a **scarce renderer** (WebGL2 GPU instancing for 5–10k agents, or SVG) and a sharper sonification (e.g. the order-parameter driving a real phase-transition in the arrangement — flock "boils" → the music breaks), which would lift it toward a #1. The Canvas2D build is a fast re-spawn.

## §444 — 2026-06-16 · kids DEEP fire (one concept — his-real-piano garden — via 2 interaction models) — 1 banked seed

Cycle 444 ran a kids **DEEP** fan-out: ONE ambitious concept (the lab's FIRST kids piece built on Karel's REAL recorded piano — granular-resynthesized, body-steered by tilt, pentatonic-quantized so no wrong notes) attacked via two **interaction models**. **Winner: `658-kids-piano-garden`** (tilt rolls a glowing seed; where it dwells his piano BLOOMS into a persistent singing flower → the garden grows, genuine long-form state). The losing approach, demoable + hand-verified (no node_modules in its sandbox), `rm -rf`'d AFTER both builders' completion notifications confirmed; resurrect by re-spawning the brief, renumber on revival — **max committed folder now 658** (note `656` deliberately left FREE for a §443 banked-seed [653/654] revival):

### `657-kids-piano-garden` — `queued` (tilt = a WIND that scatters Karel's REAL recorded piano into a swarm of singing FIREFLIES · WebGL2 point-field · kids / luminous-joyful)
The **bulletproof, instantly-joyful momentary-instrument register** of the his-real-piano concept, and the fastest single resurrect for a kids fire that wants pure delight over accumulation. Same core as the winner — loads Karel's *Welcome Home* recording via the read-only `/api/audio/549fc519-…` route (`audio.ts` verbatim, synth fallback) and **granular-resynthesizes** it with every grain `playbackRate`-quantized to a ±2-octave C-major pentatonic (no wrong notes), ≤25-voice cap, kid-safe `master ≤0.5 → lowpass ≤7.4k → comp` chain — but the **interaction is tilt-as-WIND, not a rolled seed**: vertical tilt (beta) → brightness/register, horizontal tilt (gamma) → stereo pan + the **wind direction of a flow field**; grain **density + spread scale with tilt magnitude** (level = sparse gentle motes, big tilt = a joyful dense swarm). Each grain onset spawns a glowing **firefly mote** (≤520) in a **WebGL2 additive point-sprite field** (Canvas2D fallback) that drifts on the tilt-driven wind, flares at onset, fades over its life. DeviceOrientation + iOS-gesture unlock, drag + arrow-key fallbacks (`text-rose-300` notice), ~2.5 s idle auto-demo "breeze", ~12-min lullaby fade. `page.tsx` (714, single-file) + `audio.ts` (verbatim) + `README.md`. Named refs: Curtis Roads *Microsound* · Xenakis · Eno · Karel's *Welcome Home*; love-aligned `227-paths-granular`❤️ + the `262-aurora`❤️/`130`❤️/`236`❤️ particle/swarm lane. Ambition honest 2–3/5 (**#1** first kids piece on his real recording [shared with the winner] + **#2** 4 subsystems [loader · granular engine · tilt + flow field + fallbacks · WebGL2 point-field] + **#3** named refs). **WHY IT LOST (lane-value, not quality — it was the bulletproof candidate):** it is a *momentary* instrument — motes fade and nothing accumulates — where the winner's **persistent singing flowers** give the garden genuine multi-minute state/evolution (the quality the jury praised in `624`), a stronger #2 and the bigger concept; the winner's modular engine/renderer split is also cleaner, and 657 has a dead `padGain` bus (wired to master with no source — harmless since grains always spawn, but a loose end). **Resurrect** as a kids fire when pure momentary delight beats accumulation, or as the WIND sibling of a his-music-for-kids suite; the full single-file build is a fast re-spawn.

## §443 — 2026-06-16 · adult WIDE fire (3 orthogonal explorers across the jury's open adult lanes) — 2 banked seeds

Cycle 443 ran an adult **WIDE** fan-out across 3 jury-named open adult lanes, each a different no-banned-tag input × output × technique × register, all bent toward the RESEARCH §443 "continuous, live-steered, never-landing stream" paradigm (Lyria RealTime). **Winner: `655-breath-sanctuary`** (the jury's demanded 3rd off-screen/embodied piece — an eyes-closed JI overtone-choir + singing-bowl ritual steered by your *breath*; the scarcest renderer [audio-only] + freshest input [mic-breath]; provocation #3). The two losing explorers, both demoable, `rm -rf`'d AFTER all three completion notifications confirmed; resurrect by re-spawning the brief, renumber on revival — **max committed folder now 655**:

### `654-latent-chain` — `queued` (audio → image → words → image · the lab's FIRST 2-model AI pipeline CHAIN · WebGL2 audio-warped texture · adult / latent-Anadol)
**The boldest bank of this fire AND the directest serve of Karel's most-wanted standing directive** ("AI image generation INSIDE an AV experiment — the image responds to/shapes the audio"). Karel's piano (`/api/audio/549fc519-…`, A-minor synth fallback) → `AnalyserNode` features (RMS, spectral centroid, spectral-flux onset, 12-bin chroma→Krumhansl key, valence) → a `Mood` → prompt words → **Model 1 `fal-ai/flux/schnell` text→image** → **Model 2 `fal-ai/florence-2-large/more-detailed-caption` image→caption** (caption SHOWN on screen as "the machine reads its dream", salient words re-mixed into the next prompt = a self-narrating latent walk) → the latest image becomes a **WebGL2 audio-warped texture ground** (strip-ripple ← energy+flux, energy zoom/pulse, RGB smear on onset, violet bloom); always-on procedural WebGL2 Mood-field fallback. **Budget-safe + the glance-fix it lost on last time:** guarded route (first two `POST` lines `guard(req)`, 501 w/o `FAL_KEY`), a `{probe:true}` zero-spend key check, AI strictly opt-in behind "Dream the picture" (15 s cooldown) **OR exactly ONE auto-fired step on Start when the probe confirms a key** so the chain is *visible* to a key-holding reviewer. Files (removed): `page.tsx`, `analysis.ts`, `synth.ts`, `renderer.ts`, `api/route.ts` (guarded), `README.md`. Ambition honest **3/5 with a HARD #1** (first 2-model AI chain — grep-0× + #2 four subsystems + #3 Anadol *Machine Hallucinations* / Akten *Learning to See*). **WHY IT LOST AGAIN (glance-verifiability + production-risk for an UNATTENDED ship, NOT ambition — it was the highest-ambition candidate):** the headline (the chain) is still only *seen* with a live `FAL_KEY` a reviewer triggers; at a silent 06:30 unattended deploy it defaults to the procedural field, so the very thing that makes it a #1 goes unexercised — AND it carries paid-AI + network + a builder-flagged-uncertain florence-2 model-id (could 500 every caption, graceful-degrades to Mood-only) straight onto Karel's live auto-deployed app. **Resurrect for a fire when Karel is AWAKE to click "Dream the picture" with a confirmed prod `FAL_KEY` + the florence-2 id verified live** — this is the lab's strongest unmined frontier and Karel's top directive; lead with the live chain and consider the cycle-2 hook (let Model-2's caption words bend the synth's key/timbre so the picture re-tunes the piano). The §441 bank of the same concept now has a working WebGL2 renderer + probe + auto-fire-on-key in the removed code.

### `653-endless-lift` — `queued` (continuous gospel-house key-LIFT that never drops, Shepard-folded so it climbs forever · WebGL2 rising-light · adult / ecstatic-euphoric · **tsc+eslint VERIFIED clean**)
The **directest fill of the jury's most-named open adult corner** — the ecstatic/euphoric *missing middle* ("not doom, not cozy"; the adult side has tender [637] + hypnotic [632] + old EDM-drop [387] but no euphoric ascent) AND the most bulletproof glance candidate (pure Web Audio + WebGL2, zero network/sensor/key surface). A four-on-the-floor gospel-house bed (kick · offbeat hats · backbeat clap · rising sub-bass · maj7-add9 pad · square Rhodes/organ stab · bell/voice sparkles through a dotted-eighth delay) assembled live by a **Chris-Wilson look-ahead scheduler** (25 ms poll, ~120 ms ahead); the harmonic stack **modulates up a semitone every 16 bars and never drops**, every voice **Shepard-folded** (pitch class placed in whichever octave sits highest under a fixed C4-centered bell window) so the key climbs forever in-band — perpetual rise is the point. ONE live **PRAISE** lever (▲ / ↑↓, hold-to-ramp) adds layers + brightens; **M** mutes; live "now in D♭ · +5 lifts" readout; long-form state (minute 2 higher/fuller/brighter than minute 0). **WebGL2** rising shafts of light (fullscreen-quad shader from `gl_VertexID`, no buffers; hue revolves once per octave of lift) — renderer swapped from the §441 Canvas2D bank to dodge the Canvas2D-at-3× line. Files (removed): `page.tsx`, `synth.ts`, `gl.ts`, `README.md` — **builder ran `tsc --noEmit --strict` + `eslint` both clean** (fastest, lowest-risk resurrect of the trio). Ambition honest **2–3/5** (#2 four subsystems [scheduler · gospel-house bed · Shepard-fold key-lift engine · WebGL2 renderer] + #3 named refs [**Roger Shepard 1964**; Floating-Points gospel-house lift; Lyria RealTime continuous-steered-stream frame]; **#1 SOFT** — Shepard tones foundational [40/132/187], Shepard-fold-as-gospel-lift is fresh framing). **WHY IT LOST (lane-size + #1-softness, not quality — it was the most bulletproof candidate):** a competent *register-fill* of an already-partly-worked lane vs. 655's structural diversity win (the off-screen/screen-monoculture gap the jury prizes most) + scarcer renderer. **Resurrect as the lead of a euphoric/ecstatic adult fire** (it IS the ecstatic register the jury keeps naming, and a continuous-ascent journey-engine alternative Karel asked for) — the fastest single resurrect for that corner, already build-verified clean.

## §440 — 2026-06-16 · kids WIDE fire (3 registers of the jury's "active-wonder middle") — 2 banked seeds

Cycle 440 ran a kids **WIDE** fan-out across 3 orthogonal NON-touch registers of the active-wonder middle. **Winner: `645-kids-piano-creatures`** (the lab's first kids MIDI — play a keyboard, an invisible band answers, no wrong notes). The two losing explorers, both demoable + build-clean, banked:

### `647-kids-clap-parade` — `queued` (mic-RHYTHM/onset → a marching parade that BUILDS · inline-SVG · kids / joyful-danceable groove)
**The strongest bank of this fire** — the *directest fill of the jury's named "missing middle"* (danceable/groovy) on the kids side, AND the cleanest glance (inline SVG = zero GPU failure surface, the exact quality that won `637-slow-burn` its fire). A 4-year-old CLAPS / stomps / beatboxes and a marching cut-paper band of glowing creatures grows, marches, and grooves with them. **Input is ENERGY/ONSET detection, deliberately NOT pitch** (a real departure from the trodden kids voice-PITCH lane 88/158/179/244/322): spectral-flux + low/high band energy off an `AnalyserNode`, adaptive running-mean threshold, ~120 ms refractory debounce so one clap = one onset; inter-onset-interval median → tempo estimate octave-folded to 70–150 BPM. 4 subsystems (onset/tempo detector · Chris-Wilson look-ahead scheduler driving a 5-voice C-major groove · arrangement state machine idle→build→groove gated by smoothed energy [so minute 2 ≠ minute 0] · pooled-marcher + confetti-physics SVG renderer, 14-marcher pool recycled). Named refs: New Orleans second-line / drumline; body-percussion (Stomp); call-and-response. Kid-safe chain + spacebar / big "CLAP!" button fallback + ghost-drummer auto-demo. Ambition honest 2–3/5 (#2 4 subsystems + #3 named refs; #1 soft — onset detection is known but mic-rhythm-as-groove-builder is fresh for kids). **Resurrect as the next kids-groove fire** (it IS the danceable register the jury keeps naming) — or as the seed of an ADULT groove cousin. Build-clean (`tsc --noEmit` 0), `page.tsx` ~30 KB. Lost only to 645's harder #1 (first kids MIDI) + Karel-pianist resonance.

### `646-kids-sky-conductor` — `queued` (camera body-MOTION → conduct a generative sky-orchestra · Canvas2D · kids / soaring active-wonder)
Lift your arms and the music soars to the constellation bells; sweep them and the strings swell — a non-touch whole-body conductor, no instrument, no touching. Dependency-free webcam **frame-differencing** on a 64×48 mirrored downsample → motion energy + vertical centroid (arms-up) + L/R balance (NO npm dep; optional MediaPipe-Pose via dynamic CDN import inside one try/catch, but frame-diff is the reliable default). Mapping: height → register (low gestures = cellos, high = flutes/bells), energy → dynamics, big sweep → string swell, stillness → soft sustained pad (never silent); all snapped to C Lydian → no wrong moves. 5 subsystems (frame-diff analyzer · Chris-Wilson scheduler · retuning string pad · arm-height melody · 8-beat-advancing harmonic progression). Canvas2D night-sky constellation orchestra (clusters brighten/shoot per section) + aurora ribbon trailing the conductor + energy-driven horizon glow; arrow-key / drag fallback on camera denial; ghost-conductor auto-demo. Named refs: orchestral conducting gesture vocabulary; the Theremin; Memo Akten body-as-instrument; Daniel Rozin. Ambition honest 2/5 (#2 5 subsystems + #3 refs; **#1 NOT claimable — camera/body-conducting is trodden** via 113-kids-conductor-wand, 217-dance-avatar❤️, 617-shadow-monster). Build-clean (eslint 0 / tsc 0), `page.tsx` ~29 KB. **Resurrect only with a sharper differentiator** from the existing body/conducting cluster (e.g. two-kid duet conducting, or pose-driven instrument *assignment* the child sculpts) so it isn't a fourth body-motion entry; and note Canvas2D was at 3× when banked (would hit 4× — pair a resurrect with a renderer re-audit).

## §438 — 2026-06-15 · kids DEEP fire (a TRUE cycle-2 of `624-kids-day-meadow`) — 1 banked seed

Cycle 438 ran a kids **DEEP** fan-out: ONE concept (a genuine cycle-2 deepening of the jury-named kids standout `624-kids-day-meadow` — answering JURY 2026-06-15 provocation #1 "finish a cycle-2 for once" — that swaps touch-to-plant → **tilt**, honoring provocation #4's touch ban + pulled by Karel's loved `83-kids-tilt-rain`❤️) attacked via **2 interaction models**. **Winner: `640-kids-day-wind`** (tilt steers a glowing WIND SPIRIT that plants where it dwells AND plays the garden like a wind-harp where it sweeps; swept gestures captured as a traveling motif, re-voiced into the current phase — a procedural MusicWeaver *Motif Memory Retrieval*). The losing approach, banked:

### `641-kids-tilt-meadow` — `queued` (tilt = GRAVITY on the whole scene → seed-physics garden + breeze wind-harp · Canvas2D · kids / playful-physics wonder)
The same cycle-2 of 624 (diurnal phase machine + motif-memory bank + Chris-Wilson scheduler reimplemented compactly, NOT imported; + traveling-motif MMR) but the input is **tilt-as-gravity on the entire scene**, not a steered cursor. `DeviceOrientation` beta/gamma → a gravity vector `g` (neutral-hold zeroed, smoothed, clamped); the sun/moon arc, clouds, and rolling terrain all tip with `g`. A stream of glowing **seeds** is integrated with semi-implicit Euler (gravity + slope-roll + friction + soft bounce) and rolls into the **valleys the child sculpts by tilting**; where a seed comes to rest ~0.5s it **sprouts** into a region-appropriate living thing (low valley=flower, hill=bird, night-airborne=star). The same `g.x` drives a breeze line that brushes grown things (wind-harp, rate-limited), and brushes feed the 7-slot traveling-motif buffer. 5 subsystems (diurnal machine · motif bank · scheduler · seed-physics integrator · breeze wind-harp) + named refs (MusicWeaver MMR arXiv 2509.21714; LUMIA handheld embodied composition arXiv 2512.17228; Iwai *Electroplankton*) = honest 2/5 kids. Builder reported `tsc --strict` exit 0 + eslint clean (could not run `npm run build` — no node_modules in its sandbox). **Why it lost:** (1) the gravity-physics planting is **indirect** — a 4-year-old can't reliably plant *where they intend* because physics decides where seeds settle, weakening the kids legibility/immediate-cause-effect mandate that 640's direct "steer the glowy wind" model nails; (2) it reads more as a *new physics toy adjacent to* 624 than a clean **deepening** of it (640 keeps 624's recognizable plant-a-thing-at-a-spot interaction), so 640 is the truer answer to the jury's "finish a cycle-2" ask; (3) the falling-seed-emergence core overlaps the §436-banked `634-kids-tilt-river` (tilt→falling particles) more than 640's wind-harp does; (4) the seed integrator is the only runtime-jank surface (640 has none). **Why resurrect:** it is the **bolder, more surprising** mechanic — a real particle sim a child sculpts by leaning — and genuinely fun; resurrect when a tilt-physics register is wanted, and use its own strong next-cycle hook: **persistent sculpted terrain** ("save a valley" — hold a tilt and the terrain *keeps* that low spot so seeds keep gathering there even after the world levels, each saved valley owning its own slowly-mutating chord) to make it a distinct landscape-sculpting instrument rather than 640-with-physics. Full working code is in git history at the folder path; treat as a fresh build, and de-overlap from 634 by keeping the sprout-on-rest (not emergent-chime) framing.
