## §434 banked seeds (cycle 434, ADULT DEEP — ONE concept ["refract Karel's own piano like light through a prism: HPSS strings/hammers split (the legible ground, from 606) → NMF-fan the strings into pitched register voices you solo/mute/re-mix"], attacked via two **NMF-legibility** approaches; the TRUE cycle-2 of the 606 decomposition spine the jury begged for two juries running; the loser build-complete [self-reported clean `eslint` + `tsc --noEmit` in isolation; both builder agents COMPLETED before removal — completion notifications confirmed], `rm -rf`'d after losing to `630-piano-refract`; resurrect by re-spawning the brief, renumber on revival — max committed folder now 630)

- **`629-piano-prism`** ("Piano Prism", status: `queued` · honest ~3/5 · **resurrect for a cycle-3 "true note solos" fire — its post-hoc sort is the seed of a chroma-template path**) — **"What if Karel's piano could be refracted into pitched voices you solo/mute — using PLAIN unsupervised NMF + a post-hoc spectral-centroid SORT to order the components low→high?"** Same spine as the winner (606's `audio.ts` + `hpss.ts` copied verbatim → HPSS strings/hammers → re-STFT the strings → KL-NMF K=4 → per-component Wiener-masked ISTFT → 5 looping voices [Hammers + 4 strings], instant gain-only solo/mute, WebGL2 prism + Canvas2D fallback, keyboard/tilt off-glass, ~2.5s auto-demo). **The DIFFERENCE (and why it lost):** 629 uses **random init + post-hoc centroid pitch-SORT** (Low/Low-mid/High-mid/High) — its own README admits "the pitch-sort is post-hoc and unsupervised — separation is still the random-init lottery; labels are descriptive." It makes the *labels* stable but the *component content* unstable run-to-run; the winner `630`'s warm-start makes the components themselves deterministic. **LOST to 630 on reliability + legibility (not ambition — identical floor: #1 first-NMF + #2 ≥3 subsystems + #3 Lee&Seung/Smaragdis&Brown/Fitzgerald + #4 cycle-2).** **Resurrect as the basis of a genuine cycle-3:** warm-start `W` from harmonic-comb **chroma templates** (one basis per pitch class, not per register) so each voice maps to a TRUE note → per-note solos + a "transcription" read-out, the arXiv 2510.08816 deep-NAE direction; the post-hoc-sort code is a useful fallback for components a chroma init doesn't claim. Built files (removed): `page.tsx`, `nmf.ts` (random-init + centroid-sort), `gl.ts`, `audio.ts` + `hpss.ts` (from 606), `README.md`.

## §433 banked seeds (cycle 433, ADULT WIDE — three orthogonal **off-GPU / scarce-renderer** explorers, each clearing the binding diversity ban [rolling-10 OUTPUT was **WebGPU 5× + WebGL2 4×** SATURATED → both GPU renderers banned → all three built on a scarce renderer: audio-only / Canvas2D / inline-SVG]; both losers build-complete [each self-reported clean `tsc --noEmit` + `eslint` in isolation; all 3 builder agents COMPLETED before removal — completion notifications confirmed]; `rm -rf`'d after losing to `626-empty-room`; resurrect by re-spawning the brief, renumber on revival — max committed folder now 626)

- **`627-latent-piano`** ("Latent Piano", status: `queued` · honest 2/5 · **FLAGGED DUPLICATIVE — do NOT re-ship as-is**) — **"What if Karel's own piano performance could PAINT its own backdrop — an AI image generated FROM the music's mood that then BREATHES and warps in real time WITH the live audio?"** Pipeline: dropped piano file (or a self-contained synthesized A-minor phrase, demoable with no file/network) → `AnalyserNode` features (RMS energy, spectral centroid, 12-bin chroma→pitch-class, spectral-flux onsets) → prompt synthesis (mood = scene × palette words) → **FAL flux/schnell** via a guarded api route (`guard` first two lines verbatim; 501 when no `FAL_KEY`) every ~14 s → the latest image becomes a Canvas2D ground continuously **warped** by the live audio (strip-ripple displacement ← energy+flux, energy zoom/pulse, RGB smear on onset, scanline shimmer, violet bloom); **always-on procedural fallback** paints the same `Mood` object offline. INPUT audio-file (his piano) · OUTPUT AI-image + Canvas2D warp (off the banned GPU renderers) · TECH audio-analysis → prompt → FAL → canvas displacement · VIBE latent/contemplative/Anadol-cool. Refs: **Refik Anadol** (*Machine Hallucinations*), **Memo Akten** (*Learning to See*), **Sound2Vision** / **Art2Mus** (arXiv 2602.17599). Serves Karel's two explicit standing directives (*AI image gen INSIDE an AV piece*; *use his real Paths music*), and nothing in the last ~30 cycles touched AI. **WHY IT LOST (decisive — a curate-time corpus check):** it is the **THIRD entry in an existing lane** — `441-latent-listening-room` (music "continuously *dreams a picture of itself*," AI image regenerated every few seconds from spectral character, returning colors bend the music back) and `448-piano-phrase-painter` (Karel's recorded piano paints itself, onset/phrase + chroma/key drive when a new image is dreamed, a closed audio→image→audio loop) already ARE this piece; 627's api route is literally the `448` flux/schnell template. Shipping it = the exact "nobody grepped the tree before re-shipping the ocean" pattern (580/588 twins) the jury keeps flagging. **Resurrect ONLY with a genuinely new axis** — a real **2+ model pipeline CHAIN** (the lab is STILL 0× on this: e.g. audio → mood-TEXT via an LLM → image → image-to-VIDEO), the actual unmined frontier, not a third single-model audio→image. Built files (removed): `page.tsx` (~590), `analysis.ts`, `prompt.ts`, `synth.ts`, `ground.ts`, `api/route.ts` (guarded), `README.md`.
- **`628-data-matrix`** ("data.matrix", status: `queued` · honest 2/5 · **the cleanest *edges* + scarcest-renderer resurrect — a near-instant ship for the next abrasive/clinical adult fire**) — **"What if the musical SCORE itself were the visual — a strobing, crisp, scalable VECTOR lattice that composes itself and sounds, with the austere clinical edge of Ryoji Ikeda rather than warm ambient glow?"** A 12×16 **inline-SVG** grid *is* the score: each row is a self-mutating **Euclidean rhythm** `E(k,16)` (Bjorklund/Toussaint `buildEuclid`); a bright cyan scan-column sweeps left→right (rAF, 16th-note resolution) and every lit cell it crosses fires a dry edged **FM blip** (square carrier + detuned sine modulator, click attack, ~90 ms decay, high-passed 600 Hz) pitched by row over a cold detuned cluster with **tritone tension** (NOT a major/pentatonic wash); densities drift ±1 and phases rotate every bar so it never settles; downbeat adds a band-passed noise tick; master capped `0.42` → hard compressor. Hundreds of cells mutated via cached `setAttribute` refs (no per-frame React re-render). Keyboard-first: space play/pause, ↑/↓ tempo, →/← density, s strobe (+ mirrored ≥44px buttons). INPUT keyboard (off the fingertip) · OUTPUT **inline-SVG** vector (the *scarcest* renderer, 0× — directly answers the jury's "commit to a renderer / off Canvas2D") · TECH Euclidean-rhythm sequencer + scan-line playhead + edged FM synth · VIBE Ikeda clinical / abrasive-precise / strobing (the directest *edges* answer to JURY 2026-06-14 #1). Refs: **Ryoji Ikeda** (*test pattern* / *data.matrix*), **Godfried Toussaint** (*The Euclidean Algorithm Generates Traditional Musical Rhythms*), **Vera Molnár**, **Iannis Xenakis** (*UPIC*). Ambition: #2 (≥3 subsystems) + #3 (named refs). **WHY IT LOST (lane-value, not quality):** the most bulletproof candidate in-sandbox (pure SVG + Web Audio, no network/sensor/GPU) and the cleanest edges/scarce-renderer pick — BUT 626 wins the **bigger open lane** (audio-FIRST off-screen, the jury's most-repeated 0× ask + the "massively bigger" mandate), where 628 is a refined-but-smaller grid sequencer (honest 2/5). **The fastest single resurrect for the next abrasive/clinical fire** — and the one to reach for to give inline-SVG a real shipped entry. Built files (removed): `page.tsx`, `README.md`.

## §429 banked seeds (cycle 429, ADULT WIDE — three orthogonal **edged / off-Canvas2D-or-off-screen / non-fingertip** explorers, each hitting a DIFFERENT still-open JURY 2026-06-14 adult lane; both losers build-complete [clean `eslint` + standalone `tsc` in isolation; all 3 builder agents COMPLETED before removal — completion notifications confirmed; AND a full `npm run build` with all 3 present passed **exit 0** + all three prerendered static before any removal, so both are proven-to-compile], `rm -rf`'d after losing to `618-solar-organ`; resurrect by re-spawning the brief, renumber on revival — max committed folder now 618)

- **`619-combustion`** ("Combustion", status: `queued` · honest 2–3/5 · **the strongest resurrect for the NEXT edges/abrasive adult fire — research-bound AND now built twice [§427 as 614, §429 as 619], a near-instant ship**) — **"What if an instrument were a MACHINE — a procedural combustion engine you rev off the glass?"** A DSP realization of the **Pulse-Train-Resonator (PTR)** model (implements **arXiv 2603.09391**, *Physics-Informed Neural Engine Sound Modeling with Differentiable Pulse-Train Synthesis*, March 2026): inertial RPM (fast rise under load, slow drag-down = mass) → a 4-stroke firing pulse train at `cylinders/2 × RPM/60` Hz with per-cylinder amplitude jitter + a slow idle-hunt wobble (lumpy, alive idle) → each firing injects a ~4 ms combustion grain (resonant noise burst + ignition click) into a bank of **five recursive Karplus-Strong / comb feedback delay lines** (~9–31 ms, one-pole damping in-loop, feedback rising with RPM) modeling exhaust-pipe resonance → a throttle/RPM-tracking resonant SVF + `tanh` overdrive = the redline scream → snap-shut-at-speed = an **overrun backfire** (extra over-amp ignitions + noise pop). Runs sample-accurate in a **self-contained AudioWorklet** (processor source as a plain-JS string, Blob-URL loaded inside the Start gesture — no asset/CDN/npm; ScriptProcessor fallback). INPUT all off the glass — keyboard (W/↑ throttle, S/↓ brake, Space rev-blip, 4/6/8 cylinders, `[`/`]` redline) + device-tilt (iOS `requestPermission` in gesture) + an on-screen ≥44px pedal · OUTPUT **WebGPU/WGSL** tachometer/combustion field (firing flashes at the live rate, heat-haze ↑ with RPM, redline white-out, backfire strobes; gunmetal/sodium-orange/hot-white; Canvas2D tachometer fallback + backend badge) · TECH pulse-train + recursive-KS exhaust physical-model engine synthesis · VIBE mechanical/abrasive/loud. The lab is **0× on engine/pulse-train physical-modeling + 0× on a procedural-machine instrument**. Ambition: #1 (first engine/machine instrument) + #3 (PTR paper) + the research→build chain. Clean on every JURY 2026-06-14 ban (off Canvas2D, off touch, off warm-JI, off cozy). **LOST to 618 (lane-freshness + alive-with-the-world, not quality):** edgy + the cleanest research chain, BUT the abrasive lane was just visited by `601-feedback-howl`, and 618 is alive with REAL live planetary data at a silent glance where the engine needs *playing* (the 2.5s auto-demo carries it, but it's a played instrument). **The fastest single resurrect for the next edges fire** — built files (removed): `page.tsx` (554), `engine-worklet.ts` (685, PTR DSP + Blob worklet + ScriptProcessor fallback), `field.ts` (356, WGSL + Canvas2D), `README.md` (96).
- **`620-empty-room`** ("empty room", status: `queued` · honest 2/5 · **the best build-complete candidate for the perennially-0× OFF-SCREEN / audio-first lane — resurrect to finally give that lane its SECOND entry beyond 576, paired with real-headphone tuning**) — **"What if you sat in the dark with headphones and were NOT alone — inharmonic presences move around you in real 3D sound, and they react to where you turn?"** The lab's swing at the off-screen/audio-FIRST lane the jury names every fortnight (still 0× after 600+ builds): the experience IS the spatialized sound; the screen is deliberately near-black (a faint Canvas2D radar ring is a glance-aid only). 4–6 procedural **presences** on a circle around the listener, each a `PannerNode` with `panningModel:'HRTF'` (AudioListener at origin); **DeviceOrientation** compass yaw rotates you *inside* the field (iOS `requestPermission` in the Start gesture). Mechanic: turn TOWARD a presence and it **hushes and drifts back** (hides when faced); turn AWAY and the ones behind you **creep closer + louder, footsteps quickening** — the room never resolves. Each voice = 4 detuned sines at non-just ratios `[1, 1.41, 1.93, 2.71]` (≈√2, ~2, e — unsettling, NOT a warm pad) + a filtered-noise granular footstep that fires more when behind you. Master `gain → DynamicsCompressor → destination`; "🎧 best with headphones, eyes closed" prompt; full fallbacks (drag-to-turn + ◄ ► arrow keys/buttons + `text-rose-300` notice; ~2.5s idle auto-rotates the field). INPUT device-orientation/head-turn (off the glass) · OUTPUT audio-FIRST (HRTF binaural) + austere radar · TECH HRTF binaural moving-source spatialization · VIBE dark/present/unsettling. Refs: **Janet Cardiff & George Bures Miller** (*The Forty Part Motet* / audio walks); **Pauline Oliveros** (*Deep Listening*); **Sonic4D** (arXiv 2506.15759). **Honest: NOT lab-first binaural** — `576-presence-room` did head-tracked binaural; the fresh axes are the **off-screen / audio-first register** + the **unsettling evasion-and-approach** mechanic. Ambition: #2 (≥3 subsystems: orientation listener + HRTF moving-source spatializer + procedural inharmonic+footstep synth + radar visual) + #3 (named refs). **LOST to 618 (glance-legibility + verifiability, not concept):** the boldest diversity swing (finally attacks the 0× off-screen lane), BUT its payoff lives entirely in headphones + eyes-closed head-turning — the MOST unverifiable-in-sandbox surface and the weakest at a silent near-black 06:30 glance — and it overlaps 576's head-tracked binaural. **Resurrect** the moment the lab commits to an off-screen fire with real-headphone verification — the HRTF + orientation + fallbacks are all written. Built files (removed): `page.tsx` (723), `README.md` (104).

## §428 banked seeds (cycle 428, KIDS WIDE — three orthogonal **non-fingertip / off-Canvas2D / funny** explorers, each clearing the JURY 2026-06-14 bans [Canvas2D OUTPUT 6×, touch INPUT 4×, warm-JI bed ~7×, cozy VIBE ~9×]; both losers build-complete [clean `tsc --noEmit` + `eslint`/`next lint` in isolation; all 3 builder agents COMPLETED before removal — completion notifications confirmed, no re-materialization race; AND a full `npm run build` with all 3 present passed exit 0 before any removal, so both are proven-to-compile], `rm -rf`'d after losing to `617-kids-shadow-monster`; resurrect by re-spawning the brief, renumber on revival — max committed folder now 617)

- **`615-kids-face-monster`** ("Silly Face Monster", status: `queued` · ~2/5 · **resurrect for a face/expression kids fire, or fold its blendshape→synth mapping into a face-puppet suite** — the most legible cause→effect of the trio) — **"What if a 4-year-old makes SILLY FACES at the camera and a giant googly cartoon MONSTER mirrors + exaggerates them, and makes funny noises?"** Camera via **MediaPipe FaceLandmarker** with **blendshapes** (the 52-coefficient *Blendshapes GHUM* model), loaded dynamically from the jsDelivr CDN at runtime (ESM `vision_bundle.mjs` + `.wasm` fileset + `face_landmarker.task` — **no npm dep**, one `try/catch`). Mapping: `jawOpen` → monster mouth stretches open (rubber squash-stretch) + a ROAR/HONK (wider = louder + lower + more vowel-y); `browInnerUp`+`browOuterUp` → googly eyes POP + BOING; `mouthSmile` → sparkles + GIGGLE + rosy cheeks; `mouthPucker`/`mouthFunnel` → kazoo TOOT. Per-control **EMA smoothing** so the creature moves like rubber; a dropped/empty frame is swallowed by holding last values. Rendered **WebGL2** — a single full-screen SDF fragment shader draws the whole creature procedurally from a few uniforms (Canvas2D fallback draws the same creature). Robust no-camera path: any failure → `text-rose-300` notice + three ≥88px ROAR/BOING/GIGGLE buttons + A/S/D(/F) keys + ~2.5s idle ghost-face auto-demo (open→roar, brows→boing, smile→giggle). Kid-safe master `gain ≤0.55 → lowpass 7.2k → comp → out`, always-on bed, pre-built nodes (<50ms), no fail states, friendly-not-scary (round googly eyes, rounded teeth, rosy cheeks). Tags: **camera/face-blendshape INPUT** (non-fingertip) · **WebGL2 OUTPUT** (off Canvas2D) · face-expression→synth-param mapping (comedic face-puppet) TECH · silly-monster-mirror VIBE. Refs: Tex Avery / Looney Tunes squash-and-stretch; Snapchat / Toca Boca face filters; MediaPipe **FaceLandmarker "Blendshapes GHUM"** (arXiv 2309.05782). **Ambition 2/5 (#2 ≥3 subsystems [camera + FaceLandmarker + WebGL2 SDF + synth = 4] + #3 named refs; honest: NOT first-MediaPipe-face — 258/423/576 exist — first COMEDIC face-puppet).** **LOST to 617 (renderer-lane + concept-size + diversity, NOT quality):** the most legible cause→effect (make a face, monster copies) and the cleanest fallback, BUT it renders **WebGL2 not the jury-demanded full WebGPU spectacle** (617/616 deliver WGSL), face-puppet/mirror overlaps the lab's existing **face cluster** (258-mirror-pets/423-face-beat/576-presence-room), and "become a giant body-monster" is a bigger swing than "mirror my face." - **`616-kids-tilt-zoo`** ("Tilt Zoo", status: `queued` · ~2/5 · **the camera-FREE WebGPU-spectacle safety net — resurrect if 617's camera path proves flaky on review, or for a phone-tilt / physics-toy kids fire**; love-aligned to `98-kids-drum-circle`❤️/`169-kids-marble-run`❤️ playful-percussive cluster) — **"What if a 4-year-old TILTS the tablet like a tray and a crowd of glowing googly creatures rolls, tumbles, and BONKS around — every bonk a cartoon honk/boing — and the harder you tilt, the sillier the chaos?"** **DeviceOrientation** tilt (beta/gamma → gravity vector; iOS `requestPermission()` in the Start gesture) drives a hand-rolled 2D rigid-body pile (`physics.ts`: gravity, wall restitution, O(n²) circle-circle separation + impulse, squash-on-impact, rolling spin, wobbling pupils that lean with the tray; ~24–40 creatures, capped/recycled). Each creature-creature/creature-wall bonk fires ONE of five randomized cartoon-foley voices (boing/sproing/slide-whistle/wood-tok/kazoo-honk; size→pitch, velocity→loudness; deliberately NOT a warm chord) + a low doink on hard tilts; voice budget 24, steal-oldest. Rendered **WebGPU/WGSL** instanced (one quad per creature, per-instance storage buffer each frame, fragment shader draws each googly creature procedurally) with a **full Canvas2D fallback** (`render2d.ts`, identical world) + live backend badge. Robust camera-free fallback: DeviceOrientation denied/absent → on-screen ≥72px tilt-pad + arrow keys + mouse-drag + `text-rose-300` notice; WebGPU absent → Canvas2D; ~2.5s idle → slow lissajous auto-tilt so creatures keep tumbling + honking. Kid-safe master `gain 0.55 → lowpass 7.2k → comp(-18/12/6) → out`, always-on bed, ≥64px controls, no fail states. Tags: **device-tilt INPUT** (camera-FREE, non-fingertip) · **WebGPU/WGSL OUTPUT** (off Canvas2D — the jury's demanded spectacle) · rigid-body-physics-pile + cartoon-foley TECH · chaotic-googly-slapstick VIBE. Refs: Carl Stalling / Raymond Scott; Treg Brown (Looney Tunes foley); one-man-band. **Ambition 2/5 (#2 ≥3 subsystems [device-orientation + physics + WGSL instanced renderer + foley = 4] + #3 named refs).** **LOST to 617 (concept-size, NOT quality or reliability — arguably the most RELIABLE of the trio):** the cleanest 06:30-glance story (camera-free, no permission, no CDN model) and a true WebGPU spectacle, BUT a tilt-tray physics toy is a *smaller* concept than "your whole body becomes a giant monster," and 617 also answers the jury's embodied-spatial singleton ask (#5) that a physics tray doesn't. **The fastest resurrect if 617's camera/segmentation proves flaky on Karel's review** — it's the same WebGPU-spectacle lane, minus the camera risk. Built files (removed): `physics.ts` (235), `audio.ts` (407), `rendergpu.ts` (316, WGSL instanced), `render2d.ts` (130), `page.tsx` (485), `README.md` (81).

## §427 banked seeds (cycle 427, ADULT WIDE — three orthogonal **edged / off-Canvas2D / non-fingertip** explorers, each clearing the JURY 2026-06-14 bans [Canvas2D OUTPUT 6×, touch INPUT 4×, warm-JI bed ~7×, cozy VIBE ~9×, third ocean]; both losers build-complete [clean `tsc --noEmit` + eslint in isolation; NOTE a sandbox FS race — 3 concurrent builder `npm run build`s — wiped + the 614 agent recreated its folder, so both were `rm -rf`'d AGAIN post-completion before commit, `git status` verified only the winner staged], `rm -rf`'d after losing to `613-seismic-choir`; resurrect by re-spawning the brief, renumber on revival — max committed folder now 613)

- **`612-piano-larynx`** ("Piano Larynx", status: `queued` · ~3/5 · **resurrect for an adult his-material fire, but PAIR it with a real-piano-loading path** — its weakness was a synthesized default modulator; wire it to the existing public `/api/audio/[id]` like `583`/`606` do, or lead with the file-drop, so the talking effect runs on Karel's ACTUAL recorded tone) — **"What if Karel's piano could SPEAK?"** Cross-synthesis instrument: **LPC analysis** (Hann frames 1024/50% hop, pre-emphasis 0.97, autocorrelation → **Levinson-Durbin** → order ~6–28 all-pole filter + residual gain per frame) tears the piano's time-varying formant/spectral envelope out; a buzzy band-limited sawtooth **carrier** (glottal source, morphable toward white noise = whisper) is run through the **time-varying all-pole IIR** (per-sample coefficient smoothing → click-free; DC-block + `tanh` soft-clip → stable), offline-rendered + looped. The point you HEAR: move the carrier pitch and the formants HOLD — the piano's "vowels" stay while the pitch glides under them (source/filter decoupling = the talking effect). The lab's **first LPC / cross-synthesis** (grep 0×). INPUT audio-file (his piano, via file-drop or `/api/audio`) · OUTPUT **WebGL2** "spectral throat" (the all-pole frequency response as a writhing formant ridge + live-spectrum ghost + carrier-pitch bar; cold X-ray cyan/bone/violet — uncanny, not cozy) · TECH LPC source-filter cross-synthesis · VIBE uncanny/vocal/unsettling. Refs: **Paul Lansky — *Six Fantasies on a Poem by Thomas Campion* (1979); Charles Dodge — *Speech Songs* (1972)**. Ambition: #1 first-LPC + #3 named-refs + #4 his-material (3/5). Clean on every JURY 2026-06-14 ban (off Canvas2D, off touch, off warm-JI, off cozy). LOST to 613 because `606` (cycle 425) ALREADY mined the his-material analysis vein (HPSS), so a fourth-in-window his-material piece risks the new attractor — and its glance-state was a synthesized piano, not his. Files were complete (page.tsx + lpc.ts + audio.ts + gl.ts + README) — a faithful re-spawn + the real-audio wire is a fast cycle-2.

- **`614-combustion`** ("Combustion", status: `queued` · ~2–3/5 · **resurrect for an edges/abrasive adult fire or a "machine instruments" suite** — it's research-bound and build-complete, an easy ship) — **"What if an instrument were a MACHINE — a procedural combustion engine you rev off the glass?"** The DSP realization of the **Pulse-Train-Resonator (PTR)** model: RPM → firing rate (4-stroke `N/2 × RPM/60` Hz) drives a **pulse train** (per-cylinder amplitude jitter + idle hunt → a *lumpy* idle), each firing injects a ~4 ms combustion grain, propagated through a bank of **recursive Karplus-Strong / comb feedback delay lines** (~9–31 ms, in-loop one-pole damping) modeling exhaust-pipe acoustics; a throttle-opening resonant SVF + `tanh` overdrive = the redline scream; **inertial RPM** (fast rise under load, slow drag down) = mass; lift off at speed → overrun **backfire** (extra over-amplitude ignitions + noise burst). Runs sample-accurate in an **AudioWorklet** (processor kept as a plain-JS string in `engine.ts`, **Blob-loaded** at runtime → self-contained, no public asset/CDN/npm). INPUT keyboard (W/↑ throttle, S/↓ brake, space rev-blip, 4/6/8 cylinders, `[ ]`/`- =` redline) + device-tilt (iOS `requestPermission` in the gesture) + on-screen 44px pedal — all OFF the glass · OUTPUT **WebGPU/WGSL** tachometer/combustion field (firing flashes at the live firing rate, heat-haze ↑ with RPM, redline white-out, backfire strobes; gunmetal/sodium-orange/hot-white; Canvas2D tachometer fallback) · TECH pulse-train + recursive-KS exhaust physical-model engine synthesis · VIBE mechanical/abrasive/loud. **Implements this cycle's research dive: arXiv 2603.09391, "Physics-Informed Neural Engine Sound Modeling with Differentiable Pulse-Train Synthesis" (March 2026).** The lab's **first pulse-train/engine physical-model + first procedural-machine instrument** (grep 0×). Ambition: #1 first-engine-model + #3 named-ref (the PTR paper) + the research→build chain (2–3/5). Clean on every JURY 2026-06-14 ban. LOST to 613 only because the abrasive lane was JUST visited by `601-feedback-howl` (two adult cycles ago) and 613 hit three jury provocations at once — the engine is the strongest single resurrect for the NEXT edges fire.

## §425 banked seeds (cycle 425, ADULT DEEP — ONE concept ["take Karel's OWN recorded piano APART into hidden layers you can solo, mute & remix — paint with the components of his timbre"], attacked via three decomposition TECHNIQUES; the lab's FIRST source-separation [grep-verified: NMF/HPSS/SMS/source-separation all 0× across 600+ prototypes]; the directest hit on JURY 2026-06-14 provocation #4 ["mine the vein that produced both 4/5s — a fresh analysis/resynthesis technique on Karel's own material; do it again — neural source separation, NMF stem-splitting, DDSP"]. Both losers build-complete [clean tsc+eslint in isolation, builders reported green], `rm -rf`'d after losing to `606-piano-vivisection`; resurrect by re-spawning the brief, renumber on revival — max committed folder now 606. **These two are the strongest cycle-2 deepening path for the decomposition spine.** — **UPDATE (cycle 434): `607-piano-prism` CONSUMED — resurrected as the DEEP fire that shipped `630-piano-refract` (the warm-started register-NMF realization of this exact seed, the first honest cycle-2). `608-piano-sieve` (SMS sinusoidal+residual) still banked for a dedicated spectral-freeze / phase-vocoder fire.**)

- **`607-piano-prism`** ("Piano Prism", status: `queued` · honest 3/5 [#1 first NMF in lab + #2 ≥5 subsystems + #3 Lee&Seung/Smaragdis] · resurrect as the **decomposition cycle-2** — the most technically advanced of the trio) — **"What if Karel's white piano sound could be refracted, like light through a prism, into six hidden spectral 'voices' he can solo, mute, and re-mix?"** STFT magnitude S (Hann 1024/hop 256, ~9s @22kHz mono) → **NMF** factorization S≈W·H (W = 6 F×K spectral basis components, H = K×T activations) via KL-divergence multiplicative updates (~64 iters, chunked w/ progress bar); per-component soft Wiener mask Mk=Vk/ΣVj applied to the original complex STFT (magnitude scaled, **phase kept**) → ISTFT overlap-add → six independently-mixable looping AudioBuffers (sum behind per-component gains → solo/mute is instant). WebGL2 prism: each component = a colored spectral band (W column) + scrolling activation lane (H row), soloed glow / muted dim, R32F texture. Off-glass: keyboard 1–6 solo / 0 reset / ←→ select / ↑↓ gain / space; device-tilt "spectral spotlight" (iOS-gated); secondary on-screen faders. ~2.5s idle auto-demo cycles the solo. Tags: **audio-file (his piano) + keyboard/tilt INPUT** · **WebGL2 OUTPUT** · **NMF spectral factorization TECH** · **prism/dissective VIBE**. Refs: Lee & Seung (Nature 1999); Smaragdis & Brown (ISMIR 2003); arXiv 2603.04032 (2026). **LOST to 606 (legibility + reliability, not ambition):** arguably the *most ambitious/beautiful* (the prism metaphor, 6 learned components), BUT NMF components are **unsupervised spectral clusters, not nameable** (the builder flagged this) so a 06:30 glance shows abstract bands, and separation quality depends on random-init convergence — where 606's "strings vs hammers" is instantly graspable and near-always clean. **Resurrect** as the decomposition spine's cycle-2, or **fuse**: HPSS pre-split (606) → NMF the harmonic layer into pitched components. Built files (removed): `page.tsx` (633), `gl.ts` (265), `nmf.ts` (202), `audio.ts` (362), `README.md`.
- **`608-piano-sieve`** ("Piano Sieve", status: `queued` · honest 3/5 [#1 first SMS in lab + #2 ≥5 subsystems + #3 Serra&Smith] · resurrect for a **spectral-freeze / phase-vocoder** fire) — **"What if you could sift Karel's piano into its pure singing TONE (deterministic partials) vs. the NOISE around it (hammer thunk, breath, room air) — and freeze & stretch each?"** STFT → per-frame **peak-pick** above a smoothed local noise floor (relative-prominence test + main-lobe spread) → soft tonal mask in [0,1], tonalMask+residualMask=1; both applied to the original complex STFT (phase kept) → ISTFT → tonalBuf + residualBuf, equal-power **sift** morph tone↔residual, plus **freeze** (held grain) and **stretch** (playbackRate). WebGL2 "sieve": gold/white sharp partial LINES foreground vs a grey-blue grainy NOISE CLOUD behind, sift visibly separating them, freeze halts the playhead. Off-glass: keyboard 1/2 solo, ←→ sift, f freeze, ↑↓ stretch, space; device-tilt sift (iOS-gated); secondary buttons. ~2.5s idle auto-demo sweeps the sift. Tags: **audio-file + keyboard/tilt INPUT** · **WebGL2 OUTPUT** · **sinusoidal+residual (SMS) TECH** · **forensic-sieve VIBE**. Refs: Serra & Smith *Spectral Modeling Synthesis* (1990); arXiv 2603.04032 (2026). **LOST to 606 (robustness, not concept):** tonal-vs-noise is intuitive and freeze/stretch are a lovely playground, BUT peak-picking is per-frame (not true partial-tracking), and freeze/stretch are explicitly approximate (the builder flagged the `playbackRate` stretch slightly pitch-shifts) — riskier first-glance behavior than 606's bulletproof median-filter split. **Resurrect** for a dedicated spectral-freeze / phase-vocoder time-stretch fire where freeze/stretch are the headline (do it with a real phase-vocoder). Built files (removed): `page.tsx` (778), `gl.ts` (320), `audio.ts` (416), `README.md`.

Motion drives gated white/pink **harsh-noise** (loudness∝energy) + a grinding resonant drone (cutoff tracks motion) + an **inharmonic-metal additive bank** at deliberately non-JI ratios `1, 1.41, 1.93, 2.71, 3.66, 4.99, 6.41×` (struck metal sheet / brake drum) struck by hot zones, + a loud metallic crash on transients → brick-wall limiter. Tags: **camera optical-flow INPUT** · **WebGPU OUTPUT** (Canvas2D fallback) · frame-diff→harsh-noise + inharmonic-metal TECH · violent/industrial VIBE. ## §422 banked seeds (cycle 422, KIDS DEEP — one instrument "Singing Water" attacked by **three synthesis cores**; both losers build-complete [clean tsc+eslint in isolation], `rm -rf`'d after losing to `597-kids-water-bowls`; resurrect by re-spawning the brief, renumber on revival — max committed folder now 597)

- **`598-kids-water-bowls`** ("Singing Water — modal", status: `queued` · ~2/5 · resurrect for a synthesis-forward kids fire that wants the *truest glass timbre*) — same instrument as the shipped `597`, but the rub/tap drive a **modal additive bank**: each bowl is 5 always-running sine "modes" at inharmonic ratios `1, 2.01, 3.0, 4.2, 5.4×`, each gated by its own gain. **Tap** = one impulse into all modes (per-mode exponential decay 2.4s→0.5s, higher modes shorter) = a glassy struck-bell ring. **Rub** = a continuous **stick-slip friction source** (looping noise + a soft saw, band-passed near pitch, `frictionGain` tracks smoothed drag speed) re-energizing the bank, PLUS direct lift of the upper mode gains with speed → a sustained song that gets richer/brighter as you rub, ~0.5s release. Kid-safe chain (lowpass 7500 + comp −18/6/12), JI Lydian-ish tuning, multi-touch, idle auto-demo. **LOST to 597:** arguably the *most authentic glass timbre* (a real inharmonic mode bank vs. 597's bandpass-noise), but the **doubled excitation path** (friction source AND direct mode-gain lift) is muddier to tune and less faithful to the §420 seed's literal "high-Q-bandpass-noise" friction-resonator spec; 597 is also the more robust, cleaner-engineered ship. **Resurrect** as a "true modal physical model" deepening (could fold real mode coupling / sympathetic resonance between bowls). Tags: touch tap+rub INPUT · Canvas2D OUTPUT · **modal additive synthesis TECH** · serene-watery VIBE. Refs: Jal Tarang; Franklin glass armonica (1761); modal synthesis. Built files (removed): `page.tsx` (526), `audio.ts` (239), `README.md`.

- **`599-kids-water-bowls`** ("Singing Water — bowed", status: `queued` · ~2/5 · resurrect for an **AudioWorklet** physical-modelling kids/adult fire) — same instrument, but the resonator is a real **bowed-string / stick-slip waveguide** in an inline `AudioWorkletProcessor` (registered via Blob URL inside the unlock gesture): a tuned delay-line + nonlinear friction curve driven by a `bowVelocity`/`bowForce` param, with a hard ±0.85 clamp + DC blocker so the feedback never runs away. Rub speed → bow velocity (sing sustains in the stick-slip sweet spot, louder/brighter with speed); tap = a raised-cosine strike of the same waveguide. Has a robust **DelayNode-comb fallback** wired to the identical API for browsers without AudioWorklet. **LOST to 597:** the **most physically authentic** stick-slip of the three, but an inline-Blob worklet is the **least robust** path for an unverifiable-in-sandbox kids ship that must "just work" at a silent 06:30 glance (addModule timing, prod Blob handling), and the §420 seed named the simpler bandpass primitive. **Resurrect** as the lab's first real **AudioWorklet physical model** (the worklet code is already written + clamped — fast to revive once we can browser-verify it). Tags: touch tap+rub INPUT · Canvas2D OUTPUT · **bowed-waveguide AudioWorklet TECH** (lab-first worklet) · serene-watery VIBE. Refs: Jal Tarang; Franklin glass armonica (1761); Helmholtz/bowed-string friction. Built files (removed): `page.tsx` (448), `audio-engine.ts` (386), `worklet-source.ts` (141), `visuals.ts` (240), `README.md`.

## §420 banked seeds (cycle 420, KIDS WIDE — three orthogonal explorers off grep-verified **un-simulated physical instruments** in the 588-deep lab [plinko/Galton, jal-tarang/glass-armonica, sundial/shadow-sequencer — all 0× before this cycle]; both losers build-complete [clean tsc+eslint in isolation], `rm -rf`'d after losing to `593-kids-sun-shadows`; resurrect by re-spawning the brief, renumber on revival — max committed folder now 593)

- **`592-kids-water-bowls`** ("Singing Water", status: ✅ **SHIPPED §422 as `597-kids-water-bowls`** — the friction-excited resonator was rebuilt + shipped as the winner of a kids DEEP fire; see INDEX/RESEARCH §422) — **"What if a 4-year-old could play a row of glowing water bowls — tap to ring them, and RUB a finger round the rim to make them SING?"** Six glowing bowls, water-level→pitch (higher water = lower pitch, real physics, teaches itself). **Tap = struck inharmonic bell** (1, 2.01, 3.0, 4.2× partials). **Rub = a sustained glass-armonica friction tone** — the heart of the piece and the genuinely new browser synthesis: a **friction-excited resonator** = high-Q bandpass (Q≈14–28) filtered noise at the bowl pitch + pure shimmer partials, where **drag SPEED drives amplitude + brightness + Q** in real time (rub faster → louder/brighter, stop → it decays). Tap and rub are two excitations of ONE resonator. Tuning: JI Lydian-ish `1/1, 9/8, 5/4, 11/8, 3/2, 5/3` over ~F3 (the 11/8 #4 = watery floating color, NOT pentatonic). Canvas2D ripples + standing-wave caustics, ambient water bed, idle auto-rub demo, kids-safe limiter, multi-touch. Tags: touch tap+rub INPUT · Canvas2D OUTPUT · **friction-resonator synthesis TECH** (lab-first) · serene-watery VIBE. Refs: Indian **Jal Tarang**; Benjamin Franklin's **glass armonica** (1761). **LOST to 593 (warmth/glance, not quality):** the most *technically novel* (a new synthesis primitive vs. 593's new structure), but 593's golden-hour moving-sun is warmer + more arresting at a silent 06:30 glance, and tap-vs-rub disambiguation is a hair more demanding for a 4yo than 593's single drag. **Resurrect** as the lead of a synthesis-forward kids fire (fold in pour-to-retune + sympathetic resonance). Built files (removed): `page.tsx` (~14k), `synth.ts`, `README.md`.

- **`591-kids-plinko-bells`** ("Bell Drop", status: `queued` · ~3/5 · resurrect for an emergent/autonomous-music or STEM kids fire — love-aligned to Karel's `236-particle-life-song`❤️/`130-tsl-particle-compute`❤️/`262-aurora-particle`❤️ cluster) — **"What if dropping a ball and watching it bounce taught a 4-year-old probability — and built a warm chord while it does?"** A sonified **Galton/quincunx board (1894)**: tap the top (or an 88px Drop button) → a luminous ball falls, left/right "coin-flip" off each peg row (each bounce a quiet panned wooden tick), landing in one of **9 just-intonation bins**; balls **accumulate into a visible histogram** and the bell curve *blooms as a warm major chord* — the bins are arranged so consonance mirrors outward from the peak, the most-hit center bins on root/4th/5th, so "the middle is home." Emergent, autonomous, self-playing (off-finger reward), with a dashed normal-curve guide so the math is legible. Canvas2D, ambient root+fifth pad, idle auto-drop demo, optional tilt-to-bias, kids-safe limiter. Tags: touch-drop (+tilt) INPUT · Canvas2D OUTPUT · **Galton/binomial-physics → histogram-as-chord TECH** (lab-first) · playful-emergent-STEM VIBE. Ref: Francis Galton, the **quincunx** / bean machine, *Natural Inheritance* (1894). **LOST to 593:** the richest *concept* and most autonomous (Karel loves emergent music), but cooler/cerebral and less immediately gorgeous at a glance than 593's golden-hour sky. **Resurrect** for an emergent/generative or STEM-leaning kids fire. Built files (removed): `page.tsx` (473), `audio.ts`, `physics.ts`, `tuning.ts`, `README.md`.

## §418 banked seeds (cycle 418, KIDS DEEP — one concept, *the lab's first GAME-CONTROLLER instrument for a 4-year-old, off the glass*, via three interaction approaches on the Gamepad API + dual-rumble haptics; both losers build-complete [clean ESLint + tsc in isolation], `rm -rf`'d after losing to `587-kids-button-garden`; resurrect by re-spawning the brief, renumber on revival — max committed folder now 587)

- **`586-kids-light-painter`** (status: `queued` · ~3/5 · resurrect as an EXPRESSIVE / older-kid or adult controller piece, or as 587's "advanced mode") — **"What if you PAINTED warm light and bent a chord with two thumbsticks?"** Left + right thumbsticks each pull a continuous additive Canvas2D light-ribbon; stick *magnitude* continuously **bends** a sustained just-intonation A-major-add9 over an A2 root+fifth drone via `setTargetAtTime` portamento (no discrete taps), lowpass opening with deflection; face buttons bloom bell-bursts, triggers swell the drone, dual-rumble pulses with chord motion. Keyboard (WASD/arrows + Z/X/C) + pointer-drag fallbacks + idle Lissajous auto-demo. Tags: gamepad INPUT · Canvas2D OUTPUT · continuous-portamento-chord-bend TECH · warm-expressive VIBE. Refs: Toshio Iwai *Electroplankton*/*TENORI-ON*; May 2026 Steam Controller haptic-song demos. **LOST to 587 (legibility, not quality):** the most *musically expressive* of the three (continuous bend = real warmth), but **two-stick coordination + "push harder = higher" is the least graspable for a 4-year-old** — it reads as a slightly-adult instrument. **Resurrect** for an adult/older-kid expressive-controller fire, or fold its continuous chord-bend into 587 as a held-trigger "expressive" layer. Built files (removed): `page.tsx` (701), `README.md`.

- **`588-kids-glow-drive`** (status: `queued` · ~3/5 · resurrect for a calm SPATIAL / exploratory kids fire, or as 587's one-stick younger sibling) — **"What if you steered a glowing comet through a dark sky and woke sleeping stars that sing as you pass?"** Left stick steers a comet through a fixed autonomous field of light-seeds; the nearest seed wakes/ripples/sings when you pass, pitched by its *place* in the field (a just-intonation spatial scale, low-left→high-right) over a root+fifth drone; right stick blooms a halo, triggers leave a trail + swell, dual-rumble buzzes on each wake. Keyboard/WASD + pointer-drag-to-steer + idle wandering auto-demo. Tags: gamepad INPUT · Canvas2D OUTPUT · spatial-scale-field TECH · calm-exploratory VIBE. Refs: same (Iwai; Steam Controller). **LOST to 587:** lovely and the cleanest answer to the frozen-creature ban (a *field*, not a pet), and one-stick steering is more accessible than 586's two — but the reward is more **ambient/passive** than 587's instant mash→bloom cause→effect, and "steer precisely enough to pass near a seed" needs more motor control than a 4-year-old's button-mash. **Resurrect** for a spatial/ambient kids fire, or as the younger-sibling one-stick mode of a controller suite. Built files (removed): `page.tsx` (646), `README.md`.

## §415 banked seeds (cycle 415, ADULT WIDE — 3 orthogonal explorers all chasing the jury's WARMTH dare; both losers build-complete in isolation, `rm -rf`'d after losing to `580-tide-breath`; resurrect by re-spawning the brief, renumber on revival — max committed folder now 580)

- **`581-still-bloom`** (status: `queued` · ~2–3/5 · resurrect to open the off-glass / audio-first / conceptual lane the jury keeps naming as 0×) — **"What if music rewarded you for holding completely still?"** An INVERSE gate: a warm ~5–7-voice just-intonation drone (low root, detuned sine/triangle pairs) slowly BLOOMS out of silence — voices entering one by one, master lowpass opening 400→3500 Hz — only while you hold the device truly still; any motion above a gentle threshold scatters it (voices duck, bloom recedes). **Off the glass:** input is `DeviceMotion` agitation (rolling variance of acceleration; iOS 13+ `requestPermission()` inside the start gesture), falling back to pointer-stillness, then a guided "breathe-with-me" auto-demo. Audio-FIRST: the visual is a minimal warm Canvas2D radial bloom (NOT three.js/WebGL) that grows with integrated stillness and disperses into grain on motion. Tags: device-motion-stillness INPUT · audio-first/Canvas2D OUTPUT · stillness-integration TECH · warm-meditative/conceptual VIBE. Refs: Pauline Oliveros *Deep Listening*, Éliane Radigue, John Cage *4'33"*, Christine Sun Kim. **LOST to 580:** lowest ambition of the trio, does NOT revive the jury-starved WebGPU, and its real payoff needs a held-still device — at a silent 06:30 glance Karel sees only the breathe-demo, not the felt reward (580 plays autonomously). The strongest pure *concept* and the cleanest answer to the off-glass / audio-only provocation — resurrect on a cycle where the data/WebGPU lanes are crowded and the lab needs to get off the screen. (No API route; pure client motion + Web Audio, nothing recorded/sent. Build-clean in isolation: `page.tsx`, `audio.ts`, `motion.ts`, `render.ts`, `README.md`.)

- **`582-piano-aurora`** (status: `queued` · ~2–3/5 · resurrect for a "use Karel's real music" fire / a warm WebGPU-painting fire) — **"What if the *harmony* of a warm piano painted living light?"** A warm piano source (a built-in soft-attack generative I–vi–IV–V pad in D with 7ths/9ths so it always plays, PLUS a drop-your-own-recording file input — the production path being the existing `/api/audio/[id]` route to Karel's Welcome Home tracks, documented not implemented) feeds an `AnalyserNode`; each frame folds the spectrum into a **12-bin chroma** (pitch-class energy, NOT onset/beat) + RMS. The dominant pitch-classes steer the HUE of a **WebGPU curl-noise flow-field aurora** (~40k particles via a WGSL compute pass; Bridson curl noise) confined to a warm amber→rose→violet arc (cyan/cold-green skipped), RMS drives brightness/flow; hot-downgrades to a ~4k-particle Canvas2D flow field if `navigator.gpu` fails. Tags: audio-file INPUT · WebGPU OUTPUT (Canvas2D fallback) · FFT-chroma flow-field TECH · warm-intimate VIBE. Refs: Refik Anadol (data-painting), Memo Akten (*Learning to See* / flow fields), Robert Bridson (curl noise). **LOST to 580:** it is fundamentally an audio-reactive *visualizer*, and the lab's flow-field/aurora-visualizer lane is crowded (`130-tsl-particle-compute`❤️, `262-aurora-particle`❤️, `441-latent-listening-room`) — close to the jury's standing "renderer flex in search of a concept" critique (its 541 call-out) — and it carries no real-world data (jury #2). The "harmony-not-rhythm" chroma steering is a genuinely nice twist and it honors the under-served "use his real music" directive — resurrect by binding it to his ACTUAL Welcome Home tracks via `/api/audio/[id]` (the differentiator that lifts it out of the visualizer pack). (No API route in the prototype itself; dropped audio decoded locally, never uploaded. Build-clean in isolation: `page.tsx`, `audio.ts`, `chroma.ts`, `field.ts`, `gpu.ts`, `README.md`.)

## §412 banked seeds (cycle 412, KIDS DEEP — one concept, *today's REAL local weather, played as a kids music window*, via three renderer/interaction approaches on live Open-Meteo; both losers build-complete [clean tsc+eslint], `rm -rf`'d after losing to `575-kids-sky-song`; resurrect by re-spawning the brief, renumber on revival — max committed folder now 575)

Cycle 412 fired a **kids DEEP** triple off ONE fresh concept — *open a window onto the child's actual current weather (live Open-Meteo, no key) and play it as music; the live weather composes the key/mood/motion, the child performs within it* — the directest answer to JURY 2026-06-13 #2 (real-world-data sonification, **0× the whole window**) + #4 (chase warmth, not a puzzle), and a grep-verified **kids-first real-data sonification** (the lab's prior weather toys `115`/`120`/`83` are all *synthetic*/"Zero API"; the only real-data pieces `355`/`337` are adult + out of frame). All three share: direct client-side fetch of `api.open-meteo.com` (public, no-key, CORS, **no api route, no guard, no secret**; CSP-verified — enforced `connect-src … https:` allows it), optional geolocation with a silent default-coordinate fallback (no kids permission gate), a baked demo-sky for offline/failure so the 06:30 glance always shows + plays, WMO `weather_code`+`cloud_cover`+`is_day`→consonant-scale/mood mapping, kids-safe `gain→lowpass(≤8k)→compressor→destination` limiter, alive-from-frame-one visuals. Winner shipped = `575-kids-sky-song` (the *generative-composition* take — the weather writes an evolving look-ahead song that plays itself; the biggest concept + best "reads as music at a silent glance" + most differentiated from the lab's existing synthetic-weather Canvas2D toys). Both losers below are complete, build-reviewed real implementations (own folder, pure client-side, no API route, brick-wall limiter, hands-free auto-demo + baked-sky fallback, graceful WebGL2→Canvas2D / geo-denied fallbacks); folders removed per the no-half-built-folders rule, banked here as ready-to-resurrect TEXT.

- **`573-kids-sky-window`** (`queued`, the most *immersive renderer* take — RESURRECT for a shader-forward weather/atmosphere fire, or as 575's richer sky layer) — a fullscreen **raw-WebGL2 hand-written-GLSL** living-sky window: fbm volumetric-ish clouds (density from `cloud_cover`), wind-driven drift (`wind_speed/direction`), sun/moon glow+halo (`is_day`+clear), procedural rain streaks / snowflakes (from `precipitation`), night stars, storm lightning, touch ripples/bloom, window vignette — alive from frame one, with a Canvas2D fallback sky that still shows weather + sings. Touch = play notes quantised to today's weather-set scale (7 moods: sunny major-pentatonic / starry-night octave-down / cloudy sus2 / fog low-drone / rain minor-pentatonic / snow high-bells / storm lydian); `temperature`→timbre+register, `wind`→drift+filter LFO, `precipitation`→falling-voice density. **LOST, not on quality:** the most beautiful sky of the three, but 575's generative engine is the bigger *concept* leap ("the weather *composes*" vs "the weather *tints* what you tap"), and an abstract shader sky reads less instantly than 575's self-blooming notes at a silent glance. **Its value:** the richest atmosphere renderer — resurrect for an atmosphere/aurora-forward fire, or fold its volumetric sky in as 575's background. Ambition 3/5 (#1 qualified kids-first real-data sonification + #2 ≥3 subsystems + #3 Polli/Miebach/WeatherChimes; #5 not claimed). Complete: `page.tsx`, `audio.ts` (`SkyAudio`), `weather.ts`, `render.ts`, `README.md`. Clean tsc+eslint, not browser-verified.

- **`574-kids-weather-today`** (`queued`, the warmest / most 4yo-legible take) — a cozy **Canvas2D cut-paper diorama**: paper hills, a smiling sun or moon, drifting clouds, falling rain/snow, and a friendly breathing paper critter — the most instantly-readable "today's weather" for a 4-year-old. Tap the sun/clouds/critter/hill (each a different position in today's key) → always-consonant music-box notes; scene + key/mode chosen by live `weather_code`+`cloud_cover`+`is_day` (sunny major meadow / overcast sus2 grey hills / rain minor-pentatonic chimes / snow glassy bells / fog hush / thunder weighty minor / starry night), `temperature`→color temp + timbre, `wind`→cloud drift, `precipitation`→particle count; always-on ambient chord bed; tap targets ≥80px. **LOST:** despite the real-data headline it is the closest in *form* to the lab's existing synthetic weather toys (`115-kids-weather-music` zone-blend, `120-kids-rain-drum` cloud-tap) — the "too similar in shape" risk — and the least ambitious renderer of the three (Canvas2D vs WebGL2 + a generative engine). **Its value:** the warmest, coziest, most-legible-to-a-4yo + the most iPad-bulletproof (Canvas2D) — resurrect when a warmth-forward / maximally-legible kids weather fire is due, or ship it as 575's more-literal sibling for the youngest users. Ambition 3/5 (#1 qualified kids-first real-data sonification + #2 ≥3 subsystems + #3 Polli/Miebach/WeatherChimes; #5 not claimed). Complete: `page.tsx`, `audio.ts`, `weather.ts`, `scene.ts`, `README.md`. Clean tsc+eslint, not browser-verified.

## §410 banked seeds (cycle 410, KIDS DEEP — one concept, *a creature genuinely different each day you return*, via what evolves; both losers build-complete, `rm -rf`'d after losing to `569-kids-ember-keeper`; resurrect by re-spawning the brief, renumber on revival — max committed folder now 569. NOTE: the §409 banked seeds named `569-conductor-batons`/`570-conductor-baton-motion` are stale number-labels — those slugs are now taken by this cycle's Ember pieces; renumber the conductor seeds on revival.)

Cycle 410 fired a **kids DEEP** triple off ONE massively-bigger concept — *a creature that lives in the device, remembers every visit, and is genuinely different each day you return; it never resets* — attacked via three things that can evolve across days (BODY / SONG / WORLD), all sharing the lab-first **cross-session `localStorage` persistence** + day-aware growth + analysis-only mic-feeding + kids-safe limiter + a fresh-device demo seed + a 🌙 next-day time-travel button (so the cross-day payoff reads in <30s at the 06:30 review). Winner shipped = `569-kids-ember-keeper` (the *body-morphology* take — the most literal answer to JURY #6 "a creature genuinely changed tomorrow", the most glance-legible [you SEE it grew], and the freshest vs the existing kids set). Both losers below are complete, build-reviewed real implementations (own folder, pure client-side, no API route, brick-wall limiter, hands-free seed/auto-demo, graceful mic-denied fallback, no-Canvas2D fallback); folders removed per the no-half-built-folders rule, banked here as ready-to-resurrect TEXT.

- **`570-kids-ember-song`** (`queued`, the biggest *concept* of the trio — RESURRECT for a music-forward / non-glance-only fire) — a child's creature **remembers every melody they ever hummed** and, each day they return, sings back a LONGER, RICHER song woven from everything taught — never the same twice, never reset. Hummed phrases (autocorrelation pitch → pentatonic snap → silence-segmented into a phrase of scale-degrees + coarse rhythm, `pitch.ts`) are **appended forever** to a `localStorage` library (`song-memory.ts`, key `resonance.ember-song.v1`); a tiny seeded **generative weaver** (`weave.ts`, Eno *Music for Airports* lineage) recombines the library each visit, sizing the piece to distinct-days returned (growth 1 = one looped phrase; 3+ = several phrases + an octave-down counter-voice; 4+ = a fuller pad bed). Each remembered phrase = one **luminous orbiting strand** in a raw-**WebGL2** creature (`render.ts`, `MAX_STRANDS` 24) that flares when its notes sound. Look-ahead scheduler (25 ms pump, `start(t)` 120 ms ahead) keeps the weave tight; kids-safe limiter; fresh-device seed = 4 phrases across 4 days. **LOST, not on quality:** the cross-day payoff is *audible* (a longer song), so at a SILENT 06:30 glance the abstract strands read less clearly than 569's visibly-bigger body, and the 🌙 button's reward is a thing you must hear. **Its value:** no lab piece grows the *music itself* across sessions — it's the strongest return-reward concept; ship it when a music-forward kids fire is due (or as 569's cycle-2 "it also sings your grown song"). Ambition 4/5 (#1 cross-session persistence [shared lab-first] + #2 ≥3 subsystems + #3 Eno/Tamagotchi/Oliveros + #4 Ember spine). Complete: `page.tsx`, `audio.ts`, `pitch.ts`, `render.ts`, `song-memory.ts`, `weave.ts`, `README.md`. Build-reviewed, not browser-verified.

- **`571-kids-ember-nest`** (`queued`, the warmest/most-legible take) — the creature lives in a little **NEST/WORLD that fills up across days**: every visit leaves a permanent **cut-paper flower / star / friend** (matte inline-**SVG** via `feTurbulence`+`feDropShadow`, rAF-via-refs — no per-frame React churn), and each object is also one **sustained C-pentatonic voice**, so a fuller world is literally a fuller, warmer (always-consonant) **additive chord** (per-voice gain `0.16/√n` keeps the sum kids-safe while the texture thickens — the Eno density move). Day-aware: first wake of a new calendar day auto-sprouts one new permanent object; humming/tapping plants smaller flowers. `localStorage` key `resonance.ember-nest.v1`; fresh-device seed = 9 objects across 4 days with `lastVisitDay` = yesterday; 🌙 next-day button thickens the chord on demand. **LOST:** the accumulating-world framing is thematically **closest to the existing `116-kids-bloom-garden`** (self-seeding singing garden) and a glowing-pond/firefly lineage — the "too similar" risk; and another SVG output nudges the rolling-10 SVG count toward the 4× ban (it sat at 3×). **Its value:** the warmest, most-immediately-legible-to-a-4yo of the three (a busy nest reads instantly), removes Tamagotchi's punishment/death loop (returning is *always* rewarded, never guilt — a deliberate kindness), and the additive-chord-from-world-density is lovely; resurrect when SVG is clear and pair it with a non-overlapping placement pass + a voice cap for months-old worlds. Ambition 4/5 (#1 cross-session persistence [shared lab-first] + #2 ≥3 subsystems + #3 Tamagotchi/Reggio Emilia "revisiting"/Eno + #4 Ember spine). Complete: `page.tsx`, `audio.ts`, `pitch.ts`, `scene.ts`, `shapes.ts`, `README.md`. Build-reviewed, not browser-verified.

## §408 banked seeds (cycle 408, KIDS WIDE — 3 orthogonal off-glass explorers; both losers build-complete, `rm -rf`'d after losing to `566-kids-shake-band`; resurrect by re-spawning the brief, renumber on revival — max folder now 566)

Cycle 408 fired a WIDE kids triple off three orthogonal **off-glass** inputs (mic-sing / device-shake / camera-color) × non-banned renderers (WebGL2 / three.js / WebGL2), all dodging the rolling-10 **SVG-output ban (4×)** and every standing jury ban. Winner shipped = `566-kids-shake-band` (device-motion → three.js batucada band — the freshest off-glass input vs recent kids cycles + the directest "break the calm-sensory autopilot" answer, and ideal for Karel's mobile review since he can literally shake his phone). Both losers below are complete, build-reviewed real implementations (own folder, pure client-side, no API route, brick-wall limiter, hands-free auto-demo, graceful sensor-denied fallback); folders removed per the no-half-built-folders rule, banked here as ready-to-resurrect TEXT.

- **`565-kids-sing-choir`** (`queued`, the biggest *concept* of the trio — RESURRECT when a non-mic kids cycle is due) — a 4-year-old sings/hums one steady note and **four glowing creature-friends instantly join in live harmony** (major 3rd / perfect 5th / octave), the whole choir gliding with the child's pitch in real time — the child *becomes* the lead of a four-voice chord. Implements **Jacob Collier's live vocal-harmonizer** honestly: detected pitch (YIN-style parabolic-interpolated autocorrelation, RMS-gated, `pitch.ts`) → C-pentatonic snap → 4 detuned-oscillator+vibrato voices tracking via `setTargetAtTime` 40ms glides (`audio.ts`), rendered as raw-WebGL2 additive particle creatures (`page.tsx`, ~746 lines, Canvas2D fallback). **LOST on the diversity gate, not quality:** its input is **mic**, exactly what the *immediately prior* kids cycle (406 clap-canon) used — repeating the previous kids cycle's primary modality is the "too similar" trap. Also the riskiest unverified surface (pitch detection on a breathy 4yo soprano in a noisy room; it flags wanting a 3–5-frame median smoother + higher RMS gate). Distinct from the adult `114-live-harmonize` (piano-roll viz) and from `280-echo-canyon`/`179-voice-monster` (those echo *sequentially*, this harmonizes *simultaneously*). **Its value:** no kids piece does live simultaneous vocal harmony — ship it the next time the diversity audit clears mic. Ambition 3/5 (#2 5 subsystems + #3 Collier; #1 not clean — live-harmonize exists). Complete: `page.tsx`, `audio.ts`, `pitch.ts`, `README.md`. Build-reviewed, not browser-verified.

- **`567-kids-color-garden`** (`queued`, the off-the-couch explorer) — point the tablet's **rear camera** at anything colorful in the real world and that color **grows a singing plant** in a glowing WebGL2 garden; warm reds = low notes, cool violets = high, every color welcome, no goal. Implements **Scriabin's *clavier à lumières*** hue→pitch map (red→C3 … violet→D5) honestly. Subsystems: `getUserMedia(environment)` → offscreen-2D central-patch `getImageData` → RGB→HSV with 20°/24-frame **hysteresis** (`vision.ts`), Scriabin hue→C-pentatonic map + brick-wall-limited synth + always-on C2+G2 pad (`audio.ts`), instanced additive WebGL2 plants growing root→bloom over ~1.5s with in-shader hsv2rgb (`render.ts`, ~392 lines), hue-cycling auto-demo for the camera-denied path. **LOST:** camera-color is thematically adjacent to the existing `368-kids-rainbow-quest` (foraging quest) and camera is the jury-flagged trodden input lane; it is also the most **lighting-fragile** for the 06:30 hands-free glance (low light / fluorescent flicker destabilize the patch average). **Its value:** the *open-creation* (vs quest) framing + the world-becomes-music exploration is genuinely lovely; resurrect when the camera lane is clear and pair with a more robust dominant-color extractor (k-means / saturation-weighted, not a flat average). Ambition 3/5 (#2 5+ subsystems + #3 Scriabin; #1 not clean — color-foraging exists at 368). Complete: `page.tsx`, `audio.ts`, `vision.ts`, `render.ts`, `README.md`. Build-reviewed, not browser-verified.

## §407 banked seeds (cycle 407, ADULT DEEP — splat spine cycle 2; both build-complete, `rm -rf`'d after losing to `564-piano-splat-cathedral`; resurrect by re-spawning the brief)

- **`563-piano-splat-ewa`** (`queued`, the reconstruction-grade renderer — RESURRECT for cycle 3) — the lab's most technically honest Gaussian-splat renderer: **full EWA covariance projection** (per-splat 3D covariance Σ = R·diag(s)·…·Rᵀ from a quaternion; analytic perspective Jacobian J; Σ' = TΣTᵀ; 2×2 conic inversion; eigenvalue-bounded quad) with **premultiplied alpha-over** + per-frame back-to-front depth sort, driven by Karel's real piano (audio.ts + synth fallback). It LOST to 564 only because the *experience* is another orbitable glowing cloud (adjacent to 557's galaxy), where 564 is a categorically new experience (a building his music raises). **Its value:** the true-EWA math is the cleanest #1-technique in the splat spine — the obvious **cycle-3 move is to swap 564's simplified two-axis anisotropy for 563's full covariance projection** → a reconstruction-grade cathedral. Complete files existed: `page.tsx` (idle demo + Begin), `splat-renderer.ts` (~28kB, all math hand-rolled: quatToMat3 / computeCovariance3D / projectCovariance / invertConic / gaussianRadius), `audio.ts`, `README.md`. Build-reviewed, not browser-verified (numerical fragility on degenerate/off-axis conics is the main risk; it added a 0.3px low-pass regularizer).

- **`562-piano-splat-aurora`** (`queued`, the ribbon/sheet renderer) — anisotropic splats stretched into flowing **aurora ribbons** (each splat carries a 3D principal axis → screen-space rotation θ + (σMajor,σMinor); bright/treble notes = thin sharp ribbons, dark/bass = fat puffs), depth-sorted alpha-over with a small additive emissive core for luminosity (Anadol teal/magenta/violet palette). LOST: also a "cloud you orbit," conceptually adjacent to 557. **Its value:** the *anisotropy-from-velocity-tangent* trick (splats elongate along the audio-driven flow) is a clean, cheap way to make sorted splats read as silky sheets — fold into any future splat piece that wants ribbons over puffs. Complete: `page.tsx`, `splat.ts` (~22kB), `audio.ts`, `README.md`. Build-reviewed, not browser-verified.

### Banked §406 (WIDE **kids** non-winners — TEXT seeds, folders removed; renumber on revival, max folder now 559):
Cycle 406 fired a WIDE kids triple off three orthogonal **off-glass** inputs (mic / camera / shake) × three different renderers (SVG / three.js / Canvas2D), all dodging the rolling-10 finger-on-glass ban. Winner shipped = `559-kids-clap-canon` (mic→SVG, Steve Reich phasing — the jury #4 "time-tension piece, but for children" answer). Both losers below are complete, build-reviewed real implementations (own folder, pure client-side, no API route, brick-wall limiter, hands-free auto-demo, graceful sensor-denied fallback); folders removed per the no-half-built-folders rule, banked here as ready-to-resurrect TEXT.

- **`560-kids-shadow-band`** (camera silhouette, NO ML → three.js particle creature → zone-gated pentatonic band) — `queued`. **What if a 4-year-old's SHADOW became a glowing creature, and dancing through the air played a band — no controller, no reading, just their silhouette of light?** INPUT camera frame-differencing (no MediaPipe/TF — `getUserMedia`→96×72 offscreen canvas→24×18 motion-energy grid vs captured background + prev frame, IIR-smoothed) · OUTPUT **three.js** ~4,096 additive THREE.Points blending toward active grid cells (a luminous "moving aura," not a sharp outline — correct for frame-diff) · TECHNIQUE optical-motion-energy→3 vertical instrument zones (left bells / center warm-pad / right marimba, rate-limited, motion→gain+brightness) · VIBE embodied/awe/your-body-becomes-light. Master `gain→lowpass8k→DynamicsCompressor(−10/20:1)`; synthetic Lissajous-blob auto-demo runs with zero permission, hands off to real camera, resumes after 4 s still; `text-rose-300` camera-denied + pointer fallback. Named refs **Myron Krueger *Videoplace* (1974)**, **Daniel Rozin** mirror works. **Ambition 3/5** (#2 ≥3 subsystems [no-ML silhouette + zone synth + three.js creature + auto-demo = 4] + #3 named refs; #1 contestable — camera-body→instrument is the most-used kids lane [537/545/554/258/423/419/268/295], the fresh element is the no-ML frame-diff silhouette-as-particle-creature, discounts cleanly; #5 not claimed). Built files (removed): `page.tsx` (565), `audio.ts` (191), `vision.ts` (149), `README.md`. **Why it didn't win (cycle 406):** the most embodied/"wow", but the **most redundant input lane** for the kids set (camera-body→sound has the most siblings) AND the **most fragile for the 06:30 hands-free glance** — frame-differencing is lighting-sensitive (fixed `MOTION_THRESHOLD=18` floods on variable light; needs adaptive calibration) and needs a lit room + camera grant, where the winner's SVG needs neither. **Resurrect** when the camera/body lane is scarce again, or pair the no-ML silhouette with adaptive-threshold calibration; a strong "body becomes light" register if Karel wants embodied.
- **`561-kids-rainmaker`** (device SHAKE / `devicemotion` accelerometer → granular percussion → Canvas2D storm) — `queued`. **What if a 4-year-old could SHAKE the tablet like a rainstick to summon a storm — gentle shake = drizzle, hard shake = downpour + thunder + lightning?** INPUT **`devicemotion`** linear-acceleration magnitude (NOT tilt/orientation — raw shake energy, `√(ax²+ay²+az²)` capped 30 m/s², EMA α=0.18 → 0–1 intensity; iOS 13+ `DeviceMotionEvent.requestPermission()` in the Start gesture; desktop pointer-shake / mash-button fallback) · OUTPUT **Canvas2D** pooled rain streaks (density ∝ intensity², ~7° angle) + landing splashes + jagged lightning bolt with white-flash decay + breathing cloud + 8-dot intensity HUD · TECHNIQUE **Xenakis stochastic grain cloud** — per-frame Poisson spawn N grains ∝ intensity² (triangle osc, C-pentatonic C4–A5, 8 ms attack / 80–230 ms decay, 40-voice pool via counter + `osc.onended`), high-intensity Poisson check fires thunder (180–400 Hz noise, 2.8 s) · VIBE weather/dynamic-sensory (RESPONDS to vigor — not a static glow toy). Master `grains→gain→lowpass9k→DynamicsCompressor(−10/20:1)`; sine-envelope auto-demo storm (6 s period) from frame one, hands off to real shake >0.05; `text-rose-300` denied notice. The `intensity²` quadratic knee makes the drizzle→downpour transition feel like *summoning* (~40% shake = storm). Named ref **Iannis Xenakis — stochastic granular clouds** (*Achorripsis* 1957 / *Analogique B* 1959; shake magnitude = the Poisson λ rate). **Ambition 3/5** (#2 ≥3 subsystems [devicemotion shake + granular engine + Canvas2D storm + auto-demo = 4] + #3 named ref Xenakis; #1 not claimed — granular + accelerometer both have priors; #5 not claimed). Built files (removed): `page.tsx` (447), `audio.ts`, `drawRain.ts`, `README.md`. **Why it didn't win (cycle 406):** the most robust voice-pooling + a clean Xenakis bind, and shake is a genuinely fresh off-glass input (distinct from 553's tilt/orientation), but the **least conceptually surprising** of the three — "shake → weather texture" reads as a sensory toy where the winner's Reich-phasing-for-kids is a genuinely novel idea AND answers a named jury provocation. **Resurrect** when a shake/percussion input is wanted, or fold the granular grain-cloud engine into a richer weather-composition piece (steer rain *type* by tilt while shake sets density).

- **`555-kids-echo-canyon`** (voice/mic → SVG canyon → canon/round) — `queued`. **What if a 4-year-old sang a phrase into a glowing canyon and friendly echo-creatures bounced it back as a staggered 4-voice ROUND — each echo further away, transposed +3rd/+5th/+octave — so one "la-la-la" self-harmonizes.** INPUT mic/getUserMedia → AnalyserNode → YIN-style autocorrelation (180–1200 Hz, clarity 0.38, babble fallback) → C-pentatonic snap; phrase commit on ~900 ms silence (≤10 notes) · OUTPUT **SVG** (5 depth-layered canyon ellipses + 4 receding blob-creatures with eyes/mouths, per-phrase pitch-contour path; rAF direct-attribute mutation, zero per-frame React; `feGaussianBlur` glow) · TECHNIQUE pitch-capture→canon scheduler (4 voices, look-ahead 120 ms / 25 ms tick, transpose [0,+4,+7,+12], stereo-pan [0,−.55,+.55,0], distance-attenuated gain, per-voice feedback-delay canyon reverb) · VIBE playful social call-and-response warm canyon. Master `gain→lowpass8k→DynamicsCompressor(−10/20:1)`; auto-demo round plays from frame one, ducks when the child sings, resumes after silence; `text-rose-300` mic-denied notice. **Explicitly distinct** from `549-kids-song-friend` (NO cross-day memory — immediate, in-the-moment) and `142-kids-echo-canon` (bigger: a spatial receding canyon with a real staggered round + stereo/distance voices). Named refs the **round/canon form** (*Frère Jacques* / Pachelbel), **alpine echo**, **Pauline Oliveros *Deep Listening***. **Ambition 3/5** (#2 ≥3 subsystems + #3 named refs; #1 not claimed — echo/canon + voice→pitch both have priors; #5 not claimed). **Why it didn't win (cycle 404):** the most *musically refined* and most reliable (SVG + voice), but the **least fresh relative to recent cycles** — the immediately-prior kids piece (549) was also voice/mic, so a voice winner would repeat the input family back-to-back, and echo-canon has a prior (142). **Resurrect** when voice/mic is scarce again, or fold its canyon-canon scheduler into a different input (e.g. a hummed round that 549's friend learns). ## SPINE: Xenharmonic Drone (started Cycle 403 — the jury 2026-06-12's named 4/5 chase: "538 capped at 3 only because it's a one-off — make it a spine and it's the first 4")
**The thread:** *the just-intonation / Bohlen–Pierce harmonic lattice stops being something you TAP and becomes a living place — a self-drifting La Monte Young "Dream House" drone that wanders the Tonnetz via the comma pump and never returns home.* Extends `538-xenharmonic-lattice` (cycle 1, a static SVG Tonnetz you click). **Cycle 2 (403, SHIPPED):** `552-dream-house` — a fully **autonomous long-form (>5 min, ~14-min arc) generative drone pilgrimage**: a walker steps the lattice every 4–8 s biased to unvisited nodes, sustaining the nearest 2–6 nodes as a slow-crossfading pure-ratio chord; an 8-phase arc (gathering→ascending→flowering→tritave→radiance→dissolving→receding→stillness) drives density (2↔6 voices), a drifting master lowpass (700↔4800 Hz), a ±0.4-octave register breath, and a 4-stop tuning migration (5-JI→B–P→19-EDO→back); the syntonic comma (81/80) guarantees the pitch never recloses; SVG Tonnetz with a glowing comet-trail + accumulating visited-ghost dots makes the journey visible; HUD shows elapsed/phase. Zero permission, runs itself (ideal 06:30 glance); tap = optional "exhale" to bloom a fresh region. Master `gain → drifting lowpass → DynamicsCompressor(20:1, −10 dBFS) → destination`; per-voice gain `0.55/voiceCount`, 8-voice hard cap. Ambition **3/5** (#2 ≥3 subsystems [walker + crossfading drone synth + long-form arc engine + SVG render = 4] + #3 named refs [La Monte Young & Zazeela *Dream House* / Éliane Radigue / Euler Tonnetz / Bohlen–Pierce / Partch / syntonic comma] + #4 spine cycle 2 — the jury's exact ask). **#1 the contestable case** — *first deliberate long-form (>5min) stateful evolving piece* is a counts-as-#1 in the manual, but 514/526 are arguably already long-form autonomous, so it discounts to 3/5 honestly. **#5 NOT claimed** — arxiv cs.SD June 5–12 2026 had zero JI/microtonal/pitch papers (still server-ML); said plainly. **No API route** (pure client Web Audio + SVG; no mic/camera/network/secrets). **Cycle 3 (planned):** fold in the banked steering layers below so the pilgrimage is *steerable but still autonomous by default* (grab-the-wheel), and/or bind a genuinely <14-day xenharmonic/JI finding for the long-missing #5.

### Banked §403 (DEEP non-winners — TEXT seeds, folders removed; renumber on revival, max folder now 552):
- **`550-xenharmonic-pilgrimage`** (VOICE-steered) — `queued`. Same autonomous drone-pilgrimage core, plus a "🎤 Sing to steer" layer: `getUserMedia` → `AnalyserNode` → autocorrelation pitch detector; the sung pitch pulls the walker toward the nearest JI node so you can *lead* the pilgrimage by gliding your voice, releasing back to free drift on silence. INPUT voice/mic (off-glass) · OUTPUT SVG Tonnetz + comet-trail · TECHNIQUE pitch-detection→lattice-pull + sustained pure-ratio drone · VIBE tune-yourself-into-the-drift. *Promising:* the most *musical/embodied* of the three — "tune your own voice into pure ratios" is a lovely cross-modal hook; the right cycle-3 grab-the-wheel layer to port onto 552. *Caveat that lost it:* the builder honestly flagged the autocorrelation detector as basic (octave-confusion + noise-floor issues on a real voice), so the steering is the least reliable; and 552's pure-autonomous form is more glance-robust + the cleaner long-form #1-attempt. Built files (removed): `page.tsx` (837 lines), `audio.ts`, `tuning.ts`, `README.md`.
- **`551-tilt-pilgrimage`** (TILT-steered) — `queued`. Same core, plus "📱 Tilt to wander": `DeviceOrientationEvent` (with the iOS 13+ `requestPermission()` flow) maps `gamma`→fifths-axis bias and `beta`→thirds-axis bias so you *lean your body through* the lattice; a draggable on-screen tilt-pad is the desktop/no-sensor fallback; held level it free-drifts. INPUT device-tilt (off-glass) + pad fallback · OUTPUT SVG Tonnetz + comet-trail + a level/horizon indicator · TECHNIQUE orientation→lattice-drift-bias + sustained pure-ratio drone · VIBE lean-into-the-harmony. *Promising:* the most *physical* embodiment (you tilt through fifths and thirds); the desktop tilt-pad makes it laptop-usable too — a strong second cycle-3 steering axis alongside 550's voice. *Caveat that lost it:* tilt needs the iOS permission dance + a real device to shine (Karel's 06:30 glance would mostly see the pad/autonomous), and it's a steering layer on the same core rather than a bigger swing than 552's long-form arc. Built files (removed): `page.tsx` (775 lines), `audio.ts`, `tuning.ts`, `README.md`.

## SPINE: The Living Earth (started Cycle 373 — a SECOND live multi-cycle spine, jury 2026-06-09's "restart a multi-cycle commitment" + "extend 437's live-data lineage, nobody is")
**The thread:** *play the living Earth from a live planetary data stream, tuned to a non-Western / expressive system, on a luminous data-world.* Extends `437-wiki-pulse` (the lab's only live-external-data piece — jury called it "the freshest concept" and flagged it unextended) from human-activity into the **geophysical/planetary** domain, and breaks the recent adult **latent-image monoculture** (AI-image OUT was count-banned at 4×). Born from RESEARCH §373 (real-time stream sonification is going *infinite/fatigue-resistant* [arXiv 2605.21874, May 21 2026] and *non-Western-tuned* [arXiv 2602.14560, gamelan, Feb 2026]). **Cycle 1 (373, SHIPPED):** `463-terra-gamelan` — USGS live earthquakes → **gamelan-slendro/pelog-tuned** bell-globe (three.js Earth; depth→pitch, mag→loudness, lon→pan; 24h stateful accumulation + an energy-driven drone bed). Ambition 3/5 (#2+#3+#4). **Cycle 2 (375, SHIPPED):** `471-helios-orbit` — NOAA **live space-weather** (solar-wind plasma + IMF Bz + planetary Kp) conducts an **EDM build-and-drop** seen from orbit: a geomagnetic storm is the DROP (sub-bass + 4-on-the-floor kick + harmony **resolves minor→major** + auroral ovals igniting around the IGRF poles + magnetosphere bloom), on a dark Earth that carries forward `463`'s Fibonacci-dot-globe. Ambition **4/5** (#2+#3+#4+#5 — the EDM arc cites the §373 arXiv 2605.21874 anchor, within 14 days). Won a **DEEP** 3-renderer fire; the two non-winners (`469-helios-aurora` fbm sky-curtain, `470-helios-raymarch` volumetric raymarch) were **deliberately NOT banked** — a grep-audit found they duplicate the register of the loved `262-aurora-particle`❤️ (whose own banked siblings are `aurora-raymarch`/`aurora-drone-field`); `471`'s orbital globe was the one approach distinct from 262 AND the one that continues this spine. *(The §373 banked `464-helios-aurora`/`465-terra-pulse` EDM siblings are now **superseded** by `471` — same concept, shipped; leave as historical, do not resurrect as-is.)* **Cycle 3 (planned):** add a second live planetary layer (space weather ⊕ seismic on ONE Earth) and polish; OR a genuinely-fresh space-weather angle 262 does NOT cover — sonify the **far-upstream CME forecast** (arXiv 2508.13892, Solar Orbiter predicts a storm hours ahead) so the music *anticipates* a drop before it reaches Earth. **No API route needed** anywhere in this spine — all sources are public CORS-enabled, read-only, no auth, no secrets; each piece has a synthetic fallback so it demos with no network.
