## SPINE: The Resonant Room (started Cycle 377 — the adult #4 spine; explored across temperatures)
**The thread:** *a sympathetic resonant room — an instrument played INTO a tuned acoustic space that rings back — explored from warm to cold.* **Cycle 1 (377, SHIPPED):** `475-resonant-room` — Karel's *Welcome Home* piano into a **key-tuned N=8 Householder-matrix Feedback Delay Network** (Jot / Stautner–Puckette) that rings IN KEY and **resolves to the tonic on release** (the warm partner). **Cycle 2 (379, SHIPPED):** `483-two-rooms-apart` — the *opposite temperature*: a **live-MIDI**-played room split into **two coupled resonator banks tuned a tritone apart** that beat against each other (no tonic, suspended dissonance), with a **real-time Plomp–Levelt/Sethares roughness** readout; **resolution is the player's act** — drag detune→unison and the beating fuses. Ambition **4/5** (#1 first-Web-MIDI-INPUT + #2 + #3 + #4; #5 not claimed — refs foundational). Won a DEEP 3-way fire; the jury (2026-06-10) explicitly asked to *deepen a spine* (not grab a 5th DSP primitive) — this is an *extension*, one room → two. **Cycle 3 (381, SHIPPED):** `486-third-room` — adds a THIRD room a **just-fifth (702 ¢ = 3:2)** above Room A (its partials lock onto A's, so A↔C is consonant) as a **bridge**: blend C in, find the **pivot note** where all three rooms momentarily agree, then collapse the tritone — *negotiate* the dissonance to rest rather than just collapsing it. Folds in BOTH banked cycle-3 ideas at once — the **steepest-descent pivot-note suggestion engine** (from `481`, glows the notes that most lower three-room roughness) AND the **WebGPU compute vorticity field** (from `482`, the starved renderer; roughness → per-room vorticity, Canvas2D fallback) — plus the new third-room mechanic (implements `483`'s own documented cycle-3 idea). Ambition **4/5** (#2 5 subsystems + #3 refs + #4 spine cycle 3 — the truest *extension*: one→two→three rooms; #1/#5 honestly not claimed). Hits jury (2026-06-10) #1 (tension resolved *through action*) + #2 (deepen, don't grab a primitive) + #3 (ban three.js, pick the starved WebGPU). Won a DEEP 3-way fire (resolution-mechanic axis). **Cycle 4 (planned, pre-scoped from this fire's banked siblings):** (a) port `485`'s **whisper panel** — a fixed "Notes that ease the tension →" list with predicted %-improvement + a derived gradient arrow on the detune slider (the most legible 06:30 read); (b) add `487`'s **roughness landscape + shared-control "descend toward rest" autopilot** as a second resolution axis (feel the slope of the tritone→fifth negotiation; keep it opt-in/overridable so it never becomes a pre-baked release). **No API route** — pure client-side Web Audio + Web MIDI + WebGPU/Canvas2D; no secrets.

### Banked §381 (DEEP non-winners — TEXT seeds, folders removed; renumber on revival, max folder now 486):
- **`485-resolving-room`** (whisper-panel note-hint engine) — `queued`. The two cycle-2 tritone rooms unchanged, but the centerpiece is the cleanest realization of the steepest-descent *partner*: for every candidate pitch C3–C6 it analytically estimates the marginal Plomp–Levelt roughness if that note were added, ranks by most-negative delta, and renders the top 3 as emerald keyboard glows + a live "Notes that ease the tension →" panel with predicted %-improvement; a finite-difference detune gradient (derived, not hardcoded) drives a direction arrow on the slider. INPUT live-MIDI/on-screen/QWERTY · OUTPUT WebGPU compute vorticity field (curl-noise, ~4000 particles, Canvas2D fallback) · TECHNIQUE two-room roughness + steepest-descent note suggestion · VIBE cold tension you resolve by following a whisper. *Promising:* the most legible hint UX of the fire — the explicit named-notes panel reads instantly at a 06:30 phone glance; port it onto `486` as cycle 4. *Caveat that lost it:* the smallest conceptual step (a hint layer bolted onto the existing two rooms) vs. `486`'s structural third-room extension. Built files (removed): `page.tsx` (1625 lines), `README.md`.
- **`487-guided-descent`** (roughness landscape + shared-control autopilot) — `queued`. The two cycle-2 rooms, but the roughness is sampled analytically at 120 points across the 0–600 ¢ detune axis and drawn as a live **landscape curve** (rose→emerald fill) with the current detune as a violet ball and an emerald dashed arrow to the nearest valley; a "Descend toward rest" **autopilot** walks −∇roughness at ±15 ¢/step (80 ms) that the player can grab and override at any moment (shared control). INPUT live-MIDI/on-screen/QWERTY + the landscape ball · OUTPUT WebGPU compute vorticity field (Canvas2D fallback) · TECHNIQUE two-room roughness + visible gradient-descent landscape + note hints · VIBE feel-the-slope-home. *Promising:* makes the steepest-descent idea *playable* and tangible — add it to `486` as a second resolution axis (feel the tritone→fifth negotiation's slope). *Caveat that lost it:* the auto-descent risks reading as a pre-baked release (the jury's #1 explicitly wants the *listener* to resolve through action), and the landscape-vs-detune curve leans clinical/Ikeda; keep any port opt-in + overridable. Built files (removed): `page.tsx` (1608 lines), `README.md`.

### Banked §379 (DEEP non-winners — TEXT seeds, folders removed; renumber on revival, max folder now 483):
- **`481-resonant-suspension`** (whole-tone single-room) — `queued`. A single bank of 10 high-Q biquads tuned to the **whole-tone scale** (no tonic, no leading tone, no perfect fifth) so the sympathetic ring is permanently ambiguous; resolution via consonant play OR a "let it breathe" damper. INPUT live-MIDI/on-screen/QWERTY · OUTPUT Canvas2D standing-wave lattice · TECHNIQUE whole-tone resonator room + real-time Plomp–Levelt/Sethares roughness · VIBE cold suspended ambiguity. *Promising:* the cleanest single-room ambiguity model; its **steepest-descent dissonance-gradient hint engine** (highlight the 2–3 notes that most lower roughness) is the strongest cycle-3 *interaction* idea — port onto `483`. *Caveat that lost it:* whole-tone tuning gives few consonant intervals to *play* toward, so its resolve-through-action leans on a discrete damper button rather than a continuous embodied gesture. Built files (removed): `page.tsx` (1011 lines), `README.md`.
- **`482-dissonance-field`** (WebGPU compute renderer) — `queued`. The same whole-tone tension room, but beating/roughness rendered as a **WebGPU compute particle field** (WGSL, 512 particles; measured roughness → vorticity term; Canvas2D fallback on no-`navigator.gpu`). INPUT live-MIDI/on-screen/QWERTY · OUTPUT **WebGPU** compute field (starved renderer, jury flagged 1×) · TECHNIQUE whole-tone resonator room + roughness-driven GPU vorticity · VIBE cold turbulent dissonance settling to laminar. *Promising:* the lab's **starved WebGPU path** — bank as `483`'s cycle-3 *renderer upgrade*. *Caveat that lost it:* WebGPU device fragility on Karel's phone-review workflow (he'd likely see the Canvas2D fallback anyway), and its resolve-mechanic was no stronger than `481`'s. ## SPINE: The Wave Field (kids) (started Cycle 378 — a kids #4 spine paralleling the adult Resonant Room; physical-modeled acoustic fields you tap, see, AND hear)
**The thread:** *a real solved 2-D wave field (FDTD digital-waveguide mesh, Van Duyne–Smith ICMC 1993) is the instrument — the audio and the visual are the same field — grown across cycles from one pond to many coupled ponds.* **Cycle 1 (378, SHIPPED):** `478-kids-wave-pond` — tap ONE luminous pond, watch real waves spread/reflect/interfere, hear that solved field (three.js render; FDTD AudioWorklet). **Cycle 2 (382, SHIPPED):** `489-kids-pond-pair` — the jury (2026-06-10 #2) named extension: **TWO coupled wave fields** joined by a sympathetic channel (`K=0.08` always-on whisper: a ripple in pond A excites pond B), plus a tangible floating **lily pad carrier** that bobs on the waves, drifts to the channel, and physically *carries energy across* to ring the other pond — a visible sound-messenger for a 4yo. Dual pentatonic registers (A low C3-pentatonic, B high C4-pentatonic) → always harmonize. Renderer moved OFF the jury-banned three.js → **Canvas2D** (dodges three.js + the WebGL2 4× count-ban). Ambition 3/5 (#2 two FDTD solvers + cross-pond coupling + carrier physics + pickup synth ≥3 · #3 Van Duyne–Smith 1993 + sympathetic coupling · #4 spine cycle 2 — an *extension*, not a 5th primitive; #1/#5 honestly not claimed). Won a DEEP 3-builder fire (coupling-mechanic axis). **Cycle 3 (planned, pre-scoped from this fire's banks):** add `488`'s child-controllable coupling vine and/or its WebGPU-compute FDTD path; lean into `487`'s explicit cooperative two-kids framing.

### Banked §382 (DEEP non-winners — TEXT seeds, folders removed; renumber on revival, max folder now 489):
- **`488-kids-echo-ponds`** (coupling-is-the-toy + WebGPU compute) — `queued`. Same two coupled FDTD ponds, but the **coupling constant K (0–0.25) is a draggable glowing vine/bridge** the child pulls wide (a splash floods across) or pinches shut (each pond sings alone) — the *coupling itself* is the mechanic. Renderer = **WebGPU compute** FDTD (WGSL storage-buffer ping-pong, `@workgroup_size(8,8)`) with a CPU **Canvas2D fallback** (identical sim). INPUT multi-touch + draggable bridge · OUTPUT WebGPU compute (Canvas2D fallback) — the lab's starved GPU-compute lane · TECHNIQUE two coupled FDTD fields + child-set sympathetic coupling · VIBE warm coupled water you tune. *Promising:* the sharpest answer to "the coupling is the mechanic you play" + the starved-renderer diversity prize. *Caveat that lost it:* a coupling-strength slider is abstract for a 4yo (vs. 489's concrete visible carrier), WebGPU likely falls back to Canvas2D on Karel's review phone anyway, and the GPU path has a documented half-step coupling lag; 486 also just refreshed WebGPU (no longer 0×). Build-clean (tsc + eslint zero-warning per builder). Built files (removed): `page.tsx` (~1250 lines), `README.md`.
- **`487-kids-two-ponds`** (clean cooperative core) — `queued`. Two coupled FDTD ponds joined by a fixed glowing channel (`K=0.12`); a splash in A visibly/audibly wakes B; explicit **"two kids each claim a pond"** cooperative framing (the KIDS.md social-bonding gap). Dual-palette `ImageData` Canvas2D render (pond A navy→cyan, pond B violet→rose); channel strip brightens with cross-edge energy. INPUT multi-touch (two hands) · OUTPUT Canvas2D ImageData height-map · TECHNIQUE two coupled FDTD fields + fixed sympathetic channel · VIBE warm cooperative water. *Promising:* the cleanest, lowest-risk cooperative two-player realization + a tidy dual-palette renderer. *Caveat that lost it:* the coupling is fixed (nothing to play with) → lowest surprise of the three; 489's carrier object and 488's draggable vine both give the child more to *do*. Built files (removed): `page.tsx` (860 lines), `README.md`.

## Banked from Cycle 374 (WIDE **kids** fire — three embodied registers deliberately fired OFF the GPU-physics-sim + JI-bell kids monoculture [451 soft-body / 456 ferrofluid / 460 collision were the last 3 kids cycles]; winner shipped = `468-kids-bottle-flute` [the lab's FIRST physically-modeled WIND instrument — breath-excited digital-waveguide bore]. ### `466-kids-sing-kite` — a 4-year-old SINGS to fly a paper kite along a scrolling melody contour; matching the line makes the kite glow + harmonize, and each ~24s loop ends with the kite "singing your song back" (a gift-replay of the child's matched notes). INPUT mic **pitch** (YIN/CMND autocorrelation, 120–600 Hz, octave-collapse to D3–D4) · OUTPUT **inline-SVG** matte cut-paper sky (kite/clouds/contour-path/sparkles via rAF DOM-attr writes — dodges both the jury-banned Canvas2D AND the WebGL-physics monoculture) · TECHNIQUE continuous-pitch-as-flight-control + D-major diatonic snap + delayed melodic replay · VIBE warm dusk sky that **resolves** (D-major, lands on tonic — not a pentatonic lullaby). Always-on D3+A3+D4 drone; brick-wall limiter; no-mic → synthetic-voice auto-demo sings the contour itself hands-free. Refs **Singing Carrots / SingSage** (sing-to-the-line browser pitch-training) · **de Cheveigné & Kawahara, YIN** (JASA 2002) · **Chris Wilson, cwilso/PitchDetect**. Built files: `page.tsx` (971 lines), `README.md`. *(banked sibling — ambition ~2/5 [#2 ≥3 subsystems: pitch-detect + scrolling-contour engine + SVG render + harmony/drone synth · #3 named-refs]. **#1 NOT cleanly claimable** — the lab already has voice-pitch→output [`158-kids-hum-paint`, `280-kids-echo-canyon`, `455-kids-sing-cymatics`, `413-kids-mouth-mirror`]; continuous-pitch-as-game-control is a fresh *application*, not a new primitive. Didn't win: the "sing onto the target line" mechanic is well-trodden, and child-voice pitch tracking is the least-certain subsystem [breathy/uncertain 4yo intonation vs CMND<0.20 + 46ms buffer latency]. **Strong bank — the lab's purest embodied-voice kids toy** [the jury's "sing, don't poke"]; resurrect by de-risking child-voice [lower CMND threshold, shorter buffer] and adding cubic-bezier contour + a delay reverb.)*

### `467-kids-shadow-band` — stand back and WAVE your whole body; a camera **motion-energy** field conducts a 3-zone band [bells / marimba / xylophone] and your movement explodes as a living glow of additive particles. INPUT **camera frame-differencing** (hidden 32×24 canvas, per-cell luminance-diff → per-zone motion energy, analysis-only — no ML, no identity, never recorded) · OUTPUT **three.js** additive-blended particle aura (1800 sprites, spring-home + energy-burst — a motion-driven particle field, explicitly NOT a physics sim) · TECHNIQUE zonal motion-energy → instrument activation + particle emission (more motion = louder/brighter/faster; all zones = a resolving I-chord arpeggio) · VIBE joyful get-off-the-couch stage, warm I–IV–V–I that **resolves**. Brick-wall limiter; no-camera → roaming-Gaussian-blob auto-demo + WebGL-unavailable notice. Refs **Myron Krueger, *Videoplace*** (1974) · **Golan Levin & Zach Lieberman, *Messa di Voce*** (2003) · **Davis & Bobick, Motion History Image** (MIT 1997). Built files: `page.tsx` (767 lines), `README.md`. *(banked sibling — ambition ~2/5 [#2 ≥3 subsystems: frame-diff analysis + three.js particle render + I–IV–V band · #3 named-refs]. Didn't win: its OUTPUT is **three.js** — the very WebGL-render axis at 5× in the trailing window [the monoculture this fire set out to break], so it dodges the GPU-**physics** rut but not the GPU-**render** one; and lighting-sensitive frame-diff is a real 06:30 risk. **Strong bank — the anti-screen-bias, get-up-and-move lane** the jury keeps asking for; resurrect when camera INPUT is the diversity pick and pair with a non-WebGL OUTPUT, or extend with optical-flow direction [not just energy] so a *swipe* gesture conducts.)*

## SPINE: The Latent Piano Room (started Cycle 367 — the jury's #1 ask, restarting the dead-since-414 multi-cycle axis)
**The thread:** run the closed audio→image→audio loop (born in loved `441-latent-listening-room`) on Karel's **actual recorded piano**, deepening over multiple cycles. **Cycle 1 (367, SHIPPED):** `448-piano-phrase-painter` — musical-structure listening (onset/phrase/chroma/dynamics) drives *when & what* is dreamed; image color bends the real playback back. **Cycle 2 (369, SHIPPED):** `454-piano-caption-loom` — the single template-prompt is replaced by an **iterative multi-agent caption refinement loom**: specialist proposers (scene/palette/motion/style) draft a caption, a **Critic agent** scores it against the music's valence-arousal target and the proposers **revise** over 3 visible rounds (Draft→Revised→Final, confidence rising, changed words highlighted) — the prompt visibly *improves* before the image regenerates. Realizes arXiv 2512.23320 (music-derived captions + VA emotion) + the iterative-refinement loop of T2I-Copilot/ImAgent (RESEARCH §369), and mirrors the lab's own planner→builders→critic orchestration. **Cycle 3 (371, SHIPPED):** `457-piano-image-duet` — the *other* half of the loop finally closes: the dreamed image no longer only *filters* the piano, it **re-composes** it. A glowing scan-line sweeps the dreamed image left→right reading it as a **spectral score** (image-as-spectrogram): each column's vertical brightness profile drives a 24-partial additive bank, Y→frequency **quantized to the detected key** (always consonant/warm — resolves on purpose), brightness→amplitude, producing a shimmer-choir voice that **duets** Karel's real piano. Realizes **Art2Mus** (arXiv 2602.17599, Feb 2026 — direct visual→music conditioning, no image→text; RESEARCH §371) in the **Xenakis UPIC / MetaSynth** image-as-score lineage. The scan-line makes "the image is singing the piano" *visible* — the spine's legible/instructional lane. **Cycle 4 (planned, ~373):** resurrect a sibling below (the **improviser** counter-melody or the **granular cloud**), or fold them into 457 so the duet has melody + texture + spectral layers; and/or fold in `452`'s visible council HUD / `453`'s affect-atlas as a viz layer. **Open dependency:** a real **Welcome Home** track ID (the runtime `journey_paths` IDs aren't in the static repo — `448`/`454` default to the Ghost recording `549fc519…`); the same ask blocks `424-welcome-erosion`. Karel: paste a WH recording ID and the spine runs on the album.

## Banked from Cycle 372 (WIDE **kids** fire — three genuinely-unrelated grep-clean fresh registers, the named tool against the jury's "learned to go deep, forgot how to go weird"; winner shipped = `460-kids-ball-pit` [the lab's FIRST many-body collision physics]. Both siblings are complete, **build-reviewed** real implementations [own folder, pure client-side, no API route, no `_shared` imports, brick-wall limiter, 4-s hands-free auto-demo, WebGL fallback notice]; folders removed per the no-half-built-folders rule, banked here as ready-to-resurrect TEXT. **Numbered as built (461/462) — renumber on revival; max folder now 460.**)

### `461-kids-storm-conductor` — tap the storm sky and a **branching bolt of lightning SINGS as it cracks**. INPUT touch (tap / hold-to-grow / drag) · OUTPUT WebGL2 additive-bloom line-tree (over a Canvas2D star/cloud backdrop) · TECHNIQUE **fractal lightning via recursive random-midpoint-displacement** (`buildBolt()`: subdivide cloud→tap segment, jitter midpoints ⊥ by length×roughness≈0.45, recurse depth 4–7; spawn 3–8 forks from interior points; two-layer white-hot-core + soft-blue-glow render) · VIBE dramatic-but-warm storm. Audio: main channel a band-passed sawtooth gliss 180→2000 Hz ("crack"), each branch **tip** a staggered FM bell on a D-major JI hexachord (D E F♯ A B C♯ — multiple taps = consonant stacked chords), warm low-passed-noise thunder ~120 ms later, continuous pink-noise + 38 Hz sub storm bed. Refs **Drilian "How to generate shockingly good 2D lightning effects"** (midpoint-displacement) + **Prusinkiewicz & Lindenmayer, *The Algorithmic Beauty of Plants* (1990)** (L-system branching). *(banked sibling — ambition ~3/5 [#1 fractal-lightning is a grep-clean lab-first viz technique + #2 ≥3 subsystems + #3 named-ref]. Didn't win: lightning effects are well-trodden creative-coding ground [lower surprise than a singing collision-pit], and it flags a real 06:30 risk — `gl.lineWidth` is clamped to 1 px on many GPU drivers, so the bolts can render thin; resurrect by switching to triangle-strip quads for the channel so width is driver-independent. Near-ship; the dramatic-weather lane.)*

### `462-kids-gear-garden` — spin one cog and a **garden of meshing gears turns; the gear tooth-RATIOS ARE the harmony**. INPUT circular touch-drag (spin) + tap-to-add-gear · OUTPUT WebGL procedural cogwheels (`geargl.ts`, GLSL ES 1.00, WebGL1/2-compatible) + Canvas2D glow/label overlay · TECHNIQUE **meshing gear-train kinematic solver** (BFS from driver, ω_child = −ω_parent × teeth_parent/teeth_child, exponential angular friction) where **integer tooth ratios map directly to just-intonation frequency ratios** (24:16 = 3:2 fifth, 8:16 = 1:2 octave, 20:16 = 5:4 third); spin faster → lowpass cutoff rises → brighter chord. Per-gear triangle-osc + lowpass, Karplus-ish pluck on add, soft C2-E2-G2 drone so it's never silent, master compressor/limiter. Refs **Harry Partch *Genesis of a Music*** (small-integer ratios = consonance) + mechanical **music-box/barrel-organ** lineage + **Antikythera mechanism** (gears as ratio machines). *(banked sibling — ambition ~2–3/5 [#2 ≥3 subsystems + #3 named-ref; #1 gear-ratio-as-harmony is a fresh framing but gears-as-instrument isn't a cleanly-novel primitive]. Didn't win: the most *contemplative* of the three [risk of reading as a quiet drone vs. the jury's loud/weird ask], and the "ratio = interval" payload is the subtlest to feel at age 4. The single most beautiful CONCEPT of the fire — the machine's structure literally IS the chord; resurrect as an adult/all-ages piece where the ratio-harmony pedagogy can breathe, or pair with a visible ratio-readout. Near-ship.)*

## Banked from Cycle 371 (DEEP **adult** fire — Latent Piano Room CYCLE 3, one concept *let the dreamed image **re-compose** the piano, not just filter it*, three image→music recomposition attacks; winner shipped = `457-piano-image-duet` [image-as-spectrogram additive resynthesis]. ### `458-piano-image-improviser` — the dreamed image **improvises a counter-melody**: `extractImageFeatures()` downsamples the image to 16×12 → k-means-ish hue buckets → diatonic scale degrees (in the detected key), brightness-gradient → melodic contour, local-variance/edge-density → note density/rhythm; a plucked triangle voice (8–12 dB under the piano) improvises in-key, synced to phrase boundaries, with Markov anti-repetition and **always resolves to tonic on the final note**. HUD: 4×3 region markers pulse when their note fires + a piano-roll ribbon. Refs **Art2Mus** (direct visual→music) + **David Cope EMI** / Markov improv. *(banked sibling — the most "agentic" take, the most on-brand for Karel's managed-agent-design work; folds naturally with `454`'s loom for cycle 4. ~1246-line page + 532-line improv.ts, build-reviewed. Risk to verify on real hardware: a counter-melody over expressive piano can clash rhythmically even in-key — tune density/timing on real ears.)*

### `459-piano-image-grains` — the dreamed image becomes a **granular synthesis cloud**: every sampled pixel is a grain seed — x→stereo pan, y→pitch quantized to the detected key, hue→waveform timbre (warm hues→sine, cool→triangle), brightness→grain gain; a lookahead scheduler (100ms horizon, ≤24 concurrent grains, Hann-window envelopes, limiter) layers a shimmering textural cloud whose character *is the image's character* over Karel's piano, density tracking RMS. Glowing grain particles drawn at the exact sampled image positions. Refs **Art2Mus** + **Curtis Roads** *Microsound* / **Xenakis** stochastic grain clouds. *(banked sibling — the most texturally beautiful / atmospheric; the textural cycle-4 candidate. ~927-line page + 353-line grains.ts, build-reviewed. Risk to verify: grain-cloud reads as ambience more than a legible compositional act, and ≤24-grain scheduling perf on a low-end phone.)*

## Banked from Cycle 369 (DEEP **adult** fire — one concept *a multi-agent caption pipeline composes, from Karel's real piano, the caption a latent image is dreamed from*, three technical attacks; winner shipped = `454-piano-caption-loom` [iterative propose→critique→revise]. ### `452-piano-agent-council` — the VISIBLE multi-agent studio: a council of specialist agents each propose a caption clause and a Director weights+merges them, live on screen *(banked sibling — the most legible "agentic studio" take; the strongest cycle-3 VIZ layer + the most on-brand for Karel's managed-agent-design work)*
Same real-piano chain as `454` (`<audio crossOrigin>` default `/api/audio/549fc519…`, file-drop, warm C–Am–F–G7–Cmaj9 resolving synth fallback → `createMediaElementSource` → AnalyserNode 2048 → reverb/compressor/master). `council.ts` (336 ln): five deterministic agents — **Scene** (locale from onset-density+energy+phrase), **Palette** (color from modality/consonance/dominant-pc), **Motion** (turbulence from RMS+onset-rate/arousal), **Style** (Anadol/Akten base + Russell-quadrant emotion modifier), and a **Director** that computes valence (modality+consonance) × arousal (RMS+onset-density), places the music in Russell's circumplex (serene/joyous/tense/melancholic), and **weights** the merge (Palette up when valence is decisive, Motion up when arousal is high). The visible centerpiece: a labeled **card per agent** that lights up and shows its proposed clause + a **Russell circumplex dial** with a moving valence-arousal dot + the Director's caption assembling clause-by-clause. Image at phrase boundaries / onset clusters (≥7s, ≤1 in flight); image→audio loopback (brightness→lowpass+shimmer, warmth→reverb); 501→synthesized plasma/blob/particle field, council stays live, amber "no image key" badge. Built files: `page.tsx` (1079), `council.ts` (336), `analysis.ts` (243), `field.ts` (278), `synth.ts` (180), guarded `api/route.ts` (28), `README.md` (97). Refs: arXiv 2512.23320 · T2I-Copilot 2507.20536 · ImAgent 2511.11483 · Russell 1980 · Anadol/Akten · Bello 2005. Ambition: #2 (≥3 subsystems) + #3 (named) + #4 (spine cycle-2 alt). **Why it lost:** its council is a **one-shot** weighted merge — closer to splitting `448`'s template across agents than adding a new *capability*; `454`'s self-critiquing refinement loop is the genuinely new mechanic (and the jury explicitly penalizes re-presenting an existing mechanic). **Why resurrect:** it is the **most legible at a 06:30 phone glance** ("oh, agents are writing the prompt together") and the **most directly on-brand for Karel's oCFO agentic-design competency** — the literal planner→specialists→director studio made visible. **Cycle-3 fold-in:** use 452's agent-card + circumplex-dial HUD as the visible layer ON the 454 loom (show WHICH agent proposed each clause AND watch it get critiqued).

### `453-piano-affect-atlas` — the EMOTION-CARTOGRAPHY take: the music's live valence-arousal coordinate is a glowing comet tracing the Russell circumplex, and the dreamed image is the "view from" that emotional point *(banked sibling — the cleanest VIZ-diversity dodge; the emotional-arc view)*
Same real-piano chain + analysis as `454`/`452`. Separate `affect.ts` (127 ln) computes smoothed valence (modality+consonance) and arousal (RMS+onset-density) ∈[-1,1]; `council.ts` (199 ln) runs specialist proposers whose **weights are set by the affect coordinate's quadrant** (near serene → palette warm-soft + motion sparse; near tense → motion-dominant turbulence) → one emotion-aligned caption. The centerpiece: a 2D **valence×arousal ATLAS** (four soft-zoned/labeled quadrants) with the music's current emotion as a glowing **comet + fading trail**, so you literally watch the *performance's emotional arc* over time; the dreamed image is the "view from" the current coordinate, regenerating on phrase boundary OR quadrant-crossing. Image→audio loopback + 501→synthesized field as the siblings. Built files: `page.tsx` (1112), `affect.ts` (127), `council.ts` (199), `analysis.ts` (250), `field.ts` (320), `synth.ts` (183), guarded `api/route.ts` (28). **NOTE: its `README.md` was left EMPTY by the builder (0 bytes)** — a deliverable miss; the code is complete and build-reviewed, but a revival must write the README. Refs (intended): Russell 1980 · arXiv 2512.23320 · T2I-Copilot/ImAgent · Anadol/Akten · Bello 2005. Ambition: #2 + #3 + #4 (spine cycle-2 alt). **Why it lost:** the affect-atlas is more a **HUD than a new capability** (the agents still one-shot a caption), and the empty README broke the exit contract; `454`'s iterative critique is the stronger mechanic. **Why resurrect:** the affect-comet is the most **visually distinct / fresh-modality** of the three (emotion as a place you move through) and the natural cycle-3 alternate VIZ — pair the comet-on-circumplex with the 454 loom so you see BOTH where the music *is* emotionally AND the caption sharpening toward it. **Fix-first on revival:** write the README.

## Banked from Cycle 367 (DEEP **adult** fire — one concept *a latent room that dreams Karel's real recorded piano, audio→image→audio*, three technical attacks; winner shipped = `448-piano-phrase-painter` [musically-aware phrase/harmony-driven]. Both siblings are complete, **build-reviewed** real implementations; folders removed per the no-half-built-folders rule, banked here as ready-to-resurrect TEXT. **Numbered as built (447/449) — renumber on revival; max folder now 448.**)

### `447-piano-latent-room` — the FAITHFUL port: 441's exact spectral→prompt→image→audio loop, now on Karel's real recording *(banked sibling — the safest 06:30 read + the cleanest spine foundation)*
`<audio crossOrigin="anonymous">` (default `/api/audio/549fc519…`, or file-drop, or 12-TET synth fallback that resolves) → `createMediaElementSource` → AnalyserNode → lowpass→shimmer→reverb→compressor→master. Every frame: RMS energy (8 log bands) + spectral centroid + 12-bin chromagram → dominant pitch-class (exp-smoothed). Every ~6.5 s: centroid→palette / energy→density / pitch-class→hue over the Anadol base style → guarded FAL `flux/schnell` (≤1 in flight, ≥7 s apart) → ~2.5 s cross-fade + Ken-Burns + vignette. Loop-back: 8×6 `getImageData` avg color → brightness opens lowpass + shimmer, warmth lengthens reverb — on the real playback. CORS-taint guard: analyser all-zero >2.5 s → auto-swap to synth + amber status. Built files: `page.tsx` (728), `audio.ts` (458), `field.ts` (259), guarded `api/route.ts`. Refs: Anadol *Unsupervised*/*Machine Hallucinations* · Akten *Learning to See*. Ambition: #4 (spine cycle-1 alt) + #2 (≥3 subsystems) + #3 (named refs). **Why it lost:** it is the *least differentiated from `441`* — re-presents the existing mechanic on a new source, which the jury explicitly penalizes — though it is the safest hands-free read. **Why resurrect:** the cleanest, most reliable spine foundation; ship it if a cycle wants the rock-solid real-audio loop with no analysis risk. **Next-cycle deepening:** it IS the spine's stable base — layer the cycle-2 multi-agent captioning onto this if `448`'s onset-timed regeneration proves too eager/sparse on real piano.

### `449-piano-latent-grains` — the AGGRESSIVE loop-back: the dreamed image RE-COMPOSES the piano as granular re-synthesis *(banked sibling — the strongest cycle-3 deepening candidate)*
A `GranularEngine` schedules Hann-windowed grains (≤24 voices → DynamicsCompressor) off the **decoded** piano buffer (default `/api/audio/549fc519…` → fetch signed URL → `decodeAudioData`; or file-drop; or an `OfflineAudioContext`-rendered C→Am→F→G→C resolving phrase). The image *re-composes the grains*: **brightness → grain density + read-head scatter**, **warmth → pitch-spread (±semitones) + reverb wet**, **texture/variance → grain-duration jitter** — so a bright busy image scatters the piano into a shimmering detuned cloud, a dark calm image lets it settle back toward the original. AnalyserNode on the grain output drives the ~6 s prompt; full-bleed canvas cross-fades the FAL image with grain motes (pitch-class-colored) drifting over it, or a local latent field when no key. Built files: `page.tsx` (916), `granular.ts` (334), guarded `api/route.ts`. Refs: Curtis Roads *Microsound* (2001) · Anadol *Machine Hallucinations* · rides loved `227-paths-granular`❤️ + `323-latent-condensation`❤️. Ambition: #4 (spine cycle-1 alt) + #2 + #3. **Why it lost:** graining a *real* recording is the highest browser-verification risk (mush/artifact danger) for a build-only sandbox — but on pure surprise it was the closest contender. **Why resurrect:** it is the literal answer to the spine's cycle-3 goal — the image *reshapes* the music, not just filters it; fold its granular loop-back into the spine once `448`'s base is browser-verified. **Next-cycle deepening:** add a transient-preserving grain mode so attacks survive, and let onset/phrase detection (from `448`) gate grain bursts to musical events.

## Banked from Cycle 366 (DEEP **kids** fire — one concept *a child paints a living singing GALAXY of light by moving in front of the camera*, three technical attacks; winner shipped = `444-kids-aurora-hands` [bare-hands WebGPU-compute galaxy]. Both siblings are complete, **build-reviewed** real implementations; folders removed per the no-half-built-folders rule, banked here as ready-to-resurrect TEXT. **Numbered 445/446 to avoid colliding with the now-shipped 444 — renumber on revival; max folder now 444.**)

### `445-kids-light-river` — paint a glowing, singing river of light with ANY whole-body motion — no model, no WebGPU, the most robust take *(banked sibling — the resilient maximum-06:30-reliability ship)*
Model-free **optical flow**: the webcam is downscaled to a 64×48 grid, per-cell `|frame_t − frame_{t-1}|` gives a motion-magnitude field (noise gate 0.04 + trailing-glow decay 0.82 so a quick wave leaves a streak), which **advects a 40 000-particle WebGL2 ping-pong-FBO** aurora (GLSL update pass pushes velocity by the local motion gradient — coarse Lucas-Kanade/Horn-Schunck via neighbour-texel differencing; bloom + additive composite for the glow). Audio = continuous C-major-pentatonic chord-cloud (16 voices, left-of-frame→lower+left-pan / right→higher+right-pan via StereoPanner, motion energy→density+brightness, 7-min root drift, 22s filter LFO, never-silent bed, −6dB/20:1 limiter, master 0.20). Ghost-fallback: 3 Lissajous "ghost hands" inject motion so the galaxy+music are alive with no camera; denied→ghost continues with a `text-rose-300` notice. **No MediaPipe, no WebGPU, no network, no API route** — the safest 06:30 phone read (widely-supported WebGL2). Refs: TouchDesigner optical-flow ("the machine perceives *that* something moves", derivative.ca 2026) · Memo Akten *Learning to See* · Refik Anadol particle-flow · Jos Stam *Stable Fluids* (advection). Ambition 2/5 (#2 ≥3-subsystems + #3 named refs). **Why it lost:** WebGL2 (3× recent) + optical flow (already in `221`/`295`) are the least-diverse/novel of the three, and "wave→light streams" is the least surprising vs. 444's gather/scatter-a-galaxy. **Why resurrect:** it is the most robust/reliable kids motion piece the lab has — ship it when a cycle wants maximum delivery reliability or when WebGPU/camera-model risk is unacceptable. **Next-cycle deepening:** try a true GPU optical-flow pass (Lucas-Kanade in a fragment shader) and a directional (not just magnitude) field so strokes have flow direction.

### `446-kids-star-swarm` — your whole BODY becomes a constellation: wave your arms and swarms of glowing singing stars stream off your hands, head and feet *(banked sibling — the next kids body-spatial ship, essentially ready)*
**MediaPipe PoseLandmarker** (lite, CDN-loaded) tracks 11 joints (wrists/elbows/shoulders/hips/ankles/head); each joint is an attractor/emitter for a shared ~29 000-star **three.js `THREE.Points`** cloud (AdditiveBlending, per-vertex color, `DynamicDrawUsage`, orthographic camera so screen-x maps directly). A moving joint surges its swarm bright and streams it outward; at rest stars orbit and drift back. Audio = always-on D-pentatonic pad (root/fifth/octave drifting D2→E2→F#2→A2 over ~6min) + per-joint sustained voices (head=high B3–D5, feet=low D2–E2 — BANDIMAL "big move=big low sound"), StereoPanner by joint screen-x, ~4-min filter LFO, −6dB/20:1 limiter, master ≤0.25, 13-min goodnight fade. Ghost-dancer fallback (a procedurally-dancing stick figure of attractors) runs the full swarm+audio with no camera / if the model fails within 4s; `text-rose-300` notice. **No API route** (only the MediaPipe CDN). Refs: MediaPipe PoseLandmarker / BlazePose (Bazarevsky CVPR 2020) · MediaPipe→NVIDIA-Flex interactive-particle (derivative.ca 2025–26) · Memo Akten/Refik Anadol body-particle flow · BANDIMAL (Yatatoy 2017). Ambition 2/5 (#2 + #3). **Why it lost:** three.js OUTPUT is 3× recent (less diverse than 444's WebGPU 0×) and PoseLandmarker-for-kids was just used (`419`); the whole-body register is lovely but less "massively bigger" than the 60k GPU galaxy. **Why resurrect:** the purest whole-body embodied kids instrument — every joint simultaneously an emitter AND a voice; ship it on a kids cycle when three.js isn't over-represented. **Next-cycle deepening:** add gentle constellation-line connectors between a child's own joints (legible "you are the constellation") and a phone-speaker check on the per-joint StereoPanner spread.

Also rest the camera one cycle (419/423/440 were all embodied) so the body-spatial novelty reads as fresh, not as more camera. **Ambition 2/5** (#2 ≥3-subsystems · #3 named-ref). Build-reviewed, zero new deps, no API route.

### `443-the-vanishing` — a 10-voice ensemble that can only survive your full attention: every time you look away (switch tabs / blur the window / mouse leaves the page / 20s idle) it **permanently loses one voice it can never get back**, moving only toward silence *(banked sibling — the purest "go-weird" answer to the jury's sharpest critique; the next adult "weird" ship, essentially ready as-is)*
- **Tags** (a genuinely never-used input): INPUT = **visitor attention / presence** (`document.visibilitychange` + `window` blur/focus + `pointerleave` + ~20s idle timer — never used as an instrument in any prior lab piece) · OUTPUT = **spare clinical Canvas2D** (luminous ring-glyphs that breathe with amplitude; on death a 3.5s fracture animation + a permanent dashed-ring "scar" with an ×; a fading center pulse + connection web) · TECHNIQUE = **irreversible state-erosion / entropy** (kill order ornament→roots: overtone → choirs → shimmer → arp → pulse → shadow → octave → fifth → ground; survivors grow more exposed) · VIBE = conceptual / critical / melancholy (the deliberate break from the consonant-resolving rut; expressive 12-TET C-minor, never JI-as-subject).
- **Opening state plays the full ensemble** so a passive 06:30 glance hears the complete piece and the concept reveals itself the instant they switch tabs; "Begin again" fully resets (a new session — the loss was real, but you can start over). Built files: `page.tsx` (550), `audio.ts` (622), `scene.ts` (340). **Refs:** Alvin Lucier *I Am Sitting in a Room* (1969, erosion-as-process) · Ryoji Ikeda (clinical data aesthetics) · Tehching Hsieh (duration art that costs real time) · John Cage (attention IS the score).
- **Why it didn't win:** only because `441` answers a *direct standing Karel directive* (AI-image-in-AV) and the recent window already broke the consonance rut (422/432/437); on pure surprise this was the closest contender. **Resurrect first** for the next adult cycle that wants the jury-#2 "refuses-to-resolve" piece. **Ambition 2/5** (#2 attention-input + erosion engine + Canvas2D scar-renderer + 4 event-listener subsystems · #3 named conceptual lineage). Build-reviewed, zero deps, no API route, no network.

### `438-kids-star-window` — hold the phone up like a window, turn/tilt to look around a 3D night sky, AIM at a glowing star-creature to wake it so it sings from where it sits *(banked sibling — the most immediate / kid-legible take; the cleanest cycle-2 simplification of `440`)*
The most *forgiving* and instantly-rewarding of the three: sweep your aim past a star and it swells-and-sings (no dwell, no scoop — pure immediate cause-effect, the legibility logic that won `419`/`423`/`429`). three.js, 20 Fibonacci-lattice star-creatures + ~1400 background stars, **quaternion-slerp** gyro magic-window (more frame-correct than `440`'s yaw/pitch), per-star `StereoPannerNode` pan + proximity gain, D-major-pentatonic chords, figure-8 auto-pan tour, DynamicsCompressor limiter, iOS `requestPermission` in the Begin tap, drag fallback. Ambition 2/5 (#2 ≥3-subsystems + #3 magic-window/3-DoF-spatial-audio). **Resurrect** if a future kids cycle wants the *immediate* version for the youngest players (3-year-olds who can't yet hold a steady dwell), or as a polish/companion to `440`. Build-reviewed, zero new deps, no API route.

Hand-written GLSL 300 es vertex+fragment pipeline (W3C Rz·Rx·Ry orientation → `u_view` matrix; 3-layer additive Gaussian glow: core + halo + bloom + aim sparkle-ring; `gl.ONE,gl.ONE` blending; ~500-point starfield + 20 singing stars). Same gyro magic-window + `StereoPannerNode` spatial audio + pentatonic + auto-pan + iOS permission + drag fallback as `438`, but a deeper/more-nebula atmospheric look from the raw shaders. Ambition 2/5 (#2 + #3). **Resurrect** when the kids lane wants raw-WebGL2 renderer diversity and a moodier night than three.js sprites give; slightly higher build risk (shader compile / OEM gyro-frame quirks). Build-reviewed (tsc + ESLint clean), zero deps, no API route.

The grep audit this fire killed three would-be #1 claims (fluid, shadow, hand-tracking all already in the lab — the lab is technique-saturated at 432 prototypes), so all three briefs cleared the floor honestly at **2/5** (#2 ≥3-subsystems + #3 named-ref) and competed on register-breadth + 06:30 phone read. Winner shipped = `433-kids-fluid-paint` (real Stam Navier-Stokes GPU fluid finger-paint, WebGL2 — liquid-dreamy, rides loved `84-wave-fluid`❤️ + the most-loved paint lineage). Both siblings are complete, **build-reviewed** real implementations (tsc + ESLint clean — 434: 778+64 lines, 435: 1089 lines incl. real HandLandmarker; folder-isolated); folders removed per the no-half-built-folders rule, banked here as ready-to-resurrect TEXT. **Renderer note for revival: Canvas2D is over-represented and three.js/WebGL2 are the cleaner kids lanes; renumber on revival, max folder now 433.**)

### `434-kids-lantern-sky` — tap to launch glowing paper lanterns into a 3D night sky you LOOK AROUND inside, each singing from its own place in true HRTF binaural space *(banked sibling — the cosmic-wonder / 3D-spatial register; the headphone piece)*
- **Tags** (clean dodge of all bans): INPUT = **touch + device-gyro** (tilt to look around; drag fallback) · OUTPUT = **three.js** (the freshest renderer lane, 1× recent) · TECHNIQUE = **true 3D HRTF spatial audio** (`PannerNode` `panningModel:"HRTF"` + `AudioListener` orientation re-derived from the camera quaternion **every frame**, so turning your head genuinely moves the choir around you) · VIBE = cosmic/calm/wonder.
- **The mechanic:** night-sky dome (350-point starfield, additive moon halo, warm horizon torus). Camera **rotates only, no translation** (non-nauseating). Tap → a warm glowing lantern (cylinder body + additive glow-sphere) rises with sinusoidal sway, singing a sustained slow-attack voice (G-major-pentatonic, color=pitch, any combination consonant) positioned at its 3D world coords via its own `PannerNode`; up to ~10–12 coexist, old ones fade high up. Always-on open-fifth ambient pad; `DynamicsCompressor` brick-wall limiter (−8 dB / 16:1) → master 0.22 (safe ears). iOS: `AudioContext` + `DeviceOrientationEvent.requestPermission()` inside the Begin tap; auto-demo launches a lantern every ~1.5 s + slow-pans the camera hands-free after 1.8 s. Full three.js disposal + listener teardown. Built files: `page.tsx` (778), `README.md` (64). **Refs:** Web Audio `PannerNode` HRTF / Google Resonance Audio lineage · sky-lantern / Yi Peng (Khom Loi) festival image.
- **Why it didn't win:** the most genuinely *spatial* of the three and a clean three.js dodge, BUT its core payoff — the **binaural HRTF field** — is **largely lost on phone speakers** at the 06:30 review (the README admits this; needs headphones), and it's the closest neighbour to an existing piece (`238-kids-tilt-world` already does tilt + 3D + panned audio; the differentiator here is HRTF-vs-StereoPanner, a real but headphone-dependent upgrade). **Revive:** the **headphone/installation** spatial piece, or as the explicit cycle-2 over `238` (the HRTF-tracks-camera engine IS the upgrade); pair with a visual payoff that also reads on phone speakers (e.g. lanterns visibly swirl past as you turn) so it isn't headphone-gated. Reconstructable from this spec. **Ambition 2/5** (#2 ≥3-subsystems · #3 named).

### `435-kids-air-ribbon` — paint music in the AIR: wave a hand at the camera, a glowing light-ribbon trails your fingertip and SINGS a sustained song (friendly theremin) *(banked sibling — the magical-flowing / sustained-melodic-camera register; the inverse of the recent face/pose BEATS)*
- **Tags:** INPUT = **camera / MediaPipe HandLandmarker** (index-fingertip, analysis-only — never recorded/stored/uploaded) · OUTPUT = **Canvas2D** (additive-glow trailing ribbon + sparkle particles + dim mirrored backdrop) · TECHNIQUE = **hand-landmark → continuous air-theremin** (fingertip Y → pitch snapped to C-major-pentatonic with 60 ms `setTargetAtTime` portamento GLIDE — sustained/singing, NOT discrete hits; X → `StereoPannerNode`; hand openness → timbre/volume) · VIBE = magical/flowing/dreamy.
- **The mechanic:** HandLandmarker via `webpackIgnore` CDN dynamic import (zero npm dep), `detectForVideo`, fingertip drives a fading glow-ribbon (hue=pitch, violet→cyan) with a bright Gaussian head + gravity sparkles. 3-osc voice (triangle + sine octave + sine fifth) → voice gain → panner → `DynamicsCompressor` (−8 dB / 16:1) → master 0.22; always-on C3 ambient pad (never silent). **Three-tier always-demoable fallback** (the strength): camera denied / CDN fail → scripted figure-8 "ghost hand" melody loop immediately; no hand within 2.5 s → ghost auto-kicks alongside the live feed; pointer/drag fallback always playable on a laptop. Full camera + landmarker + audio teardown. Built files: `page.tsx` (604), `audio.ts` (220), `hand.ts` (142), `README.md` (123). **Refs:** Léon Theremin (1928) gestural-pitch instrument · Myron Krueger *Videoplace* (1975) camera-as-instrument · MediaPipe HandLandmarker (Google).

Reconstructable from this spec. **Ambition 2/5** (#2 ≥3-subsystems · #3 named).

## Banked from Cycle 361 (WIDE adult fire — *three genuinely unrelated go-weird directions*, one per renderer lane, against the jury's "the lab learned to go deep and forgot how to go weird." Winner shipped = `432-three-body` (N-body gravity sim → sound, Canvas2D — grep-verified lab-first technique, the most elegant "refuses to resolve"). Both siblings are complete, **build-reviewed** real implementations (tsc + ESLint clean, ~670/841 lines, folder-isolated); folders removed per the no-half-built-folders rule, banked here as ready-to-resurrect TEXT. Both are strong ADULT resurrections — they deliberately diversify OUTPUT (three.js, raw-WebGL2) and INPUT (external-data, keyboard) away from the winner.)

### `430-wiki-pulse` — the LIVE Wikimedia recent-changes firehose sonified: every edit on Earth, right now, bots vs humans *(banked sibling — the real-world-data + "music about something other than music" take; the freshest data SOURCE the lab has)*
- **Tags** (clean dodge of all bans): INPUT = **external SSE data** (Wikimedia EventStreams, public/keyless/CORS, client-side `EventSource` — NO api route, no guard) · OUTPUT = **three.js** (rising glowing nodes, additive bloom) · TECHNIQUE = real-time event-stream sonification · VIBE = restless/global/never-resolving.
- **The mechanic:** connects to `https://stream.wikimedia.org/v2/stream/recentchange`, keeps `type==="edit"||"new"`, reads `wiki`/`bot`/`user`/`length.{old,new}` → `delta`. **delta** (log-scaled) drives amplitude/duration/base-freq (big additions bloom deep & swelling, tiny edits soft high ticks; sign changes timbre — deletions glide down with a gritty high-passed breath); **bot vs human → two distinct timbres** (bots dry/mechanical/quantized square blips; humans warm/breathy/resonant triangle-through-moving-LPF + noise breath — "you can hear the machines vs the people," the emotional core); **wiki/language → stereo pan** (west→east spread + micro-detune); always-on detuned-sine bed; master `DynamicsCompressor` brick-wall limiter + voice cap. Each edit also spawns a rising three.js sphere (size = log|delta|, humans warm / bots cool, x = pan). **Reliability:** a single Begin gesture creates/resumes the AudioContext (iOS); a **deterministic seeded synthetic firehose** drives the EXACT same pipeline if no live event arrives within ~2.5s (amber "offline demo" notice → green "● live" when real events flow), so it always comes alive hands-free in ~2s. Built files: `page.tsx` (~670), `README.md`. **Refs:** **Hatnote *Listen to Wikipedia*** (LaPorte & Hashemi, 2013 — the canonical Wikipedia-edit sonification; this is a darker, non-consonant, three.js take) + **Wikimedia EventStreams**.
- **Why it didn't win:** the highest-*concept* surprise of the slate and the most direct answer to the jury's #4 ("zero real-world-data sonification in 15 cycles — sonify an external API, it breaks the mic rut AND the consonance rut in one fire"), BUT data-sonification is **explicitly NOT a lab-first** (`314-solar-wind` / `337`/`418-seismic` already did it — corrected IDEAS §359), so it tops out at **ambition 2/5** (#2 ≥3-subsystems: EventSource ingest + Web-Audio synth + three.js render · #3 named-ref) where `432` clears 3/5 with a grep-verified #1. **Revive:** the obvious next **real-world-data ship** — it's the freshest data SOURCE the lab has (not seismic/solar) and breaks the mic+consonance ruts together; **fix-first:** add per-region timbre color (currently language is pan-only) and orchestrate dense bursts instead of thinning at the voice cap. Reconstructable from this spec.

### `431-test-signal` — clinical Ikeda / Alva-Noto GLITCH-WALL instrument, raw-WebGL2 data-card *(banked sibling — the purest clinical refuse-to-resolve swing; jury #2)*
- **Tags:** INPUT = **keyboard/touch** (toggle + sculpt channels) · OUTPUT = **raw WebGL2** (hand-written GLSL monochrome test-card) · TECHNIQUE = sine-sweep + look-ahead impulse-grid + bit-crush glitch (no scale) · VIBE = clinical/abrasive/unresolved.
- **The mechanic:** three independent signal channels, nothing pitched to a scale. **SWEEP** (key 1) — a continuous log-glide sine (top capped 9 kHz, amplitude rolled off as freq climbs) + gliding sub. **IMPULSE** (key 2) — band-passed filtered-noise clicks scheduled with a Web-Audio **look-ahead scheduler** (Chris Wilson "Two Clocks", 25ms tick / 100ms ahead), `RATE`/`DENS` controls, no pitch. **GLITCH** (key 3) — bit-crush via quantizing `WaveShaperNode` + sample-hold playbackRate + stutter gate, `RATE`/`BRGHT`. All → master gain → brick-wall `DynamicsCompressor` (−6 dB / ratio 20) → analyser → out. Raw-WebGL2 GLSL data-card: sweep-tracking scan line, density-shifting barcode field, impulse-strobed columns (localized + soft, **no full-screen strobe**), glitch displacement/block-noise, a live oscilloscope trace of the master output; `prefers-reduced-motion` → `u_calm` dampens everything. **Reliability:** Begin creates/resumes the ctx in-gesture (iOS), then auto-sequences SWEEP→IMPULSE→GLITCH (~0.3/2.1/4.2s) hands-free, then fully interactive (cards ≥44px + keys). Built files: `page.tsx` (~841), `README.md`. **Refs:** **Ryoji Ikeda** (*test pattern* / *dataplex* / *data.matrix*) + **Alva Noto / Carsten Nicolai** (*Xerrox*, clicks-and-glitch).
- **Why it didn't win:** a genuine, safety-serious commitment to the Ikeda/Alva-Noto idiom (un-snapped glide, real look-ahead scheduler, dependency-free bit-crush, GLSL data-card driven by live audio state) — but **ambition 2/5** (#2 + #3; refuse-to-resolve is the *framing*, not a novel technique) and the lab already swung at refuse-to-resolve twice recently (`357-shatter`, `422`), so it's the second piece at the same mandate rather than a new capability. **Revive:** ship next time the adult lane wants the *pure clinical* register on its own renderer (raw-WebGL2, distinct from 357's DOM/CSS and 422's grid); **deepen-first:** make the impulse grid a programmable pattern sequencer and the glitch channel player-triggerable as discrete one-shots (both self-flagged) to push it from "ambient generator" to "instrument." Reconstructable from this spec.

## Banked from Cycle 360 (DEEP kids fire — *finger-paint where the brush's TEXTURE is its SOUND, noise not notes*. Winner shipped = `429-kids-texture-paint-tap` (impulse+resonator foley, Canvas2D, discrete crunch/pop/tap/scratch/splash — the lab's first pure-NOISE kids instrument by touch, jury #1 for kids). Both siblings are complete, **build-reviewed** real implementations (~900–1300 lines, folder-isolated, ESLint-discipline followed); folders removed per the no-half-built-folders rule, banked here as ready-to-resurrect TEXT. Shared thesis: every kids sound toy maps to pentatonic PITCH; these make the whole palette NOISE/TIMBRE. **Renderer note for revival: Canvas2D is now 4× in the trailing window → 428 (WebGL2) is the cleaner lane; renumber on revival, max folder now 429.**)

### `428-kids-texture-paint-morph` — subtractive noise-morph on a raw-WebGL2 painterly canvas *(banked sibling — the BEAUTIFUL take + the obvious cycle-2 deepening)*
- **Concept:** same toy as 429 (paint a texture, hear it) but the sound is **continuous subtractive synthesis** — an always-running noise bed (white + brown) sculpted live by resonant biquad filters: finger X → filter centre/brightness, Y → Q/resonance, drag-speed → energy. Textures: 🌬️ Wind (bandpass-swept brown noise) · 💧 Water (resonant bubbling) · 🏜️ Sand (narrow high hiss) · 🔥 Fire (low noise + irregular crackle bursts) · ⚡ Fizz (wide bright noise + fast tremolo).
- **Render:** **raw WebGL2** (hand-rolled, no three.js) — 3 shader programs + **ping-pong FBO accumulation** so strokes glow and accrete; per-texture procedural appearance (fbm gradients, ripple rings, grain stipple, turbulent fire, sparkle hash). The most visually striking candidate of the fire — a glowing accreting paint canvas, the strongest pull on Karel's loved-paint lineage.
- **Why it didn't win:** continuous morph is less kid-legible than 429's discrete one-dab-one-sound; it uses 3 sub-audible LFO oscillators (marginally less "pure noise" for a zero-pitch-thesis piece); and the 3-program FBO pipeline is the highest perf/build risk on low-end phones.
- **Resurrect as:** the **cycle-2 visual deepening** — port 429's discrete foley *voices* onto 428's WebGL2 painterly accumulation canvas → a noise-paint that both *crunches satisfyingly* AND *looks gorgeous*. Best kids renderer move while Canvas2D is over-represented. Refs: Köhler bouba/kiki 1929 · Farnell *Designing Sound* 2010 · Kandinsky synesthesia. Touch IN · raw-WebGL2 OUT · subtractive-noise TECH · kids non-tuned VIBE.

### `427-kids-texture-paint-grains` — granular-noise foley brush, Canvas2D *(banked sibling — the granular-texture take)*
- **Concept:** same toy; sound = **granular synthesis of NOISE** via a Web-Audio look-ahead grain scheduler. While the finger moves, a stream of tiny windowed filtered-noise grains plays; drag speed + texture control grain **density / size / brightness / spread**: Sand = dense tiny dry grains · Silk = sparse long soft low-passed grains · Sparkle = very short bright high grains · Rumble = larger low-passed grains · Fizz = rapid medium grains. 0 oscillators (genuinely pitch-less). Canvas2D strokes.
- **Why it didn't win:** solid but not the standout on any axis — granular-continuous like 428 but less visually rich; Canvas2D like 429 but less legible than discrete events.
- **Resurrect as:** the granular-texture variant, or fold its grain engine into 428's WebGL2 canvas as a "soft/continuous" mode alongside 429's discrete "dab" voices. Refs: Curtis Roads *Microsound* 2001 (granular) · Farnell 2010 · Köhler 1929. Touch IN · Canvas2D OUT · granular-noise TECH · kids non-tuned VIBE.

## Banked from Cycle 359 (WIDE adult fire — *three ways to leave the consonant monastery*, three emotional registers, one per clean lane. Winner shipped = `426-euclid-engine` (adult Euclidean polyrhythm + Reich phasing on **three.js** — the freshest renderer lane, 0× recent — pure percussion / zero tuning, jury #1 for adults). Both siblings are complete, **build-reviewed** (substantial real implementations, ~900–1100 lines each; folder-isolated, ESLint-discipline followed) demoable builds — folders removed per the no-half-built-folders rule, banked here as ready-to-resurrect TEXT. Shared thesis (jury 2026-06-08 #1+#2): ~13/15 recent pieces resolve to calm consonance; these three each refuse it differently. **Also corrected this fire (RESEARCH §359): real-world-data sonification is NOT a lab-first and is saturated** — `314-solar-wind` already fetches live NOAA SWPC, so the banked `421-solar-drone` would DUPLICATE it; and `9-reaction-diffusion` already exists. Don't revive those as "firsts.")

### `424-welcome-erosion` — Karel's REAL Welcome Home piano, long-form 5-min generative EROSION (a piece about forgetting) *(banked sibling — the intimate register + the vehicle for Karel's actual music)*
- **Question:** "What if a warm intimate piano phrase were played once, then over five minutes slowly eroded through self-feedback + granular decay until it can never return home?"
- **Tags** (clean dodge of all bans): INPUT = **audio-file** (Karel's tracks via the existing `/api/audio/{id}` door + a synth-phrase fallback; NO mic/camera) · OUTPUT = Canvas2D (memory-strip + grain motes + fraying waveform) · TECHNIQUE = **long-form stateful granular/spectral erosion** (monotonic, one-way) · VIBE = intimate/decaying/unresolved.
- **The mechanic:** a pure `computeErosion(elapsed)` drives a monotonic trajectory over ~5 min — grain density 12→1.5/s, grain dur 80→850 ms, pitch spread 0.05→~7 st, position jitter 1%→90%, amplitude 100%→20%, reverb send 28%→95%, LPF 7 kHz→380 Hz — so phrases thin, smear, detune and fragment into a resonant wash that never cadences back. Web-Audio look-ahead grain scheduler (25 ms tick / 120 ms ahead), `DynamicsCompressor` brick-wall limiter, 5-phase HUD label (intact→fraying→dissolving→eroding→forgotten) + a falling "memory remaining" bar. Auto-erodes the synth phrase ~2 s after Begin (hands-free). Built files: `page.tsx` (~1105), `README.md`. **Refs:** William Basinski *Disintegration Loops* · Alvin Lucier *I Am Sitting in a Room* · Roads *Microsound*.
- **Why it didn't win:** the strongest fit for Karel's STANDING #1 directive (use his real music) and the most poignant, but (a) it defaults to a **synth** phrase at 06:30 unless Karel pastes a recording ID, so it doesn't actually *showcase* his music hands-free; (b) Canvas2D is 2× in the last-10 (allowed but not the freshest lane the way 426's three.js is); (c) the erosion is granular-adjacent to 422's shatter (distinct — 5-min stateful vs 10-s freeze — but the same neighbourhood). **Revive:** the ship that finally puts a real Welcome-Home track on screen (hand it a known recording ID), or fold its erosion engine onto 422's material. Reconstructable from this spec. **Ambition 2/5** (#2 ≥3-subsystems · #3 named).

### `425-test-signal` — clinical Ikeda / Alva-Noto GLITCH-WALL, WebGL2 test-card *(banked sibling — the purest *refuse-to-resolve* / "go weird" swing; jury #2)*
- **Question:** "What does a piece that was NEVER consonant sound like — a self-mutating wall of sine-grids, impulse clicks and band-noise in Ikeda's cold register?"
- **Tags:** INPUT = **generative** (no device; optional density/intensity sliders) · OUTPUT = **raw WebGL2** scanline/datamosh test-card · TECHNIQUE = **glitch-DSP** (clocked impulse trains + inharmonic sine pings + filtered-noise bursts + a mutation engine that re-rolls the grid) · VIBE = clinical/abrasive/monochrome/unresolved.
- **The mechanic:** a look-ahead scheduler (25 ms / 100 ms ahead) across 4 layers — HF sine pings at 8 mutually-inharmonic freqs (6317–13711 Hz), 1–7 ms impulse clicks, 5 sub pulses at irrational ratios (37.13–91.53 Hz, no implied root), and an LFO-swept band-noise — with a mutation event every 5–12 s re-randomizing densities/BPF within clinical bounds so it permanently drifts and never locks. `DynamicsCompressor` brick-wall (−8 dB, 20:1). Auto-starts ~2 s after load (with a "TAP TO START" overlay if the AudioContext is gesture-gated); WebGL2 fails → DOM fallback meter. Built files: `page.tsx` (~908), `README.md`. **Refs:** Ryoji Ikeda *test pattern* / *datamatics* · Carsten Nicolai / Alva Noto. **This finally realizes the §356 banked "test-pattern" Ikeda counter-piece idea.**
- **Why it didn't win:** the purest answer to the jury's #2 (abrasive / never-consonant — the boldest "go weird"), but (a) a wall of 6–14 kHz pings + sharp clicks is a real **06:30-on-phone-speakers fatigue risk** (could read as "annoying" not "compelling" at the review moment); (b) WebGL2 was just used by 423 (1×), where 426's three.js is the cleaner renderer dodge; (c) builder-flagged node-churn at max density on low-end mobile. **Revive:** ship on a fire where a harsh-HF clinical piece fits the review context, or as the desktop/headphone Ikeda reference; pair the mutation engine with a non-mic input. Reconstructable from this spec. **Ambition 2/5** (#2 ≥3-subsystems · #3 named).

## Banked from Cycle 357 (WIDE adult fire — three *refuse-to-resolve* explorers, one per clean output lane after the diversity audit banned BOTH Canvas2D (5×) and SVG (4×) on top of the jury's mic/Kuramoto/just-intonation bans, leaving only WebGPU / DOM-CSS / audio-only. Winner shipped = `422-shatter-piano` (DOM/CSS granular spectral-freeze that takes a consonant phrase and denies its resolution). Both siblings are complete, **build-verified** (tsc + ESLint clean, project-wide tsc 0 errors) demoable builds — folders removed per the no-half-built-folders rule, banked here as ready-to-resurrect TEXT. Shared thesis (jury 2026-06-08 #2): ~13 of 15 recent pieces resolve to calm consonance and there is no Ikeda/Akten counter-piece — all three of these *refuse to resolve*. Also logged this fire: **WebGPU compute is NOT a lab-first** — it already shipped in `16-particle-life-gpu`/`55-webgpu-audio-fx`/`130-tsl-particle-compute`❤️; the jury/STATE "biggest unclaimed first" framing was wrong (see RESEARCH §357).)

### `420-slime-static` — WebGPU compute physarum slime-mold, sonified as an abrasive non-resolving ROAR *(banked sibling — the biggest GPU swing)*
- **Question:** "What if a living slime-mold network simulated entirely on the GPU were not calm and beautiful but a roaring, ever-shifting wall of sound that refuses to resolve?"
- **Tags** (clean dodge of all bans): INPUT = generative (no mic/camera) · OUTPUT = **WebGPU** (3 `@compute` WGSL pipelines + render pass; CPU/Canvas2D fallback) · TECHNIQUE = physarum agent sim (sense→rotate→move→deposit→diffuse/decay) · VIBE = abrasive/industrial/non-resolving (Ikeda/Akten energy).
- **The mechanic:** 262,144 agents in a storage buffer; per-frame agent-step (`atomicAdd` pheromone deposit) → diffuse+decay ping-pong on a 1024² trail → a non-blocking async readback (~12 Hz, staging buffer, never stalls the GPU) extracts density / agitation (variance) / center-of-mass-pan. Those drive 5 sawtooth oscillators at **prime-ratio inharmonic frequencies** (83.2/137.7/211.3/307.1/431.9 Hz), each ring-modulated by an irrational-ratio LFO, + a Q-swept bandpass noise bed + a stochastic 3–8-bit bitcrush waveshaper + irregular burst gating → DynamicsCompressor brick-wall limiter. Nothing ever tunes to a scale or settles. Built files: `page.tsx` (~1178 lines, all self-contained), `README.md`.
- **Why it didn't win:** the flashiest technical swing, but (a) **WebGPU compute is no longer a lab-first** (corrected this fire), so the headline ambition deflated; (b) its GPU path most likely shows the smaller CPU/Canvas2D fallback on Karel's 06:30 review device; (c) physarum is an **emergence-sim** — the jury flagged the emergence-sim *reflex* (#6). **Revive:** the lab's reference abrasive-GPU piece, or a desktop-WebGPU-confirmed fire; reuse its physarum compute as a sonification substrate for other inputs. Reconstructable from this spec.

### `421-solar-drone` — live NOAA solar-wind sonification, audio-only / off-screen *(banked sibling — the real-world-data + off-screen take; the natural data-lane cycle-2 to 418-seismic-pulse)*
- **Question:** "What does the live solar wind hitting Earth's magnetosphere sound like — as an eyes-closed drone that, because the data never stops or repeats, can never resolve?"
- **Tags:** INPUT = **external data API** (NOAA SWPC, public/keyless/CORS) · OUTPUT = **audio-only / minimal-DOM** (near-black + breathing dot + big numeric readouts; the off-screen lane) · TECHNIQUE = real-world-data audification (raw → synth params, no scale) · VIBE = cosmic/vast/unresolved.
- **The mechanic:** fetches plasma-1-day (speed/density/temp) + mag-1-day (Bz/Bt) + planetary-K-index JSON (5s AbortController, falls back to a deterministic 144-row G1–G2-storm offline sample with an amber notice). Walks the 24h series in ~75s, looping but live-different each session. **Speed→raw-Hz fundamental** (no scale), density→partial richness + noise, temperature→filter brightness, **Bz(±)→detuning/beat-width that swings with magnetic polarity**, Kp→agitation + crackle-burst rate. Partials at 1×/2.0×/3.07×/4.13× (irrational) so no two ever land on a shared harmonic node; 1/23 Hz irrational stereo LFO; DynamicsCompressor limiter. Built files: `page.tsx` (~436), `synth.ts` (~357), `data.ts` (~111), `sample.ts` (~154), `README.md`. **Refs:** NOAA SWPC DSCOVR/ACE → IMAP I-ALiRT 2026 transition (RESEARCH §357, current this month) · NASA space-weather sonification heritage (R. Alexander/Goddard) · Ikeda *data-cosm*.
- **Why it didn't win:** the strongest **jury-data-lane** fit (extends the loved 418 real-data lane + off-screen + refuses to resolve), but (a) a near-black audio-only screen is the riskiest 06:30 read (the breathing dot + readouts hedge it, but `422`'s legible phrase→denied arc reads instantly), and (b) real-world-data is no longer a lab-first (418 claimed it). **Revive:** the obvious **cycle-2 of the real-world-data lane** (pair with 418-seismic — earth tremor + sky weather), or fold a minimal aurora-curtain visual onto it. Reconstructable from this spec.
