## Banked §384 (cycle-384 kids DEEP — non-winner siblings of the explorable harmony-tension Sound-Map; winner shipped: `493-kids-feeling-forest`, camera/body navigation. Folders removed after curation; renumber on revival, max committed folder now 493.):
*All three implemented the SAME concept — a continuous 2-D harmony-tension MAP (IDW-blended chord voicings, dissonance→consonance spatial gradient, no scale-snapping, no pre-baked cadence; binds **FXplorer**, arXiv 2606.08286, NIME 2026, 2026-06-09) — differing only in the navigation input. The winner uses BODY/camera (the jury's explicitly-starved depth-camera input). These two are complete, build-reviewed implementations.*

### 494-kids-weather-walk — the DRAG baseline (RESURRECT only if a touch cycle is wanted)
Drag a glowing cloud-spirit across a top-down weather world: storm corner (Bb2 + m2 + tritone + m9, heavy detune, audible beating, 7.8 Hz tremolo, 310 Hz LP) → sunshine corner (C4 open fifth/sus2, 5400 Hz, no tremolo), with overcast/foggy/bittersweet (simultaneous minor+major third) middles. 5 anchors, IDW power-2 blend, Lissajous auto-demo after 3 s, Canvas2D field with lightning flicker in the storm quadrant. Clean (810 lines, 0 ts/0 eslint). **Why it lost:** its INPUT is **touch/drag** — the exact over-represented tag the cycle-384 diversity audit BANS (touch ~5× in last 10 shipped). It's the reliable baseline, not a rut-breaker. **Resurrect:** as the guaranteed-working fallback skin folded into 493, or on a future cycle where touch is no longer over-represented. Tags: touch / Canvas2D / harmony-map-IDW / weather-tension. Ref: FXplorer (2026).

### 495-kids-singing-valley — VOICE as the 2-D cursor (STRONG bank — high surprise, the real next lab-first)
SING to navigate: hand-written YIN-style pitch detector (`pitch.ts`, de Cheveigné & Kawahara 2002) maps **pitch → vertical** (low voice = thorny cave at the bottom, high voice = sunlit meadow at top) and **loudness/RMS → horizontal** (soft = misty left, loud = open right). The child's voice walks a glowing creature through the harmony-tension valley (5 anchors: minor-2nd+tritone cave → open-fifth meadow, with quartal/whole-tone/sus4 middles). Mic is analysis-only (separate context, never routed to output → zero feedback); mic-denied → `text-rose-300` notice + drag fallback + 17 s auto-demo. Canvas2D, 655 lines, 0 ts/0 eslint. **Why it lost narrowly:** voice→XY is the most *surprising* controller of the three, but the jury #3 explicitly named **depth-camera (0×)** as THE starved input to pick this cycle, and camera kids pieces are loved (104❤️/101❤️/234❤️) — so 493's body-camera navigation is the more on-target diversity + love answer. Mic was also used recently (468-bottle-flute). **Resurrect:** ship next kids voice cycle, OR push it past "one voice = one cursor" into the real next lab-first — **two children landing a consonant interval together** (genuine polyphonic pitch detection), or "lingering in the cave for >5 s slowly warms it" (tension-resolving-through-presence, not movement). Tags: voice/mic / Canvas2D / harmony-map-IDW + YIN-pitch / sung-tension-navigation. Refs: FXplorer (2026); de Cheveigné & Kawahara, YIN, JASA 2002.

## Banked §385 (cycle-385 adult DEEP — non-winner siblings of the **Machine Ensemble / Voyager** concept; winner shipped: `496-voyager-room`. Folders removed after curation; renumber on revival, max committed folder now 496.):
*All three implemented ONE concept — **an ENSEMBLE of autonomous machine improvisers who listen to each other AND to you, which you CONDUCT (you never play notes)** — George Lewis's _Voyager_ lineage, binding **StreamMUSE** (frame-synchronous streaming inference, arXiv 2606.11886, 2026-06-10) as the on-device lock-step generation loop, with a real Plomp–Levelt/Sethares roughness meter as the live tension readout. They differ in the agent-brain + the conducting gesture. These two are complete, build-reviewed implementations — strong cycle-2/3 candidates of the new Machine Ensemble spine.*

### 497-call-ring — a RING-CANON of imitation, conducted by a SPREAD knob (STRONG bank — the spine's canon branch)
Six agents on a circle; each beat the next agent listens ONLY to its upstream neighbour's most-recent short phrase and answers with its own probabilistic **transform** — {transpose-to-consonant-degree, melodic inversion, retrograde, augment/diminish, octave displace}. One primary **SPREAD** slider re-weights the transform distribution + toggles scale-snapping: low spread = a tight near-unison round (motif circulates near-intact, reads Resolved/Settling); high spread = inversion/octave-scatter + chromatic nudges fragment the motif into clashes (Tense/Clashing) before it re-converges. Conduct by **tossing in a seed motif** (inject a 3–5 note contour at a node) + SPREAD + TEMPO + tap-an-agent to freeze (pass-through gap) or mute. Canvas2D: luminous packet arcs node→node, circle-of-fifths node hue, arcs jitter/fragment at high spread. Auto-demo sweeps spread low→high→low. 519 lines, tsc+eslint clean. **Why it lost:** elegant, but the canon/round register overlaps the lab's existing Mirror-Canon (302/305) + tempo-canon (375) threads — slightly less of a leap than 496's "conduct collective roughness." **Resurrect:** spine cycle-2 as the *imitation* branch, or fold its transform palette into 496 so spotlighted agents can develop a tossed-in motif. Tags: seed-motif/conductor INPUT / Canvas2D / multi-agent-ring-imitation TECH / round⇄tension VIBE. Refs: Lewis _Voyager_; StreamMUSE (2606.11886, 2026-06-10).

### 498-swarm-score — EMERGENT chord from a 48-agent CONSENSUS + tritone DISSIDENTS, WebGL2 (STRONG bank — the spine's emergence branch + a starved-renderer)
48 lightweight agents each hold a continuous **opinion** = a real-valued pitch class on the **circle of fifths**; each frame they gossip toward the Gaussian-weighted local average (consensus) + toward the conductor's **attractor** (your drag). The **sounding chord = the emergent weighted consensus** (densest opinion-clusters become chord tones; cluster mass = loudness) — you literally hear the swarm agreeing. **Genuine tension via dissidents:** N dissident agents refuse consensus and steer to a tritone against the live majority, dragging a clash into the chord; add/remove them = the tension knob. Frame-synchronous re-voicing each beat (StreamMUSE). Raw **WebGL2** gravity-well + additive point cloud (circle-of-fifths hue so consensus reads coherent, split swarm reads clashing; dissidents hot rose/amber), **Canvas2D fallback required + present**, full GL dispose. Conduct by drag-to-nudge-attractor + ±dissidents + tempo. Auto-demo wanders the attractor + breathes 2 dissidents in/out. 563 lines, tsc clean. **Why it lost:** conceptually elegant and dodges Canvas2D over-use (WebGL2), but "chord emerges from a swarm" drifts toward the lab's existing a-life register (236-particle-life-song, 410-conchordal-garden) and reads a touch more abstract than 496's legible *named musicians you conduct*. **Resurrect:** spine cycle-2/3 as the *emergence* branch (it's the strongest WebGL2 surface of the three); or extend dissents beyond tritone-only (m2/M7 flavours), or map the attractor drag to a real harmonic-function space. Tags: drag-attractor/conductor INPUT / WebGL2(+Canvas2D fallback) OUTPUT / swarm-consensus-emergent-harmony TECH / dissident-tension VIBE. Refs: Lewis _Voyager_; StreamMUSE (2606.11886, 2026-06-10); Plomp–Levelt 1965 / Sethares 1998.

## Banked §386 (cycle-386 kids WIDE fire — non-winner siblings; winner shipped: `500-kids-aurora-tilt`. Folders removed after curation per orchestration rule; renumber on revival, max committed folder now 500.):
*Three orthogonal kids explorers, each clearing the ambition floor via DIFFERENT tags (none touching the cycle's banned tags: touch INPUT, Canvas2D + three.js OUTPUT, physics-sim/DSP-first/AI/real-piano TECH, warm-resolve VIBE). Each carries genuine player-shaped tension. Both below are complete, build-reviewed real implementations.*

### 499-kids-sky-bells — the REAL sky, singing (STRONG bank — the highest-surprise concept of the fire; ship next ALL-AGES / adult real-world-data cycle)
On Start, fetches **live current weather** for the child's geolocation from **Open-Meteo** (no API key, CORS-enabled; falls back to San Francisco if location denied, and to a synthesized default-weather object if the fetch fails — always sings). Maps the live data to a warm always-on generative soundscape rendered as a glowing **WebGL2** sky field: **temperature → key/register** (cold C-minor-pent/low → warm A–D-major-pent/high), **wind → arpeggio/shimmer density**, **cloud cover → pad lowpass** (clear bright ~2600 Hz → overcast muffled ~900 Hz), **precipitation → soft rain-bell chime rate**, **is_day → gold-day vs indigo-night palette + pad gain**. Re-polls every 60 s so it drifts with the real sky; tap the sky / weather-friend orbs (≥64 px) to add the child's own in-key bell. DynamicsCompressor limiter + 9 kHz lowpass (kids-safe). Files (removed): `page.tsx` (353 lines), `audio.ts`, `weather.ts`, `gl.ts`, `README.md`. Ref: **Gregory Kramer (ed.), *Auditory Display: Sonification, Audification, and Auditory Interfaces* (1994)** + aeolian/wind-harp tradition. **Ambition 2/5** (#2 ≥3 subsystems: live weather API + sonification engine + WebGL2 sky renderer + tap interaction; #3 named ref). Tags: **live-weather-API + tap INPUT (real-world-data — the starved menu category the jury flagged untouched all window) / WebGL2 OUTPUT / data-sonification TECH / living-real-sky VIBE** — fully orthogonal to everything recent. **Why it lost (narrowly):** the highest *surprise* of the fire ("the real sky singing" — a genuine "huh") and it lands a starved menu category, BUT its tension is **observational/passive** (the weather is what it is; tapping adds notes but doesn't *resolve* tension), which is weaker on the jury's #1 "tension resolved **through action**"; and weather-sonification is abstract for a *4-year-old* (it's really an all-ages/adult ambient piece). **Resurrect:** ship next adult/all-ages cycle as the lab's real **real-world-data sonification** entry — it's the strongest seed of the fire. Consider tuning the scale to one of Karel's published journey keys, or adding satellite/ISS-pass or tide data as alternate "what's happening right now" sources. Honest warts: Open-Meteo CORS + geolocation not browser-verified in sandbox (fallback covers both); WebGL2 not GPU-verified.

### 501-kids-hum-garden — bloom by HOLDING STEADY, not by hitting a note (STRONG bank — the freshest *resolution mechanic* of the fire)
Hum into the mic to grow a glowing **inline-SVG** flower; **any pitch is welcome (no wrong notes)** — what blooms it is **steadiness**, not target-hitting. A self-coded **YIN-style CMND pitch detector** (de Cheveigné & Kawahara 2002; RMS gate + clarity gate ≥0.55 + parabolic interp) plus a rolling-variance **pitch-stability** metric: a wobbly/nervous hum keeps the plant a quivering thorny bud on a tense beating cluster (root + detuned maj-2 + min-7); a **steady sustained hum (~1.5–3 s)** glides the harmony open into a warm root+maj3+P5 bloom, petals open, sparkles burst. Pitch sets hue (low = warm amber, high = cool violet), snapped to C-pentatonic. Mic is **analysis-only** (connected to the AnalyserNode only, never to output → zero feedback, never recorded/uploaded). Mic-denied → `text-rose-300` notice + a synthesized **demo-hum state machine** (wobble→steady→rest) that blooms hands-free; the demo also runs on load before the child sings. DynamicsCompressor limiter + 9 kHz lowpass. SVG attributes written imperatively via refs in the rAF loop (no per-frame React re-render). Files (removed): `page.tsx` (826 lines), `README.md` (self-contained). Ref: **de Cheveigné & Kawahara, "YIN," JASA 2002**. **Ambition 2/5** (#2 ≥3 subsystems: mic/pitch+stability detection + warm bloom synth + SVG garden + demo-hum fallback; #3 named ref). Tags: **voice/mic INPUT (fresh, not in last 10) / SVG OUTPUT (0× in last 10 — the cleanest output-dodge of the fire) / pitch-STABILITY-as-resolution TECH (steadiness, not target-hitting — genuinely novel) / tension-resolved-by-calm-steady-presence VIBE**. **Why it lost (narrowly):** the "calm presence blooms it" mechanic is the **freshest resolution idea of the fire** and pedagogically lovely, BUT it's the largest/most-complex build (826 lines), the voice-garden register is somewhat trodden (`322-kids-voice-garden`, `158-hum-paint`), and **YIN on a real 4-year-old's high/breathy voice (200–800 Hz) is unverified** — a genuine demo-reliability risk vs. 500's permission-free auto-demo. **Resurrect:** next kids voice cycle; tune the RMS/clarity gates to child-voice ranges first, swap the SVG sparkle DOM-churn for a pooled-node approach, and consider pushing past one-voice into **two children landing a consonant interval together** (the standing voice lab-first), or **dwell-warms-the-cave** presence-resolution folded into the Sound-Map spine (493). Also a natural home for the banked **ParaBridge prosody-mood** seed (RESEARCH §386) — let the *feeling* in the voice, not just steadiness, shape the bloom.

### 503-atmosphere-front — the planet's weather as roiling volumetric storm-fronts (STRONG bank — DEEP cycle-387 runner-up; the most *literal/cinematic* read of the atmosphere concept)
DEEP sibling of the shipped `502-atmosphere-veil` (Living Earth spine cycle 3). Same spine: one batched **Open-Meteo** request for 12 globe-spanning cities, polled every 75 s; per-city instability = high wind + low/**falling** surface pressure + high cloud; a global tension scalar drives a sustained just-intonation drone that detunes into beating intervals + tension tones (m2/tritone/b9) as the real sky destabilizes and leads back toward open fifths/octaves **only when the data calms** (asymmetric ~6 s glide, no timer); drifting tonal center = long-form memory. **Its distinctive output:** a raw **WebGL2** raymarched, fbm domain-warped **volumetric cloud field** — calm = luminous dawn cirrus, storm = dark roiling cumulonimbus with electric-violet edges and **lightning flashes on sharp tension rises** (a real dramaturgical transient). Files (removed): `page.tsx` (359), `audio.ts`, `weather.ts`, `shader.ts`, `README.md`. Ref: **Éliane Radigue** (sustained beating drones) + raymarched-volumetrics tradition. Ambition 2/5 (#2 ≥3 subsystems: live-data + drone synth + volumetric raymarch + long-form memory; #4 spine cycle 3). Tags: **live-weather-API INPUT / WebGL2-raymarch-volumetric OUTPUT / data-sonification+spectral-tension TECH / sustained-unresolved-atmospheric VIBE.** **Why it lost (narrowly to 502):** the volumetric clouds are the most *evocative* read of "atmosphere," BUT the **diversity audit** decided it — the 2026-06-10 jury flagged raw-WebGL2 as over-represented (4×) while WebGPU is starved (1×), so 502's WebGPU-compute particle field is the cleaner diversity pick AND the more ambitious renderer. **Resurrect:** ship as an alternate "weather mood" or fold the lightning-on-rise transient + volumetric look into a future spine cycle; or pair the two (particles + volumetric) once WebGPU is proven on Karel's device.

### 504-atmosphere-isobar — the weather as a precise isobar/pressure-field instrument (bank — DEEP cycle-387 also-ran; the Ikeda/data-grid read)
DEEP sibling of `502-atmosphere-veil`. Same Living-Earth-cycle-3 spine + Open-Meteo data + sustained-tension drone (open fifths at rest → beating + m2/tritone/b9 as the real field destabilizes, relaxing only when gradients flatten). **Its distinctive idea:** interpolate the 12 city pressures into a smooth **pressure FIELD** (inverse-distance weighting on an equirectangular projection), take the **gradient magnitude** as local tension — **tight isobars = a storm = dissonance**, even parallel isobars = calm — and render marching-squares **contour bands + sheared streamlines** in a clean Ikeda-ish data-grid aesthetic; storm longitude pans the audio. Files (removed): `page.tsx`, `audio.ts`, `weather.ts`, `field.ts`, `README.md`. Ref: **Ryoji Ikeda** (data aesthetics) + **Éliane Radigue** (drone). Ambition 2/5 (#2 ≥3 subsystems; #4 spine cycle 3). Tags: **live-weather-API INPUT / Canvas2D-data-grid OUTPUT / pressure-gradient-as-tension TECH / clinical-geometric VIBE.** **Why it lost:** the pressure-gradient-as-tension mapping is the most *rigorous* of the fire (gradient magnitude is a genuine physical instability proxy), BUT it rendered in **Canvas2D** — a banned over-represented output (4× last-10) — so it failed the diversity gate that 502 cleared cleanly; and the builder noted real worldwide pressure spreads are gentler than the demo, so live tension may read low. **Resurrect:** re-skin the isobar/streamline field as a **WebGL2** line instrument (dodges the Canvas2D ban), and keep the inverse-distance pressure-FIELD + gradient-magnitude tension math — it's the most defensible data→tension mapping the spine has and would make a strong standalone "weather as an instrument you read."

## Banked §388 (cycle-388 kids DEEP fire — "Together" concept, three synchrony approaches; winner shipped: `505-kids-pulse-bridge`. Folders removed after curation per orchestration rule; renumber on revival, max committed folder now 505.):

### 506-kids-orbit-meet — two children SPIN moons into the same swing; resolution by CONTINUOUS motion-phase sync (STRONG bank — the spine's continuous-motion branch)
DEEP sibling of the shipped `505-kids-pulse-bridge`. Same "Together" concept (interpersonal synchrony gates the harmonic resolution — apart = gentle unresolved tension, locked = warm bloom; the resolution is *earned*, never a pre-baked cadence), but the input is **continuous circular drag** instead of discrete taps: each child drags one glowing moon (gold / violet) around a shared centre; each moon is a phase oscillator θ; a **Kuramoto order parameter** r = |½(e^{iθ₁}+e^{iθ₂})| + a velocity-match term, asymmetrically smoothed (fast lock α=0.06, slow drift α=0.02), drives two pentatonic sine voices that voice-lead (`setTargetAtTime`) from a wobbling beating cloud toward an open-fifth/major resolution as the orbits entrain; trails braid into a blooming **mandala** at the centre on lock. Canvas2D; DynamicsCompressor + ≤9 kHz lowpass; ambient pad bed; Pointer-Events `pointerId` multitouch; 32 s auto-demo sweeps phase diff π→0→π hands-free. Files (removed): `page.tsx` (806 lines), `README.md`. Refs: **The Moving Mandala** (Carreras et al., *IJCHP* 2025–26) + **"Finding Our Tempo," TEI 2026**. **Ambition 4/5** (same as winner: #1 interpersonal-synchrony-gates-resolution lab-first + #2 ≥3 subsystems + #3 named refs + #4 "Together" spine). Tags: **two-child-continuous-drag INPUT (fresh) / Canvas2D OUTPUT / Kuramoto-motion-phase-sync TECH / prosocial-tension-resolved-by-spinning-together VIBE.** **Why it lost (narrowly to 505):** the *most beautiful* visual of the fire (braided orbital trails → mandala) and arguably the more embodied/Reggio reading, BUT the builder itself flagged the core risk — **whether a real 4-year-old can sustain a smooth circular drag** (fine-motor-hard; tapping a beat is far more natural for the age) — making 505's discrete tap the more demo-reliable realization of the same concept. **Resurrect:** "Together" spine cycle 2; first replace circular drag with a simpler to-and-fro / back-and-forth swing (the builder's own suggested fix), low-pass the drag velocity to de-noise the phase estimate, and consider it as the **older-kids / all-ages** member of the spine where sustained circular motion is achievable. The mandala-bloom renderer is worth keeping verbatim.

### 507-kids-hum-meet — hum to MEET a drifting partner-tone and HOLD together; resolution by vocal-pitch-lock sync, in SVG (bank — the spine's voice branch + the cleanest output-dodge)
DEEP sibling of `505-kids-pulse-bridge`. Same "Together" concept via **vocal synchrony**: a synthesized "ghost friend" orb hums a slow C-pentatonic tone that drifts between pitches; the child hums back and a self-coded **child-voice-tuned YIN** (de Cheveigné & Kawahara 2002 — difference fn → CMNDF → 0.12 abs-threshold → parabolic interp; RMS gate 0.008, clarity gate <0.5, 180–900 Hz range, 2048-sample window) detects their pitch; a **synchrony index** scores consonance (11 intervals, 55-cent tolerance) × a **hold/togetherness timer** (≥~1.5 s consonant together) and voice-leads a beating cluster → root+M3+P5 bloom as they meet and hold; an **inline-SVG** flower (0× output in last 10 — cleanest diversity dodge of the fire) opens its petals on lock. Mic **analysis-only** (AnalyserNode only, never to output, never recorded). Mic-denied/unsupported → `text-rose-300` notice + a ~14 s synthesized demo-hum state machine (wobble→approach→lock→bloom→rest) that also runs on load pre-permission, so it always blooms hands-free. Files (removed): `page.tsx` (685), `yin.ts` (120), `README.md`. Refs: **YIN (JASA 2002)** + **Moving Mandala** + **TEI 2026**. **Ambition 4/5** (#1 + #2 ≥3 subsystems + #3 named refs + #4 spine). Tags: **voice/mic INPUT (fresh) / SVG OUTPUT (0× last-10 — best dodge) / YIN-pitch-lock-sync TECH / prosocial-tension-resolved-by-singing-together VIBE.** **Why it lost:** best *output diversity* (SVG) of the fire and folds in the §386 banked `501-kids-hum-garden` voice work, BUT (a) the partner is a **virtual ghost, not a second real child** — the weakest fit to the genuine *two-children-together* prosocial concept the research is about, and (b) **YIN on a real 4-year-old's high/breathy voice remains unverified** (demo is the safety net, but it's a real 06:30 demo-reliability risk vs. 505's deterministic permission-free auto-demo). **Resurrect:** the true two-child voice lab-first still wants **two real children landing a consonant interval together** — the one-mic-can't-separate-two-voices problem is the open challenge (try stereo-mic spatial separation, or a turn-taking call-and-response so only one sings at a time); failing that, ship this virtual-partner version as a solo **all-ages** voice-meditation where the unverified-child-voice risk is lower. The child-voice-tuned `yin.ts` is reusable across the lab.

## §389 — banked seeds from the WIDE adult fire (cycle 389, 2026-06-11)

Cycle 389 ran WIDE with three orthogonal adult explorers, each hitting a *different* jury-named starved menu category. Shipped `508-accord-call` (WebRTC P2P, multi-user category). The other two are complete, build-reviewed implementations that were `rm -rf`'d before the authoritative build — resurrect from these specs.

### 509-spectral-loom — `queued` (MIDI + long-form-evolving-adult category)
**What if every note you play on a MIDI keyboard wove into a slowly-souring harmonic memory you must keep tending — a living tapestry that drifts toward dissonance unless you actively resolve it?** Live-performance ADULT long-form instrument. **Web MIDI** (`navigator.requestMIDIAccess`) + on-screen piano + computer-keyboard + auto-arpeggiator fallbacks (all route one note-trigger path). **Harmonic-memory engine** (`memory.ts`): each note deposits a decaying set of partials (fundamental + 7 overtones) into a ~128-thread buffer with ~20–40 s time-constants, so you hear the *accumulation* of recent playing; a slow detuning accumulator sours the field whenever it's dissonant (Plomp–Levelt roughness over up to 48 partials), and consonant intervals voice-lead it back; tonal center wanders so minute 8 ≠ minute 1, never loops. **WebGPU** renderer (`gpu.ts`): a full-screen WGSL pass over a thread storage-buffer — Gaussian thread cross-sections, gold→red per-partial dissonance color, turbulence on sour threads, shimmer on consonant; Canvas2D fallback if `navigator.gpu` absent. Ref: **Gérard Grisey, *Partiels* (1975)** (consonance↔dissonance as a continuous spectral axis). **Ambition 2/5** (#2 ≥4 subsystems + #3 Grisey). **Why it didn't win:** lower ambition than 508 (no clean #1 — Web MIDI is used in the lab: 365/391/483/486), and its WebGPU output duplicates the most recent adult build (502). **Why promising:** the long-form-evolving-adult + MIDI/OSC menu categories are both jury-named-starved; the harmonic-memory "tend the tapestry" mechanic is a genuinely fresh tension model. **Resurrect** on a non-WebGPU-recent cycle, or re-skin the renderer to SVG/strings to dodge the 502 output overlap. (Built clean: page 620 / audio 156 / memory 190 / roughness 88 / gpu 237 lines, tsc+eslint 0/0.)

### 510-the-withholding — `queued` (conceptual/critical + non-screen category)
**What if a piece refused to resolve until you set the phone down and gave it your stillness — a sound you can only hear bloom by doing nothing and waiting in quiet?** Conceptual/critical ADULT piece; subject is patience and the *cost* of resolution. A sustained 4–3 suspension enriched with a tritone (45/32) + m7 colour breathes and will NOT resolve on a timer or a tap. It resolves only on **sustained stillness** detected two ways fused: **mic RMS** (`getUserMedia`, AnalyserNode-only, never recorded/sent — quiet < 0.012) AND **device stillness** (`deviceorientation`/`devicemotion` angular velocity < 0.8 deg/s). ~50 s of stillness+quiet → an irreversible, once-only bloom; motion/noise makes it recede. **Non-screen-dominant output** (the starved modality, 0×): near-black, ONE thin breathing arc of light encoding tension via width/glow, one `font-serif` state-line ("be still…" → "almost…" → "here."). Fallbacks: mic-denied → motion+patience; motion-absent → patience (no-touch timer); plus a labeled 20 s-idle auto-demo of one full withhold→resolve→reset cycle so a hands-free glance hears both states. Refs: **John Cage *4′33″* (1952)** · **Alvin Lucier *I Am Sitting in a Room* (1969)** · **Éliane Radigue** (sustained-tone patience). **Ambition 2–3/5** (#1 stillness-as-input / resolution-gated-by-detected-stillness — arguable lab-first cross-modal technique; #3 named refs; could be #4 a "Stillness" spine cycle 1). **Why it didn't win:** opens a NEW spine rather than deepening one (jury provocation #2 favors deepening), and its #1-technique claim is more arguable than 508's unambiguous WebRTC lab-first. **Why promising:** the purest answer to the jury's #1 (genuine withheld suspension, resolution earned not consumed) and it hits TWO untouched-all-window menu categories at once (conceptual/critical + non-screen). **Strong future ship** — the boldest "massively bigger concept" of the fire on the conceptual axis. (Built clean: page 445 / audio / stillness / draw + README, the builder's own tsc+eslint clean.)

---

## Cycle 390 — banked siblings (DEEP "vanish into your shadow" kids fire, comb-filter / phase-cancellation)

Cycle 390 ran DEEP: one concept — *make a sound vanish into its own shadow* via **comb-filter / phase-cancellation** (the lab's first; the JURY 2026-06-11 provocation-#1 primitive that's "tension you can't hear beating") — three approaches by input. Shipped `513-kids-shadow-still` (camera-motion vs stillness). The other two are complete, build-reviewed implementations `rm -rf`'d before the authoritative build — resurrect from these specs.

### 511-kids-ghost-bell — `queued` (tilt input · richest visuals · needs one physics fix)
**What if a 4-year-old could tilt a tablet to slide a bell's ghost-twin toward it — and discover that doubling a sound can make it vanish?** A glowing bell rings a soft C-major chime; a translucent ghost-twin slides with **device tilt** (`DeviceOrientationEvent` + pointer-drag fallback, no permissions needed for drag). Distance bell↔ghost = comb **delay**; a 32-bar SVG comb-spectrum strip bites into notches as it hollows; sparkle particles eject from the bell rim when full. SVG only; `DynamicsCompressor` limiter + 9 kHz lowpass; 7 s auto-demo. Refs: **Alvin Lucier** interference works; flanging/comb DSP (Julius O. Smith CCRMA). **Ambition ~3/5** (#1 comb-filter lab-first shared with 513 + #2 subsystems + #3 Lucier). **Why it didn't win:** its headline mapping (*ghost overlaps bell → cancel*) is physically backwards for the fundamental — at delay→0 the copies are **in phase** and the fundamental is *reinforced*, not cancelled (deepest null is at delay ≈ 1/(2·f₀) ≈ 1.9 ms for 261 Hz, a *middle* tilt, not overlap); the builder honestly flagged this. Tilt INPUT is also over-used (276/500). **Why promising:** the richest, most playful visuals of the fire (two bells + sparkles + comb teeth) and the most robust permission-free input. **Resurrect with the fix:** remap so the **mid-tilt** position lands on 1/(2·f₀) (or sweep the bell's pitch so the current overlap really is the null), then it's the strongest tilt sibling. (Built clean: page 670 lines, builder's own tsc+eslint clean.)

### 512-kids-echo-shadow — `queued` (voice input · most magical premise · headphone-only)
**What if a child could hum a note and watch their own voice get swallowed by its own shadow?** The child's live **mic** voice is run through a comb filter (dry + delayed copy); as they **sustain**, the delay ramps toward the anti-phase point (τ = 1/2f₀) and the voice thins to a ghostly whistle while an SVG "cyan ribbon" (voice) and "purple shadow ribbon" (echo) converge and a 320-step comb-teeth path deepens its notches. SVG only; limiter + 9 kHz lowpass + ≤0.42 master; 7 s sawtooth auto-demo (no mic needed). Refs: **Alvin Lucier** *I Am Sitting in a Room* (the canonical "recirculate a voice until interference reshapes it" — this is the single-pass child version); **Pauline Oliveros** *Deep Listening*. **Ambition ~3/5** (#1 comb lab-first + #2 + #3). **Why it didn't win:** **headphone-only** — on a tablet's open speaker the wet output feeds back into the mic and howls, making it a poor hands-free 06:30 phone demo; and the "vanish" depends on detecting the child's actual hum pitch. **Why promising:** the most *magical* premise of the three (your own voice erasing itself) and the purest Lucier homage. **Resurrect for a headphone/installation context** (museum kiosk, or an adult "Deep Listening" version where the howl caveat is acceptable), or pair with a known internal carrier tone the child sings *over* so f₀ is fixed. (Built clean: page 770 lines, builder's own tsc+eslint clean; carries the feedback-howl caveat in its README.)

---

## §391 — banked from the cycle-391 ADULT WIDE fire (2 non-winner explorers)

Cycle 391 ran WIDE: theme — *one presence, never resolves, tension NOT on Canvas2D* (JURY 2026-06-11's headline). Shipped `514-polytempo-loom` (irrational-ratio polytempo lattice, SVG, Nancarrow — tension in TIME). The other two are complete, build-reviewed implementations `rm -rf`'d before the authoritative build — resurrect from these specs.

### 516-slow-presence — `queued` (THE STRONGEST SEED · WebGPU long-form · the lab's best #4+#5 5/5 shot)
**What if a single living field evolved continuously for 5+ minutes with MEMORY — never looping, never returning, different at minute 5 than at second 30 — its sound and image two faces of ONE slowly-mutating presence you sit with, not a puzzle you solve?** A real **WebGPU compute** Gray-Scott reaction-diffusion field (512², ping-pong `rgba16float` storage textures, 8 WGSL steps/frame, full-screen-triangle render pass, near-black→indigo→teal→gold palette). The feed `f` and kill `k` rates drift along **two independent non-repeating 7-irrational-harmonic LFOs over a 420 s base period**, so the field traverses the Gray-Scott zoo (solitons→mitosis→labyrinths→coral) at geologic pace — it has memory, it can't return to its seed. A consonant **Lydian drone** (no cadence) mirrors the SAME two LFO functions (filter cutoff + osc mix updated ~4×/s — NO GPU readback, cheap). `navigator.gpu` absent → SVG/DOM drifting-radial fallback (**not** Canvas2D); audio always runs. `DynamicsCompressor` limiter; AudioContext in the Begin gesture. Refs: **Brian Eno** *Ambient 1: Music for Airports* (1978); **Refik Anadol** *Machine Hallucinations*; **Pearson 1993** (Gray-Scott, Science 261). **Ambition ~3/5** (#2 ≥3 subsystems: compute sim + render + audio-mirror + drift engine = 4 · #3 named refs · #4 multi-cycle long-form spine, cycle 1). **Why it didn't win:** its tension is *entropy / non-repetition*, not the jury's headline untouched **tension** primitive (TIME/TUNING), and WebGPU is the riskiest demo surface (iOS WebGPU still uneven). **Why it's the strongest seed:** it is LITERALLY the `490-disintegration` successor the JURY asked for (#2 "490 should have had children") on the jury-blessed WebGPU lane (#3, "extend the 2 WebGPU pieces") — and the lab's best path to the elusive **#4+#5 5/5**: ship it cycle 1, then cycle 2 binds a fresh <14-day reaction-diffusion / generative paper. **Resurrect as the next DEEP build.** (Built clean: 858-line page, builder's own `tsc --noEmit` + eslint clean; `/// <reference types="@webgpu/types" />`.)

### 515-two-tempos-one-body — `queued` (the audio/haptic-only screen-test lane)
**What if a piece had almost NO screen — a single presence you inhabit with eyes closed, the tension being two slowly-drifting tempi you sense in TWO senses at once: one pulse in your EARS, a different pulse in your HAND?** Cross-modal polytempo: a warm consonant drone (A2 + E3 + A3) with a soft **audio** swell at period A=1.20 s, and `navigator.vibrate(38)` **haptic** pulses at period B=A/√2≈0.85 s — irrational ratio, the heard and the felt tempo drift forever and never realign. Near-black screen = two faint CSS `radial-gradient` breathing rings (no canvas/WebGL visualizer — deliberately anti-screen) + "Close your eyes. One pulse is in your ears, one is in your hand." Vibration API absent (iOS/desktop) → amber notice + the inner emerald ring carries tempo B visually; audio always works. Self-correcting setTimeout chain for haptics (200 ms cooldown, vibrate(0) on stop). Refs: **Conlon Nancarrow** (polytempo) + **Pauline Oliveros** *Deep Listening* / **La Monte Young** (attention as the instrument). **Ambition ~2/5** (#1 cross-modal haptic-as-a-tempo-channel — distinct from `400-soundwalk-room`'s haptic-as-proximity, though haptics aren't strictly lab-first · #3 named refs). **Why it didn't win:** haptics already exist (400) so #1 is shakier, and on Karel's likely-iOS review phone the full *felt* effect degrades to two glow rings (a visual, not the cross-modal bet). **Why promising:** the genuine audio/haptic-only lane the JURY explicitly dared (#3 "take the 0× lane: an audio-only / haptic-only piece") — the lab's purest test of its screen bias. **Resurrect for an Android-targeted or installation (chair-transducer / wristband-haptic) context** where the felt tempo is guaranteed. (Built clean: 339-line page, builder-reported clean.)

## §392 — banked from the cycle-392 KIDS DEEP fire (2 non-winner explorers)

Cycle 392 ran DEEP: ONE concept — *a single warm companion you keep company by humming; never won, never lost, never resolves; over minutes it BECOMES something new, carrying everything you gave it* (the kids successor to `490-disintegration` / the `516` lane, grounded in **teamLab Sketch Aquarium / Future Park**) — attacked via THREE renderers. Shipped `518-kids-living-ember` (WebGPU Gray-Scott RD field). The other two are complete, build-reviewed implementations `rm -rf`'d before the authoritative build — resurrect from these specs. Both share the winner's spine: mic-RMS hum input (no pitch matching), monotonic memory, two banks of incommensurate-LFO drift (π/e/√2/φ over ~4–6 min so minute 5 ≠ second 30, never loops), a never-silent warm pentatonic/just-intonation lullaby pad that never cadences, soft bell on each hum, always-on auto-demo, AudioContext-in-gesture, DynamicsCompressor + ≤8 kHz lowpass.

### 517-kids-glow-friend — `queued` (the iOS-safe companion · three.js)
**Same concept, rendered as a soft glowing 3D blob-creature in three.js** — a core displaced sphere with emissive `MeshStandardMaterial`, inner+outer additive-blending halo spheres, three orbiting satellite metaball-lobes, and `THREE.Points` **petal particles** that orbit the body, one per remembered hum (the creature literally accretes a halo of everything the child gave it). Color, scale, glow, orbit radii driven by the evolution state + live mic energy. Refs: teamLab *Sketch Aquarium* · Eno *Music for Airports* · Tamagotchi. **Ambition 3/5** (#2 4 subsystems · #3 named refs · #4 Companion/Presence spine cycle 1). **Why it didn't win:** equal ambition to 518 but lost on the JURY 2026-06-11 #3 steer ("extend the WebGPU pieces" — 518 is the jury-named lane); three.js at 2× is genuinely under-represented and clean on diversity. **Why it's the strongest of the two banks:** it is the **most legibly "a friend" for a 4-year-old** (an actual cuddly glowing being with a halo of petals, vs. an abstract RD field) AND the **safest renderer on iOS/iPad** — where 518 falls back to a flat CSS ember, 517 runs the full 3D creature. **Resurrect for an iOS-targeted kids context, or as the spine's cycle-2 if 518's WebGPU proves too iOS-fragile in review.** (Built clean: 258-line page + `creature.ts` 352 + `memory.ts` 151 + `audio.ts` 292; builder-reported clean. Two minor edges: two rAF loops could be unified; the "metaball" look is orbiting lobes, not a true isosurface — a vertex-displacement pass would deepen it. Bump the corner `text-white/40` notes link to `/55+` on resurrection per typography rule.)

### 519-kids-quiet-bloom — `queued` (the growing-seedling companion · SVG)
**Same concept, rendered as a growing organism in inline SVG** — a slowly-branching luminous seedling/coral via a **space-colonization / differential-growth** algorithm: new nodes spawn from leaf nodes, stepping in a direction biased by incommensurate irrational-frequency LFOs (no shared rational period → never cycles back), capped at ≤300 `<circle>`/`<line>` nodes with `feGaussianBlur` glow, attributes updated directly per-rAF (no React re-render). Growth is **monotonic — nodes are NEVER pruned**, so the organism carries every tendril it ever grew; a 32-note bell ring-buffer re-plays as a slowly-thickening lullaby ostinato. Refs: teamLab *Future Park* · Eno *Music for Airports* · Runions (2007) space-colonization. **Ambition 3/5** (#2 4 subsystems · #3 named refs · #4 spine cycle 1). **Why it didn't win:** purely a **diversity** call — SVG output is now 3× (`508`/`513`/`514`); shipping it would make SVG 4× = the new monoculture the JURY just warned about ("Canvas2D is the new three.js"). Quality was not the issue. **Why promising:** the "growing plant you tend by humming" metaphor is arguably the most *legible* "alive" of the three for a 4yo, and the never-prune growth is the cleanest embodiment of "carries everything you gave it." **Resurrect once SVG is no longer over-represented** (e.g. after a few WebGPU/three.js/audio-only cycles), or as the spine's cycle-2 "draw-then-grow" (child sketches a seed, it grows from there teamLab-style). (Built clean: 384-line page + `growth.ts` + `audio.ts`; builder-reported clean. Edge: RMS sensitivity `*12` tuned for typical mic gain; auto-demo covers quiet rooms.)

## §393 — banked from the cycle-393 ADULT WIDE fire (2 non-winner explorers)

Cycle 393 ran WIDE: THREE orthogonal "one presence / never resolves" adult concepts, each dodging every JURY 2026-06-11 banned tag via a different input×output×technique. Shipped `520-singing-dune` (WebGPU MLS-MPM granular dune). The other two are complete, build-reviewed implementations `rm -rf`'d before the authoritative build — resurrect from these specs.

### 521-overpass — `queued` (real-world-data · three.js · the cleanest resurrect)
**What if you could hear ONE distant presence pass overhead — a real satellite, live — rising from silence as it climbs over your horizon, peaking at zenith, fading as it sets, and silent between passes?** Live ISS position from `wheretheiss.at` (lat/lon/altitude/velocity/visibility, polled ~5 s); optional `navigator.geolocation` → topocentric elevation/azimuth of the satellite over the observer. ONE solo JI voice (A3 root + 3:2 fifth + octave bell partial): elevation→squared gain curve (silent below horizon, swelling to zenith), range→lowpass cutoff (300–4000 Hz), line-of-sight radial velocity→±35-cent Doppler detune, sunlit→bright bell / eclipsed→muffled shadow hush. **three.js** dark sky-dome: 800 twinkling stars, a horizon ring with compass ticks, ONE warm-white spark with an additive glow sprite + a fading trail arc; camera drifts to follow. **Synthetic-orbit fallback** (deterministic ~4-min rise/transit/set loop) runs on load and on any fetch/CORS failure, with an honest `LIVE ↔ SIMULATED` pill + telemetry HUD. No harmonic progression; the ground track precesses so no two passes are identical — never resolves. `DynamicsCompressor` limiter; no API route (public read-only fetch, no secret). Refs: **Trevor Paglen** *Orbital Reflector* (a single satellite as a contemplative sky-presence) · the amateur satellite-listening tradition (SatNOGS / heavens-above). **Ambition 2/5** (#2 ≥3 subsystems: live-data engine + orbital-geometry sonification + three.js render; #3 named refs). **Why it didn't win:** strong and genuinely fresh (real-world-data + three.js + one-presence — all clean, dodges every ban), but lower ambition than 520 (no lab-first technique — external-API sonification exists at 437/471/502) and its headline live-API/CORS is the riskiest demo surface (the synthetic fallback covers it). **Why it's the strongest of the two banks:** the cleanest *real-world-data, one-presence* seed available — a beautiful, contemplative, genuinely-new-feeling piece. **Resurrect** as the next real-world-data cycle, or fold into the Living Earth spine as its "look UP" chapter (orbital, after earthquakes/space-weather/atmosphere). Could deepen with TLE-based orbit propagation (predict the *next* real pass time + count-down) for a true "wait for the sky" piece. (Built clean: `page.tsx` 328 + `orbit.ts` 263 + `sky.ts` 272 + `audio.ts` 191 + README; builder-reported clean.)

### 522-chladni-voice — `queued` BUT note overlap (Chladni point-cloud · three.js)
**What if your sustained voice became a single vibrating Chladni plate — tens of thousands of sand grains migrating to the nodal lines of the pitch you hold — so the tuning of your tone becomes a geometric figure that forms and dissolves but never holds still?** Hum/sing → hand-written **YIN** pitch detector → mode numbers (n,m) for a square-plate standing wave `S(x,y)=cos(nπx)cos(mπy)−cos(mπx)cos(nπy)`; 60k **three.js `Points`** grains do gradient descent on |S| toward the nodal lines, with antinode jitter; higher pitch→higher mode→finer figure; as pitch glides the figure reorganizes (mode-blended). A 5-partial plate tone + octave-below sympathetic drone sings even with no mic (auto-glide demo + slider/keyboard fallback); `DynamicsCompressor` limiter; mic is local-analysis only. Tension lives in TUNING, not roughness. Refs: **Ernst Chladni** *Klangfiguren* (1787) · **Hans Jenny** *Cymatics* (1967) · **Faraday** waves (1831). **Ambition ~2/5** (#2 ≥3 subsystems: YIN pitch + Chladni field + point-cloud render; #3 named refs). **⚠️ Why it didn't win — and why this seed carries a warning:** the builder claimed "first Chladni sim in this lab," but a curation grep found **`19-cymatics` AND `165-cymatics` already simulate sand settling into Chladni nodal lines** — so the headline novelty is **false**; mic-input + a 60k GPU point-count are incremental re-skins of an existing concept. This is exactly the "too similar" trap the JURY keeps flagging; the WIDE fan-out catching it is the process working. **Do NOT resurrect as a fresh Chladni piece.** Resurrect ONLY as an *explicit, acknowledged* deepening of `19`/`165` — e.g. a true 3D vibrating membrane (z-displacement + real eigenmodes), Faraday-wave fluid surface, or a polyphonic/chord-driven multi-mode superposition that 19/165 never did. (Built clean: `page.tsx` + `plate.ts` 10.8 kB + `pitch.ts` 5.4 kB + `audio.ts` + README; builder-reported clean.)

## §394 — banked from the cycle-394 KIDS DEEP fire (2 non-winner explorers)

Cycle 394 ran DEEP: ONE concept — *your hand becomes a little singing creature (MediaPipe Hands, 21-landmark tracking); open the hand → it blooms and SINGS, close → it gathers and goes quiet; wrist height → pentatonic pitch; nothing is ever wrong* — attacked via THREE renderers. Shipped `524-kids-hand-firebird` (raw WebGL2 GPU-particle light-creature). The other two are complete, build-reviewed implementations `rm -rf`'d before the authoritative build — resurrect from these specs. All three share: MediaPipe Hands via CDN dynamic ESM import (the `493`/`444` pattern — `@mediapipe/tasks-vision`, `HandLandmarker`, model `hand_landmarker.task`, GPU delegate, VIDEO mode, mirror-x); openness = avg fingertip distance from palm normalized by hand span; a scripted **virtual-hand auto-demo** that sings on load before any camera permission (and on camera-denied / MediaPipe-fail); AudioContext-in-gesture (iOS unlock); `DynamicsCompressor` brick-wall limiter (−6 dB / 20:1) + ≤8 kHz lowpass; always-on ambient pad; no API route, no guard, no npm deps. The diversity gate was decided on OUTPUT renderer (WebGL2 was the scarcest in the rolling-10 at 1×; Canvas2D and SVG were both already 3× → either would hit 4×), so 524 won the renderer slot and these two lost on diversity, not concept quality.

### 523-kids-hand-puppet — `queued` (the most-legible-for-a-4yo · Canvas2D shadow-puppet)
**Same concept, rendered as a warm Canvas2D shadow-theatre DOG** rigged to the hand skeleton: thumb = lower jaw (swings open with openness → the dog "sings"/barks a note), fingers = ears that flare with finger spread, a wagging tail that speeds up while singing, a warm amber eye. Wrist Y (mirrored, up=higher) → C-major pentatonic; finger spread → lowpass cutoff (brighter timbre). Triangle fundamental + sine overtones; C3+G3 ambient pad. Refs: MediaPipe Hands (arXiv 2006.10214) · shadow puppetry (wayang kulit / Lotte Reiniger). **Ambition 3/5** (#1 lab-first MediaPipe Hands · #2 ≥3 subsystems · #3 named refs). **Why it didn't win:** Canvas2D was already 3× in the rolling-10 → shipping it = 4× (the JURY's "Canvas2D is the new three.js" trap), and a Canvas2D dog is the least "massively bigger" of the three renderers. **Why it's the strongest bank for a literal 4yo:** it is the **most legibly-a-creature** — "my hand is a puppy that sings when I open it" reads in one second, where the firebird is more abstract; and Canvas2D is the **most permission/hardware-robust** render (no WebGL2/GPU dependency). **Resurrect** when Canvas2D is no longer over-represented, or for a low-end-device / maximum-legibility kids context. (Built clean: `page.tsx` 836 lines + README; builder-reported clean. Two edges flagged by the builder: the `computeSpread` angular formula may invert on some hand orientations — swap to `maxSpan/π` if spread feels backward; openness thresholds are tuned for an adult hand and may need lowering for small hands.)

### 525-kids-hand-choir — `queued` (the cleverest concept · SVG five-fingertip choir)
**Same hand-tracking input, but each of the 5 fingertips becomes its own glowing singer sprite** (a friendly cut-paper orb with eyes that widen and a mouth that opens), connected by a shimmering harmony web of bezier threads. **Spread the fingers wide → the five voices fan OUT into a full five-part pentatonic chord** (ratio-tuned 1, 5/4, 3/2, 5/3, 9/4 over a root); **close the hand → all five slide to UNISON** (a warm hug, sprites cuddle, hearts appear). Wrist height → root register (C3→C5, exponential); per-finger height nudges its own voice ±2% within the scale. Always-on 3-voice pad (C3/G3/E3); five voices kept low-gain to avoid clipping the limiter. **Inline SVG** render. Refs: MediaPipe Hands (arXiv 2006.10214) · Imogen Heap *Mi.Mu* gloves (hand-as-ensemble) · SVG cut-paper character lineage. **Ambition 3/5** (#1 lab-first MediaPipe Hands · #2 ≥3 subsystems: hand-landmark + 5-voice chord engine + SVG choir + auto-demo · #3 named refs). **Why it didn't win:** SVG was already 3× in the rolling-10 → shipping it = 4× (the new monoculture the JURY warned of), AND the builder self-flagged a perf edge — it drives the SVG via a `setSvgFrame` counter state so React reconciles the **full SVG tree at ~60fps**, which may drop to ~30fps on low-end phones (still smooth via lerp, but not free). **Why it's the cleverest bank:** "hand = a five-part choir you conduct, spread for chord / close for a hug" is arguably the most *musical-pedagogically rich* of the three (open chord ↔ unison is a real harmonic lesson a 4yo feels in their fingers). **Resurrect** when SVG is under-represented, **switching to ref-based direct SVG-attribute mutation** (per-`<circle>`/`<line>` refs updated in the rAF loop, no React re-render) to erase the perf edge. (Built clean: `page.tsx` + `audio.ts` + README; builder self-built `✓ Compiled successfully`, 4.97 kB route, zero TS/ESLint issues.)

## Banked §395 (cycle 395 ADULT WIDE non-winners — TEXT seeds, folders removed; renumber on revival, max committed folder now 526)
Cycle 395 went WIDE with three autonomous **genre / narrative "journey-engine alternative" arcs** (Karel priority #4), each dodging BOTH rolling-10 banned VIBE poles (solitary-never-resolves 4× AND tension-resolved-through-action 4×) and avoiding WebGPU (3× → would be 4×). Winner shipped: `526-jazz-room`. The two banked here are complete, build-reviewed implementations:

- **`527-cipher-score`** (type text → a living performed score) — `queued`. INPUT **keyboard/text** (the starved input lane) · OUTPUT **SVG** animated score (staff, per-character glyphs, sweeping playhead) · TECHNIQUE a deterministic **letter→pitch+duration musical cipher** (D-Lydian, 26 letters across D3–A6; vowels=triangle/held at resonant scale degrees, consonants=sine/crisp; punctuation→dynamics: `.`=fermata, `!`=sforzando, `?`=+1 semitone rising inflection, capitals=accent) + a 25 ms lookahead performance scheduler + a continuous D-Lydian drone pad · VIBE playful-literary, ink-on-luminous-parchment. **Named ref:** Olivier Messiaen's *langage communicable* (Méditations, 1969 — an actual letter→pitch+duration alphabet) + the musical-cryptogram tradition (B-A-C-H, Schumann ABEGG, Shostakovich DSCH). **Ambition 2/5** (#2 ≥3 subsystems + #3 named ref; no clean #1 — text→music is adjacent to `119-poem-fluid`, so it doesn't claim a lab-first primitive). *Promising:* the most delightful of the three and the only one on the genuinely starved keyboard-text input; arbitrary prose sounds *intentional* because everything quantizes to Lydian. *Why it lost:* lowest ambition (2/5 vs the winner's 3/5), and "type-to-make-music" is a smaller swing than a whole generative jazz combo. **Resurrect** as a clean SVG/text piece when the text-input lane is wanted, or DEEP it with word-level phrase grammar + a second cipher mode. Built files (removed): `page.tsx` (714 lines), `audio.ts` (251), `cipher.ts` (234), `README.md`.

- **`528-market-veil`** (live markets → an evolving ambient drone) — `queued, with a caveat`. INPUT **live crypto-market data** (Binance public 24hr ticker BTC/ETH/SOL, Coinbase spot fallback, polled 15 s, direct client fetch — no API route/secret) + a robust synthetic random-walk fallback · OUTPUT **WebGL2** domain-warped fBm "veil" shader (cool indigo calm → amber greed → violet/crimson fear) · TECHNIQUE data-sonification (24h %change→interval width/momentum, rolling volatility→±18¢ beating + tremolo + turbulence, a "fear index"→minor-2nd/tritone/♭9 fear-chord fading in quadratically), one sustained JI voice per instrument · VIBE documentary, anxious-calm, observational, never resolving on a timer. **Named ref:** Brian Eno *Music for Airports* (long-form no-cadence) + Andrea Polli / Carla Scaletti (data sonification). **Ambition 3/5** (#1 lab-first finance/market piece + #2 + #3). **CAVEAT — why it lost (honest):** its "live-data sustained-drone that tracks one aggregate scalar" concept — *and even its `-veil` name* — **overlaps `502-atmosphere-veil`** (cycle 387, live-weather→sustained-tension WebGPU drone), the exact "too similar" trap the jury keeps flagging; and **Binance geo-blocks US**, so for Karel (US) it would almost certainly run **SIMULATED**, undercutting the "live markets" headline (the synthetic fallback keeps it alive but the promise degrades). **Resurrect only as an explicitly-different angle** from 502 (e.g. a *discrete* market event-sequencer / order-book rhythm piece, not another aggregate drone) and swap Binance for a US-CORS-reachable source. Built files (removed): `page.tsx`, `audio.ts`, `market.ts`, `gl.ts`, `README.md`.

## Banked §396 (cycle 396 KIDS DEEP non-winners — TEXT seeds, folders removed; renumber on revival, max committed folder now 529)
Cycle 396 went DEEP on ONE kids concept — *a 4-year-old draws a simple doodle; it is recognized, comes alive, and sings* — attacked via THREE technical approaches. Winner shipped: `529-kids-doodle-choir` (TF.js DoodleNet → 8 storybook archetypes, Canvas2D). The two banked here are complete, build-reviewed implementations `rm -rf`'d before the authoritative build. All three share: a big finger-drawing canvas; a classifier→archetype map; per-archetype animation + a warm C-pentatonic motif (so a "misread" is still delightful); a scripted **ghost-finger auto-demo** that draws + recognizes + sings on load with ZERO interaction and even if no model loads (it bypasses the classifier and drives the archetype pipeline directly); AudioContext-in-gesture (iOS unlock); master `DynamicsCompressor` brick-wall limiter (−6 dB / 20:1) + ≤8 kHz lowpass; no API route, no guard, no npm deps (TF.js, where used, via CDN dynamic import — the `493`/MediaPipe pattern). The diversity gate decided on TECHNIQUE/CONCEPT, not build quality.

### 530-kids-sketch-garden — `queued` (the bulletproof offline version · WebGL2 glowing creatures)
**Same draw-it-comes-alive magic, but recognized by SHAPE alone — no neural net, no network.** A fully-local **hand-coded geometric classifier** (closedness, aspect ratio, path wiggliness, corner/direction-change count, convex-hull roundness, stroke count → 6 archetypes) decides what the doodle is; the drawing then transforms into a **WebGL2 additive-blended particle creature** (600 points seeded along the drawn path: a bloom radiates, a tree floats up, a bird flutters, a wave drifts, a critter scuttles, a star pulses) that sings its motif and joins a luminous night-garden choir. Canvas2D glow fallback if WebGL2 is absent. **Named refs:** Jono Brandel & Lullatone *Patatap* (instant playful shape→sound) + the **$1 Unistroke Recognizer** (Wobbrock et al.) geometric-gesture lineage. **Ambition 2/5** (#2 ≥3 subsystems: geometric classifier + WebGL2 particle renderer + Web-Audio garden choir + auto-demo; #3 named refs — **no clean #1**: geometric/feature analysis is adjacent to existing pieces, and it deliberately forgoes the TF.js lab-first). *Why it didn't win:* this was the DEEP cycle whose entire ambition story is the **TF.js lab-first** — 530 is the approach that gives that up, so it clears the floor at only 2/5 while 529/531 hit 3/5. *Why it's a strong bank:* it is the **most robust** (zero network/model dependency — it can NEVER fall to a fallback because it has no model to lose) and has the **most beautiful renderer** (WebGL2 additive glow vs 529's Canvas2D). **Resurrect** as a guaranteed-offline / low-connectivity skin of 529, OR — best — fold its WebGL2 glowing-particle creatures into 529 as the winner's render upgrade (529's Canvas2D storybook + 530's glow = the ideal merge). Builder-reported: zero TS/ESLint errors across all five files. Built files (removed): `page.tsx` (574), `gl.ts`, `audio.ts`, `classify.ts`, `README.md`.

### 531-kids-drawing-band — `queued, with a diversity caveat` (the most musical · TF.js + tempo-locked groove)
**Same DoodleNet recognition, but every doodle JOINS A BAND** — each recognized drawing becomes one looping voice in a shared 96-BPM groove (critter→soft kick, sun→warm pad, bird→high sparkle, fish→bubbly bass, tree/flower→pluck arp, star/cloud→hi-hat shimmer), all quantized to one "Tale of Two Clocks" lookahead scheduler (25 ms interval, 120 ms horizon, single shared `originTime` so there's zero per-voice drift). More doodles = a richer little song; the auto-demo "draws" 5 doodles over ~10 s into a full looping groove. C-pentatonic so every layer fits. **Named refs:** Quick, Draw! (Jongejan, Ha, et al.) + DoodleNet (yining1023) + C. Roberts "A Tale of Two Clocks." **Ambition 3/5** (#1 TF.js lab-first + #2 ≥3 subsystems: DoodleNet classifier + groove scheduler + per-role synth voices + Canvas2D band = 4 + #3 named refs) — **tied with the winner on ambition.** *Why it didn't win (diversity, not quality):* its "build a band / loop station" CONCEPT lands squarely in the lab's **most-saturated kids lane** — `98-kids-drum-circle`, `35/121/172-loop-station`, `147-kids-beat-pulse`, `150-kids-beat-builder`, `358-beat-mirror`, `423-kids-face-beat` and more — exactly the "too similar in design and theme" trap Karel/the jury keep flagging; 529's "your drawings come alive and sing" is the fresher framing and the more direct fit to Karel's *loved* draw/paint pieces (the recognition is the novelty, so spend it on the less-worn lane). *Why it's the strongest musical bank:* it is genuinely the most **musically satisfying** of the three — a real song builds, not just simultaneous motifs — and its tempo-locked groove engine is reusable. **Resurrect** on a cycle when the kids groove/loop lane is NOT over-represented, or fold its shared-scheduler quantization into 529 so the choir's motifs lock to a gentle pulse instead of free intervals. Builder-reported: two clean `npm run build` runs, ESLint + TS clean. Built files (removed): `page.tsx` (582), `audio.ts` (322), `classify.ts` (290), `README.md`.

## §397 — banked from the cycle-397 ADULT WIDE fire (2 non-winner explorers; winner shipped = `532-vocoder-veil`, the lab's first channel vocoder)

Cycle 397 ran WIDE with three orthogonal adult explorers on three non-saturated renderers (WebGL2 / three.js / audio-first), each clearing the ambition floor and dodging the rolling-10 banned "solitary-never-resolves" VIBE + the 4× renderer trap (Canvas2D/WebGPU/SVG all at 3×). Shipped `532-vocoder-veil` (channel vocoder, Karel's real piano as carrier — the cleanest lab-first + the standing his-real-music directive). The other two are complete, build-reviewed implementations `rm -rf`'d before the authoritative build — resurrect from these specs.

### 533-hollow — `queued` (the cleanest sculptural resurrect · three.js · comb-filter / phase-cancellation tension)
**What if the music lived in the SILENCE you carve — tension made not by adding dissonance but by SUBTRACTING, hollowing a sustained tone until the gaps between the teeth become the melody?** A rich detuned-saw/triangle/noise drone (E2 ~82 Hz, many partials) passed through **two stacked feedforward comb filters** (`y[n] = 0.5·x[n] + 0.5·(−g)·x[n−D]`); the combs carve a harmonic ladder of **destructive-interference notches** at `f_k=(2k−1)/(2D)`. A 2-D field maps **x = comb delay (notch pitch, 0.3→22 ms log)** × **y = feedback depth (notch depth, 0.05→0.88, hard-clamped so it can never self-oscillate)**; a second comb at an irrational ≈1.42 delay ratio (notch grids never align) produces compound cancellations that collapse whole spectral regions to near-silence. **three.js** subdivided cylindrical shell whose vertices displace **inward** at the analytic notch frequencies (confirmed against a live FFT) and swell where the spectrum survives — the carving is literally visible. A Lissajous **auto-carve** path sweeps the field on load (≈16 s/cycle) so it sounds + morphs hands-free; touch takes over, 4 s idle resumes. Canvas2D notch-spectrum fallback. There is **no resolve button** — the tension is permanent and structural. **Named refs:** Alvin Lucier *I Am Sitting in a Room* (1969); Maryanne Amacher (*Music for Sound-Joined Rooms*); the phaser/flanger comb lineage. **Ambition 3/5** (#1 *scoped* — comb-filter cancellation as the *compositional center* is lab-first, though the DSP primitive appears elsewhere as FX/flute-model; +#2 ≥3 subsystems: rich drone synth + two-comb cancellation engine + analytic-notch→mesh displacement + FFT + auto-carve; +#3 named refs). **Why it didn't win:** ties 532 on ambition, but its #1 is the *scoped* one where 532's vocoder is a clean verified 0×, and 532 fulfills the standing his-real-music directive 533 doesn't. **Why it's a strong bank:** the most *sculptural* of the three — the three.js carving glances beautifully — and a real answer to the jury's standing "put tension in a never-touched primitive" provocation. Resurrect as a psychoacoustic-tension spine (pair with 534). Builder flagged the displacement is conservatively tuned (0.42 inward max, to avoid open-cylinder normal artifacts); a subdivided torus/sphere would carve more viscerally. Built files (removed): `page.tsx` (535), `audio.ts` (258), `README.md` (166).

### 534-third-ear — `queued` (the highest-surprise concept · audio-first · combination/difference Tartini tones)
**What if the melody you hear isn't actually being played — what if it only exists inside your own ear, conjured by two pure tones your ear can't help but combine?** Two pure `'sine'` oscillators at f₁ (~600 Hz fixed) and f₂; because human hearing is a **nonlinear transducer**, the ear manufactures **combination / difference / Tartini tones** that are not present in the air — the difference tone (f₂−f₁), the often-stronger cubic difference tone (2f₁−f₂), the sum tone. By sliding f₂ so that (f₂−f₁) traces a **just-intonation pentatonic phrase** (C→E→D→C→G→A→G→E→D→C, root ≈130 Hz), a third melody emerges *from nowhere*. **Audio-first / minimal Canvas2D** (the jury's under-tested non-screen lane): near-black field, an amber orb (f₁), a teal orb (f₂), and a faint pulsing violet **ghost** orb where the difference tone "lives" — plus a dim second ghost for the cubic. Drag the orbs to explore; 5 s idle resumes the auto-demo. An honest **"phantom only / reinforced" toggle**: by default a low (≈18%) reinforcement oscillator at f₂−f₁ guarantees the melody is audible on phone speakers; "phantom only" removes it so on good headphones at volume *your own ear* makes the tune — the gap between the two modes IS the demonstration. **Named refs:** Giuseppe Tartini *il terzo suono* (1714, used it as a violin tuning aid); Maryanne Amacher *Third Ear Music* / "additional tones"; Vincenzo Galilei; Jean-Philippe Rameau *Génération harmonique* (1737). **Ambition 2/5** (#1 clean lab-first — combination/difference tones grep-verified 0×; +#3 named refs; #2 NOT cleanly claimed — fewest subsystems: a few oscillators + a difference-tone sequencer + a deliberately spare visual). **Why it didn't win:** the most *surprising* idea of the three, but (a) lowest ambition (2/5), (b) audio-first so the 06:30 phone GLANCE is weak, and (c) the acoustic effect is admittedly unreliable on the very phone speakers Karel reviews on (the reinforcement crutch saves audibility but undercuts the magic). **Why it's a strong bank:** the highest-*surprise* concept the lab has queued — the listener's body is part of the instrument. **Resurrect** for a headphone/all-ages listening cycle, or pair with 533 as the psychoacoustic-tension spine. Built files (removed): `page.tsx` (422), `audio.ts` (214), `README.md` (109).

### 535-kids-starling-choir — `queued` (the GPU-scale technical resurrect of the "living flock that sings" spine · raw WebGL2 · kids 4+)
**What if a murmuration of thousands of glowing birds of light flew with REAL emergent flocking, and the flock itself sang as the child shepherds it?** Cycle 398's DEEP sibling that attacked the singing-murmuration concept via **raw WebGL2 transform feedback** (vs the shipped 537's three.js). ~2,500 agents in ping-pong VBOs (interleaved `vec2 pos, vec2 vel`); each frame an update vertex shader applies Reynolds separation/alignment/cohesion (sampling ~30 neighbours from a packed **RGBA32F** texture) and writes new state via `gl.beginTransformFeedback`; the render pass draws additive-blended point-sprite glow; a CPU shadow copy is read back async via `getBufferSubData` (`PIXEL_PACK_BUFFER`) every ~200 ms to compute audio state without stalling the GPU. Flock state → harmony exactly as 537 (cohesion→richness, centroid-height→pentatonic register, up to 4 quadrant clusters → 4 sustained pentatonic voices that split/merge audibly, turbulence→tremolo+noise wash). CPU-boids/Canvas2D fallback (700 agents) when WebGL2/transform-feedback is absent. Ghost-shepherd auto-demo splits the flock every ~8–14 s. **Named refs:** Reynolds SIGGRAPH 1987; Cavagna et al. *Scale-free correlations in starling flocks* PNAS 2010; W.D. Hamilton *Geometry for the Selfish Herd* 1971. **Ambition 3/5** (#2 ≥5 subsystems + #3 named refs + #4 spine cycle 1). **Why it didn't win:** the most ambitious *technically* (a true GPU flocking sim), but it self-flags genuine iOS-Safari fragility (`texStorage2D RGBA32F` neighbour texture + `PIXEL_PACK_BUFFER`/`getBufferSubData` readback) on the very iPad Karel reviews on, and WebGL2 is 2×→3× in the rolling-10 where three.js (537) is 0×. **Resurrect** when we want the flock to climb to 5–10k birds — the GPU path is the only way to scale past ~700 CPU agents. Built files (removed): `boids.ts` (918), `page.tsx` (306), `audio.ts` (244), `README.md`.

### 536-kids-lantern-shoal — `queued` (the best-legibility resurrect / character-art donor for 537 · Canvas2D · kids 4+)
**What if a shoal of big, friendly glowing lantern-fish swam together with real flocking, each fish a little FRIEND with eyes and a tail of light — and the shoal sang as a 4-year-old herds it?** Cycle 398's DEEP sibling — the same singing-murmuration concept on **Canvas2D** with **rich per-creature character** (radial-gradient glow bodies, simple eyes, dot/bezier tails, additive `'lighter'` glow), ~380 fish so each reads as an individual. Spatial-hash boids (Reynolds; 80 px cells; velocity clamp 40–180 px/s; boundary repulsion); touch lure (single + two-finger split) + optional device-tilt "current." Shoal state → harmony as 537 (cohesion→warmth, centroid-Y→register, up to 3 k-means clusters → sustained pentatonic voices, speed→tremolo). Ghost-lure auto-demo splits the shoal every ~8 s. **Named refs:** Reynolds SIGGRAPH 1987; Reynolds GDC 1999 *Steering Behaviors*; Partridge 1982 *The structure and function of fish schools*. **Ambition 3/5** (#2+#3+#4). **Why it didn't win — diversity, not quality:** the **best literal-4yo legibility** of the three (you can see individual friends moving together), but Canvas2D was already 3× in the rolling-10 and shipping it would push Canvas2D to **4×** — the monoculture trap. **Resurrect** when Canvas2D is scarce again, OR fold its character-art (eyes/tails) into 537's `THREE.Points` so the murmuration's birds read as individuals. Built files (removed): `page.tsx` (575), `shoal.ts` (311), `audio.ts` (226), `README.md`.

### 539-tremor-globe — `queued` (banked WIDE non-winner, cycle 399 · live USGS earthquake sonification · three.js globe · adult)
**What if Resonance made music about the living Earth — sonifying real, live earthquakes so you inhabit one planetary presence that never stops trembling?** Fetches the public CORS-open **USGS real-time GeoJSON feed** (`.../summary/all_day.geojson`, no key, re-polled ~90s) + a 20-quake embedded fallback. Each quake → an audio event: **magnitude→pitch+loudness+duration** (M0≈420 Hz bell → M8≈28 Hz groan), **depth→lowpass timbre**, **longitude→stereo pan**; a stochastic wave-scheduler fires 3–8 events every ~18 s over an 8 s window with downward pitch-glide, over a 16 Hz sub-bass drone that breathes with the dataset's mean magnitude. three.js Fibonacci dot-sphere globe auto-rotates; markers flare in sync. Voice pool capped at 8. **Named refs:** USGS feeds; Andrea Polli *Atmospherics/Weather Works*; Florian Dombois *Auditory Seismology*; Carla Scaletti. **Why it didn't win (cycle 399):** its ambition-#1 claim ("first real external-API sonification") is **false** — the lab already has `233-earth-pulse`, `314-solar-wind`, `437-wiki-pulse`, `463-terra-gamelan`, and `337-seismic-globe` (a near-direct sibling) — so it falls to #2+#3=2/5 and is the most *redundant* of the three explorers. Competently built (1,135 lines, 4 subsystems, build-clean in isolation). **Resurrect** only if it differentiates hard from 337/233 — e.g. drop the globe entirely for a non-screen audio-only Earth-drone, or sonify a feed the lab hasn't (live lightning/Blitzortung, ISS pass, ocean-buoy wave-height). Built files (removed): `page.tsx` (323), `audio.ts` (263), `globe.ts` (298), `quakes.ts` (112), `README.md`.

### 540-slime-cantor — `queued` (banked WIDE non-winner, cycle 399 · WebGPU Physarum transport network · adult)
**What if Resonance grew a single living organism — a Physarum slime-mold transport network on the GPU — and the shape of the network it builds IS the evolving voice?** Jeff Jones (2010) agent sense-steer-deposit + diffuse-decay on WebGPU compute (WebGL2 / CPU-Canvas2D fallback); field statistics (coverage→brightness, connectedness→consonance, turbulence→tremolo) drive a long-form never-resolving drone; pointer = food-source attractor. **Named refs:** Jeff Jones *Artificial Life* 2010; Sage Jenson (mxsage) "38"/Physarum; Toshiyuki Nakagaki (Nature 2000); Refik Anadol. **Why it didn't win (cycle 399):** (1) its ambition-#1 ("first physarum / raw-GPU compute") is **false** — `327-physarum-choir` already exists (plus WebGPU at 486/518/520), so physarum is not lab-first; (2) at curation time it *looked* incomplete (only `audio.ts` + `sim.ts` written) — **corrected post-commit:** a second, very slow 540 builder (~34 min) actually finished it fully (`page.tsx` 213 / `sim.ts` 935 / `audio.ts` 199 / `README.md` 172) with all three GPU paths (WGSL 65k agents / WebGL2 16k / CPU 3k), so it was *late*, not broken — but the false #1 stands regardless; (3) its late-finishing agent kept re-materializing its folder *after* every removal (incl. after the commit) and broke the first authoritative build (its triple-slash `webgpu.d.ts` path-reference fails ESLint) — a worse repeat of the cycle-398 race. **Resurrect** only with a *differentiated* angle vs 327 (e.g. two organisms competing for one food field = back toward "two parties", or physarum solving a maze shaped by the live audio spectrum) AND `TaskStop` the agent before removing its folder. Built files (removed): full `page.tsx`/`sim.ts`/`audio.ts`/`README.md`.

### 542-kids-liquid-light-gl — `queued` (banked DEEP non-winner, cycle 400 · WebGL2 ping-pong stable-fluids · kids 4+)
**Same "singing liquid light" concept as the shipped `541-kids-liquid-light`, but on WebGL2 instead of WebGPU.** A full Jos Stam (SIGGRAPH 1999) stable-fluids solver implemented in **WebGL2 fragment shaders with ping-pong half-float FBOs**: splat → curl → **vorticity confinement** → divergence → 24-iter Jacobi pressure → gradient-subtract → velocity & dye advection → luminous tone-mapped display with cheap bloom. Same kids-safe pentatonic sonification (swirl→brightness, height→register, C3–C5, always-on pad, −6 dB/20:1 limiter), throttled (~15 Hz) `gl.readPixels` energy sampling (NOT per-frame), ghost-finger auto-demo, Canvas2D "lite fluid" fallback, `text-rose-300` notice. **Why it didn't win (cycle 400):** lost purely on the **diversity audit** — WebGL2 is at 2× in the kids rolling-10 (524/500) and Canvas2D-class renderers are crowding toward 4×, while WebGPU was 0×; the winner's WebGPU path is the harder diversification. It is complete + clean (`page.tsx` 332, `fluid-gl.ts` w/ vorticity, `fallback.ts`, `audio.ts`, README). **Resurrect** as the **iPad-reliable production path** if Karel reports the WebGPU `541` doesn't run on his device — WebGL2 is far more uniformly supported on iOS Safari than WebGPU. `CURL_AMT`/dissipation/splat-radius are eyeballed and want a real-GPU tuning pass. Built files (removed): `page.tsx`, `fluid-gl.ts`, `fallback.ts`, `audio.ts`, `README.md`.

### 543-kids-liquid-light-flow — `queued` (banked DEEP non-winner, cycle 400 · Canvas2D particle-advection · kids 4+)
**The most-compatible "singing liquid light" approach: no GPU shaders at all.** A coarse **56×56 velocity grid** the finger stirs (impulse splats + diffuse + decay) carrying a slow ambient **curl-noise** (Bridson et al. 2007) so the pool always swirls on its own, advecting **~6500 glowing dye particles** rendered additively (`globalCompositeOperation = "lighter"`) over a low-alpha dark fade-trail → ink-in-water tails, not dots. Same kids-safe pentatonic sonification + always-on pad + ghost-finger auto-demo; tuned for ≥30 fps on older iPads (particle count is the obvious knob). **Why it didn't win (cycle 400):** lost as **essentially the winner's own Canvas2D fallback** promoted to a standalone piece — and Canvas2D is trending toward 4× in the kids window. It is the best legibility/compatibility option and genuinely reads as flowing liquid. **Resurrect** if a future cycle wants a *zero-GPU* guaranteed-everywhere kids fluid, or fold its 56×56-grid + 6500-particle tuning into `541`'s fallback to upgrade the lite path. Built files (removed): `page.tsx`, `flow.ts`, `audio.ts`, `README.md`.

### 544-conductors-canon — `queued` (banked DEEP non-winner, cycle 401 · MediaPipe Hands two-hand conducting → SVG scrolling loom · adults / polytempo)
**The literal "conduct the loom with your two hands."** Cycle 2 of `514-polytempo-loom` via MediaPipe Hands: each hand is one conductor — its vertical wrist/fingertip trajectory is read for "visual beats" (downward velocity-reversal at the ictus, ±0.04 normalized-Y threshold), inter-beat intervals (0.35–1.55 s = 40–170 BPM) averaged over 3–5 beats and smoothed (τ=1.5 s, never quantized). Left hand → voices 0–1 (×1, ×1/√2), right hand → voices 2–4 (×1, ×1/φ, ×φ/2) → a conducted 5-voice Nancarrow canon on the same "Tale of Two Clocks" lookahead engine + D-pentatonic bell timbre + −12 dBFS brick-wall limiter as the winner. Renders in **514's exact SVG scrolling loom** (5 color-coded lanes, marks scroll at 58 px/s, NOW line at 22%, glow on fire, direct-DOM mutation — zero React re-renders) so it reads instantly as "514, but now I conduct it." Ghost conductors (72 & 72√2 BPM) drive it from frame one; resume after 4 s of no hands. MediaPipe via `webpackIgnore` CDN dynamic import (`@mediapipe/tasks-vision@0.10.14`, no npm dep); careful self-report (helpers non-`use*`, the two MediaPipe `any` casts `eslint-disable`'d, exhaustive-deps obeyed). Files (removed): `page.tsx` (1081), `README.md` (169). **Ambition 3/5** (#2 + #3 Nancarrow/SMT + #4 514 cycle 2; #1 contestable — MediaPipe Hands used at 524, beat detection at 106/147). **Why it didn't win (cycle 401):** diversity audit — SVG is already 3× in the jury rolling-15 (508/514/538), so shipping it would push SVG to **4× = newly over-represented**, the exact renderer-monoculture the jury is fighting; the winner's three.js (1×) lowers concentration instead. **Resurrect FIRST** if Karel wants the literal two-hand conducting, or if the winner's three.js / no-ML motion→tempo underperforms on his device — this is near-shippable and its hand-beat detection is likely more responsive than autocorrelation-on-optical-flow. Unverified: the ±0.04 beat threshold wants real-gesture tuning; left/right hand assignment uses an x-position heuristic (mirrored feeds can invert).

### 546-clock-canon — `queued` (banked DEEP non-winner, cycle 401 · MediaPipe Pose full-body two-arm conducting → SVG orbital phase-clock · adults / polytempo)
**Your whole body conducts the canon, and you watch it as orbiting clock-hands that sweep and NEVER realign.** Cycle 2 of `514-polytempo-loom` via MediaPipe Pose (full-body, works at a distance, dance-like): left wrist (landmark 15) vs right wrist (16) vertical beat → two conducted tempi (same visual-beat ictus detection + smoothing as 544), driving the same 5-voice "Tale of Two Clocks" Nancarrow engine. The distinguishing move is a **fresh visual idiom** (not 514's scrolling loom): each voice is a **dot orbiting a circle** at angular velocity = its tempo (one revolution per beat); incommensurable rates mean the dots sweep and never realign — the *visual proof of the math* — with fading trails, a glow/pulse as each dot crosses 12 o'clock, and a **phase-proximity chord** that brightens between two dots when they near-align (a near-miss, NOT a downbeat — implements 514's own "phase proximity heatmap" cycle-2 hook). Ghost arms (72 & 72√2 BPM) sweep from frame one; resume after 4 s of no body. MediaPipe Pose via `webpackIgnore` CDN import (`pose_landmarker_lite`, no npm dep). Complete (`page.tsx` ~39.5 KB, `README.md` ~6.8 KB; its builder's "completed" notification arrived before the files flushed — a delayed-write race, handled). **Ambition 3/5** (#2 + #3 Nancarrow/SMT + #4 514 cycle 2; #1 contestable — Pose used at 493). **Why it didn't win (cycle 401):** same SVG-would-hit-4× diversity reason as 544, AND full-body Pose is the least reliable of the three at the 06:30 phone-glance distance (the winner's no-ML room-motion needs no body in frame). **Resurrect** for the **orbital-clock visual itself** — the clock-hands-that-never-realign is arguably the most legible single image of "incommensurable tempi" in the whole spine; fold it into 545/544 as an alternate view, or ship it when SVG is scarce again. Built files (removed): `page.tsx`, `README.md`.
