## SPINE: Companion / Presence — kids (518-living-ember cycle 1; the "Songkeeper" family, cycle 402)
**The thread:** *a kids creature that is a RELATIONSHIP, not a toy — it remembers what the child gave it and is demonstrably different on return.* Jury 2026-06-12 provocation #6 named `518-kids-living-ember` (the one kids piece with real long-form memory) as the thing to extend: "a creature a child finds genuinely changed tomorrow," not another 90-second glow toy. **Cycle 2 (402, SHIPPED):** `549-kids-song-friend` — the creature remembers musical *content* (actual sung pentatonic melodies via forgiving autocorrelation + babble-note fallback), persists them across **calendar days** in localStorage, and its **SVG body is literally drawn from the melodic contours** (each song = a petal tracing its pitches; the friend has a face that sings) — transcends 322-kids-voice-garden (a timer-aged plant) by changing because of *what was sung*, not a clock. Won a kids DEEP 3-approach fire. **Ambition 3/5** (#2 ≥3 subsystems: pitch + cross-day memory + recall-synth + SVG render · #3 named refs: Tamagotchi/Maita-Bandai 1996, Eno · #4 spine cycle 2; honest #1 case for "melodic-content cross-day memory recalled as woven counterpoint," discounted because localStorage-persistence itself isn't lab-first). **Diversity** clean on every jury ban (voice INPUT not finger-on-glass · SVG OUTPUT not Canvas2D/WebGPU · pitch-recall TECH not GPU-physics-sim · companion VIBE not calm-sensory-kids). **Cycle 3 (planned):** the two banked siblings below are the obvious next renderers; OR deepen recall — let the creature *combine* taught melodies into a genuinely new song it "composes" overnight, or give it a simple call-and-response game ("sing the part it forgot").

### Banked §402 (DEEP non-winners — TEXT seeds, folders removed; renumber on revival, max folder now 549):
- **`547-kids-songkeeper`** (three.js orbiting-strand creature) — `queued`. Same "remembers your songs across days, sings them back woven" concept, rendered as a **three.js glowing creature where each remembered song is an orbiting strand whose shape traces that song's pitch contour**; richest "alive" feel of the three, polished YIN-style detector, a "forget everything" reset + "pretend it's tomorrow" demo, CSS-fallback creature. *Promising:* the most beautiful idle presence; resurrect when three.js is scarce (it had just shipped at 545, so a 2nd consecutive three.js worked against diversity here). *Caveat that lost it:* "song → orbiting strand" is more abstract / less legible for a 4-year-old than 549's petals-with-a-face, and three.js was 2-in-a-row. Built files (removed): `page.tsx` (620), `creature.ts`, `audio.ts`, `memory.ts`, `pitch.ts`, `README.md`.
- **`548-kids-songbloom`** (raw WebGL2 bioluminescent shader) — `queued`. Same concept, but the memory is compressed into shader-seed uniforms (arms/branch/hue/twist/richness/pitchField[8]) that **grow a domain-warped bioluminescent deep-sea creature in a GLSL ES 3.00 fragment shader**, live-pulsed by RMS/pitch; first-strong-peak autocorrelation (octave-error-resistant), DOM-fallback creature. *Promising:* the lab's freshest *generative-shader-from-persistent-memory* idea — bank as the cycle-3 renderer upgrade if a child's unique song-history-as-a-unique-glowing-organism is wanted. *Caveat that lost it:* memory→uniform-hash is the *least legible* "you can see your own songs" of the three (no face/character; a 4-year-old can't read the connection), and a full-screen shader reads less as a companion. Built files (removed): `page.tsx` (451), `gl.ts`, `audio.ts`, `memory.ts`, `README.md`.

## §405 — 2026-06-12 (UTC) · banked non-winners from the adult DEEP "first Gaussian-splat" fire (cycle 405)

Cycle 405 ran DEEP: ONE massive concept — **the lab's first Gaussian-splatting renderer, sculpted by Karel's real Welcome Home piano** — via three parallel WebGL2 approaches. Shipped `557-piano-splat-galaxy`. The other two are complete, build-reviewed, and ready to resurrect:

- **`556-piano-splat-spectrogram`** (`queued`) — Karel's piano becomes a navigable 3D **spectrogram solid**: X = 96 log-spaced frequency bands, Z = scrolling time-history slices, Y/brightness/size = amplitude; ~7,680 additive Gaussian splats you orbit through. The most *legible/analytical* of the three (you literally see the spectrum as a turnable sculpture). Resurrect as the "teaching/analysis" variant, or fuse its time-axis scrolling into a future score-following splat piece. Files were complete (page.tsx 339, splat.ts 342, audio.ts 262, README 81) before removal — rebuild from this spec.

- **`558-piano-splat-body`** (`queued`) — Karel's piano given a **body of light**: ~20k Gaussian splats over a parametric Fibonacci-sphere "torso," deformed in the **vertex shader** by 24 FFT band-lobes (bass = global breathing, mids = spherical-harmonic lobes, highs = shimmer, onset = expanding shockwave ring). The most *conceptually ambitious* and the tightest bind to the genuinely-fresh **DNE×Gracia 4DGS performance of Amy May (2026)** — "a musician as a streamable volumetric splat scene." Didn't win because it's conceptually adjacent to the existing `283-piano-isosurface` ("piano deforms a breathing 3D volume") — the novelty was mostly the splat *technique*, not the behavior. **Strong cycle-2 path:** load a REAL captured `.splat`/4DGS scene (the actual browser-splatting wave) instead of a procedural torso, so it becomes a true radiance-field performer, not a procedural blob. The elegant ~27-floats/frame vertex-shader displacement is worth keeping.

**Splat spine (multi-cycle, #4):** 557 opens the lab's Gaussian-splat lane. Cycle 2 = load a real captured `.splat` scene + depth-sorted alpha compositing (true 3DGS, not just additive billboards); cycle 3 = a 4D (animated) splat performance synced to his music, à la the Amy May piece. Bank 556/558 as the alternate renderers for that spine.

## §409 — banked non-winners from the adult DEEP "embodied polytempo" fire (cycle 409, 2026-06-13)

Cycle 409 fired a **DEEP** triple on ONE concept — *cycle 2 of the `514-polytempo-loom` spine made EMBODIED: the human's gestures set two incommensurable tempi (snapped to the nearest of √2 / φ / e÷2 / π÷2), so the two consonant voices drift through every phase relationship and can NEVER share a downbeat* — the jury's exact #1+#4 ask (chase a 4/5; build the SECOND time-tension piece, embodied). Three input/embodiment approaches × non-SVG renderers (SVG output was the rolling-10 ban at 4×). Winner shipped = **`568-conductor-hands`** (camera MediaPipe-Hands → three.js phase-space — the directest literal answer to jury #4 "two gestures set incommensurable tempi": each hand conducts a voice; the freshest off-glass input vs the last 4 cycles; the most embodied/surprising). Both losers below are complete, build-reviewed real implementations (own folder, pure client-side, no API route, brick-wall limiter, hands-free silent auto-demo, graceful degradation); folders removed per the no-half-built-folders rule, banked here as ready-to-resurrect TEXT. Renumber on revival; max committed folder now **568**.

- **`569-conductor-batons` (tap-tempo, raw WebGL2 — the cleanest + most legible).** Two big multitouch thumb pads; tap each rhythmically → median-of-3 inter-tap interval sets that voice's tempo; voice B snaps to the nearest irrational ratio vs A. A **scrolling Nancarrow player-piano-roll** in a hand-written GLSL fullscreen fragment shader: two lanes drop a bright mark per scheduled beat, scrolling left at constant px/sec, the two columns **fanning apart forever** + a faint moiré interference band between lanes so the drift reads at a glance. "Tale of Two Clocks" dual look-ahead schedulers, limiter master chain, frame-one silent auto-demo at e÷2. **Why it lost (not quality):** finger-on-glass is the jury's flagged default-reflex lane, and tap-tempo is the least *embodied* of the three (vs jury #4's "embodied gestures"). **Why resurrect:** lowest-risk + most portable (no camera/sensor), and the literal scrolling-piano-roll is the MOST legible visualization of incommensurability in the trio — **a strong cycle-3 viz to borrow into 568** (swap/augment 568's abstract Lissajous ribbon for a scrolling roll), or ship standalone when finger-input clears the diversity audit. `page.tsx` ~22kB, `render.ts` (GLSL roll) + `audio.ts` complete.
- **`570-conductor-baton-motion` (DeviceMotion baton, three.js).** Wave the phone like a baton; `accelerationIncludingGravity` magnitude → high-pass + adaptive-threshold peak detector (180 ms debounce, median-of-3) extracts the conductor's pulse (voice A); voice B locked to base × a user-selectable irrational (φ default). Two glowing batons swing at the two periods tracing a **never-closing Lissajous/rose** in three.js; iOS `requestPermission()` fired synchronously in the Start click; tap-tempo fallback on desktop. **Why it lost (not quality):** device-motion repeats the *immediately prior* cycle's input modality (`566-kids-shake-band`, cycle 408) — the "too similar" cross-cycle trap; also tilt was recent (553/500). **Why resurrect:** the most phone-native of the three — Karel can literally wave his phone to conduct — so revive when device-motion is clear of the recent window, or as the mobile companion mode to 568. `page.tsx` ~18kB, `render.ts` (fixed a ring-buffer trail bug) + `motion.ts` + `audio.ts` complete.

## §411 — 2026-06-13 (UTC) · banked non-winners from the adult WIDE fire (cycle 411)

Cycle 411 ran **WIDE**: three orthogonal adult explorers, each clearing the ambition floor via different tags, none using the rolling-10-banned three.js OUTPUT (4×) or the standing JURY 2026-06-12 bans. Shipped `570-spoken-spell` (live speech recognition → SVG word-river → accreting linguistic canon; the directest answer to jury #2 "find sound in language, not a simulated physical field"). The other two are complete, build-reviewed real implementations (own folder, pure client-side, no API route, graceful degradation, hands-free auto-demo); folders removed per the no-half-built-folders rule, banked here as ready-to-resurrect TEXT. Renumber on revival; **max committed folder now 570.**

### 571-the-withholding — `queued` (banked WIDE non-winner, cycle 411 · mic-silence inverse-gate · minimal SVG · adult / conceptual-critical)
**What if there were a piece of music you could only hear by GIVING SOMETHING UP — a composition that exists only inside your restraint, playing only while the room stays silent and withdrawing the instant you make a sound?** The ordinary listener relationship (press play, receive) is inverted: the visitor must continuously *earn* the sound by withholding speech/movement/coughs. Mic `AnalyserNode` RMS read as an **inverse gate**: below a quiet threshold a 0..1 "depth" state integrates UP and the drone reveals more voices one by one (root → fifth → octave → third → upper partials, just-intonation above A2/110 Hz, multi-second `setTargetAtTime` swells); any sound above threshold makes depth DECAY and the upper voices fade — so the piece is audibly different after ~60s of sustained silence than at second 5. Minimal SVG: a single slowly-breathing aura that brightens as the music is "received" and contracts on interruption, a depth bar, voice indicators, and a kind `text-white/75` "you made a sound — it withdrew" notice for unmistakable cause/effect. Master `gain → lowpass → DynamicsCompressor → destination`, quiet + warm. Mic-denied OR noisy-room → an **"imagine the silence"** button raises depth on a slow timer so the full reveal still plays in a ~20s glance. **Named refs:** John Cage *4'33"* (1952); Christine Sun Kim (sound/silence and the cost of access); Manfred Werder / Wandelweiser (extreme quiet as material); Jem Finer *Longplayer* (patience as medium). **Ambition 2–3/5** (#2 ≥3 subsystems: inverse mic-gate + stateful integrating reveal + JI drone synth + SVG render; #3 named refs above; **#1 contestable** — a *conceptual/critical* category the lab has barely touched, but localStorage/state isn't a novel *technique*). **Why it didn't win (cycle 411):** the **strongest pure concept** of the three and the cleanest fill for the genuinely-missing conceptual/critical lane the jury's menu keeps daring (#3 non-screen-ish, #2 silence-not-physical-field) — but its payoff requires either a quiet room or pressing the demo button, making it the **least glance-legible** at a 06:30 silent phone review, where 570's auto-demo simply speaks-and-sings itself. **Resurrect FIRST** when a WIDE cycle wants the conceptual lane and language/voice is crowded — it is near-shippable. Self-flagged rough edges: `setTargetAtTime` called every RAF frame (throttle to ~100ms); breathing animation drives off `performance.now()` so it drifts vs the audio envelope (aesthetically OK, not intentional). Built files (removed): `page.tsx`, `audio.ts`, `README.md`.

### 572-orrery-tunings — `queued` (banked WIDE non-winner, cycle 411 · device-orientation gyro · raw WebGL2 aurora shader · adult / meditative-spatial)
**What if you held your phone up and slowly turned and tilted through the room to tune into invisible "stations" of a just-intonation harmonic sky — so navigating PHYSICAL space (where you point) navigates HARMONIC space (which pure-ratio chord you hear)?** `DeviceOrientationEvent` (alpha/beta/gamma; iOS 13+ `requestPermission()` inside the Start tap) places 7 just-intonation stations (1/1, 9/8, 5/4, 4/3, 3/2, 5/3, 15/8) at specific compass headings + tilts across the celestial sphere; the current heading/tilt picks a **great-circle inverse-distance-weighted blend** of the nearest stations → a smoothly crossfaded just chord (root osc + partial bank at EXACT rational ratios, `setTargetAtTime` ~0.3–0.6s crossfades). Holding still near a station → a clean beating-free pure chord + a "Lock %" climbs to 100; between stations → a richer tense blend. **Raw hand-written WebGL2 GLSL ES 3.00** fullscreen-triangle aurora whose brightest warped region tracks the tuned-in direction; compass-rose SVG HUD + station/ratio readout. Fallbacks: desktop pointer-drag "look around"; no-input auto-demo pans through all 7 stations; WebGL2-absent → `text-rose-300` + CSS-gradient that still shifts hue with heading. **Named refs:** La Monte Young & Marian Zazeela *Dream House* (a sustained-JI environment you move through); Pauline Oliveros *Deep Listening*; Maryanne Amacher (sound that changes as you move your head); Harry Partch / Ben Johnston (JI ratio lattices). **Ambition 2/5** (#2 ≥3 subsystems: orientation input + JI oscillator bank + IDW field math + WebGL2 shader; #3 named refs above; **no clean #1** — gyro [238/253/308/…], JI synthesis, and "a sound-room you move through" [394-soundfield-room/400-soundwalk-room/441/475/486/496/552] are all well-trodden in the lab). **Why it didn't win (cycle 411):** the **lowest ambition + most conceptually redundant** of the three (it's a navigable variant of the many soundfield/tilt/JI-room pieces), and the builder self-flags a **6-octave FBM domain-warp evaluated 2× for warp + 2× for the curtain** that "may drop frames on older iPads" — the exact device Karel reviews on. **Resurrect** only with a hard differentiator: derive the station headings from a *principled projection of the 5-limit lattice onto the sphere* (not artistic placement), and a much lighter shader (single-octave warp / precomputed gradient). Built files (removed): `page.tsx`, `audio.ts`, `field.ts`, `README.md`.

## §413 — 2026-06-13 (UTC) · banked non-winners from the adult WIDE fire (cycle 413)

Cycle 413 ran **WIDE**: three orthogonal adult explorers, each clearing the ambition floor via different tags, none using a JURY-2026-06-13-banned tag (three.js/SVG OUTPUT, mic/voice INPUT, onset/beat/tempo TECH, pentatonic-wash/adult-cerebral-puzzle VIBE). Shipped **`576-presence-room`** (webcam head-pose → Web Audio `AudioListener` → HRTF JI Dream-House field; the lab's first head-tracked binaural / first embodied-spatial piece — the directest answer to the jury's twice-repeated "zero embodied-spatial" gap). The other two are complete, build-reviewed real implementations (own folder, pure client-side, no API route, graceful degradation, hands-free auto-demo); folders removed per the no-half-built-folders rule, banked here as ready-to-resurrect TEXT. Renumber on revival; **max committed folder now 576.**

### 578-magnetosphere — `queued` (banked WIDE non-winner, cycle 413 · live NOAA space-weather data · WebGPU compute aurora · adult / awe-contemplative) — **RESURRECT FIRST (next non-adjacent-to-data adult cycle)**
**What if you could hear the Earth's invisible magnetic weather right now — the LIVE solar wind and planetary K-index turned into a warm, slowly-shifting choir, so a quiet sky sings calm and a geomagnetic storm makes it shimmer and swell?** Direct client-side `fetch()` of three CORS-open, no-key NOAA SWPC endpoints — planetary K-index (`services.swpc.noaa.gov/products/noaa-planetary-k-index.json`), solar-wind plasma (speed/density, `.../solar-wind/plasma-2-hour.json`), solar-wind mag (Bz/Bt, `.../solar-wind/mag-2-hour.json`) — newest-valid-row parsed, per-field **baked fallback** so it always plays (the 412 pattern: no route, no secret). Mapping: **Kp**→mode/tension (0–2 calm ionian → 3–4 lydian shimmer → 5+ storm adds a faint minor-second beating layer), **wind speed**→ostinato tempo/density, **density**→partial count/timbre, **Bz** (southward = storm-driver)→drone root warmth. A warm additive glass-bell/choir ostinato on a **Chris-Wilson look-ahead scheduler** with Eno-style recombination (minute 5 ≠ minute 1); master ends in a `DynamicsCompressor` limiter. Visual: **WebGPU compute** aurora (20k particles advanced in a WGSL compute pass, instanced additive splat render; data-driven hue/turbulence/flow), with a **fully-functional Canvas2D curtain fallback** when `navigator.gpu` is absent. Live HUD shows the real Kp / wind / Bz + timestamp + a LIVE-vs-sample badge; auto-fetches + auto-plays a gentle demo on load (one tap to unlock audio) — the **best phone-glance** of the three. **Named refs:** Andrea Polli (*Sonic Antarctica* / space-weather sonification); Semiconductor (*Brilliant Noise* — raw solar data); Brian Eno (generative recombination); NASA/CARISMA sonification lineage. **Ambition 3–4/5** (#1 first live geomagnetic/space-weather sonification AND a WebGPU-compute generative-data piece; #2 ≥3 subsystems: multi-endpoint live fetch + look-ahead scheduler synth + WebGPU compute aurora; #3 named refs; #5 ties WebGPU's now-cross-browser-shipped status, Safari 26 / Firefox 141). **Why it didn't win (cycle 413):** the **highest raw ambition + best glance**, and it would **revive WebGPU** (jury-starved at 1×, explicitly wanted back) — but its input modality (real-world-data API → generative music) **repeats the immediately prior cycle's `575-kids-sky-song`** (weather→generative), the cross-cycle "too similar" trap. **Resurrect FIRST** on the next adult cycle that is NOT adjacent to a data-sonification ship — it directly serves jury #5 (WebGPU) + the standing data-sonification menu, in a different register (invisible magnetosphere, adult, WebGPU) from 575. Built files (removed): `page.tsx`, `data.ts`, `audio.ts`, `gpu.ts`, `README.md`.

### 577-still-bloom — `queued` (banked WIDE non-winner, cycle 413 · device-motion stillness · audio-first + minimal Canvas2D · adult / warm-meditative)
**What if music only blooms when you go STILL — hold yourself (and your phone) calm and a warm drone slowly unfolds voice by voice; move and it gently recedes — so the piece rewards stillness, not action?** A "stillness depth" in [0,1] integrates UP when `DeviceMotionEvent.accelerationIncludingGravity` jitter (high-passed magnitude variance) is low and DECAYS on movement (desktop fallback: pointer-speed proxy). Depth crossings reveal an additive **just-intonation drone** voice-by-voice over a warm A2/110 Hz root (1/1 → 2/1 → 3/2 → 5/4 → 9/8 → 5/2 → 15/8), each with multi-second `setTargetAtTime` swells (throttled ~90ms — fixes 571's per-frame bug) + per-voice/filter drift LFOs so it never loops and is audibly fuller after 60s than at second 5. **Audio-first** (the 0× off-glass lane): only a minimal **Canvas2D** breathing warm aura + voice ring + depth bar. Master `gain → lowpass → DynamicsCompressor → destination`, quiet + warm. iOS `DeviceMotionEvent.requestPermission()` fired synchronously in the Start tap; denied/unavailable → pointer-stillness; neither → an "imagine you're still" auto-demo blooms within ~25s and cancels on real input. **Named refs:** Éliane Radigue (*Trilogie de la Mort* — extremely slow drone drift); Pauline Oliveros *Deep Listening*; La Monte Young (sustained tones). **Ambition 2/5** (#2 ≥3 subsystems: motion-stillness sensing + stateful integrating gate + long-form JI additive synth + Canvas2D aura; #3 named refs; no clean #1 — stillness-as-input is conceptual, not a novel *technique*). **Why it didn't win (cycle 413):** the **warmest + cleanest fill for the off-glass / audio-only lane** the jury keeps daring (#1 "spend a cycle off the glass entirely"), but the **lowest ambition** of the three and conceptually adjacent to banked **`571-the-withholding`** (mic-silence inverse-gate ↔ motion-stillness gate — same "earn the sound by restraint" spine, different sensor). **Resurrect** when an adult cycle wants the genuine audio-only lane and the silence/restraint concept hasn't shipped recently — give it a hard differentiator vs 571 (e.g. the stillness *teaches* a slow breath rhythm, or ties to real circadian/HRV-style data). Built files (removed): `page.tsx`, `audio.ts`, `motion.ts`, `README.md`.

## §414 banked seeds (cycle 414, KIDS DEEP — one concept, *a luminous elastic sheet you grab and stretch; its tension bends a CONTINUOUS warm chord*, via three renderer/interaction approaches; both losers build-complete [clean tsc+eslint in isolation], `rm -rf`'d after losing to `579-silk-choir`; resurrect by re-spawning the brief, renumber on revival — max committed folder now 579)

Cycle 414 fired a **kids DEEP** triple off ONE fresh concept — *grab a luminous sheet of light and pull it like silk; the membrane's tension (verlet / position-based dynamics) bends a warm choir/bowed-string chord — you bend the chord with your hands, you never tap a discrete note.* The directest break from JURY 2026-06-13's banned kids **pentatonic-tap-wash** (≈6 of 7 kids pieces are "tap → a discrete consonant note → pretty viz"), on the **jury-starved WebGPU** renderer it explicitly wanted back, with the warmth the jury keeps daring. All three share: a 2-D verlet/PBD elastic membrane, multi-touch grab/flick, **continuous `setTargetAtTime` pitch-bend** of a sustained just-intonation chord over A2/110Hz (NO note-tapping anywhere), kids-safe `gain→lowpass≤8k→compressor→destination` limiter, alive-from-frame-one shimmer + a silent idle auto-demo so the 06:30 glance sings with zero setup. Winner shipped = `579-silk-choir` (WebGPU compute solver + Canvas2D fallback + runtime hot-downgrade; sustained grab-stretch bends the chord up to ~5 semitones + brightening). Floor: honest **3/5** (#2 ≥3 subsystems + #3 Jakobsen verlet PBD / Ondes-Martenot-Theremin continuous-pitch lineage; #1 NOT claimed — verlet exists at 303 as 1-D strings; #5 NOT claimed — no <14-day bind).

### 580-silk-veil — `queued` (banked DEEP non-winner, cycle 414 · multi-touch grab-stretch · **Canvas2D** silk · kids / warm-tactile) — the bulletproof-iPad path
**Same concept as 579 (grab and pull a silk sheet; tension bends a continuous warm chord), rendered entirely in Canvas2D** — a CPU verlet solver (32×22 grid, 4 relaxation iterations) drawn as glowing translucent silk (additive gradient quad fills warming with stretch, warp/weft filament lines, a drifting specular highlight, grab halos), held to 60fps on a mid iPad; six column-regions each a voice of a JI major-add9 over A2, pitch gliding ~4 semitones with stretch + lowpass opening, via `setTargetAtTime` portamento. **Why it didn't win (cycle 414):** the **most reliable on every device** and a genuinely beautiful, warm, physical-feeling drape — but it is **Canvas2D-only, so it does NOT revive the jury-starved WebGPU** (the jury's explicit ask this cycle), whereas 579/581 run WebGPU *when available* AND fall back to Canvas2D, making them strictly better on that axis. **Resurrect** when the cycle's priority is maximum iPad reliability / the lowest-end-device path, or to ship as 579's more-portable sibling; it is the most bulletproof of the three. Built files (removed): `page.tsx`, `membrane.ts`, `audio.ts`, `render.ts`, `README.md`.

### 581-silk-ripple — `queued` (banked DEEP non-winner, cycle 414 · touch FLICK/slap → traveling waves · **WebGPU** + Canvas2D · kids / playful-splashy) — the dynamic-gesture variant
**Same membrane, different interaction: flick or slap the sheet and an impulse propagates as visible traveling WAVES that roll across the silk and reflect off the pinned edges over ~2s; as each crest rolls through a vertical band, that band's voice pitch + brightness glide up and back continuously with the local wave amplitude — so a flick sends an audible *bend* rippling through a 7-voice JI chord.** WebGPU compute solver (ping-pong displacement buffers, additive point-cloud render) + CPU/Canvas2D fallback. **Why it didn't win (cycle 414):** the **most immediately fun for a 4yo** (slap it, watch waves splash, hear the chord ripple) and it also revives WebGPU — but (a) it is a *variant* of the headline concept rather than the headline itself (the brief's core is "grab and PULL," which 579 IS), and (b) its bend is subtler (~2.2 semitones vs 579's ~5), so the continuous-pitch break from the pentatonic-tap is less pronounced and it reads slightly less warm/expressive at a silent glance. **Resurrect** for a playful/percussive kids fire, or fold its wave-propagation into 579 as a "flick to send a ripple through the held chord" gesture (the clean cycle-2 deepening — sustained-pull AND flick-ripple in one sheet). Built files (removed): `page.tsx`, `membrane.ts`, `gpu.ts`, `audio.ts`, `render.ts`, `README.md`.

## §416 — banked from the cycle 416 KIDS DEEP fire (whole-body camera instrument)

Two non-winners from the 416 "dance and the room sings" DEEP fire. Both are built, demoable, and were `rm -rf`'d after curating `582-kids-dance-sky` — resurrect from these specs (re-allocate fresh numbers).

### `kids-shadow-band` (built as 581, banked) — status: `queued`
Pure **Canvas2D frame-differencing** whole-body instrument, **zero deps, bulletproof on any iPad** (no model download, instant). Front camera → 160×120 offscreen diff → motion painted as warm additively-glowing colored light-trails (~0.5s fade) over a dark calm room — never a mirror photo. 6 vertical zones, each a warm voice; *where* you move picks the voice, *how much* drives loudness/brightness, stillness settles to a root+fifth JI-Lydian drone bed. Krueger *Videoplace* / Daniel Rozin "body as light." **Resurrect when:** MediaPipe pose proves flaky on Karel's real iPad (this is the no-download fallback path), OR as an adult-mode bulletproof Videoplace. Was a hair closer to the spatial-grid reflex than 582's semantic body mapping — the only reason it lost.

### `kids-light-ribbons` (built as 583, banked) — status: `queued`
**Canvas2D dense optical-flow** (16×12 grid, one-iteration Lucas-Kanade) → a **continuous body-bowed warm choir**: speed+upward motion glide the 5 voices up a JI-Lydian scale (log-space portamento via `setTargetAtTime`, never a clicked note), speed opens the lowpass, horizontal direction tilts the voicing; stillness → root+fifth+octave drone. Warm particles advected along the flow leave glowing ribbons. Ondes Martenot/Theremin continuous-pitch lineage + Krueger. Tension you **feel**, not solve — exactly the jury ask. **Resurrect when:** `579-silk-choir` (the other continuous-bent-warm-choir) is out of the last-10 diversity window — it lost THIS cycle only on adjacency to 579, not on quality. The freshest *musical* idea of the three.

### Banked §417 (cycle 417 ADULT DEEP non-winners — TEXT seeds, folders removed; renumber on revival, max folder now 583):
Both extend the jury-blessed + Karel-loved "his real piano as the seed, warm not puzzle" lineage; both lost ONLY on diversity-overlap with existing loved clusters, not on quality. Each is built-and-proven (lint/type-clean) and can be resurrected fast.

- **`584-piano-spectral-tide`** (phase-vocoder spectral FREEZE) — `queued`. Load + STFT Karel's real piano (fftSize 2048, 75% overlap, magnitude+phase per frame); drag a playhead along his timeline and wherever you pause the spectral frame **freezes** into a sustained, infinitely-held warm drone (additive bank of the top-48 magnitude bins as continuously-phased sine oscillators — no metallic frozen-FFT buzz); slow drag = a continuous time-stretched legato scrub through his harmonies; a vertical **depth** axis crossfades crisp-frozen ↔ lowpassed-veil. WebGL2 fragment-shader **spectral tide** (1-D spectrum texture → drifting warm indigo→violet→amber→rose strata + playhead light), Canvas2D fallback, idle auto-self-scrub. Refs: Flanagan & Golden (1966) / Mark Dolson "The Phase Vocoder: A Tutorial" (1986); Éliane Radigue / William Basinski *Disintegration Loops*. **Promising:** the warmest, most oceanic of the three; a true "stop time inside his chord" piece; nods his loved ocean/aurora cluster (107❤️/262❤️). **Caveat that lost it THIS cycle:** spectral-freeze/spectral-cloud overlaps his already-loved spectral cluster (`243-spectral-cloud`❤️, `267-spectral-drift`❤️, `321-spectral-flight`❤️) — too close to ship in the same window. **Resurrect** once that cluster is out of the 10-window, or fold in as a "freeze lens" on 583's corpus. Built files (removed): `page.tsx`, `audio.ts` (STFT), `vocoder.ts`, `gl.ts`, `README.md`.

- **`585-piano-grain-orrery`** (n-body physics → granular retrigger) — `queued`. Slice his piano into ~130 grains (each remembering source offset + brightness + RMS); each becomes a luminous **body orbiting a soft central attractor** in a 2-D gravity field (central pull + short-range repulsion). **Stir** with pointer/touch — a moving gravity well + a swirl impulse from drag velocity perturbs the orbits; a body **voices** its grain (panned by x, filtered by velocity/radius) when it swings near the core, the cursor, or crosses an outward sounding-ring. The music is the **emergent chorus** of his grains orbiting — dense while you stir, sparse and meditative when it settles (≤16 simultaneous voices, limiter). WebGL2 additive point-sprites with ping-pong feedback-texture motion trails; Canvas2D fallback; idle auto-demo. Refs: Curtis Roads *Microsound* / Xenakis grain-clouds; Ventrella/Mohr *Particle Life*; music-of-the-spheres orrery framing. **Promising:** the richest *visual* of the three (852-line render), the most "alive while idle," cosmic-but-intimate. **Caveat that lost it THIS cycle:** physics→granular is lovely but particle/n-body systems are heavily represented in the lab (`130-tsl-particle-compute`❤️, `236-particle-life-song`❤️, `262-aurora-particle`❤️) and "physics→retrigger" is less of a lab-first than 583's target-matched concatenation. **Resurrect** for a physics-forward fire, or as 583's "orrery lens." Built files (removed): `page.tsx`, `audio.ts`, `physics.ts`, `README.md`.

## §419 — 2026-06-14 (UTC) · two banked DEEP/WIDE explorers from cycle 419 (built-and-proven, fast to resurrect)

Cycle 419 ran **WIDE** (3 orthogonal adult explorers, all warm / off-finger / clean on the 2026-06-13 jury bans). Shipped `588-tidal-organ` (live Open-Meteo marine swell → just-intonation sea-organ drone over a WebGPU ocean field — the directest hit on jury provocation #2, real-world-data "music about something other than music"). The two non-winners were fully built, lint/type-clean in isolation, then `rm -rf`'d (never committed). Both are strong; resurrect by re-spawning from these seeds + the prior folder structure.

- **`589-still-bloom` (banked, was demoable):** an instrument that rewards **stillness**. Input = the visitor's bodily quiet via **DeviceMotion** (gravity-subtracted high-pass of accel + rotation → smoothed motion-energy → a slow "stillness accumulator"). Hold the device perfectly still and a glacial **Éliane-Radigue / La Monte Young Dream-House** just-intonation drone (root ~61.7 Hz; partials 1/1·3/2·2/1·9/4·5/2·3/1·15/4 revealed one-by-one) blooms voice-by-voice; the smallest tremor drops the accumulator and the chord recedes on a multi-second release. Visual = **WebGPU Gray-Scott reaction-diffusion** "coral that grows the stiller you are" (256² ping-pong compute, feed/kill driven by bloom), Canvas2D additive-bloom fallback. Pointer-stillness + ~22 s auto-demo breath so a motionless silent glance still hears it. **Why it didn't win:** strong + warm + off-glass, but it resurrects a banked §413 seed and overlaps the already-touched stillness/withholding lane (cf. `571-the-withholding`) — and the jury's loudest *named* adult ask was real-world-DATA, which 588 hit head-on. **Resurrect when:** a cycle wants an off-glass/embodied or WebGPU-compute piece and the data lane is fresh. Honest 3/5 (#2 ≥3 subsystems: motion sensing + JI drone + GPU RD + bloom envelope · #3 Radigue/Young/Gray-Scott · soft #1 first stillness-rewarding instrument).

- **`590-star-atlas` (banked, was demoable):** **aim your phone at the real sky and play the actual stars overhead** — a live, embodied **John Cage *Atlas Eclipticalis* (1961)**. Bundled ~120-bright-star catalog `{name, ra, dec, mag, hue}` + self-contained positional astronomy (`astro.ts`: Julian Date → GMST → LST → hour-angle → alt/az, Meeus low-precision) from `navigator.geolocation` + clock; **DeviceOrientation** (compass alpha = azimuth, beta = altitude) chooses the view window; stars inside it become soft sustained voices (altitude/center-proximity → loudness + lowpass; magnitude → gain; pitch quantized to a JI/harmonic chord over a low D2 root, 12-voice cap). Canvas2D star-field with magnitude-sized hue-tinted points, constellation lines (Orion/Plough/Cassiopeia/Crux/Gemini/Leo/Scorpius/Bootes/Cygnus/Pegasus), "now sounding" halos, az/alt readout. Pointer-drag + idle auto-pan + default 37°N/122°W so a silent glance hears a drifting star-chord within ~2.5 s. **Why it didn't win:** the most *surprising* of the three and the best named reference, but it renders on Canvas2D (not the jury-starved WebGPU), astronomy already exists in the lab (`347-the-place`), and it isn't live-data-API sonification (bundled catalog), so it doesn't answer provocation #2 the way 588 does. **Resurrect when:** a cosmic / "Cosmic Homecoming" cycle wants an embodied off-glass piece with a literary score-reference; could be upgraded to WebGPU and to a live catalog fetch. Honest 3/5 (#2 ≥3 subsystems: orientation sensing + alt/az astronomy + JI voice bank + Canvas render · #3 Cage *Atlas Eclipticalis* · soft #1 first sky-aimed instrument).

## §421 — 2026-06-14 (UTC) · two banked DEEP non-winners from cycle 421 (the disintegration fire)

Cycle 421 ran **DEEP** — one concept ("a recording that audibly *crumbles* as it plays, with genuine accumulating state, and one lever between letting-go and holding-on"; refs **William Basinski _The Disintegration Loops_ 2002** + **Music Thing Modular "Degenerator"** self-overwriting looper, Synthtopia 2026-05-28) attacked by three techniques. Shipped `594-disintegration-loop` (the literal **self-overwriting tape**). Both non-winners were fully built, lint/TS-clean in isolation, then `rm -rf`'d (never committed). Both are strong and lost only on diversity-overlap with Karel's loved clusters, not on quality — resurrect by re-spawning from these seeds (renumber on revival; max committed folder now 594).

### `595-disintegration-spectral` ("Fading Photograph") — `queued` (spectral-domain erosion · Canvas2D · adult / elegiac)
**The same disintegration, in the FREQUENCY domain.** A warm JI chord (~130.81 Hz C3 root, ratios 1, 9/8, 5/4, 3/2, 15/8, 2, weighted low for body) held as a bank of `OscillatorNode`+`GainNode` partials. Three persistent arrays survive every frame and are mutated, never rebuilt — `oxide[i]` (0..1 surviving coating per partial), `detune[i]` (cents of accumulated drift), `live[i]` (eased render value) — so damage compounds. Each pass (every 2–5.5 s, cadence set by the lever) irreversibly attenuates bins (highs first, loss spreading downward), adds slow per-bin detune drift that grows as oxide is lost, and lets survivors flicker; at the very top of the lever a tiny stochastic re-seed can lift a near-dead bin a fraction but never restores it. Visual = a scrolling **spectrogram** driven directly by the live map (freq vertical, magnitude as ember→cream brightness fading to ash) — a developing print run in reverse. Master `gain → DynamicsCompressor → destination`; Begin-in-gesture; idle auto-run pulls the lever toward *let go* so a silent glance shows the field thinning. Refs: Basinski 2002 + Music Thing Degenerator 2026-05-28. **Why it didn't win:** beautiful and the cleanest "watch the photograph fade," but spectral-domain treatment overlaps Karel's already-loved spectral cluster (`243-spectral-cloud`❤️, `267-spectral-drift`❤️, `321-spectral-flight`❤️) and the banked `584-piano-spectral-tide` — too close to ship in the same window (exactly why 584 was banked). **Resurrect when:** the spectral cluster is out of the rolling-10 window, or fold in as a "fade lens" on his real piano. Honest 3/5 (#2 ≥3 subsystems · #3 Basinski/Degenerator · soft #1). Built files (removed): `page.tsx`, `README.md`.

### `596-disintegration-dust` ("Dust") — `queued` (granular crumble · **WebGPU** compute particles + Canvas2D fallback · adult / elegiac) — the WebGPU-spectacle path
**The same disintegration, GRANULAR.** A warm JI phrase (1/1, 5/4, 3/2, 15/8, octave over ~88 Hz) rendered once into an `AudioBuffer`, read back as overlapping raised-cosine grains panned across the field. A single monotonic `wear` variable climbs a little on **every grain** and only ever increases; from it the engine derives grain **density** (~38 → ~3 /s), **duration** (0.32 → 0.05 s), **pitch jitter/de-pitch** (grows), and a **drop-out fraction** (grows), so the cloud genuinely thins from a continuous texture to sparse motes over ~5 min. The lever (top *hold on* slows the fall + lends back ≤0.22 recoverable density; bottom *let go* accelerates) can never drop below the accumulated wear floor — it slows the fall, can't undo what flaked away. Visual: each surviving grain spawns a glowing mote via the real per-grain callback (dropped grains spawn nothing), so the picture is the audio state. A **WebGPU compute-particle renderer** (`gpu.ts` — storage-buffer particles integrated in a compute pass, drawn additively; feature-detected with a minimal local typing surface, no `@webgpu/types` dep) runs when an adapter is present, falling back cleanly to a warm Canvas2D mote field (weighted as the primary aesthetic). Master `gain → slow lowpass → DynamicsCompressor → destination`; idle auto-start. Refs: Basinski 2002 + Music Thing Degenerator 2026-05-28; Curtis Roads *Microsound* / Xenakis grain-clouds. **Why it didn't win:** the richest *visual* of the three and it revives the jury-starved WebGPU — but granular-into-particles overlaps Karel's loved particle/granular cluster (`227-paths-granular`❤️, `236-particle-life-song`❤️, `262-aurora-particle`❤️, `130`❤️) and is the least distinct on the TECH axis. **Resurrect when:** a cycle wants a WebGPU spectacle / the renderer well, or as 594's "dust lens." Honest 3/5 (#2 ≥3 subsystems · #3 Basinski/Degenerator/Roads · soft #1). Built files (removed): `page.tsx`, `gpu.ts`, `audio.ts`, `README.md`.

## §430 — 2026-06-15 (UTC) · two banked WIDE non-winners from cycle 430 (KIDS) — built-and-proven, lint/tsc-clean in isolation, fast to resurrect (renumber on revival; max committed folder now 619)

Cycle 430 ran **WIDE** (3 orthogonal kids explorers, fired to break the silly-googly-creature 3-peat 603/609/617). Shipped `619-kids-plinko-bells` (the Galton-board / quincunx emergent instrument — the only clean ambition-#1 of the three). Both non-winners were fully built, demoable, and self-reported clean tsc + ESLint in isolation; the full 467/467 build passed with all three present, so both are confirmed buildable. `rm -rf`'d after losing (twice — the §417 re-materialization race recurred). Resurrect by re-spawning from these seeds + the prior folder structure.

### `620-kids-music-mill` ("Music Mill") — `queued` (tap-to-place pegs · **WebGL2** rotating music-box cylinder · Karplus-Strong pluck · kids / maker-mechanical-wonder, self-playing)
**What if a 4-year-old could BUILD a music box and watch it play itself forever?** Tap to plant glowing pegs around a slowly-turning glowing wheel (one turn ~6–10s); concentric **rings = pitch** (7 rings, inner=low), positions around the wheel = **time steps** (16). As the wheel turns, every peg crossing the striker wedge (12 o'clock) is plucked into a warm note; tap a peg to remove it — nothing is destroyed by accident, nothing is ever "wrong." A **rotating-drum step-sequencer reimagined as a music-box / barrel-organ cylinder** the child authors live: the loop plays itself and reshapes as pegs are added/removed. Warm **Karplus-Strong** plucks (~6ms attack, exp decay) baked per-ring into `AudioBuffer`s for instant placement; slowly-detuning ambient bed; kid-safe master chain (lowpass 7.5k → comp −16/6/12). JI Lydian-ish (`1/1, 9/8, 5/4, 4/3, 3/2, 5/3, 2/1` over F3), NOT pentatonic; per-ring bold hue (violet→indigo→sky→teal→green→amber→pink). Raw **WebGL2** (GLSL ES 3.00, full-screen wheel/striker shader + instanced glowing pegs flaring white-hot at the striker) + Canvas2D fallback + backend badge. Seeded melody on load so the wheel turns + plucks within ~2s (silent-glance self-play). Files: `page.tsx`, `sequencer.ts`, `audio.ts`, `gl.ts`, `README.md`. **Why it didn't win (cycle 430):** the strongest *constructive / self-play loop* toy and love-aligned to Karel's loop cluster (`172-loop-station`❤️, `111-kids-shape-loop`❤️) — but honest **2/5**: a step-sequencer is **not a lab-first** (overlaps the loved loop-builders) and Karplus-Strong overlaps `140-kids-string-bridge`❤️, whereas 619's Galton physics is a clean grep-0× #1. **Resurrect when:** a kids "maker / build-your-own-loop" cycle wants a self-playing constructive instrument, or as a charming mechanical re-skin of the loop-station lineage. Honest 2/5 (#2 ≥3 subsystems: rotating-drum sim + striker-strike sequencer + Karplus-Strong pluck synth + WebGL2 render · #3 Swiss cylinder music box 1796 / barrel organ / Wintergatan).

### `621-kids-duet-bloom` ("Duet Bloom") — `queued` (TWO-finger cooperative multi-touch · **Canvas2D** luminous meadow · cooperative-consonance harmony · kids / social-blooming) — the cooperative two-player lane
**What if two little kids — or a parent + child — had to COOPERATE to make a garden bloom?** Each player drags their own glowing flower vertically (violet left / amber right; tracked independently by `Touch.identifier`, two pointers/handles on desktop) to set its **gliding** singing pitch (`setTargetAtTime` portamento — a sung tone, never a clicked note; each voice pleasant alone over an ambient bed). The interval between the two voices is measured continuously in cents (`harmony.ts`) and compared to a just consonance table (1/1, 6/5, 5/4, 4/3, 3/2, 5/3, 2/1); as they approach a consonant ratio a **magnetic snap-assist** pulls the held flower toward it and the meadow brightens, so a 4-year-old can FEEL the "click" of locking in (±45¢ lock, ±130¢ cue). On a lock a warm shared triad swells, the flowers lean toward each other along a strand of light, and a wave of glowing flowers opens outward from the midpoint (`bloom.ts`, Canvas2D, ≤140 particles for ~60fps). Drift apart → the bloom gently recedes (never a fail sound, just *less light*). Single-player + idle: a slow **ghost partner** auto-hunts consonance so a lone child (or a silent 06:30 glance within ~2.5s) still sees the meadow bloom; real touch preempts instantly. Kid-safe master chain (lowpass 7k → comp −16/6/12). Files: `page.tsx`, `harmony.ts`, `audio.ts`, `bloom.ts`, `README.md`. **Why it didn't win (cycle 430):** the **freshest interaction of the three** — cooperative two-player is a genuinely thin, developmentally-valuable kids lane (group-synchrony / joint-attention pedagogy) — but it renders **Canvas2D** (the standing JURY-14-flagged renderer) and its cooperative-consonance CORE overlaps adult `508-accord-call`, and it's honest **2/5** vs 619's clean #1. **Resurrect when:** a kids **social / two-player / multi-touch** cycle wants the cooperation lane (it's the cleanest fill of that 0×-ish gap), or upgrade its render off Canvas2D. Honest 2/5 (#2 ≥3 subsystems: multi-touch tracking + cooperative-consonance harmony engine + bloom envelope + Canvas2D meadow · #3 group-synchrony pedagogy / cooperative-resolution model / Pythagorean consonance).

---

## §431 — 2026-06-15 (UTC) · two banked DEEP non-winners from cycle 431 (ADULT) — **CAUTIONARY: both duplicate existing prototypes; do NOT resurrect as-is** (max committed folder now 622)

Cycle 431 ran **DEEP**: one concept (cellular automata as an autonomous composer) attacked by 3 CA paradigms. Shipped `622-wolfram-rhythm` (1D elementary / Wolfram — the only **grep-0×** sub-lane). The two non-winners were fully built + demoable, but the curate-step corpus grep caught that **both duplicate prototypes already in the lab** — so they are banked as cautionary records, not enthusiastic seeds. The umbrella concept ("CA-as-composer") is *not* fresh; only un-mined CA *paradigms* are.

### `620-life-engine` — `do-not-rebuild` (Conway's Game of Life → scanning-playhead composer · WebGL2 instanced + Canvas2D fallback · adult / severe-mechanical)
2D Conway Life on a 64×40 toroidal board; a vertical playhead scans L→R and any live cell it crosses fires a metallic FM-bell note (row→inharmonic pitch), so oscillators become ostinati and gliders become traveling motifs. Long-form: a rolling population+hash window detects extinction / short-period loops / stalls and injects fresh material (glider, LWSS, pulsar, R-pentomino, Gosper gun, soup). FM bells with √2-ish inharmonic ratios, stretched-octave Dorian-with-tritone, 14-voice steal-oldest, limiter. WebGL2 instanced quads with birth-glow/death-fade + Canvas2D fallback. **Why it lost / DO NOT REBUILD:** it is a near-duplicate of **`25-cellular`** AND **`180-cellular`** — both are already "Conway's Life as musical composer" (column→pitch, every column with a live cell sounds per tick). Shipping it would be the exact 580/588-ocean / 549-569 duplication the jury keeps flagging. **If anything is salvageable:** the *severe FM-bell / inharmonic sound design* could be a polish-pass re-voicing of the existing `25`/`180` (which use plain triangle waves) — but that's a polish cycle on those, not a new build.

### `622-quickening` (Lenia) — `do-not-rebuild` (continuous-state CA → organism-voice · WebGL2 R8 ping-pong + Canvas2D fallback · adult / organic-uncanny)
Lenia (Bert Chan 2019) continuous CA on a 150² grid — canonical orbium kernel/growth/dt, published orbium seed glides smoothly; field descriptors (mass, centroid, velocity, spread, alive) drive an inharmonic-partials + formant-bandpass + motion-gated-breath voice (deliberately not a warm chord), audio smoothing decoupled from the visual rAF; keep-alive reseed/bleed. **Why it lost / DO NOT REBUILD:** the builder reused the Lenia step + R8 render **verbatim from `264-kids-lenia-pond`** (which already ships Lenia orbium creatures that sing) and said so — it's a straight duplicate of an existing prototype, just adult-skinned. **If anything is salvageable:** the *field→sound descriptor mapping* (mass/centroid/velocity/spread → an inharmonic organism voice) is a more sophisticated sonification than 264's, so it could be a polish-pass on `264` — not a new folder.

**Standing adult lesson (carried to §432+):** the genuinely 0× adult lane after this cycle is **off-screen / audio-only / embodied-spatial**, BUT the spatial-audio back-catalog is occupied (`80-room-acoustic` image-source, `394-soundfield-room` ambisonics, `400-soundwalk-room` 6DoF+haptics, `576-presence-room` head-binaural) — so any new spatial piece must bring a **fresh technique AND a distinct concept**, not another presence-room. And: **grep the corpus for the CONCEPT, not just the technique name, and never trust a `grep -E` with `\|`** (a false-negative there nearly shipped a duplicate this cycle).

## §432 — 2026-06-15 (UTC) · two banked DEEP non-winners from cycle 432 (KIDS) — **both fully built + demoable; same concept ("A Whole Day"), different renderer; resurrect either as a renderer-variant or a dedicated fire** (max committed folder now 624)

Cycle 432 ran **DEEP**: ONE concept — the lab's first kids **long-form generative journey-arc with state/memory** ("A Whole Day": a ~9-min self-evolving dawn→day→dusk→night→dawn journey; a diurnal phase state-machine + a motif-memory bank that re-voices each planted creature's abstract scale-degrees into the current region's scale, anchor-style per arXiv 2604.05343, so it coheres yet differs at minute 8 vs minute 1) — attacked by 3 non-GPU renderers (the diversity audit banned WebGPU 5× + WebGL2 4×). Shipped **`624-kids-day-meadow`** (Canvas2D — most legible/warm/bulletproof for a 4yo). Both losers were demoable + self-reported clean tsc/eslint; each shares the SAME `engine.ts` (diurnal phases + motif memory) + kid-safe `audio.ts`, so they are cheap to revive.

### `623-kids-day-journey` — `queued` (the "massively bigger" 3D version · **three.js** immersive day-world + Canvas2D fallback · kids 4+ / calm-awe)
Same "A Whole Day" engine, rendered as an immersive **three.js** (v0.182) 3D world: a gradient sky **dome** whose vertex colors lerp with phase, glowing **sun & moon spheres traveling a circular arc**, low-poly parallax hills, soft fog, a star field that fades in at night, planted things as glowing low-poly meshes (cone flowers / octahedron birds / icosahedron stars), gentle idle camera drift; geometries/materials disposed on unmount; **Canvas2D fallback** if WebGL/three fails (still day-cycles + sings). `page.tsx` 878 / `engine.ts` 235 / `audio.ts` 202. **Why it lost:** the concept's ambition is renderer-independent, so the renderer should serve the audience — and for a calm pre-sleep 4yo a 3D abstract world reads the day-arc *less* legibly than a side-on landscape, plus it re-introduces a GPU failure surface the recent ship-streak is saturated with. **Resurrect when:** GPU renderers cool in the rolling-10 and you want the bolder immersive swing (3D is a loved kids lane — `537-kids-sky-murmuration` three.js was a gem); or as a "stand-inside-the-day" companion. The strongest "massively bigger" resurrect of this pair.

### `625-kids-day-paper` — `queued` (the calmest version · **inline SVG** matte cut-paper storybook · kids 4+ / contemplative)
Same engine, rendered as matte **cut-paper inline SVG**: layered paper hills with `feDropShadow`, a sun/moon on an arc path, a sky `<linearGradient>` whose stops lerp with phase, paper stars fading in at night, friendly paper creatures — NO glow/canvas/WebGL; per-frame attrs driven through element refs (no React churn per frame). `page.tsx` 645 / `engine.ts` 415 / `audio.ts` 187. **Why it lost:** warmest/calmest register but the flattest reading of a day-journey (no depth, less motion-energy at a glance) vs 624's painterly diorama. **Resurrect when:** you want the storybook/bedtime register explicitly, or to give SVG (≈0× recently) a fresh entry. 

**Kids lesson (carried forward):** the binding diversity audit (rolling-10) had inverted away from the jury's stale 2026-06-14 Canvas2D ban — by cycle 432 the lab had over-corrected INTO GPU (WebGPU 5× + WebGL2 4× of last 10), making **Canvas2D the scarce renderer**. Don't reflexively apply an old jury ban once the rolling window has moved; re-run the audit each cycle. And: **funny went 3× in a row (603/609/617)** before 619/624 broke it — vary the kids emotional register deliberately (calm/awe/contemplative, not only silly).

## §435 banked seeds (2026-06-15) — two strong missing-middle explorers from the WIDE fire; NEITHER is a duplicate — resurrect both

Context: cycle 435 was an adult **WIDE** fire attacking JURY 2026-06-15 provocation #2 (the missing middle — danceable/groovy/tender; the lab has 630 prototypes and *zero* in a groove register). Three registers were built; `632-polymeter-loom` (hypnotic Reich/Euclidean) shipped. These two lost on margins (output diversity + honest-ambition + vibe-threading), NOT on quality — both are real future builds.

### `631-drop-engine` — `queued` (euphoric EDM build-and-drop journey engine · Canvas2D · adult / ecstatic)
A generative festival arc: kick on every beat; bass+pad bus routed through a dedicated `pumpBus` GainNode that **ducks on each kick and ramps back** — the signature EDM **sidechain "pump"** (verified **grep-0× in the lab** — a genuine lab-first technique, #1-eligible); swung noise hats; a 3→7-voice detuned **supersaw** lead on the drop; a white-noise riser sweeping pitch+volume through the build; lowpass filter automation; reverb tail in the breakdown. Auto-advances **BUILD → DROP → BREAKDOWN → loop** on the audio clock (long-form state — minute 3 ≠ minute 1). One "Energy" lever (slider + keyboard ↑/↓/+/−) biases climb speed / filter brightness / drop density; Space = manual drop. Canvas2D rings/core/strobing-bars/particle-burst pulses 1:1 on the shared pump value. Builder self-reported tsc+ESLint clean (~28 KB single file). **Why it lost:** Canvas2D output (632's three.js was the scarcer renderer); the drop *visual* is the most conventional of the three (EDM visualizers are everywhere). **Why resurrect:** it is the **most literal match to a Karel-named directive** — "journey engine alternatives: EDM build-and-drop" (his care #4) — AND it carries the lab's **first sidechain-pump** (#1 grep-0×), so it can claim an honest **#1 + #2** = a clean floor-clear. The euphoric register is the most viscerally "off the doom autopilot." **Resurrect next adult cycle** unless a fresher #1 appears; pair the pump with a bolder drop visual (three.js particle wall or feedback bloom) to lift it past 3/5.

### `633-slow-burn` — `queued` (tender neo-soul microtiming groove · Canvas2D · adult / sensual — WATCH the warm-ban)
~75 BPM lo-fi/neo-soul vamp. The heart is a **microtiming engine**: a 25ms lookahead scheduler queues 16th-notes ~120ms ahead on the audio clock, and each voice carries a per-voice timing offset (snare **late**/laid-back, hats **early**/pushed, kick **dead-on**) scaled by a global **"feel/looseness"** lever from 0 (robotic) through "in the pocket" to "too loose" — toggling it makes the beat audibly start to *breathe*. Palette: soft kick, brushed-noise snare, lazy hats, gliding sub bass, **FM Rhodes** (sine carrier + 1:1 sine modulator, decaying tine) on a smoky Fm9→Bbm9→Eb9→Abmaj9 vamp; vinyl crackle + lowpass haze. **Reharmonization state machine** drifts 9ths/walking-bass/fills over the minutes (living vamp, not a loop). The standout: a Canvas2D viz that draws the **grid vs. where each hit actually landed** — a hollow anchor at the grid time, a line out to the actual onset, a glowing dot — making microtiming **visible**. Refs: *Microtiming Deviations and Swing Feel in Jazz* (Sci. Rep. 2019) + Dan Charnas, *Dilla Time* (2022). Builder self-reported tsc+ESLint clean (~30 KB). **Why it lost:** the tender/sensual register is the most genuinely absent corner — but it sits adjacent to the jury-**banned** "warm/cozy" vibe (warm went 5× and got banned), so shipping it this cycle risked reading as the very thing the jury is tired of; and Canvas2D (vs 632's scarcer three.js). **Why resurrect:** it's the lab's **only tender/sensual candidate** and the **grid-vs-actual visualization is the most original idea of the three** (makes an invisible musical mechanism legible — high Karel-surprise). **Resurrect when** the rolling-10 is cold on warmth so it can't be misread as cozy-autopilot, OR lean it harder into "grown/after-hours/sensual" (more dissonance, sparser, darker palette) to distance it from kids-warm. The microtiming engine itself is reusable infrastructure for any future groove piece.

**WIDE lesson (carried forward):** when the jury names a *vibe* gap (here: danceable/groove), the cleanest WIDE fan-out is **three registers of that one gap** (euphoric / hypnotic / tender), each via a different *mechanism* (sidechain-pump / Euclidean-phasing / microtiming) — it *maps* the gap instead of scattering across unrelated concepts. And: find the mechanism that produces the vibe ("build joyful" is unactionable; "build interlocking microtiming + sidechain energy" is buildable).
