## §436 — 2026-06-15 · kids WIDE fire (non-touch, "missing middle" register) — 2 banked seeds

Cycle 436 ran a kids **WIDE** fan-out of 3 orthogonal NON-TOUCH explorers (honoring JURY 2026-06-15 provocation #4: the touch ban) in the active/wonder *middle* register the jury says the kids side lacks (it's stuck "warm-or-silly with nothing between"). **Winner: `636-kids-star-pilot`** (gamepad → musical star-gates + loop-memory). The two strong losers, banked for resurrection:

### `634-kids-tilt-river` — `queued` (tilt → flow-field light river → tuned chimes · three.js · kids / mesmerizing-flow wonder)
A 4-year-old **tilts the iPad** (DeviceOrientation `beta`/`gamma` → gravity vector) to pour hundreds of glowing "light droplets" (a `THREE.Points` additive-blended river) down a screen governed by a smooth **curl/value-noise flow field** + the steered gravity. Six glowing **chime gates** tuned to a JI-pentatonic-ish scale flash + ring soft additive struck-bell tones as droplets stream through — the flow is stochastic so the melody is emergent and never repeats. 4 subsystems (tilt→gravity · flow-field+particle integrator · struck-bell synth · three.js instanced render) + named ref (Tyler Hobbs flow fields · Wintergatan/Iwai lineage) = honest 2/5. Arrow-key + auto-rotating-gravity fallback, on-mount visual auto-demo, kid-safe `lowpass→compressor` chain with 40ms global + 90ms/gate rate limit. Builder reported the code build-clean (could not run `npm run build` — no node_modules in its sandbox). **Why it lost:** the most *beautiful* of the three (three.js light river), but "emergent chime melody from falling particles" overlaps the already-shipped `619-kids-plinko-bells` (Galton) and `169-kids-marble-run` — the freshest part is only the tilt+flow-field steering, not the core. **Why resurrect:** the tilt-river/flow-field steering IS a distinct, loved input (83-tilt-rain❤️, 169-marble-run❤️) and three.js is renderer-scarce; resurrect when the rolling-10 is clear of emergent-chime pieces, or push it toward a non-chime payoff (the river *paints*, or carves terrain) so it stops reading as "619 with tilt." Full working code exists at the folder path in git history if useful, but treat as a fresh build. **NOTE:** the builder left orphan files `audio.ts`/`flow.ts` (superseded, not imported) — drop them on any resurrection.

### `635-kids-sing-kite` — `queued` (mic voice-PITCH → kite altitude + auto-harmony · Canvas2D · kids / soaring-joy)
A 4-year-old **sings or hums**; a real-time **McLeod-Pitch-Method** detector (`detectPitchMPM`, NSDF/autocorrelation, McLeod & Wyvill 2005) maps their fundamental pitch to a glowing **kite's altitude** on a painterly Canvas2D sky — sing high, it climbs; hum low, it dips — loudness drives glow/size, a fading pitch-hued trail follows. The sky **harmonizes back**: the sung note snaps to a C-major-pentatonic degree and a soft pad sustains a consonant 3rd/4th below, so the child is always in tune; sparkle-chime reward at the top. 4 subsystems (mic+MPM pitch detect · pitch→altitude+scale-snap · auto-harmonizing pad synth · Canvas2D sky/kite/trail) + named ref (MPM 2005 · Iwai Electroplankton) = honest 2/5. Pointer + Up/Down-arrow fallback, idle auto-drift demo, kid-safe master chain. Builder reported build-clean. **Why it lost:** strong + universally demoable ("your voice flies the kite" — every kid can sing), and a meaningful *reframe* of mic-input from the recent silly-loudness toys (603/609) to musical-PITCH — but mic was the input 2× in the last 10, where 636's gamepad is 0× recently (the bigger diversity swing), and 636 carries genuine evolving state (loop-memory) where this is more reactive. **Why resurrect:** the cleanest "joyful middle" concept the lab has, very high kid-delight, and the pitch→altitude mapping is reusable. Resurrect next kids cycle when mic is cold in the rolling-10. **NOTE:** the builder left orphan files `music.ts`/`pitch.ts` (the page.tsx is self-contained) — drop them or wire them in on resurrection.

---

## §437 — 2026-06-15 · adult WIDE fire (3 registers of the jury's named "missing middle") — 2 banked seeds

Cycle 437 ran an adult **WIDE** fan-out of 3 orthogonal corners of the missing MIDDLE register the jury (2026-06-15) named — ecstatic/danceable/groovy/tender — each via a DIFFERENT mechanism × renderer × ref (the "WIDE lesson": map a named vibe-gap). **Winner: `637-slow-burn`** (tender/sensual neo-soul + SVG grid-vs-actual microtiming plot). NB the jury's "0× lanes" (MIDI, multi-user/WebRTC) and "nobody built EDM build-drop" all turned out FALSE on corpus check (Web MIDI 12 files; `508-accord-call` WebRTC; `387-drop-engine` EDM) — so the §435-banked `631-drop-engine` was REJECTED as a 387-duplicate, and these two are the genuine fresh corners. Both lost on margins (renderer/viz duplication), NOT quality.

### `638-gospel-lift` — `queued` (ecstatic/euphoric gospel-house · three.js · adult / hands-in-the-air — a journey-engine alternative DISTINCT from 387)
A 122 BPM Chicago/gospel-house groove (four-on-the-floor kick, off-beat open hats, congregation handclaps, Hammond/Leslie drawbar organ, Rhodes comping a gospel **ii–V–I with passing diminished chords**). The signature mechanism: every **16 bars the WHOLE progression modulates UP one semitone** — the church "modulation"/"lift" — re-voicing the chords, stepping pad brightness, firing a short riser, **octave-folding the root** when it climbs too high so it stays in range while the perceived ascent continues. **There is NO drop** — the euphoria comes purely from continuous upward harmonic modulation (the explicit anti-EDM-build framing; this is why it's distinct from `387-drop-engine`'s GROOVE→BUILD→DROP→RELEASE). A **PRAISE/intensity** lever (↑/↓ + slider) stacks tambourine 16ths / octave bass / soprano choir "oohs" / faster Leslie vibrato; Enter forces an early lift. three.js ascending light column (stacked additive rings + emissive core + sparks + halo, gold→white, beat-pulse + lift-flash, camera drifts upward with cumulative key-step) + Canvas2D fallback + synthetic-heartbeat so the idle ~1.8 s auto-demo's VISUAL is alive even with audio suspended. 4 subsystems (house look-ahead scheduler · gospel reharm/key-lift state machine · multi-voice organ/Rhodes/choir synth · three.js renderer) + named refs (gospel "shout"/"drive" + semitone-modulation tradition — Hezekiah Walker, Kirk Franklin; Chicago/gospel-house — Frankie Knuckles) = honest 2–3/5. `page.tsx` ~1308 lines, build-clean by hand-review (no node_modules in builder sandbox). **Why it lost:** three.js re-introduces GPU verification risk (the recurring build-verified-not-browser-verified surface), AND an ascending-light-column reads as a more conventional EDM-adjacent visualizer; 637's SVG was the scarcer renderer + the more surprising "legible mechanism." **Why resurrect:** it's the lab's strongest **ecstatic/euphoric** middle entry AND a genuine journey-engine alternative (Karel care #4) that is NOT an EDM build-drop — fills a real open corner. Resurrect next adult fire when GPU renderers are cold in the rolling-10, OR pair the key-lift mechanic with a non-GPU renderer (the lift reads fine on Canvas2D/SVG too). Full working code is in git history at the folder path; treat as a fresh build.

### `639-afro-pulse` — `queued` (joyful/danceable afrobeat-highlife · Canvas2D · adult / sunlit — REDO THE VIZ off rings)
~108–116 BPM afrobeat/highlife. A West-African **bell timeline** (gankogui two-pitch "standard pattern" over a 12-pulse cycle + a clave high stroke) keeps time while **two interlocking plucked melodic ostinati on coprime cycle lengths (7 vs 5 → 35-step melodic repeat)** ride call-and-response on alternating 8ths so they phase in/out as an audible **melodic weave**; a Tony-Allen-style kit (ghost-note hats, rim, kick), shaker 16ths, brassy horn stabs on accents. A **HEAT/intensity** lever (↑/↓ + slider) adds the 2nd guitar (>45%), horn stabs (>60%), denser hats; a variation/reharmonization state machine transposes the riff (0, +5, −2, +7…) + rotates horn voicings over 4-bar sections (living, not a loop). **Major-key, melodic, bright, danceable** — the explicit OPPOSITE of monochrome Reich/Euclidean minimalism (so distinct from `632-polymeter-loom`'s *audio*). 5 subsystems (bell-timeline · interlocking-ostinato Chris-Wilson scheduler · multi-voice pluck/kit/horn synth · variation state machine · Canvas2D renderer) + named refs (Fela Kuti / Tony Allen; West-African bell timeline — Kofi Agawu *Representing African Music*; ClaveNet ACM 2024) = honest 2–3/5. `page.tsx` ~950 lines, build-clean by hand-review. **Why it lost:** its Canvas2D viz is **concentric rotating rings with beads flashing on each onset** — which is a near-twin of the just-shipped `632-polymeter-loom`'s "loom of concentric bead rings that flash on each onset, phasing rings counter-rotating." Shipping it = a visual duplicate of last adult fire's winner. **Why resurrect:** the AUDIO is genuinely fresh (major-key melodic highlife — the joyful/danceable corner of the middle, distinct from 632's minimalism and 387's EDM) and very demoable. **Resurrect with a NON-RING payoff** — dancing cut-paper figures, a sunlit street/market scene, light-trails per ostinato voice, anything but concentric rings — so it stops reading as "632 with a different palette." Full working code in git history; treat as a fresh build, redo the renderer.

### `642-piano-chroma` — `queued` (cycle-3 of 606→630 piano spine · supervised harmonic-comb NMF · SVG chroma wheel · adult / clinical — **banked, build-verified**)
The most FAITHFUL reading of 630's banked cycle-3 ("supervised NMF, fixed basis → true pitch classes, not register bands"). Keeps the proven HPSS ground, then fixes the NMF basis `W` to **12 pitch-class harmonic-comb templates** (Gaussian-in-log-freq bumps at fundamental + 6 partials for every octave A0..C8 of each chroma; positive floor; `H` updated full-strength while `W` blends only 25% toward the seed each iteration → components stay nameable). 72 KL iters chunked. Per-pitch-class Wiener mask on the original complex harmonic STFT → ISTFT → up to 13 instant click-free solo/mute/gain voices (Hammers core + present chromas; classes <8% peak energy collapse out). Renderer: **SVG chroma WHEEL** — 12 annular-sector wedges pulsing to live level, soloed wedge glows (feGaussianBlur), chromatic↔circle-of-fifths toggle. Honest 3/5: #4 (real cycle-3) + #2 (4 subsystems) + #3 (Lee&Seung'99, Smaragdis&Brown'03, Fujishima'99, Fitzgerald'10). **#1 NOT claimed** (NMF used by 630). **Why it lost to 643:** more INCREMENTAL — 4→12 voices is finer resolution but adds no NEW interaction (still solo/mute masked voices), so it's a clean 3/5 not the 4/5 REACH the jury wants; 643's REPLAY layer turns the decomposition into a playable instrument (the genuine cycle-3 "add the interactive layer" move). **Why resurrect:** it's the cleanest, most build-bulletproof piece (self-reported a FULL `npm run build` pass; pure SVG = zero GPU surface) and the SVG chroma wheel is a beautiful, scarce-renderer visual. Resurrect as the safe-glance companion, OR fold its supervised-NMF isolation into 643's salience-mask for sharper note separation. Full working code was built this cycle (treat as fresh build from this spec).

### `644-piano-respeak` — `queued` (cycle-3 of 606→630 piano spine · masked↔additive-resynth PURITY lever · Canvas2D · adult / clinical — **banked, diversity-blocked this cycle**)
The BOLDEST reconstruction reading, driven by the 2026 restoration reframe (arXiv 2603.16926, Mar 2026: masking leaves degraded stems → add a reconstruction stage that RE-SYNTHESIZES a clean source). Per pitch class, build TWO sample-aligned loop renderings: **MASKED** (softmax-normalized Wiener mask on the original complex STFT → his real isolated timbre) and **RESYNTH** (extract each note's per-frame amplitude envelope, drive a continuous-phase additive sine-oscillator bank → a glassy DDSP-lite reconstruction; cf. Serra&Smith SMS, "Sines Transient Noise" arXiv 2409.06513). A global **PURITY lever** equal-power crossfades masked→resynth for all 12 notes at once — at purity=1 you hear Karel's exact performance, same notes/timing, rebuilt entirely from pure tones. 12 Canvas2D lanes morph grainy "real" fill ↔ concentric "resynth" rings as purity moves. Honest 3/5: #4 + #2 (4 subsystems) + #3 (four named refs incl the Mar-2026 paper). **Why it lost:** **diversity gate** — Canvas2D is at 3× in the rolling-10 (624/636/640); shipping 644 would push it to 4× (the banned threshold). The concept is arguably the most surprising of the three. **Why resurrect:** next adult fire when Canvas2D is COLD in the rolling-10 — this is the truest "reconstruction beyond masking" piece and a strong 4/5 reach; OR re-skin its viz to SVG/three.js to dodge the Canvas2D count. Also a great cycle-4 fold INTO 643 (a per-note purity fader on top of the replay grains). Full working code was built this cycle (treat as fresh build from this spec).

## §442 — banked from cycle 442 kids WIDE fan-out (2 non-winners, built-and-proven, fast to resurrect)

Both were built to demoable in isolation this cycle (tsc-clean, hand-audited against the ESLint pitfalls) and benched only because **652-kids-dance-band** was the directer hit on the jury's "build the danceable register nobody is building." Resurrect either as a near-drop-in.

- **651-kids-choir-leader** (mic VOICE-pitch → in-key choir, Canvas2D, TENDER). *"What if a 4-year-old could lead a choir with their own voice?"* Real-time monophonic pitch detection (normalized autocorrelation + parabolic interp over `getFloatTimeDomainData`, RMS-gated, clamped 180–950 Hz, median+exp smoothing) → C-major-pentatonic snap (no wrong notes) → 4-voice additive choir (root/3rd/5th/octave, warm low-pass) under a scrolling luminous pitch-ribbon; always-on detuned-fifth pad; degrades to 84px in-key note buttons on mic-deny. **This is the realization of banked seed `635-kids-sing-kite`** (RESEARCH §436) and is **love-aligned** (`158-kids-hum-paint`❤️). Ambition #2 (mic+pitch-detect+additive-harmony+canvas ribbon=4) + #3 (Bobby McFerrin call-and-response / Imogen Heap harmonizer / Toca Band). **Resurrect-deepen:** swap autocorrelation → YIN/CMNDF in an AudioWorklet to kill octave-halving on breathy child voices; this is the one fragility flagged. Tender is the cleanest *un-built* kids register — strong next-kids-cycle pick.
- **653-kids-bounce-band** (device-TILT → collision-quantized beat, Canvas2D, BOUNCY/JOYFUL). *"What if tilting the tablet turned bouncing balls into a danceable BEAT?"* Tilt steers gravity (gamma/beta, first-reading=neutral zero); 2D circle physics (softened restitution, speed cap, dt-clamp); **every collision is queued onto the next 16th-note tick** via a Chris-Wilson look-ahead scheduler so chaos self-organizes into a pentatonic groove; ball identity→timbre/register; ≤3 notes/tick voice-allocator + compressor for pile-up safety; legible groove playhead ring; pointer/arrow/≥64px-D-pad fallback. Ambition #2 (tilt+collision-physics+groove-quantizer+canvas=4) + #3 (Iwai Electroplankton/SimTunes · Reactable · BlockBob). **Resurrect-deepen:** per-corner instrument zones (pour balls to swap the kit) + tilt-magnitude→tempo/swing so the steering→arrangement link is unmistakable. Note overlap risk to flag at resurrection: 619-plinko / 169-marble-run (physics→bells) — the quantized-groove twist is what keeps it distinct.

## §447 — 2026-06-16 (UTC) · two banked DEEP non-winners from cycle 447 (the piano-spine cycle-4 fire) — built-and-proven, tsc-clean in isolation, fast to resurrect (renumber on revival; max committed folder now 666)

All three cycle-447 explorers were the SAME cycle-4 concept ("the piano remembers you": a looper/phrase-memory built on Karel's 12 isolated piano notes from the 643 engine, growing itself over minutes via an **asymmetric-consonance** growth rule [De Roure 2026, arXiv:2606.16412] + level decay — NO scale-snap). They differ only in renderer/interaction/register-thread. `666-piano-still` (audio-first / minimal SVG) shipped because it uniquely answers the jury's three-juries-running standing #1 ask ("get off the glass") in the scarcest renderer while still hitting tender + cycle-4. The two below lost on the off-glass axis ONLY — both are real future builds. Each reuses the proven 643 engine (`audio.ts`/`hpss.ts`/`chroma.ts` copied verbatim) + a `consonance.ts` growth rule.

### `665-piano-loom` — `queued` (banked DEEP non-winner, cycle 447 · tap-to-stamp · **raw WebGL2** scrolling phrase-tape · adult / tender) — **the most legible "looper" visual**
**What if Karel's 12 isolated piano notes became a LOOPER you can SEE — a horizontal scrolling tape-ribbon where each tap stamps the current pitch class at the playhead, the loop re-fires stamps as the beam crosses them, un-refreshed stamps decay over ~90–130s, and the asymmetric-consonance rule buds new consonant notes every few cycles?** Raw hand-written **WebGL2 (GLSL ES 3.00)** single fullscreen-quad glow-field: cells = note stamps (hue per pitch class), flaring on fire, fading on decay, fixed warm playhead beam at center; `backend:"none"` → `text-rose-300` notice, audio still plays. Keyboard `A W S E D F T G Y H U J` + `space` for selected note; `grow⇄rest` lever. Auto-seeds a consonant C–G–E–A phrase + loops on load (alive in ~2s). Files: `page.tsx`, `ribbon.ts` (WebGL2), `consonance.ts`, + 643 engine. **Why it didn't win (cycle 447):** the clearest *visual metaphor* for the cycle-4 idea (you literally watch the loop remember you) and the boldest render — but it is **another glass-screen render** (WebGL2 at 4× in the window), exactly the monoculture the jury's Karel-facing line told this fire to break, whereas 666 gets off the glass. **Resurrect FIRST** when an adult cycle wants the cycle-4 piano looper as an ON-glass immersive piece, or fold its visible-tape metaphor into 666 as an optional "show the tape" view. Ambition equal to winner: #4 (cycle-4) + #1 (first looper of a performer's own isolated notes) + #2 (≥3 subsystems: HPSS+salience isolation + phrase-memory/decay sequencer + grain-replay synth + WebGL2 ribbon) + #5 (De Roure 2606.16412, dated cycle-day).

### `667-piano-tide` — `queued` (banked DEEP non-winner, cycle 447 · drop-a-note · **three.js** reflective ripple pool · adult / tender-meditative) — **the most immersive/beautiful**
**What if the looper were a still reflective POOL — drop one of his notes and a slow ring spreads across the water, rings interfere and persist, and as a ring crosses the center it re-fires his note, so a quiet phrase accretes on the surface and grows itself over minutes?** **three.js** horizontal water surface: interfering-ripple height field summed in a fragment shader, soft specular glints + caustic banding, deep teal→indigo water with warm amber/rose note glows — deliberately NOTHING like 643's orbiting-star constellation (no points/orbits/core). `calm⇄grow` lever; ~13s loop; decay over minutes. Files: `page.tsx`, `scene.ts` (three.js), `consonance.ts`, + 643 engine. **Why it didn't win (cycle 447):** the most beautiful/immersive of the three — but (a) **another glass-screen render** (off-glass was the jury's explicit ask for this fire) and (b) **three.js is at 3× in the window AND is 643's own renderer family**, so it's the closest of the three to the thing it extends (the jury wants a cycle-N to open a *new world*, not re-skin the prior renderer). **Resurrect** for an immersive-visual adult cycle when off-glass isn't the priority, or fold its ripple-interference water shader into a future water/tide piece. Ambition equal to winner (#4 + #1 + #2 ≥3 subsystems incl. three.js water shader + #5 De Roure).

## §450 — 2026-06-16 (UTC) · two banked WIDE non-winners from cycle 450 (KIDS) — both fully built to demoable + self-reported tsc/ESLint-clean in isolation; fast to resurrect (renumber on revival; max committed folder now 675)

Cycle 450 ran **WIDE** — 3 orthogonal kids "real harmonic event" explorers (honoring JURY 2026-06-16 provocation #1: kill the pentatonic/scale-snap crutch, let a 4yo *hear* harmony move), each a DIFFERENT harmonic event × input × output × palette. **Winner: `675-kids-deep`** (touch stones over a migrating drone → functional recontextualization; WebGL2 abyss). Won on the §448/§450 developmental anchor: for an actual 4yo the perceptible harmonic events are (a) *change itself / a moving drone* and (b) *closure & its denial* — NOT the major/minor emotional flip (that emerges ~age 7–8). 675 lands (a) directly, is the freshest concept vs the recent kids stream, and uses the renderer-scarce WebGL2. The two strong losers, banked:

### `674-kids-two-moons` ("Two Moons") — `queued` (tilt → continuous MODE-GLIDE → **three.js** crossfading sun-moon↔ghost-moon · kids/family / cosmic awe, bright↔eerie) — realizes banked §448 seed `668-two-suns`
**What if tilting a tablet turned a happy song spooky — and back — so a child discovers the SAME notes feel completely different?** A single mood dial `m∈[0,1]` (tilt `gamma`, or a big gradient slider, or arrow keys) drives **parallel-mode interchange done as a continuous portamento morph**: a sustained chord's voices keep their identity and `setTargetAtTime`-glide between bright (Lydian-ish: maj3, #4, leading tone) and dark (Phrygian-ish: min3, b2, b7) tunings — the third audibly melts maj→min, the 7th flattens. Six 72px melody pads play the "same" degrees, recolored by `m`. **Strictly NO pentatonic scale-snap** (continuous semitone interpolation, no quantization). three.js: gradient sky + star field + two crossfading celestial bodies hue-shift with the mode. Kid-safe master chain, ~2s auto-demo, full degradation (slider/keys), `text-rose-300` no-sensor note. Ref: **Kate Hevner, "The Affective Character of the Major and Minor Modes" (Am. J. Psych. 47, 1935)**. Ambition honest **2/5** (#2 ≥3 subsystems: tilt→mode-engine + reharmonizer/portamento synth + three.js render = 4 · #3 Hevner 1935). Files: `page.tsx` / `mode.ts` / `audio.ts` / `gl.ts` / `README.md`. **Why it lost:** the most visually spectacular of the three, BUT the lab's own §448 developmental research says the major↔minor *emotional* flip isn't reliably legible to a 4yo (emerges ~7–8) — it's a **family / older-kids (6+)** piece, not a 4yo one; the continuous-glide mitigation makes it "the change itself" but the central *discovery* still leans on mode-affect the target age lacks. **Resurrect when:** a "Resonance Kids 6+ / family" tier opens (it's the flagship for that audience), or as an ADULT cosmic mode-morph (the three.js + portamento-reharmonization engine is reusable and renderer-scarce). Strongest visual of the batch.

### `676-kids-night-boat` ("A Little Boat on the Night Sea") — `queued` (rock-the-tablet DeviceOrientation → **deceptive→authentic CADENCE** → audio-first / minimal inline-SVG · kids / night-voyage mystery→arrival) — **the top OFF-GLASS resurrect**
**What if a 4yo could rock a little boat across a night sea, eyes closed, and hear an unseen choir get *almost* home, turn to mystery, then land on home?** OFF-GLASS / audio-first: the screen is a minimal inline-SVG horizon + bobbing boat + a glow that warms only at homecoming. A **functional-cadence engine** (`cadence.ts`, I/ii/IV/V/vi/♭VII in D) voices one chord per detected rock (gamma zero-crossings; fallback ≥64px button + Spacebar; iOS motion permission from the Start tap). Three phrases loop: **deceptive V–vi** (withheld home, "the boat turns to mystery") ×2, then **authentic V–I** (arrival, audio + glow bloom warmer). The V carries the real leading tone (C♯) so the deceptive turn registers as genuine denial. **NO pentatonic snap** — real triadic functional harmony; night-voyage register, NOT cozy-lullaby (warm color reserved strictly for the home cadence). Kid-safe master chain, never-silent sea/drone bed, auto-demo that real rocking preempts. Ref: **deceptive cadence (V–vi) in J.S. Bach's four-part chorales**. Ambition honest **2/5** (#2 ≥3 subsystems: motion-rock detect + cadence state-machine + choral pad synth + SVG = 4 · #3 Bach chorale deceptive cadence). Files: `page.tsx` / `audio.ts` / `cadence.ts` / `README.md`. **Why it lost:** excellent and on the firmest developmental ground (closure & its denial IS perceptible to a 4yo), and it's the OFF-GLASS piece the jury keeps asking for — BUT `669-kids-hum-answer` shipped off-glass kids built on tension→resolution just **2 cycles ago** (cycle 448); shipping a second off-glass closure piece back-to-back would cost diversity. **Resurrect FIRST when off-glass is cold in the rolling window** (it's the strongest banked off-glass candidate the lab has) — likely the very next kids cycle that isn't adjacent to 669. Near-drop-in.

## Banked from Cycle 461 (adult DEEP fire — ONE concept: *the music dreams a picture, and the dream changes the music* — a closed audio→image→audio loop, JURY 2026-06-17 #4 "extend the ceiling builds [689], not a sixth tonal-tension toy." Two technical approaches to **how the image returns to audio**. Winner shipped = `704-spectral-seance` [the LITERAL return trip — image re-sonified as a spectrogram]. Loser banked below; folder `rm -rf`'d after the winner's authoritative build passed. Renumber on revival — max committed folder now 704.)

### `703-latent-oracle` — `queued` ⭐ (the SOFT-closure twin of 704: the loop closes via **color**, not literal re-sonification. A 5–7 voice D-Dorian pad ensemble auto-evolves through four weather phases [calm→luminous→veiled→resolving]; each phase self-composes a prompt → flux image → **WebGL2 ping-pong feedback dissolve** [Anadol latent-flow — old dreams smear into new, never fully vanish] → dominant color sampled offscreen → glided back into the synth [hue→filter cutoff/detune-spread, brightness→shimmer/reverb send, saturation→dissonance], so the SOUND drifts toward the color of what it just dreamed; the HUD shows the fed-back color swatch + resulting cutoff/shimmer/diss values.) This is the long-banked **687-latent-oracle** seed, fully built this cycle (Refik Anadol latent data-paintings + Memo Akten *Learning to See*; guarded flux route + procedural domain-warped fog fallback, runs zero-network). **Why it lost to 704:** the closure is a *soft parameter-nudge* whose audible range **narrows without a `FAL_KEY`** (the procedural fog skews cool/dark, so the keyless drift is subtle) — and Karel reviews on the public preview, which is usually keyless, so 704's *literal* image-as-spectrogram return trip demonstrates the closed loop far more legibly with no key. 703 is also closer to existing parameter-mapping than 704's never-before-seen re-sonification. **Why resurrect:** it's the more *beautiful* of the two (the Anadol feedback smear is gorgeous) and the only one that closes the loop back into continuous *synthesis* rather than into a re-played score — **resurrect FIRST on a night Karel is awake to trigger a live `FAL_KEY`**, when vivid flux images would push the color-drift through its full range. Built files were complete & build-clean (`page.tsx`/`audio.ts`/`render.ts`/`dream.ts`/`api/route.ts`/`README.md`); near drop-in (renumber). This finally retires the standing **687-latent-oracle** queue entry above (still listed there for the WebGL2-cold resurrect note — now realized).

## §467 banked seeds (cycle 467, ADULT DEEP — ONE concept *Shadow Duet: trade fours with a machine that answers in the gaps* built via THREE "minds" for the duet partner; winner shipped = `718-duet-paths` [concatenative shadow of Karel's REAL recorded piano — the truest realization of his MORNING ask "wired to your Paths piano"]. Renumber on revival; max committed folder now 718.)

### `716-shadow-duet` — `queued` ⭐ (the **heuristic / Voyager-taste** mind — the lab's most BULLETPROOF duet build: pure synth, zero network/key dependency, so it's a perfect no-fetch companion if 718's grain-answer proves unmusical OR for an offline/installation context). **Play a phrase; when you pause, a rule-based responder answers — complementary register, inverted contour, density-matched, weighted scale step-walk, forced consonant chord-tone landing — a reply structurally guaranteed never to echo your pitches.** Key+mode inferred by scoring 12 tonics against Dorian/Mixolydian/Aeolian templates (D Dorian fallback). Built files (removed; clean tsc+eslint self-reported): `page.tsx` (36 KB — three inputs [Web MIDI hot-plug + `asdfghjkl`/`wetyuo`/`z/x` keyboard + on-screen ≥44px keys], phrase detection, heuristic responder, ducking, idle auto-demo, Canvas2D river, FM-epiano L / flute R + binding pad, master→gain(0.45)→comp(−3/20:1)) + `README.md`. Named refs: **George Lewis *Voyager*** · jazz trading-fours. Ambition honest 2/5 (#2 ≥4 subsystems + #3 named refs). Tags: MIDI/keyboard INPUT · Canvas2D OUTPUT · heuristic call-response TECH · jazz-responsive VIBE. **Why it lost:** Karel's ask was specifically "wired to your *Paths* piano" → 718 (his real recording) is the truer realization, and 718 binds this cycle's MACataRT dive; 716 is the generic-synth version. **Resurrect** as the dependency-free Shadow Duet (installation/offline), or merge its key-inference + step-walk taste INTO 718 as a cleaner answer-line generator. **Deepen:** velocity-sensitive dynamics (soft phrase → tender answer); harmonic memory (a pad following a slow form seeded by your recurring pitch-sets).

### `717-duet-mirror` — `queued` ⭐ (the **Continuator / style-learner** mind — the genuinely SURPRISING one: the partner *learns you* live and its answers grow to sound like your own playing recombined; the lab's first real-time live-trained style model). **As you play, a variable-order [orders 1–3, back-off] Markov model absorbs your pitch-INTERVAL sequence [key-agnostic, ±12] + your rhythm [fast/med/slow buckets]; on each phrase-end it folds the phrase into a persistent session model and answers by SAMPLING that model seeded from your tail — so it starts generic-Dorian and, over ~a dozen phrases, mirrors *your* vocabulary back transformed.** A "learned: N phrases" + "memory richness" meter make the growth legible. Built files (removed; clean tsc+eslint self-reported): `page.tsx` (31 KB — same three inputs, the live Markov learner, ducking, idle demo that FEEDS the model + ticks the count, Canvas2D river, FM-epiano L / bell R + pad, master→gain(0.42)→comp limiter) + `README.md`. Named refs: **François Pachet *Continuator* (2002)** · IRCAM **OMax / Somax2**. Ambition honest 3/5 (**#1** first lab live-trained style model [grep-checked: no prior dream piece builds a variable-order Markov over the player's own live input] + #2 ≥4 subsystems + #3 named refs). Tags: MIDI/keyboard INPUT · Canvas2D OUTPUT · live-Markov style-learning TECH · jazz-responsive VIBE. **Why it lost:** the "it sounds like *you*" payoff is the LEAST legible at a silent 06:30 glance (the learned-count climbs but you can't HEAR the learning without playing a minute yourself), and it doesn't bind this cycle's concatenative dive. **Why resurrect ⭐:** it carries the only #1 of the three and the freshest *surprise* — lead a future adult fire framed as "**the instrument learns you over a session**" (deepen: a "play me back what I taught you" button that improvises a solo entirely from the accumulated model; persist the model to localStorage so it remembers you across visits). Strongest when the diversity pick wants a *learned* technique, not a sensor reskin.

## §469 — 2026-06-18 · adult DEEP fire ("fly inside your own recording" — ONE concept, 3 technical approaches; answers JURY 2026-06-18 #3 "extend the ceiling builds 710/718, reward depth" + #1 "ban Canvas2D, use the scarce WebGPU stack" + #5 "use his real music") — winner `720-paths-grainfield`; **2 banked seeds**

Cycle 469 ran an adult **DEEP** fan-out. Research path (b)/(c): extends ceiling builds **710-presence-bloom** (WebGPU compute particle field) + **718-duet-paths** (concatenative grains of Karel's real Welcome Home piano), grounded by CataRT + Audio Latent Space Cartography (RESEARCH §469). All three turn his real recording into a navigable corpus you move through to re-sound it. **Winner: `720-paths-grainfield`** — the only approach on WebGPU compute (lab's scarcest renderer; JURY #1's literal ask) + truest 710 cycle-2. Both losers were demoable + self-reported clean tsc+eslint; authoritative winner-only `npm run build` (exit 0, 720 prerendered static) is the gate; losers moved to `/tmp/dream-losers/` then removed before it.

**NUMBERING NOTE (important):** `720-paths-grainfield` is now a **real committed folder** → **max committed folder after this fire = 720.** The §468 banked kids seed-labels "720-kids-shake-pop" / "721-kids-blow-balloon" were never committed folders — when resurrected they must be **renumbered to ≥721** (suggest `721-kids-shake-pop` ⭐, `722-kids-blow-balloon`). The two adult seeds below are likewise just text — renumber to ≥721 when consumed.

Resurrect by re-spawning the brief:

- **`paths-spectral-cloud`** ⭐ (was built as 722; renumber ≥721 when consumed) — **the most conceptually novel of the three.** His recording's full **STFT becomes a 3D point cloud** (time × log-frequency × loudness) you **orbit and fly the camera through**; flying into a (time,freq) region re-sounds the grains living there — *navigating the SHAPE of his sound plays it back*. Hand-written radix-2 FFT (1024 window / 512 hop), sparse-peak cloud capped ~55k three.js Points, additive + fog, CataRT-style region resynthesis. Named refs: **Xenakis UPIC** (drawing sound in a time-freq plane) + **Ikeda** data-as-points. Extends loved **243-spectral-cloud** + **321-spectral-flight** + 718. Lost only because it shares three.js (already 3× in the window) with 721 while 720 carried the scarcer WebGPU compute — but its interaction ("fly the spectrogram to play it") is arguably the lab's freshest. **Next-cycle deepening:** STFT in a Web Worker w/ progress bar; k-d-tree nearest-grain (replace stride-scan); TSL compute pass so points pulse with their grain's envelope as they sound; UPIC-style "draw a path, auto-fly it" re-composition.
- **`paths-constellation`** (was built as 721; renumber ≥721 when consumed) — Karel's recording as a **drifting three.js star-galaxy**: each grain a star, *angle* = time / *radius* = brightness / *height* = loudness (spiral arms); a soft violet attractor eases toward the pointer and re-sounds the nearest ~3 stars with curl-noise drift + orbiting camera. Rides the loved GPU-points cluster **130-tsl-particle-compute** + **262-aurora-particle** + 718. Warmest/most-legible of the three; lost on renderer-scarcity (three.js, not WebGPU) and a ZCR brightness proxy vs a true FFT centroid. **Next-cycle deepening:** Web-Worker/GPU kd-tree for 100k+ grains; a "constellation-line" overlay tracing the attractor's path (the actual concatenative score); reverb send sized by attractor speed.

**Standing adult seeds still unshipped (carried):** `717-duet-mirror` ⭐ (the live "learns-you" Continuator style-learner, the only #1 banked from cycle 467) · the **WebGPU-compute audio-reactive field** modulated by his piano FFT (Ghost-Arcade June-2026 reference, RESEARCH §468; now that 720 lands a real WebGPU-compute foundation in the lab, this is the natural deepening) · **real cross-device multi-user** (JURY #4 — all 3 multiplayer pieces are same-browser BroadcastChannel; 0× truly cross-device).

### IDEAS §473 — banked from cycle 473's adult DEEP cross-device fire (winner shipped `729-piano-portal-jam`)

Both are complete, build-reviewed implementations (compile+lint-clean) moved to `/tmp/dream-losers/` this cycle — resurrect by copying the folder back in and renumbering above the then-current max. The shared concept: **a genuine cross-device WebRTC jam on Karel's real Welcome Home piano** (jury #4 lab-first), with the "send note-events not PCM — both peers re-sound from the same corpus" twist. They differ on the **signaling handshake × renderer**.

- **`728-piano-relay-jam`** ⭐ (queued · adult · zero-server **copy-paste two-code** WebRTC handshake · raw **WebGL2** two-color shared field · concatenative grains of his recording) — the purest zero-dependency handshake (native `CompressionStream` gzip → base64url SDP codes, non-trickle ICE, no backend at all) on the **scarcer renderer** (WebGL2 vs the winner's three.js). *Why it lost:* the two-codes-back-and-forth copy/paste is higher-friction than 729's shareable link, so it's less likely a real person completes the two-phone handshake — and retiring the BroadcastChannel asterisk *in practice* was the deciding axis. *Why resurrect:* it's the most robust-by-construction link (no server, no lambda fragility) AND carries the scarcer renderer; pair its WebGL2 field with 729's `navigator.share` link flow for the best of both. Files exist verbatim.

- **`730-piano-room-jam`** ⭐ (queued · adult · friendly **4-letter room code** via a **guarded serverless SDP relay** [`api/route.ts`, POST-only, guard-compliant] · **WebGPU** compute particle field · concatenative grains) — by far the **friendliest UX** (type the same 4 letters, no copy/paste) and the **scarcest renderer** (WebGPU, 1×). *Why it lost — the decisive flaw, in its own README:* the relay stores `{code→{offer,answer}}` in a **module-level `Map` in per-lambda memory**, and Vercel routes the two peers to *different* lambda instances where that map isn't shared → the offer one peer deposits may **never reach the other**, so on real Vercel it likely **won't connect** — which fails the jury's exact ask ("make it *actually* cross-device"). *Why resurrect (high value):* it's the **best piece the moment a backing store exists.** The dedicated next step is to ask Karel to approve moving the relay store to **Vercel KV / Upstash Redis** (a `package.json` change, which only he can make per the scope fence); with that one swap, 730 becomes the real magic — frictionless room-code cross-device jamming on the scarcest renderer. Flag this dependency ask explicitly in a morning question.

## §474 — 2026-06-19 · kids DEEP fire (ONE concept — *Star Scoop: reach bare hands into a 3D night sky and scoop handfuls of glowing stars that chime* — 3 renderer × synth approaches, orchestrated; resurrecting the twice-banked ⭐ `726-kids-star-scoop` seed = the directest answer to JURY 2026-06-18 #3 "stop banking and walking away") — winner `731-kids-star-reach` (raw WebGL2); **2 banked seeds**

Cycle 474 ran a kids **DEEP** fan-out (deliberate alternation off the last two kids WIDE fires, §470/§472). ONE embodied/off-glass concept — bare-hands MediaPipe scoop of a glowing star-field that rings consonant bell clusters, awe/wonder, **no beat/loop/groove** — attacked via three renderers: three.js (730) · raw WebGL2 (731, WON) · WebGPU compute (732). Research path (c): consumed the twice-banked ⭐ seed `726-kids-star-scoop` (§472/§470, born of RESEARCH §470's iOS-WebGPU finding); RESEARCH §474 confirmed gesture→particle-swarm is a live 2026 commodity but the *musical-scoop* mapping is the open Resonance move. **Winner = `731` (raw WebGL2)** on the deciding axis of **renderer-scarcity + iOS reviewer-path reliability**: WebGPU is warming hard in the rolling window (721/724/727, the scarce renderer becoming its own monoculture) and three.js was just used last cycle (729), where **raw WebGL2 is the scarcest renderer AND the most bulletproof on Karel's 06:30 phone** (iOS has WebGL2 reliably; zero CDN renderer dep beyond the shared MediaPipe). All three were demoable + self-reported clean tsc+eslint; the two losers `mv`'d to `/tmp/dream-losers/` before the authoritative winner-only build. ⚠️ The authoritative `npm run build` **compiled + linted clean** (zero issues in the winner folder) but couldn't run static-generation — the container's anomalous **4096 fd ceiling** killed it with `EMFILE`; proven environmental (pristine `main` fails identically — see STATE.md cycle 474). **Max committed folder after this fire = `731`.** Resurrect by re-spawning the brief + the prior folder structure (both losers were fully built in `/tmp/dream-losers/` this fire):

### `730-kids-star-scoop` — `queued` ⭐ (the three.js sibling — the renderer every 2026 hand-particle repo reaches for; warmest/most-legible glow). **Reach bare hands into a 3D night sky and scoop handfuls of glowing stars that chime.** MediaPipe Hands (CDN, analysis-only, numHands 2) → a **three.js** `THREE.Points` field of ~2,400 additive glow sprites with `FogExp2` depth, swaying camera, per-star twinkle. Close a fist → nearest ~20 stars gather to the palm + a soft sparse C-major-pentatonic cluster; open wide → owned stars spill upward with a 5-step rising glissando; hand height indexes register. Kids-safe `voices → gain 0.28 → lowpass 6.5k → comp −10/20:1`, always-on open-fifth (C2+G2) drone, ghost two-hand auto-demo on camera-deny/idle >3.5s (`text-rose-300` notice), Canvas2D fallback reproducing the gather/spill physics, full teardown (three.js dispose + forceContextLoss). **Tags:** MediaPipe-hands camera INPUT (off-glass) · three.js 3D-points OUTPUT · gesture particle-gathering + spatial bell-cluster TECH (NOT loop/groove) · awe/wonder VIBE. **Named refs:** MediaPipe Hands · *Journey* (thatgamecompany) · Refik Anadol. **Why it lost:** three.js was just used last cycle (729) and is the warmest of the trio in the rolling window; the WebGL2 sibling is the scarcer, more iOS-bulletproof scarce renderer. **Resurrect when** the kids side wants the richest-looking glow and three.js is cold in the rolling-10, or as the direct three.js comparison to 731. Honest 2–3/5 (#2 ≥3 subsystems [MediaPipe hands + three.js 3D point-field + spatial bell-cluster audio] + #3 named refs). Full build exists at `/tmp/dream-losers/730-kids-star-scoop/` this fire (treat as a fresh build on revival).

### `732-kids-star-cradle` — `queued` ⭐ (the WebGPU-compute sibling — the scarcest, biggest-particle-count renderer; the bolder swing once WebGPU cools). **Same Star Scoop concept on a WebGPU compute particle field.** MediaPipe Hands → ~**12,000 particles** in a storage buffer integrated each frame by an **inline WGSL compute pass** (ambient drift + per-hand attract/repel falloff + a *cradling swirl* when a fist closes), drawn as instanced additive glow sprites (compute + render passes). Hand position(s) + grab strength (0..1, 1 = fist) passed as uniforms; fresh fist-close/open events edge-detected in JS ring a pentatonic bell cluster / rising glissando; hand height = register. **First-class Canvas2D fallback** runs the *same* gather/spill physics (~2,600 glow blobs) so the reviewer's likely no-WebGPU path still feels good; a badge shows the live renderer ("WebGPU compute · 12k stars" vs "Canvas2D fallback"). Kids-safe `gain 0.28 → lowpass 7k → comp −10/20:1`, always-on open-fifth drone, ghost two-hand auto-demo, all GPU init try/caught (never throws into the loop), full teardown (destroy buffers + device). **Tags:** MediaPipe-hands camera INPUT (off-glass) · WebGPU-compute particle OUTPUT (Canvas2D fallback only) · gesture particle-gathering + spatial bell-cluster TECH (NOT loop/groove) · awe/wonder VIBE. **Named refs:** MediaPipe Hands · *Journey* · Refik Anadol. **Why it lost:** WebGPU is the most ambitious renderer but is warming to a monoculture itself (721/724/727 — 3 of the last kids/adult ships) and WebGPU-on-iPad (Safari 26) is still fragile, so Karel's 06:30 phone glance most likely lands on the Canvas2D fallback — whereas 731's WebGL2 runs the real scarce renderer reliably on iOS. **Resurrect when** WebGPU is cold in the rolling window AND there's a real iPad to verify the compute path, OR as the spectacle/installation version (12k GPU particles read bigger than 731's 2,600). Honest 2/5 (#2 ≥3 subsystems [MediaPipe hands + WebGPU compute particle field + spatial bell-cluster audio] + #3 named refs). Full build exists at `/tmp/dream-losers/732-kids-star-cradle/` this fire.

## §475 (2026-06-19) — banked from the adult DEEP disintegration fire (winner: `734-paths-tape-erosion`)

Two non-winners from cycle 475's 3-way bake (one concept — *Karel's real Welcome Home recording slowly disintegrates & re-forms over a 6–8 min arc; Basinski* The Disintegration Loops *+ Eno* Music for Airports/Reflection — three renderer attacks). Both are demoable-grade and clear the ambition floor; resurrect either when the renderer rotation favors it.

### `733-paths-disintegrate` ⭐ — WebGPU compute, 40,000 GPU particles
The boldest, scarcest-renderer swing at the same concept. A WGSL **ping-pong storage-buffer compute shader** updates 40k particles in parallel; per-movement behavior (*Intact* orbit → *Eroding* fly-away → *Sparse* dim-drift → *Ghost* near-invisible flashes → *Reforming* home-pull), color warm-amber→cool-blue→violet→amber. Same audio engine as 734 (Hann grains of his recording, look-ahead scheduler, per-movement LP/reverb/dropout, soft pad drone, offline fallback synth). Canvas2D fallback (~8k CPU particles). **Why it lost:** a 40k-particle storm visually overlaps the lab's recent particle-storm pieces (`710-presence-bloom`, `724-presence-drift`) and 735 — the *spectral-feedback spectrogram* of 734 was the fresher register; AND WebGPU may show only its Canvas2D fallback on an iPad (WebGPU is Baseline Jan 2026 but Safari support is newer than WebGL2's). **Why resurrect:** WebGPU compute is the scarcest renderer in the window AND the boldest; once we confirm Karel's review device runs WebGPU (it likely does on desktop Safari 26 / Mac), 40k GPU particles disintegrating read much bigger than the Canvas2D fallback. Built monolithic (1255-line page.tsx) — refactor to the 734 file-split on resurrection. Full code was preserved off-tree this cycle; rebuild from the README spec.

### `735-paths-revenant` ⭐ — three.js, 60,000 `Points` volumetric ghost (Anadol lineage)
The warmest-looking attack: 60k `THREE.Points` with `AdditiveBlending` + `FogExp2` in three radial shells (dense core 55% / mid 30% / outer veil 15%), warm-amber-core→cool-blue-veil; every frame the active-point fraction (18%–100%), scatter, opacity, fog density and slow camera-orbit are driven by the 5-movement state + live FFT; a core glow mesh pulses with bass. Same grain/movement audio engine; Canvas2D 3k-particle fallback. Named ref **Refik Anadol — data-as-fog** (*Melting Memories* / *Living Architecture*). **Why it lost:** three.js was just shipped last cycle (`729-piano-portal-jam`) so it's the least-diverse renderer of the three, and a point-cloud ghost again overlaps the particle-storm register; the *Reforming* movement only restores ~60% density (intentional but reads incomplete), and 60k per-frame JS position updates risk <60fps on low-end devices. **Why resurrect:** it is the warmest, most "Anadol installation" of the three — pull it when three.js has cooled in the rotation and pair it with `@react-three/postprocessing` `UnrealBloomPass` (the README notes bloom is currently faked via additive blending) for a genuinely richer glow.

### Process note for next cycle (worktree quirk)
Builder subagents this cycle self-isolated into **git worktrees** (`.claude/worktrees/agent-*`), so one builder's files (734) were invisible in the main tree until the agent finished — nearly got curated out as "empty/failed." Next fire: after spawning, check `git worktree list` / the worktree paths as well as the main tree before judging a builder "broken." (`.claude/worktrees` is gitignored — safe, just invisible.)

### 734 next-cycle deepening (the multi-cycle commitment)
`734-paths-tape-erosion` is designed to extend: (cycle-2) drive the read-head + erosion params off **onset/phrase detection** of his recording (spectral flux) so the decay reflects his *musical* structure, not a fixed scrub; add a **longer algorithmic reverb tail** (current convolver is short — the *Ghost* movement wants seconds of wash); optional **WebGPU compute** port (resurrect 733's engine) for a denser field on capable devices. (cycle-3) a gentle single-touch "press to slow the decay / hold a motif" interaction so the listener can *resist* the erosion — the Basinski tension made playable.

## §479 banked seeds (2026-06-19, from the cycle-479 adult WIDE fire)

### ⭐ `745-room-listener` — the lab's FIRST in-browser neural net (status: queued, HIGH priority)
**The brief that was built and banked, not shipped.** The room, sung: a real neural network (**TensorFlow.js + COCO-SSD**, `lite_mobilenet_v2`, loaded from `esm.sh` CDN via `webpackIgnore`'d dynamic `import()`; weights from `storage.googleapis.com`, CORS-open — no npm install, no API route) watches the live camera at ~5fps on a 320px offscreen copy and turns **each detected object into a sustained Web Audio voice** — class name → hashed D-dorian pitch, box-x → pan, box-y → filter cutoff, size×confidence → loudness; objects fade in/out as they enter/leave frame. The chord is a living portrait of your space. **Why promising:** ambition **3/5** (#1 first TFJS/neural inference in the lab — grep-verified never used; #2 ≥4 subsystems; #3 Memo Akten *Learning to See*), the exact "novel technique → ceiling" the jury keeps asking for, and **triple love-aligned** (`101-camera-song`, `86-sound-to-video`, `323-latent-condensation`). **Why banked not shipped:** for an unattended cycle I couldn't verify, its entire headline lives inside an **unverifiable CDN model load** (ghost fallback would show a generic substitute if it fails), and the builder shipped it on **Canvas2D** — the renderer the 2026-06-18 jury most loudly told the lab to stop defaulting to. **How to resurrect (next adult cycle, DEEP):** rebuild on a **scarce renderer** (WebGL2/WebGPU per JURY #1); **verify the esm.sh model load actually resolves** under the deploy network (or self-host weights / pin a known-good CDN); keep the robust ghost fallback. This is the strongest banked seed in the queue — the boldest swing and the most jury-and-love-aligned. (Working code exists in this cycle's transcript / the curated-out folder if a fast restart is wanted.)

### `746-orbit-bell` — live ISS telemetry → cosmic globe (status: queued)
Hear the ISS overhead in real time: `https://api.wheretheiss.at/v1/satellites/25544` (keyless, CORS-open, read-only GET — no `guard`), polled ~every 4s. Altitude → drone register, velocity → tremolo rate, latitude → harmonic tilt + hemisphere pan; the instant `visibility` flips `daylight`↔`eclipsed` between polls, ring an **inharmonic struck bell** (bright entering sun, dark entering shadow). Visual: three.js shader globe with a **live-computed day/night terminator** (sub-solar point from UTC), pulsing ISS marker on the real ground track, rolling orbit trail, starfield, crossing flash synced to the bell. **Why promising:** genuine live real-world sonification on a source the lab hasn't used (orbital telemetry); event-detection (terminator crossings) drives a designed bell; self-contained ghost-orbit sim (~51.6° track, compressed eclipse cadence) keeps it fully alive offline. **Why banked:** honest 2/5 (#2 + #3 NASA/*Quindar*); cosmic-space-data overlaps existing `314-solar-wind` territory, and `744`'s compass was the higher-confidence, more-novel pick this cycle. **Resurrect** as a cosmic palette-extension (loved-cosmic-adjacent) or fold the terminator-bell idea into a future space piece.

## §482 banked seeds (2026-06-19, from the cycle-482 kids DEEP fire — "a child says a word and it becomes music")

Both were built to demoable and curated out (winner was `752-kids-word-band`). Working code existed in this cycle's transcript / the curated-out folders; rebuild from the brief below. Both clear the floor (#2 ≥3 subsystems + qualified-#5 RESEARCH §411/§482 + kids-first speech) and dodge every JURY 2026-06-19 ban.

### ⭐ `751-kids-say-a-zoo` — semantic word → singing creature (status: queued, HIGH priority — the most pure-4yo-delightful of the three)
**The brief that was built and banked.** A 4-year-old SAYS a word — "dog!", "sunshine!" — and it instantly becomes a bright bouncy **creature** that sings, filling a sunny Canvas2D meadow with the child's own vocabulary as a living consonant chord-garden. Mapping: word LENGTH → creature size; stable HASH → bold hue + base pitch (quantized C-major pentatonic, nothing wrong); VOWEL count → sung-phrase length (1–4 notes); hash bit-fields → eye/leg/wing style. Cap ~12 creatures (oldest hops off). Web Speech API (auto-restart) + 8 always-visible ≥64px emoji picture buttons (🐶🐱☀️⭐🐟🐦🌸🌈, identical mapping, zero-mic playable) + 3s auto-demo. Kids-safe chain (master ≤0.27 → lowpass 5.5kHz → 20:1 compressor), always-on C2/G2/C3 pad. Refs: Toca Boca / Sago Mini. **Why promising:** the clearest, most magical cause-effect for a literal 4-year-old ("say dog → see+hear a dog"); clean compile+lint. **Why banked not shipped:** the winner `752` filled the JURY-named SVG/DOM-no-shader gap (#2) and had the more *musical* (phonetic-riff) mapping; `751` is Canvas2D (fine, scarce) but the semantic one-chord-per-creature is less musically rich. **Resurrect FIRST** next kids cycle — strongest delight, and Canvas2D vs SVG diversifies off whatever 752 establishes.

### `753-kids-spell-parade` — rhythmic word → accumulating marching-band groove (status: queued)
**The brief that was built and banked.** A child SAYS a word; each letter parachutes down as a giant Canvas2D character, lands on one of five colored drum pads, and adds its beat to a **growing parade groove** (Chris-Wilson scheduler, 90 BPM, 16th grid). Letters→pads via stable mapping (KICK/SNARE/HAT/TOM/BELL); a C-pentatonic bell layer under all percussion (nothing wrong). **Long-form stateful:** the last ~5 words form the living loop, older characters march off-screen — genuinely different at minute 5 than minute 1 (the thin kids-stateful category the jury keeps asking for). Web Speech + 8 picture buttons + auto-demo; kids-safe chain + C-major drone. Refs: Incredibox; marching-band toys. **Why promising:** the only of the three that is long-form/stateful (jury-valued); "the parade grows as you talk" is a strong sustained-play hook. **Why banked:** self-reported risk — at ~40 characters across 5 pads, visual crowding/overlap on small phone screens needs tuning, and groove musicality depends on word choice (consonant-heavy words stack on one pad). Resurrect with per-pad character-spacing fixes + a light per-word rhythmic-spread so any word grooves.

## §483 — Conducted Room banked explorers (from cycle 483 DEEP fan-out; winner shipped = 754-conducted-table, Canvas2D)

The cycle-483 DEEP concept: **"The Conducted Room"** — extend 729-piano-portal-jam's WebRTC note-event ceiling to a 3+ player CONDUCTED ensemble, but replace 729's (banned) grain engine with a **non-grain phrase-remix** of Karel's real recording (onset/silence-segmented 0.6–3s phrases; each seat plays whole phrases via playbackRate register-shift). Long-form stateful arc (migrating harmonic center, breathing density, per-seat drift; minute 5 ≠ minute 0). Star/conductor-hub WebRTC topology (zero-server shareable-link handshake from 729). Ghost ensemble keeps it fully alive + sounding solo on one device; remote players fill chairs. Both banked builds are demoable + tsc/eslint-clean — they were de-selected on the renderer-scarcity axis, NOT on quality.

- **`753-conducted-orbit` ⭐ (three.js).** "Concert in the round": glowing central conductor + ~5 orbiting luminous musician-orbs over a warm cream-amber-rust radial-gradient stage (genuinely bright, not a dark void). **Conduct by dragging a baton anywhere on the stage** — vertical = dynamics, horizontal sweep speed = tempo, release holds. ~6-min arc; ghost conductor auto-sweeps after ~2.6s idle. `Hub` class stacks one Portal per chair (star). Per-pulse rings + drifting Points sparks. **Why banked:** three.js was used *last cycle* (729) → warmest renderer of the trio in the rolling window; the baton-on-a-3D-stage gesture is the most physically expressive conducting model of the three. **Resurrect when:** three.js cools in the rolling window, or as the DEEP companion if a future cycle wants the in-the-round spatial reading. Clean next step noted in its own README: true full-mesh (guests hear each other, not just host broadcast) + phrase-aware harmony (pick phrases whose detected pitch fits the current chord).

- **`755-conducted-stage` (SVG/DOM).** "Conductor's dashboard": the distinctive feature is a **long-form TIMELINE RIBBON** across the screen (6 sections, density-silhouette strokes, crawling playhead) that makes the 5.5-min stateful arc legible at a glance — past scrolls behind, structure hints ahead. Seats as DOM/SVG cards; baton control (vertical=dynamics, sweep speed=tempo). Bright warm amber/rose palette. **Why banked:** SVG/DOM shipped the **two prior cycles in a row** (748, 752) → a third would itself be a monoculture; and its self-reported caveat is that phrases fire at the current audio clock rather than sample-accurately at each future beat (tighten the look-ahead before resurrecting). **Resurrect when:** the timeline-ribbon idea is wanted as a long-form-legibility layer — it is the best of the three at *showing* the arc, and could be grafted onto the shipped 754 as an optional "score view." First fix the scheduler to schedule each beat sample-accurately.

## §484 banked seeds (2026-06-19, from the cycle-484 kids WIDE fire — "the bright/joyful/active-middle kids register, off every JURY-2026-06-19 ban")

Cycle 484 ran a kids **WIDE** fan-out (deliberate alternation off the two prior DEEP fires §482/§483; and the jury's loudest 2026-06-19 message — *every ban hardened into a new monoculture* — is a WIDE-shaped anti-monoculture ask). Three divergent **bright daylight, active/joyful** explorers (JURY #4), each off every 2026-06-19 ban (no grain-resynthesis · no WebGPU/raw-WebGL2 shader-field · no mic/camera · no dark-glow), diverging on INPUT × OUTPUT × TECHNIQUE × PALETTE. Winner shipped = **`755-kids-bounce-house`** (Canvas2D Verlet trampoline membrane — the research→build chain from RESEARCH §484, the literal JURY #2 "no-GPU-shader, Canvas2D-is-scarce-again" answer + JURY #4 bright/joyful). Both non-winners are demoable + self-reported clean tsc/eslint (no `any`, no `use*` helpers, no API route, SSR-safe); de-selected on ambition-floor height + the renderer-scarcity tiebreak, NOT on quality. Rebuild from the briefs below (full briefs are in this cycle's STATE entry / the curated-out folders).

### ⭐ `756-kids-hill-roller` — sculpt sunny hills, then TILT to roll a ball over them = play the melody you drew (status: queued, HIGH — the love-aligned tilt sibling)
**The brief that was built and banked.** A bright daylight landscape: ~10 rounded hills the child shapes by **dragging each up/down** (taller hill = higher major-pentatonic note, color shifts green→gold with pitch, immediate audio on shape). A happy ball rolls across under gravity driven by **device tilt** (`gamma`); every crest it rolls over **chimes that hill's note** — so the child *composes by sculpting terrain* then *performs by tilting*. Robust **no-tilt fallback** (giant left/right tap zones + a "Play the hills" auto-sweep that rolls the ball across the whole contour) so it's fully playable + sounding on the reviewer's desktop glance with zero tilt. SVG/DOM only (zero canvas/WebGL/shader), kids-safe chain (`gain ≤0.3 → lowpass 7500 → comp −10/20:1`), always-on pad, 3s-idle auto-demo. **Why promising:** the *sculpt-the-terrain-then-tilt-to-play* loop is a genuinely fresh kids mechanic (distinct from the saturated drop→collision pieces), it's embodied (KIDS.md tilt principle), and it is **love-aligned** (`83-kids-tilt-rain`❤️, `82-kids-color-piano`❤️). **Why banked not shipped:** honest 2/5 (#2 ≥3 subsystems [tilt input + 1D rolling-ball integrator on a child-editable height field + crest-detection synth + SVG render] + #3 [xylophone/color-pitch pedagogy · Loopimal/Toca Boca]); the winner `755` had the higher floor (research chain + lab-first cloth membrane) and Canvas2D was the cleaner JURY-#2 renderer answer than another SVG/DOM ship. **Resurrect FIRST** next kids cycle — strongest banked seed, love-aligned, and SVG/tilt diversifies off whatever ships next. Code was clean + demoable this fire.

### `757-kids-balloon-band` — inflate balloons whose pitch rises with size; released, they accrete a poppable sky-chord (status: queued)
**The brief that was built and banked.** A bright daytime sky: **press-and-hold anywhere** to inflate a bright balloon — it grows and its note **glides UP in pitch with size** (continuous gesture→pitch, quantized to C-major-pentatonic on release, theremin-glide brought down to a 4yo). **Release** → it detaches, floats up with gentle buoyancy/sway, and holds a **sustained voice**; the drifting balloons form an evolving, always-consonant **chord in the sky** the child builds. **Tap a floater** → soft bright "ding" + sparkle `THREE.Points` burst; its note leaves the chord. Cap ~10 (oldest drifts off top + fades). three.js mesh scene-graph (MeshStandardMaterial spheres, NOT a full-screen GLSL/WebGPU shader field), kids-safe chain (`gain 0.26 → lowpass 6800 → comp −10/20:1`), always-on pad, 3s-idle auto-demo, full three.js disposal (`renderer.dispose()` + `forceContextLoss()`). **Why promising:** `growth = pitch` is a clear, tactile, fresh-for-the-lab cause-effect, and the accreting *poppable* harmony is a lovely "build then take apart" loop; bright/joyful. **Why banked:** honest 2/5 (#2 ≥3 subsystems + #3 [Toca Boca/Sago Mini · balloon play · theremin glide]); three.js is warmer in the rolling window than Canvas2D, and the winner's research chain + lab-first membrane outranked it. **Resurrect when** three.js is cold in the rolling-10 and a bright accreting-harmony toy is wanted; pair the pop with a slightly richer particle burst. Code was clean + demoable this fire.
